title: Robotics Co-Founder / Head of Engineering (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

  • You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Robotics

  • You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management

  • You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs

  • You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding

  • You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively

  • You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate

  • You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues

  • You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: Business Improvement Manager
description:
The Business Improvement Manager will be responsible for supporting the business core teams in initiating and implementing improvement projects and initiatives across all elements of warehouse and transport operations, as well as supporting functions.

Working hours: Monday to Friday, 08.00-17.00

We are flexible for this role to be based at any of our sites across the country: Kent (Teynham), Evesham, Washington, Hilsea, Newton Abbot or Broxburn.

Key Duties of a Business Improvement Manager:

  • Co-ordinate and work with site senior teams to identify improvement opportunities, which have a positive impact on operational and business performance across quality, cost, service and safety elements.
  • Identify, agree and deliver improvement initiative objectives and timescales with site & regional leadership teams (Including as appropriate, local C.I. Champions).
  • Produce clear operational improvement proposals, change plans and communicate to the relevant stakeholders as required, ensuring consideration for the cost benefit and complexity of delivering the change.
  • Liaise with key stakeholders and subject matter experts to ensure the creation of fully considered and objective proposals and manage effective change processes where necessary.
  • Ensuring plans and teams maintain structure, focus and deliver on the identified improvement projects for the region.
  • Ensure savings generated through improvement initiatives are quantified and validated by Finance.
  • Complete in depth and complex analysis to identify current performance and/or warehouse/transport trends used to support any improvements.
  • Support the operational improvement implementation process; ensuring relevant operational teams have support and coaching throughout the change cycle.
  • Utilise business central support teams including IT expertise to develop potential new ways of working as part of a depot / business improvement initiative.
  • Complete analytical activity to measure potential and delivered benefits of any operational improvement proposal at either site, regional or business level ensuring capture of actual vs expected value.
  • Provide regular communication updates on the progress of improvement initiatives at either depot, regional or business level.
  • Act as a business control point to identify best practice and facilitate information sharing across the business, ensuring “lessons learned” are identified and shared.
  • Ongoing collaboration - sharing best practice and generating ideas for future projects across the FW business, as well as wider Chill Division peers
  • Support and evolve tools and techniques to improve the business continuous improvement agenda.
  • Logistics/Industry experience is highly desirable
  • Ability to apply Project Management and LEAN Methodologies to support the delivery of any given improvement initiative to completion.
  • High level of communication and influencing skills required to ensure business stakeholders deliver the required savings and productivity initiatives.
  • A proven track record managing project change within warehouse/transport operations.
  • Ability to stay focused and proactively prioritise initiatives.
  • Flexibility within the role to be able to contribute to a variety of areas within the operation.
  • Able to demonstrate a high level of attention to detail.
  • A strong operational warehousing/transport background.
  • Able to work to tight deadlines.
  • High level of expertise in MS Word, Excel, Power Point and Outlook.
  • Literate in warehouse/transport management systems
  • A proactive self-starter with the ability to be work remotely.
  • Should demonstrate the drive and desire to progress.
  • Excellent time management and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong interpersonal, communication skills and the capability to interact at local to Senior levels.

As part of our drive to make Fowler Welch a great place to work. We are proud to be an inclusive and diverse organisation where we are committed to employee development and recognising success for hard working performers.

Our dedicated learning and development programmes are open to every employee to give you the opportunity to shape your own future within logistics and continue to work in an environment where team culture thrives.

Our People are the driving force behind our success, which is why we offer a wide range of benefits which include:

  • Annual Leave – Competitive holiday entitlement of 25 days plus the normal bank bank holidays.
  • Company Bonus – We do our best work to succeed together. When we achieve our goals you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.
  • Private Medical Cover – This gives you peace of mind, you have choice, flexibility and speed of access to the most clinically appropriate care via Bupa.
  • Pension scheme – we want colleagues to enjoy a comfortable retirements so we offer a great contribution of 4.5% employee and 8% employer.
  • Life Assurance - x3 your annual salary.
  • Wellness – Via our Employee Assistance Programme we offer immediate access to a confidential telephone counselling and legal information service that operates 24 hours a days, 365 days a year.
  • Eye Care Vouchers – We can provide you with substantial savings with free eye tests and discounts on prescription glasses.
  • Reward & Recognition – We recognise that employees have gone the extra mile via Employee of the month and year, special recognition and long service awards.
  • Everyday discounts - via our benefit platform you will have access to over 50 retailer discounts for everyday savings!

If you meet the requirements for the above role and are looking for your next career opportunity please apply now and become a part of our #WinningTeam!

title: Desktop engineer (VIP support )
description:
*Job Summary:

We are looking for a Desktop Engineer to provide technical support and maintenance for desktops, laptops, printers, and other IT hardware and software.The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience supporting end users in a professional environment.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Provide technical support to end users for hardware, software, and networking issues (onsite or remotely).
  • Install, configure, and maintain desktops, laptops, printers, and other peripherals.
  • Troubleshoot system issues, including operating systems (Windows/macOS) and standard office applications.
  • Set up and support user accounts, email, and access permissions.
  • Maintain asset inventory and documentation of IT support activities.
  • Coordinate with vendors for hardware replacements and warranty claims.
  • Ensure timely resolution of tickets and maintain service level agreements (SLAs).
  • Educate and guide users on best practices and preventive maintenance.

Required Skills & Qualifications:

  • 1–3 years of experience in desktop support or a similar IT support role.
  • Strong knowledge of Windows OS (Windows 10/11) and Microsoft Office suite.
  • Basic understanding of networking (LAN, Wi-Fi, IP addressing).
  • Experience in end-user support, including remote assistance tools.
  • Good communication and problem-solving skills.
  • Ability to work independently and manage time effectively.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Certification in CompTIA A+, Microsoft MCP, or similar is a plus.
  • Experience with ticketing systems (e.g., ServiceNow, Jira, Zendesk).
  • Knowledge of Active Directory, Outlook, and remote desktop tools.*

    Disclaimer: The company is committed to ensuring the privacy and security of your information. By submitting this form, you consent to the collection, processing, and retention of the information you provide. The data collected (which may include your contact details, educational background, work experience and skills) will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating your qualifications for the position you're applying for. Your data will be stored securely and retained for the duration necessary to fulfill our hiring process. If you are not selected for the position, your data will be kept on file for a limited period in case future opportunities arise. You have the right to access, correct, or delete your data at any time by contacting us at Quess Singapore | A Leading Staffing Services Provider in Singapore (quesscorp.sg)

  • e2i is the empowering network for workers and employers seeking employment and employability solutions. e2i serves as a bridge between workers and employers, connecting with workers to offer job security through job-matching, career guidance and skills upgrading services, and partnering employers to address their manpower needs through recruitment, training, and job redesign solutions. e2i is a tripartite initiative of the National Trades Union Congress set up to support nation-wide manpower and skills upgrading initiatives. By applying for this role, you consent to Quesscorp Singapore’s PDPA and e2i’s PDPA

title: Senior Staff Nurse, Endoscopy
description:
Roles and Responsibilities.

  • To assist in the implementation of clinical policies, procedures and practice including utilization of new technologies.
  • To assist the specialist doctors with the endoscopic procedures and ensure that appropriate documentation is done.
  • To participate in quality improvement initiatives/ projects according to ESS strategic work plan.
  • To participate in the implementation of clinical practices including utilisation of new technologies, regular clinical nursing audits to ensure the standards of care is of high quality.
  • To participate in the review nursing protocols, policies and procedures for continuous improvement.
  • To assist Nurse Manager (NM)/Nurse Clinician (NC) on scheduling of staff to ensure the appropriate skill mix and competencies are in adequate proportion to number of cases scheduled on a daily basis.
  • To ensure correct billing of consumables used and procedures performed for patients.
  • Any other tasks assigned by supervisor.

Requirements

  • Diploma/Degree in Nursing or equivalent.
  • Valid Singapore Nursing Board Practicing Certificate.
  • At least 2 years of experience in Endoscopy.
  • Good interpersonal and communication skills.

title: Kinderpfleger:in (m/w/d) - Löwenkinder in 42277 Wuppertal
description:
Du möchtest Kinder in ihrer Entwicklung begleiten, ihre Neugier fördern und gemeinsam mit einem engagierten Team einen Ort zum Wachsen schaffen? Dann bist Du bei uns genau richtig!

Für unsere Kita Löwenkinder in Schwarzbach 135 in Wuppertal suchen wir eine Kinderpflegerin / einen Kinderpfleger in Teilzeit mit 25 Stunden zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt.
Bei Partou steht die hochwertige Qualität der Kinderbetreuung an erster Stelle. Unsere pädagogische Haltung basiert auf dem Kinderperspektivenansatz: Wir sehen Kinder als aktive Gestalter:innen ihrer eigenen Entwicklung und schaffen Räume, in denen sie mitbestimmen, entdecken und gemeinsam lernen können. Ergänzt wird dies durch unser Konzept „Ein Haus für alle“ sowie projektorientierte Lernformate.
Unser Haus bietet Platz für 80 Kinder im Alter von einem Jahr bis Schuleintritt. Du wirst Teil eines multiprofessionellen Teams, das Kinder aufmerksam begleitet, eng mit Familien zusammenarbeitet und sich gemeinsam Schritt für Schritt weiterentwickelt.
Gemeinsam schaffen wir einen Ort, der weit über Betreuung hinausgeht – einen Ort zum Wachsen.
  • Du betreust und unterstützt Kinder im Alter von einem Jahren bis Schuleintritt mit viel Herz und Engagement
  • Du begleitest die pädagogischen Fachkräfte im Kita-Alltag und trägst so zu einer liebevollen Atmosphäre bei
  • Du hilfst bei der Gestaltung von Spielen, Aktivitäten und kreativen Angeboten
  • Du beobachtest die Kinder aufmerksam und unterstützt ihre individuelle Entwicklung
  • Du bist Ansprechpartner:in für Kinder, Eltern und das Team
  • Abgeschlossene Ausbildung als Kinderpfleger / Kinderpflegerin mit staatlicher Anerkennung oder vergleichbare Ausbildung
  • Gute Kommunikationsfähigkeiten in Deutsch (mindestens B2) mit entsprechendem Nachweis / Zertifikat
  • Freude und Leidenschaft für die Arbeit mit Kindern
  • Einfühlungsvermögen, Geduld und Teamgeist
  • Kreativität und Offenheit für spielerische Förderangebote
  • Zuverlässigkeit und Kommunikationsstärke
  • Attraktives Vergütungspaket in Anlehnung an den TVöD Sozial- u. Erziehungsdienst (SuE)
  • Gehaltsspanne (Vollzeit): 3.249,00 € - 3.978,00 €
  • Jahressonderzahlung (Weihnachtsgeld)
  • Teilnahme an Fortbildungen, Seminare und echte Entwicklung – über unsere Partou Akademie
  • Betriebliche Altersvorsorge mit überdurchschnittlich hohem Arbeitgeberanteil
  • 30 Tage Urlaub zzgl. zwei Regenerationstage
  • Intensive Einarbeitung, regelmäßige Feedbackgespräche
  • Arbeitgeberzuschuss zur Fitnessmitgliedschaft
  • (E-)Bike-Leasing
Bitte bewirb dich direkt über diese Stelle mit deinem Lebenslauf.
Bei Bewerbungen per E-Mail können wir leider keine zeitnahe Bearbeitung garantieren.
Bei Fragen zur Stelle oder allgemein erreichst du uns gerne per E-Mail: jobs@partou.de

title: Anleitung Verkauf (m/w/d) Standort Iserlohn
description:
Die Beschäftigungs- und Qualifizierungsgesellschaft Werkhof gem. GmbH ist ein kommunaler Beschäftigungs- und Qualifizierungsträger mit vielfältigen Aktivitäten im Geltungsbereich des SGB II und SGB III. Unsere Schwerpunkte liegen in der Begleitung und Qualifizierung von Langzeitarbeitslosen sowie Jugendlichen im Übergang Schule-Beruf und Menschen mit gesundheitlichen Einschränkungen. In diesem Zusammenhang betreiben wir unter anderem mehrere Sozialkaufhäuser, in denen gespendete Möbel, Kleidung sowie Haushaltsgegenstände aller Art zu fairen Preisen angeboten werden.

Den Teilnehmenden unserer Arbeitsmarktmaßnahmen wird im Rahmen unserer Sozialkaufhäuser ein niederschwelliger Arbeitsalltag geboten sowie die Möglichkeit, wieder Tagesstruktur und sinnvolle Beschäftigung zu erleben.

Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt eine engagierte Anleitung im Verkauf (m/w/d) für unseren Standort Iserlohn. Es handelt sich um eine zunächst befristete Stelle mit einem Umfang von 38,5 Wochenstunden.

Deine Aufgabenschwerpunkte

  • Verantwortung für die verschiedenen Verkaufsbereiche und die jeweiligen Teams

  • Ausgestaltung der Preise und Räumlichkeiten in Absprache mit der Standortleitung und der Verwaltung

  • Gestaltung und Steuerung eines arbeitsmarktnahen Arbeitsalltages (nach dem Konzept der Arbeitsmarktmaßnahme) in Absprache mit der Standortleitung

  • Organisieren, Erklären und Zeigen von Aufgaben und Arbeitsschritten mit Teilnehmenden auf unterschiedlichen Niveaus

  • Regelmäßiges Einschätzen, Entwickeln und Überprüfen des Tätigkeits- und Aufgabenrahmens der geförderten Teilnehmenden

  • Motivation und Unterstützung der Teilnehmenden bei der Heranführung an den Arbeitsalltag und Entwicklung von Arbeits- und Sozialkompetenzen

  • Arbeitsplanung und strukturierte Dokumentation im Rahmen des Konzeptes der Arbeitsmarktmaßnahme nach § 16 d SGB II

  • Vermittlung und Einhaltung von Qualitätsstandards

Dein Profil

  • Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im sozialen Bereich / im Verkauf und/oder eine abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung im sozialen Bereich / Verkauf

  • Kommunikationsgeschick, Offenheit, interkulturelle Sensibilität sowie Belastbarkeit

  • Verständnis für die Zielgruppe und Einfühlungsvermögen für die Bedürfnisse der Teilnehmenden

  • Engagement für eine teamorientierte und kooperative Zusammenarbeit

Wünschenswert wären:

  • Zusatzausbildung: Ausbildereignungsschein oder die Bereitschaft, diesen zu
    erwerben

  • IT-Kenntnisse: MS-Office

  • Führerschein Klasse B

Das erwartet Sie:

  • Eine abwechslungsreiche und kreative Tätigkeit in einem engagierten Team unterschiedlicher Professionen

  • Ein besonderer Rahmen der sozialen Arbeit, integriert in ein lebendiges Second-Hand Kaufhaus mit stetig wechselndem Sortiment

  • Freiraum zur Gestaltung und Strukturierung des eigenen Arbeitsalltags

  • Eine familiäre Atmosphäre mit niedrigen Hierarchien

  • Attraktive Sozialleistungen (Corporate Benefits)

  • 30 Urlaubstage im Jahr bei einer 5-Tage-Woche, dienstfreie Tage: 24.12. und 31.12.

Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt?
Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

Bei Rückfragen wenden Sie sich an:
Hagener Versorgungs- und Verkehrs-GmbH,
Personalmanagement - Karina Schäfer, Am Pfannenofen 5, 58097 Hagen
Tel.-Nr. 02331/208-258

Bei fachbezogenen Fragen wenden Sie sich an:

Werkhof gem. GmbH,

Standortleiter - Jan Scharwächter

Tel.-Nr. 02371/436630

title: Client Services Associate
description:
Tradeweb is a globalleader in electronic trading for rates, credit, equities, and money markets. Asfinancial markets become increasingly interconnected, our technology enablesefficient, multi-asset trading on a global scale. We serve more than 3,000clients in more than 85 countries, including many of the world’s largest banks,asset managers, hedge funds, insurers, corporations, and wealth managers.

Creative collaborationand sharp client focus have helped fuel our organic growth. We facilitatedaverage daily trading volume (ADV) of more than $2.2 trillion over the pastfour fiscal quarters, topping $2.5 trillion in ADV for the first quarter of2025.

Since our IPO in 2019,Tradeweb has completed four acquisitions and doubled our revenues – and 2024was our 25th consecutive year of record revenues.

Tradeweb is a greatplace to work, recognized in 2024 by Forbes as one of America’s BestCompanies (2024) and by U.S. News & World Report as one of the BestFinancial Services Companies to Work For.

Tradeweb Markets LLC("Tradeweb") is proud to be an EEO Minorities/Females/ProtectedVeterans/Disabled/Affirmative Action Employer.

Mission: Move first andnever stop. Collaborate with clients to create and build solutions that driveefficiency, connectivity, and transparency in electronic trading.


https://www.dol.gov/ofccp/regs/compliance/posters/pdf/eeopost.pdf

GroupDetails

An exciting opportunityhas arisen to join Tradeweb's Client Service team covering the whole APACregion. Working in a fast paced, challenging and dynamic environment, the teamenjoys the responsibility of delivering the Tradeweb suite of products to ourclients on the buy side. As a Client Services Representative, you will beexpected to deliver an excellent level of service to all clients, dealing withrequests for information, queries and problems in an efficient and friendlymanner and developing and maintaining a thorough understanding of our productsand services in order to meet with our clients’ demands.

JobResponsibilities

• Support buyside clients with generalplatform / product queries but will have a focus on supporting our Repo Workflows

• Monitor trades and actively follow up to identify and resolveany issues

• Support internal teams with client connectivity and deliveringinitial user training

• Work directly with the Sales Teams to onboard new clients,including handling legal documentation and training coordination

• Take ownership some buyside procedures and documentation

• Fostering productive relationships with both Clients and otherSales & Client Services Representatives; and supporting team efforts todeliver great service, particularly around product launches

• Coordinate with clients' Back and Middle offices to configurethe relevant post-trade solutions, tailored to their transaction reporting andmonitoring requirements

• Participate in client calls to discuss their workflows andexpectations on our pre-trade, trading and post-trade offerings.

• Ensuring the system is prepared for daily trading and monitorperformance throughout the trading day

Qualifications

• Results oriented and motivated to deliver excellent clientservice

• Keen interest in financial markets; prior exposure to orenthusiasm for Repo Products is highly desirable

• Client services or financial services background preferred

• Fluent in North Asian languages (Mandarin, Japanese, Koreanetc) is beneficial

• Ability to multitask with strong organizational and timemanagement skills

• Strong MS Office skills and must be comfortable usingfinancial application

• Able to support colleagues in achieving collective goals anddemonstrate initiative and resourcefulness in independent task.

• Eager to learn, results focus and requires a high degree ofself-motivation

Additional Information

Weoffer a comprehensive range of benefits to support our employees at every stageof life and career. Our programs include enhanced parental leave,family-building and postpartum support through Maven, subsidised gym membershipand a wide range of learning and development opportunities, to name a few!While specific offerings may vary by location, our teams will be happy toprovide more detailed information about the benefits available in your regionas you move through the recruitment process.

title: Site Project Manager |HVAC|Up to $7k {hkhdv}
company: THE SUPREME HR ADVISORY PTE. LTD.
description:
Site Project Manager

  • Working Days: 5.5days
  • Working Timing: Mon to Fri 8am to 5:30pm & Sat 8am to 12:30pm
  • Working Location: Woodlands
  • Salary: $5,000 to $7,000 depending on experience

The role requires a strong understanding of HVAC systems, project management methodologies and the ability to work in both office and field settings.

Requirements

  1. Candidate must possess at least a Diploma, Advanced/ Higher/ Graduate Diploma or Degree in Mechanical, Electrical Engineering
  2. Have Site Project Management Skills
  3. Experience in chiller & air conditioning system, ACMV & HVAC (M&E)

Responsibilities & Duties

  • Knowledge of M&E project requirements and contract requirements
  • Able to manage projects/ claims/ sub-contracts

title: Feature Owner, ADAS
description:
The Role

In this pivotal role as a Product Feature Owner, you will take end-to-end responsibility for specific ADAS functionalities, such as TFL, High Beam Assist, Blind Spot Monitoring, Rear Cross & Front Cross Traffic Alert, Pre-Safe, Door Open Warning, Perception for HMI. Your focus will be to ensure these features meet customer (OEM) requirements, align with internal capabilities and roadmaps, and are delivered to spec. You will collaborate closely with OEMs, product teams, and engineering teams to define feature scope, set performance targets, and ensure compliance with industry standards. Your dedication to aligning customer needs with our strategic vision will directly enhance our vehicles’ capabilities and user experience.

Key responsibilities:

  • Feature Oversight and Delivery: Take full accountability for specific ADAS features, ensuring on-time delivery and alignment with agreed-upon specifications. Monitor the feature’s lifecycle from concept to production, addressing any deviations promptly.

  • Technical Collaboration and Hands-on Engagement: Collaborate with feature group owners and engineering teams to create feature specifications. Engage actively with the teams throughout feature development, providing early feedback, participating in testing, and supporting validation processes.

  • Customer Advocacy and Alignment: Deeply understand OEM customer needs and requirements, representing their interests internally without compromising Wayve’s objectives. Harmonise OEM requests across different customers to maximise the development of reusable assets and ensure scalability.

  • Cross-functional Communication and Coordination: Serve as the central liaison between OEMs, product teams, and engineering teams for your feature(s). Facilitate streamlined communication and reduce misunderstandings. Participate in agile processes and rituals.

  • Risk Mitigation and Strategic Alignment: Proactively identify and address potential issues before they escalate. Maintain and evolve the strategic feature roadmap as our technology progresses towards higher autonomy levels, ensuring alignment with both customer expectations and Wayve’s strategic objectives.


About you

In order to set you up for success as a Product Feature Owner at Wayve, we’re looking for the following skills and experience.

Essential

  • Experience with ADAS Feature Development: Expertise in specifying and facilitating ADAS feature delivery from concept to production, such as TFL, High Beam Assist, Blind Spot Monitoring, Rear Cross & Front Cross Traffic Alert, Pre-Safe, Door Open Warning, Perception for HMI, etc.

  • Automotive Background: Experience with automotive processes, including requirements management, V-model, ASPICE, quality, and validation.

  • Customer Advocacy and Communication Skills: Strong ability to understand and represent OEM customer needs, negotiate requirements, and align expectations between customers and internal teams.

  • Technical Competence and Hands-on Approach: Technical understanding of ADAS features, capable of engaging in discussions about trade-offs and feasibility, and actively involved in testing, evaluation, and validation processes.

Desirable

  • Experience Shipping ADAS Features into Production: Demonstrated success in delivering complete ADAS features into large-scale production automotive programs.

  • Knowledge of Automotive Quality and Safety Standards: Good understanding of automotive quality and safety standards (ASPICE, SOTIF, FuSa, …) as well as the compliance regime around them (GSR-II, DCAS, …)

  • Experience with machine learning, LLMs and recent generative AI methods and architectures

  • Strong Collaboration Skills: Ability to effectively bridge technical and business perspectives, ensuring clear communication and alignment across teams and stakeholders.

  • Innovative and Forward-thinking: A proactive approach to exploring and developing new ADAS features, keeping Wayve at the forefront of technological advancement in the industry.

This is a full-time role based in our office in Herzliya. At Wayve we want the best of all worlds so we operate a hybrid working policy that combines time together in our offices and workshops to fuel innovation, culture, relationships and learning, and time spent working from home. We operate core working hours so you can determine the schedule that works best for you and your team.

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
• Promotes internal mobility and encourages continuous development
  • Offers exposure to diverse projects and technologies

About Our Client

Our client is one of the largest Singapore-based company that provides engineering and technology solutions. In general, it works on building, maintaining, and supporting systems that help industries and organisations run smoothly.

Job Description

  • Manage and support platform services leveraging GitOps principles and best practices.
  • Design, build, and enhance CI/CD pipelines to streamline application and infrastructure deployments.
  • Develop automation solutions using scripting languages and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) frameworks.
  • Monitor platform performance, investigate system issues, and conduct root cause analysis to ensure service reliability.
  • Partner with development teams to optimize software delivery, deployment, and operational efficiency.
  • Oversee application deployments across development, testing, staging, and production environments.
  • Administer and support containerized applications and orchestration platforms.
  • Implement and maintain observability capabilities, including monitoring, logging, metrics, and distributed tracing.
  • Participate in incident management, problem resolution, and continuous improvement initiatives to enhance service quality.
  • Ensure adherence to security, compliance, and configuration management standards across the platform.
  • Create, update, and maintain technical documentation, operational procedures, and support runbooks.



The Successful Applicant

  • Hands-on experience with Kubernetes administration, configuration, and application deployments.
  • Exposure to GitOps methodologies and tools such as Argo CD or Flux.
  • Experience working with containerization technologies, including Docker and OCI-compliant containers.
  • Familiarity with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as Terraform, Ansible, or similar automation frameworks.
  • Knowledge of observability and monitoring solutions, including Elastic Stack, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, and related technologies.
  • Experience managing artifact repositories such as Nexus or JFrog Artifactory.
  • Good understanding of platform security, secrets management, vulnerability management, and security best practices.
  • Strong experience supporting and administering Linux-based environments.
  • Familiarity with Agile, Scrum, or other modern software development methodologies.



What's on Offer

As a growing firm with a tightly-knit team, the successful candidate will get the chance to contribute to a highly performing team while having the autonomy to make certain decisions for the team.

title: Application Consultant für ERP-Bausoftware (m/w/d)
description:
Wir suchen Dich. Standort: Bielefeld - Vollzeit

Hybrid: Mischung aus Büro und Homeoffice möglich

Darum Husemann & Fritz als Arbeitgeber:

Wir sind ein erfolgreiches mittelständisches Softwareunternehmen, das seit über 30 Jahren innovative Lösungen für unsere Kunden entwickelt. Mit über 16.000 Anwendern zählen wir zu den führenden Spitzenanbietern von baubetrieblicher Standardsoftware in Deutschland. Unser Erfolg basiert auf einem motivierten Team mit rund 50
Mitarbeitenden, das gemeinsam anpackt, Verantwortung übernimmt und Freude an neuen Ideen hat. Bei uns zählt nicht nur, was Du kannst – sondern auch, wie Du bist.

Aufgaben

Du möchtest die Digitalisierung der Bauwirtschaft aktiv mitgestalten? Dann bist Du bei uns genau richtig. Als Teil unseres Expertenteams bist Du die direkte Schnittstelle zu unseren Kunden. Du begleitest die Einführung und optimale Nutzung unserer ERP-Lösung im täglichen Betrieb und bist der zentrale Ansprechpartner für alle prozessualen und fachlichen Fragestellungen unserer Kunden.

  • Beratung & Konzeption: Du analysierst die betrieblichen Abläufe unserer Kunden im Bereich Bau-Technik (z. B. Angebotskalkulation, Aufmaß, Auftragsbearbeitung) und entwickelst darauf aufbauend passgenaue, nachhaltige Lösungskonzepte für den Einsatz unserer ERP-Software.
  • Implementierung & Einführung: Du begleitest Kunden bei der Einführung und Anpassung unserer Softwarelösung – von der Anforderungsaufnahme über die Konfiguration bis zur produktiven Nutzung, remote oder vor Ort.
  • Schulung & Enablement: Du führst Workshops und Schulungen durch (persönlich oder per Microsoft Teams), damit unsere Kunden das volle Potenzial der Software
    ausschöpfen und Prozesse eigenständig optimieren können.
  • Prozessoptimierung: Du identifizierst Optimierungspotenziale in Kundenprozessen, erarbeitest Lösungskonzepte und stimmst diese eng mit der Softwareentwicklung sowie dem Support-Team ab
  • Dokumentation: Du erstellst und pflegst fachliche Konzepte, Schulungsunterlagen und Projektdokumentationen, damit Kunden und Kollegen stets aktuell informiert sind.

Qualifikation

  • Qualifikation: Du hast eine fachbezogene Berufsausbildung oder ein Ingenieurstudium im Bereich Bau, Elektrotechnik Maschinenbau erfolgreich abgeschlossen oder verfügst durch Deine Vita über eine vergleichbare Qualifikation für diese Stelle (z. B. Bauleiter). Wichtig ist uns nicht Dein Lebenslauf, sondern Deine Motivation, Neues zu lernen und Kunden erfolgreich zu unterstützen. Auch engagierte Quereinsteiger sind herzlich willkommen.
  • Erfahrung: Idealerweise konntest Du bereits Erfahrung im Consulting oder Support für ERP-Systeme sammeln.
  • Branchennähe: Grundlegendes Verständnis für betriebswirtschaftliche Abläufe,
    idealerweise mit Bezug zum Baugewerbe oder Handwerk (z. B. Angebotskalkulation, Projektabrechnung, Materialwirtschaft oder Massenermittlung), ist ein großes Plus.
  • Mindset: Du hast gern Kontakt mit Menschen, verfügst über eine ausgeprägte
    Kommunikationsstärke und willst dich beruflich weiterentwickeln.

Benefits

  • Teamgeist: Ein erfahrenes und hilfsbereites Team mit einer offenen Feedbackkultur und flachen Hierarchien.
  • Sicherheit: Eine unbefristete Festanstellung in einem krisensicheren und kontinuierlich wachsenden Softwareunternehmen.
  • Arbeitsumgebung: Eine topmoderne Arbeitsumgebung mit digitalen Tools und der Flexibilität eines hybriden Arbeitsmodells (Büro & Homeoffice).
  • Onboarding: Gezielte, individuelle Einarbeitung in unsere Module sowie kontinuierliche Produktschulungen durch erfahrene Berater ab dem ersten Tag.
  • Benefits: Attraktive Extras wie Firmen-KFZ auch zur privaten Nutzung, betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Obst & Co. sowie regelmäßige Mitarbeiterevents.

Bereit, die digitale Zukunft des Bauwesens mitzugestalten?

Dann freuen wir uns auf Deine Bewerbung! Sende uns Deine Unterlagen (Lebenslauf mit Gehaltswunsch) an ----- oder bewirb dich online auf unserer Webseite. probau-s(.)de unter Karriere.

title: Assistant Manager/Manager - Asset Management (Retail)
description:
We are recruiting for a large cap-listed property investment, management and development group in Singapore. They are looking to beef up their asset management team.

Reporting to the Senior Asset Manager, you will be responsible in managing and enhancing their retail assets. The role will focus on retail planning, trade mix optimisation, tenant performance analysis, and asset enhancement initiatives to strengthen positioning, drive footfall, and maximise asset value.

Key responsibilities include supporting asset strategy for retail positioning, analyse tenant performance, sales productivity, footfall and occupancy, manage net property income (rental income and operating expenses) and capital expenditures as well as assisting in budgeting, forecasting, annual business plans and performance reporting for retail assets.

You will review and optimise trade mix to ensure strong brand representation and tenant synergy, support zoning and adjacency planning to enhance customer journey and brand visibility as well as evaluating new retail concepts.

You will support the formulation and execution of retail asset enhancement initiatives and reconfiguration projects, including financial feasibility studies and business case preparation, review floor plans and design proposals as well as supporting due diligence exercises.

Requirements

  • Degree in Real Estate, Business, Marketing, or related discipline.
  • 5 years of relevant experience in retail asset management or leasing.
  • Prior experience in asset enhancement initiatives, redevelopment or repositioning projects will be an advantage.
  • Strong understanding of retail dynamics, tenant mix strategy, and mall positioning.

All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence. Personal data provided will be used for recruitment purposes only.

Please apply with your CV in word document format. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. For other positions related to real estate or other alternative investments, please go to https://careers.tri-cap.com.sg/.

title: Leiterin /Leiter für das Sachgebiet 12 „Informationstechnik und Innerer Dienst“ (m/w/d)
description:
### Öffentliche Stellenausschreibung

### Für Beschäftigte des Landes Schleswig-Holstein und externe Bewerberinnen und Bewerber

Im Geschäftsbereich des Ministeriums für Allgemeine und Berufliche Bildung, Wissenschaft, Forschung und Kultur ist beim Schleswig-Holsteinischen Institut für Berufliche Bildung – Landesamt - (SHIBB) in Kiel zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt die Stelle

**einer Leiterin/eines Leiters**

** für das Sachgebiet 12 „Informationstechnik und Innerer Dienst“ (m/w/d)**

auf Dauer in Vollzeit oder Teilzeit zu besetzen.

### Über uns

Das Schleswig-Holsteinische Institut für Berufliche Bildung ist ein Landesamt am Standort Kiel, in dem die Aufgaben der beruflichen Bildung des Landes gebündelt werden. Dazu gehören Angelegenheiten der betrieblichen Berufsausbildung, staatliche Angelegenheiten der nichtakademischen Gesundheits- und Pflegeberufe, die obere Schulaufsicht über Berufsbildende Schulen und Regionale Berufsbildungszentren, die Personalverwaltung der Lehrkräfte an berufsausbildenden Schulen sowie die Aus-und Fortbildung der Lehrkräfte.

###

### Ihre Aufgaben

Das ausgeschriebene Aufgabengebiet umfasst insbesondere:

  • Leitung Sachgebiet 12,
  • Stellvertretende Leitung Dezernat 1 im Bereich IT,
  • Beauftragter für Informationstechnik und Digitalisierung sowie IT-Management (Konzeption, Strategie, Anforderungen, Architektur) im Geschäftsbereich SHIBB,
  • IT-Budgetgesamtverantwortung,
  • Steuerung von Projekten in den Bereichen IT, Digitalisierung, OZG, E-Akte sowie Infrastruktur,
  • Koordination Arbeitsschutz und Arbeitssicherheit für die Behörde SHIBB,
  • Leitung Innerer Dienst.

### **                                                  ** Das bringen Sie mit

Voraussetzungen für die ausgeschriebene Stelle sind:

  • Laufbahnbefähigung der Laufbahngruppe 2, 2. Einstiegsamt, Fachrichtung Allgemeine Dienste oder
  • ein erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium (Master oder Universitätsdiplom) in Informatik, Informationstechnik oder Wirtschaftsinformatik,
  • nachgewiesene mehrjährige (mindestens zwei Jahre) Berufserfahrung im Bereich IT,
  • nachgewiesene mehrjährige (mindestens zwei Jahre) Berufserfahrung im Bereich IT
  • Darüber hinaus ergeben sich für die Besetzung der Stelle folgende Anforderungen:
  • Die Fähigkeit und Kompetenzen die Zusammenarbeit zu fördern, Mitarbeitende zu fördern und zu unterstützen, Feedback zu geben und anzunehmen, analytisch zu denken und innovativ zu sein.
  • Zudem wäre wünschenswert:
  • nachgewiesene mehrjährige (mindestens zwei Jahre) Berufserfahrung im Landesdienst,
  • nachgewiesene berufliche Erfahrungen in der Führung von Mitarbeitenden,
  • nachgewiesene berufliche Erfahrungen im Digitalisierungs- und Innovationsmanagement sowie in Strategieentwicklung,
  • nachgewiesene berufliche Erfahrungen und gute Kenntnisse in Aufgaben des Inneren Dienstes,
  • nachgewiesene Kenntnisse und Erfahrungen in der Projektsteuerung gemäß „PRINCE II“.

-

Das Auswahlverfahren wird durch die Durchführung eines Assessment Center umgesetzt

###

### Wir bieten Ihnen

Bei Vorliegen der beamtenrechtlichen und stellenmäßigen Voraussetzungen kann auf der ausgeschriebenen Stelle eine Besoldung bis zur Besoldungsgruppe A 15 Besoldungsgesetz Schleswig-Holstein erreicht werden. Bei einer Tätigkeit im Beschäftigtenverhältnis ist bei Vorliegen der tariflichen und persönlichen Voraussetzungen eine Eingruppierung bis zur Entgeltgruppe 15 TV-L möglich

Darüber hinaus bieten wir:

  • ein vielfältiges und gemeinwohlorientiertes Aufgabenspektrum
  • ein kollegiales Arbeitsklima
  • ein vielseitiges Angebot in- und externer Fortbildungen
  • individuelle Personalentwicklung
  • ergänzende Altersvorsorge für Tarifbeschäftigte (VBL)
  • eine gute Vereinbarkeit von Familie und Beruf durch die Möglichkeit mobil und flexibel zu arbeiten
  • 30 Tage Urlaub im Jahr
  • ein vielseitiges betriebliches Gesundheitsmanagement
  • Zuschuss für den ÖPNV (NAH.SH-Jobticket/Deutschlandticket),
  • eine gute Anbindung an den ÖPNV

### Wir freuen uns auf Sie!

Das Land Schleswig-Holstein setzt sich für die Beschäftigung von Menschen mit Behinderung ein und prüft, ob freie Arbeitsplätze mit schwerbehinderten Menschen, insbesondere mit bei der Agentur für Arbeit arbeitslos oder arbeitssuchend gemeldeten schwerbehinderten Menschen, besetzt werden können.

Wir möchten die Vielfalt der Biographien und Kompetenzen in der Landesverwaltung fördern. Deshalb begrüßen wir Bewerbungen, unabhängig von Nationalität, ethnischer und sozialer Herkunft, Religion und Weltanschauung, Alter sowie sexueller Identität.

Ausdrücklich begrüßen wir es, wenn sich Menschen mit Migrationshintergrund bei uns bewerben, gleiches gilt für Menschen mit Kenntnissen in niederdeutscher, friesischer oder dänischer Sprache.

Wir streben in allen Beschäftigtengruppen eine chancengleiche Beteiligung von Frauen an. Daher werden Frauen im Falle einer Unterrepräsentation bei gleichwertiger Eignung, Befähigung und fachlicher Leistung vorrangig berücksichtigt.

Die Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Familie sowie die Förderung der Teilzeitbeschäftigung und der Wohnraumarbeit liegen im besonderen Interesse der Landesregierung. Gehen entsprechende Bewerbungen ein, wird geprüft, inwieweit den Teilzeitwünschen oder dem Wunsch nach Wohnraumarbeit im Rahmen der dienstlichen Möglichkeiten entsprochen werden kann.

### Jetzt bewerben!

Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung mit den üblichen Unterlagen (mindestens Lebenslauf, Schul-, Ausbildungs-, Arbeitszeugnisse), bei Bewerbungen aus der öffentlichen Verwaltung mit einer aktuellen Beurteilung und ggf. einer Einverständniserklärung zur Einsichtnahme in die Personalakte, richten Sie bitte bis zum

18.08.2026

an SHIBB Landesamt, SG 10, Muhliusstr. 38, 24103 Kiel gerne in elektronischer Form an bewerbungen@shibb.landsh.de. Bei Bewerbungen in Papierform bitten wir um Übersendung von Kopien, da die Bewerbungsunterlagen nicht zurückgesandt werden

Auf die Vorlage von Lichtbildern/Bewerbungsfotos verzichten wir ausdrücklich und bitten daher, hiervon abzusehen.

Ihre personenbezogenen Daten werden zur Durchführung des Bewerbungsverfahrens auf der Grundlage des § 85 Absatz 1 des Landesbeamtengesetzes und § 15 Absatz 1 des Landesdatenschutzgesetzes verarbeitet. Weitere Informationen können Sie unseren Datenschutzbestimmungen auf der Weitere Informationen können Sie unseren Datenschutzbestimmungen auf der Internetseite des Ministeriums (www.schleswig-holstein.de/mbwfk) unter Service/Formulare/Datenschutz entnehmen.

Für beamten- oder tarifrechtliche Fragen sowie Fragen zum Verfahren steht Ihnen Herr Olbert ( E-Mail-Adresse dennis.olbert@shibb.landsh.de oder Telefon 0431-9889715 ), gern zur Verfügung. Bei fachlichen Fragen zum Anforderungsprofil und den damit verbundenen Aufgaben wenden Sie sich bitte an Sie sich bitte an die Leiterin des Dezernat 1 Frau Wrütz (Telefon 0431-988/9710).

Antworten auf häufig gestellte Fragen finden Sie hier.

title: Senior Marketing Copywriter — French
description:
We exist to be the go-to companion, empowering everyone to transform their free time into experiences. At Smartbox Group, we believe that real-life experiences help us thrive, connect, and become the best versions of ourselves.


With nearly 15,000 different products across leisure themes — Stays, Wellness, Adventure, Gastronomy — we allow people to enrich their lives with moments of sharing, emotions, and unforgettable memories.

Present in 11 countries, Smartbox Group employs over 700 people across Europe and works with more than 40,000 trusted partners, including hotels, restaurants, wellness centres, and outdoor activity providers.


Living more than owning, sharing more than having — this is more than ever a fundamental trend in our society, and it’s exactly what Smartbox offers.


Our Culture:



At Smartbox, we’re on a mission to unbox our world and unbox the potential of our People. We’re not just building a workplace — we’re cultivating a movement where bold ideas, authentic connections, and real-world experiences redefine what work can be.

As our teams’ trusted companion on this journey, we empower our people to take ownership, break boundaries, and dive headfirst into challenges that spark growth and impact. Together, we’re creating a culture of daring curiosity, relentless ambition, and transformative purpose — where every team member is driven to experience life, experience potential, experience growth, shape their future, and leave a lasting mark on the world around us.


The Role:



This is a creative role with strategic reach. You’ll shape how our experiences come to life in French — across every customer-facing touchpoint, from experience pages on the Beneficiary platform to CRM and paid channels for Smartbox Direct. You’ll study what performs — open rates, CTR, conversion — and use that to sharpen your craft. Beyond writing, you’ll set the creative direction for the wider copywriting team, training them to write comms that inspire, engage, entertain, and convert.

We’re looking for someone who understands what makes people feel something — and come back. Someone who reads cultural shifts, knows how younger audiences discover and choose experiences, and uses that understanding to shape copy that’s both emotionally compelling and genuinely useful. This is a role where you’ll influence how a brand finds its voice, work with emerging AI tools, and grow alongside a business in its next chapter.


Key Responsibilities:



Creative — Copy, Concepts & Tone of Voice



  • Write high-performing marketing copy for the French market across all customer-facing touchpoints — experience pages, landing pages, CRM, paid channels, and more — copy that stops scrolling, builds desire, and converts
  • Develop campaign concepts that work across markets — sometimes originating the master concept and briefing other copywriters, sometimes receiving a concept and being first to execute it for French
  • Direct and train the wider copywriting team on writing that inspires, engages, entertains, and converts — teaching them to read audiences, adapt tone for cultural context, and raise the bar across markets
  • Own and protect the French tone of voice, ensuring it stays authentic, culturally grounded, and consistent across all channels
  • Stay close to cultural trends and audience behaviour — particularly with younger demographics — and use those insights to keep the brand’s voice relevant, fresh, and commercially effective
  • Write for experiences, not just products — your copy should make someone feel the weekend away before they’ve booked it


UX Copy — Platform & Product


  • Write and review UX copy across the Beneficiary platform and Smartbox Direct — navigation, onboarding flows, empty states, CTAs, confirmation messages, error messages, and transactional communications
  • Ensure platform language feels consistent with the broader brand voice — the experience of using the product should feel as considered as the campaign that brought someone there
  • Work closely with Product and UX/Design teams to shape copy at the right moment in the process — before designs are locked, not after
  • Understand the user journey and write copy that reduces friction, builds confidence, and nudges action — particularly at key conversion and retention moments
  • Balance clarity and brand personality — UX copy must be precise enough to guide, warm enough to feel human


Operational — Project Management & Delivery


  • Manage the content request pipeline — intake, prioritisation, and delivery across multiple concurrent briefs
  • Track and meet SLAs across stakeholders; flag risks early and manage expectations proactively
  • Prioritise work based on business impact, not just creative interest — understand what matters most and sequence work accordingly
  • Maintain content calendars aligned to campaign timelines, seasonal moments, and product launches
  • Work within cross-functional systems — brief designers, coordinate with CRM, eCommerce, and Marketing teams — and understand how content fits into broader workflows
  • Coordinate with external agencies or freelancers where needed, ensuring outputs meet the bar


Iterative Thinking & Performance


  • Approach content with a test-and-learn mindset — know what’s worth testing, ship it, read the data, and refine
  • Use performance data (CTR, engagement, conversion) to build an evolving understanding of what works for French audiences — and share those learnings with the team
  • Contribute to structured A/B testing with a clear hypothesis, not just variations for variation’s sake
  • Build on each iteration — the goal is gradual, compounding improvement, not constant reinvention


Human-Led, AI-Augmented


  • Lead every piece of work with human creativity and strategic thinking — use AI tools to accelerate research, ideation, iteration, and scaling
  • Help the team develop a healthy, practical approach to using AI: where it adds speed, and where it flattens quality
  • Be the quality filter — the person who can tell the difference between copy that’s been written and copy that’s been generated


About You:


We’re looking for someone who not only has the right skills but also embodies our guiding principles:

The Owner – You take accountability, act with integrity, and treat challenges as opportunities.

The Ambitious Maverick – You bring bold ideas, challenge the status quo, and are unafraid to take smart risks.

The Radical Optimist – You maintain a solutions-focused mindset, inspiring others with your positivity and resilience.

The Connector – You build authentic relationships, foster collaboration, and create space for diverse voices to thrive.


Skills & Experience:


  • C2/native-level French, business-fluent English (C1+) — you think and write in French, you brief and collaborate in English
  • Around 5 years in a copywriting or creative role — in-house, agency, or both. Lifestyle, experience, or consumer brands a plus
  • A strong portfolio — work that shows creative range and commercial impact. We want to see thinking, not just headlines
  • Creative collaboration — you’ve worked with designers and creative partners to develop ideas from concept to execution
  • Operational discipline — you can manage a full pipeline of briefs, meet deadlines, and keep stakeholders informed
  • Experience sharing your craft — through training workshops, feedback, or coaching. You know how to teach an approach, not just demonstrate it
  • UX copy experience — you’ve written for product interfaces and understand how microcopy inside a user journey differs from campaign copy
  • Experience with AI writing tools — you use them to enhance your work and workflows, not replace your thinking
  • Cross-functional confidence — comfortable working across Content, Marketing, CRM, Product, Tech and eCommerce
  • Audience and cultural instinct — you understand how different audiences — especially younger ones — discover, choose, and talk about experiences
  • Data-informed copywriting — you study performance — open rates, CTR, conversion — and use what you learn to make your next piece better
  • Test-and-learn mindset — you know what’s worth testing and how to read results. You iterate based on evidence, not assumption


What We Offer:


  • Transparency: clear, fair salary bands and career levels
  • Hybrid work: 2–3 remote days + 20 days abroad per year
  • Growth Mindset: 4 Growth Days, Growth Leave + training budget
  • Flexible benefits: personalise with health, wellbeing & learning options
  • D&I commitment: parental leave, D&I Day, gender equity in rewards
  • Unbox Your World: Smartbox experience days + paid sabbatical after 5 years
  • Essentials: health cover, pension, extra leave, and more

title: Brand & Marketing Designer*
description:
Wir suchen ab sofort für unser Team in Deutschland einen Brand & Marketing Designer*

Du gestaltest Print- und Digital-Assets für eine globale B2B-Marke konsequent innerhalb eines klar definierten Brand-Systems. Kein Blank-Page-Syndrom, sondern Gestaltung mit Verlässlichkeit und Qualität innerhalb eines bestehenden Rahmens.


* Geschlecht egal, Hauptsache Du passt zu uns! Deine Aufgaben:
  • Du setzt unser Digitales Marketing-Material auf und pflegst es eigenständig
  • Du erstellst Kampagnen-Visuals
  • Du entwickelst Content-Assets für unsere digitalen Medien
  • Du arbeitest innerhalb der bestehenden Evora Group Brand Guidelines
Das bringst du mit:
  • Mindestens 3 Jahre Berufserfahrung als Designer
  • Nachweisbare Kompetenz in Print und Digital
  • Ein Portfolio mit Corporate-/Brand-Design im B2B-/Enterprise-Kontext
  • Die Fähigkeit, innerhalb eines definierten Brand-Systems zu arbeiten
  • Verhandlungssicheres Deutsch und Englisch für die Zusammenarbeit in unserem internationalen Team

Das ist ein Plus:
  • Erfahrung mit KI-gestützter Content Creation
  • Kenntnisse in Animation/Motion
Bei uns erwartet dich nicht nur ein Job, sondern eine werteorientierte Zusammenarbeit in einem globalen Team. Wir leben und fördern unsere selbstbestimmten Core Values Collaboration, Happiness, Trust, Commitment, Evolution und Entrepreneurship.

Was wir Dir abseits vom Standard anbieten:

  • Deine mentale Gesundheit liegt uns am Herzen! Deshalb bieten wir dir Zugang und Unterstützung zu allen mental health bezogenen Themen durch unsere Kooperation mit nilo.health. Von Präventionsangeboten bis hin zur Akutbetreuung - wir sind für dich da.
  • Flexibilität ist uns wichtig! Mit einem Remote Arbeitsvertrag kannst du bequem von zu Hause aus arbeiten. Wir unterstützen dich auch bei der Ausstattung deines Home-Office Arbeitsplatzes, damit du dich optimal entfalten kannst.
  • Vereinbarkeit von Job & Privatleben! Neben flexiblen Arbeitszeiten, bieten wir zusätzliche Kind-Krankheitstage an, um dir familiäre Herausforderungen wie die Kinderbetreuung zu erleichtern.
  • Wir investieren in deine Weiterbildung! Mit einem jährlichen Budget hast du die Möglichkeit, dich fachlich und persönlich weiterzuentwickeln. Nutze diese Chance, um neue Fähigkeiten zu erlernen und dich kontinuierlich zu verbessern.
  • Bei uns wird Teamarbeit großgeschrieben! Neben Gesundheitszuschüssen bieten wir dir auch Team-interne Wertschätzungstools, um deine Leistungen zu würdigen. Zudem erwarten dich spannende Veranstaltungen wie unsere Internationale Co-Working Week, Sommerfest, Weihnachtsfeier und Team-Co Workings.
  • Wir stehen für Gelebte Chancengleichheit! Bei uns sind alle willkommen, unabhängig von Geschlecht, Herkunft oder Lebenshintergrund. In Initiativen wie unserem Evora-Women-Call geben wir den Raum, spezifische Themen direkt und ohne Umwege ans Management zu bringen.
  • Wir belohnen deinen Erfolg! Nach Abschluss deiner Probezeit stehen dir verschiedene Optionen wie Firmenwagen, Firmenrad oder Bahncard zur Verfügung. Zudem bieten wir eine gehaltsbezogene Beteiligung an den Unternehmenszielen und -erfolgen.
Bist du bereit, ein Evorian zu werden und von unseren einzigartigen Benefits zu profitieren? Dann bewirb dich jetzt! Wir freuen uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen.

title: Applied AI Co-Founder / CPTO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

  • You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Applied AI

  • You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management

  • You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs

  • You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding

  • You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively

  • You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate

  • You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues

  • You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: Celador/a
description:
La primera mano que ayuda, la primera mirada que cuida.

Como parte esencial del equipo asistencial, colaborarás en el acompañamiento y atención a pacientes y familiares durante su estancia en el hospital, garantizando un entorno seguro, humano y respetuoso. Tu labor contribuirá a nuestro propósito, acompañando a quienes más lo necesitan en cada paso de su proceso sanitario, y contribuyendo en nuestro compromiso.

Requisitos:

  • Estudios de Educación Secundario (E.S.O.) o formación relacionada con el puesto.

  • Valorable experiencia mínima de 5 años en puesto similar

  • Se valorará nivel B2/C1 de inglés, así como otros idiomas extranjeros

Funciones:

  1. Traslado y movilización de pacientes, garantizando su comodidad y seguridad.

  2. Apoyo en el transporte interno de material clínico, documentación y equipamiento médico.

  3. Colaboración con el personal sanitario en la atención y cuidado básico de pacientes.

  4. Asistencia en situaciones de emergencia, siguiendo los protocolos establecidos.

  5. Mantenimiento del orden y condiciones básicas de higiene en las áreas asignadas.

Lo que ofrecemos:

  • Contrato Indefinido.

  • Incorporación abierta a profesionales con vocación y compromiso, para formar parte de un equipo colaborativo y especializado.

  • Un entorno que potencia su desarrollo.

  • Formación continua: estimularemos su aprendizaje y desarrollo a través de nuestros planes de formación específicos, que permitirán su desarrollo personal y profesionalmente.

title: Services Sales Analyst
description:
Job Title: Thermal Service Sales Analyst

Experience: 1–2 Years
Location: Bengaluru
Department: Service Sales

Job Summary

We are seeking a motivated and technically skilled Thermal Service Sales professional with 1–2 years of experience to drive service revenue for thermal systems. The role involves promoting aftermarket services, AMC contracts, spare parts, and retrofit solutions to existing and new customers while ensuring strong customer relationships.

Key Responsibilities

  • Drive sales of thermal service offerings, including:
    • Annual Maintenance Contracts (AMC)
    • Spare parts & consumables sales
  • Identify and develop service business opportunities in existing and new accounts
  • Conduct site visits and equipment inspections to understand customer needs
  • Prepare and present technical/commercial proposals aligned with customer requirements
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with maintenance teams, and procurement
  • Achieve sales targets and service revenue goals
  • Coordinate with internal teams (service engineers, operations, supply chain) for project execution
  • Track customer equipment lifecycle to identify cross-selling and upselling opportunities
  • Maintain accurate CRM data, sales reports, and forecasts

Required Skills & Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering
  • 1–2 years of experience in:
    • Service sales / aftermarket sales
    • Thermal systems (boilers, heat exchangers, chillers, HVAC, or similar)
  • Basic understanding of thermal engineering concepts
  • Strong communication, negotiation, and presentation skills
  • Ability to read and interpret technical drawings/specifications
  • Proficiency in MS Office and CRM tools

Preferred Skills

  • Experience in industries such as HVAC or Industrial product AMC sales.
  • Customer-facing experience in B2B industrial sales

Key Competencies

  • Customer-focused approach
  • Result-oriented mindset
  • Problem-solving ability
  • Strong follow-up and execution skills

KPIs / Success Metrics

  • Service revenue growth
  • AMC conversion rate
  • Customer retention & satisfaction
  • Pipeline development and closure rate

A Place Where You Can Be You
We take it as our responsibility to create an environment where everyone feels welcome, exactly as they are.
Different backgrounds and perspectives make us stronger and shape our culture in ways that matter.
What we stand for internally, we stand for as a brand: acceptance, inclusion, and fairness.

title: Driver
description:
ACDC Express is looking for a responsible, customer-focused, and reliable Driver to join our team. The successful candidate will be responsible for the safe and timely delivery and collection of stock, ensuring accurate documentation, maintaining the company vehicle, and providing excellent customer service while supporting daily store operations.

Key Responsibilities

  • Safely deliver and collect stock from customers and suppliers.
  • Ensure deliveries and collections are completed accurately and on time.
  • Verify delivery documentation and product information before dispatch and collection.
  • Perform routine vehicle inspections and report maintenance requirements.
  • Maintain the cleanliness and roadworthiness of the company vehicle.
  • Build positive relationships with customers and suppliers.
  • Assist with inventory and store operations when required.
  • Adhere to all road traffic laws and company policies.
  • Deliver exceptional customer service at every interaction.


Requirements

  • Grade 12 (NQF Level 4) or equivalent.
  • Valid Code B driver's licence.
  • 1–3 years' driving or delivery experience.
  • Experience in a retail or electrical environment will be advantageous.
  • Good geographical knowledge of the local area.
  • Good communication and organisational skills.
  • Basic computer literacy.
  • Clear criminal and credit record.
  • Legally authorised to work in Namibia.

title: Relatiebeheerder
description:
Relaties bouw je niet met een belscript. Als relatiebeheerder bij JEX Backoffice ken jij je klanten straks bij naam, en zij jou. Ons portfolio groeit, en die groei vraagt om iemand die er sturing aan geeft.

Jij voert de eerste kennismaking, sluit new business en bouwt een portefeuille van twintig tot vijfentwintig klanten. Van Zeeland tot Groningen. Soms sta je een hele dag in het noorden, van klant naar klant. Je bespreekt wat er speelt: finance, verzuim, groeiplannen, vaak rechtstreeks met de eigenaar. Zeggen wat nodig is, ook als dat 'nee' is. Zo til je klanten naar een volgend niveau. Niet in uren. In vertrouwen.

Dit vind jij leuk om te doen:

  • Je voert gesprekken over new business en haalt nieuwe klanten binnen;

  • Een portefeuille van 20 tot 25 klanten, verspreid door het hele land: die beheer jij;

  • Je bent het eerste aanspreekpunt bij kennismakingsgesprekken en zet het traject in gang;

  • Met klanten, vaak de eigenaar zelf, bespreek jij wat er speelt: van finance tot verzuim;

  • Onze eigen software geef je een gezicht: je verzorgt demo's en denkt mee hoe we die blijven verbeteren, samen met onze klanten;

  • Onze kennis vertaal je naar concreet advies, zodat klanten weten wat JEX Backoffice voor ze kan doen;

  • Kansen om klanten te laten groeien herken jij, in persoonlijk advies én in vertrouwen;

  • Jij monitort én viert successen: een verjaardag, een afgestemd doel, een behaalde mijlpaal. Via je dashboards houd je alles scherp en speel je er op tijd op in;

Hier herken jij jezelf in:

  • Ervaring in de uitzendbranche? Mooi, maar geen must;

  • Commercieel tot in de vingertoppen, met lef om een 'nee' te verkopen;

  • Je pakt verantwoordelijkheid zelf, niemand hoeft erom te vragen;

  • Behulpzaam, ook als de agenda overloopt;

  • Communicatief sterk: overtuigen doe je zonder te pushen;

  • Een lastige casus? Daar word jij warm van;

  • Een SEU-certificering is mooi meegenomen, maar geen must;

  • Nieuwe ideeën breng je zelf op tafel. Wachten op een uitnodiging is niks voor jou;

  • Affiniteit met software: je geeft met plezier een demo en snapt wat techniek voor een klant oplost;

Wat biedt JEX Backoffice:

  • Bij JEX Backoffice krijg je de kans om jezelf continu uit te dagen en te ontwikkelen. Daarnaast mag je ook minimaal onderstaande van ons verwachten:

  • Een salaris tussen de €3.500 en €4.500 (o.b.v. je ervaring, skills en potentie);

  • Een riante optieregeling;

  • Een passende reiskostenvergoeding of een leaseauto;

  • 30 vakantiedagen o.b.v. 40 uur en 8% vakantiegeld;

  • Een premievrije pensioenregeling;

  • Inwisselbare feestdagen en de optie om extra vrije dagen bij te kopen of te verkopen;

  • Een laptop en telefoon van de zaak;

  • Elke dag ontbijt, uitgebreide lunch en onbeperkt fruit, snacks en fris;

  • Een gym en gebedsruimte in ons prachtige kantoor;

  • Hele toffe events op interne én externe locaties;

  • Een inhouse skybar waar we de gezelligste borrels hebben;

  • Diverse opleidingsmogelijkheden, wij moedigen je aan om de beste versie van jezelf te worden.

Hier kom je terecht:

Bij JEX Backoffice geloven we dat technologiebedrijven vooruit moet brengen. Slimmer werken, sneller groeien en meer focus op wat écht telt. Met toegankelijke tech- en AI-oplossingen automatiseren we complexe en tijdrovende processen voor B2B-organisaties.

Enthousiast?

Dat snappen we! Deel je cv en motivatie en krijg binnen twee werkdagen na je sollicitatie bericht van ons. We zijn erg benieuwd naar wat jou bijzonder maakt.

Tip: kort is de kunst, krachtig de key!

title: Lorry Crane Driver
description:

title: Azure Cloud Engineer
description:
The Company


Smart Electric Vehicle Charging Solutions. That’s the business we are in!

GreenFlux offers a cloud-based charge point management SaaS solution. This software platform connects Charge Point Operators (CPOs) and e-Mobility Service Providers or eMSPs – offering charging services to EV (Electric Vehicle) drivers and enables them to successfully launch their businesses. With its offering, GreenFlux plays a pivotal role in facilitating the uptake of electric mobility and the renewable energy revolution. To keep up with the rapid market and business expansion and to support the company’s dynamic growth, we are looking for new ambitious colleagues.

GreenFlux is part of DKV Mobility Group, a major European Mobility company, and we play a leading role in the transition towards sustainable mobility for our ‘own’ customers and those of the Group.

The Job

We are looking for a skilled and motivated Azure Cloud Engineer to join our Development Department. As an Azure Cloud Engineer at GreenFlux, you will play a pivotal role in designing, implementing, and maintaining our Azure Cloud infrastructure to ensure high availability, scalability, and security. You will collaborate closely with our Development and Operations teams to drive automation, streamline processes, and optimize our Cloud environment

The Azure Cloud Engineer we are looking for is someone who loves to think in innovative ways to improve our solutions, both from a technical and process point of view. Your work style can be described as structured and precise, but you also are able to take on a helicopter view. You are a great communicator and listener, and you actively use these skills to bridge the gap between development, operations, and product. You know how to explain things in a clear and comprehensive way to the rest of the team and are not shy to ask critical questions when needed.


Key Responsibilities

  • Support and maintain our Azure DevOps environment, with a strong focus on automation, scalability, and security.

  • Design, implement, and maintain reusable CI/CD pipelines, ensuring consistency and efficiency across projects.

  • Apply Infrastructure as Code (IaC) practices using Bicep to automate infrastructure provisioning and deployment.

  • Collaborate closely with development teams to modernize and streamline CI/CD processes, aligning deployment strategies across multiple products.

  • Deploy, configure, and manage Azure cloud infrastructure, with emphasis on services such as Networking, Web Apps for Containers, Key Vault, Managed Identity, Azure Functions, Azure SQL, Cosmos DB, Service Bus, and Azure Synapse.

  • Develop and maintain scripts to automate operational tasks and deployment workflows.

  • Monitor system performance, analyze logs, and implement proactive improvements to optimize resource usage and reliability.

  • Ensure robust security and compliance of Azure resources, including identity and access management.

  • Stay current with Azure and DevOps best practices, continuously driving innovation and improvement within the infrastructure team.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, information technology, or related field (or equivalent work experience).

  • Minimum 4–5 years of hands-on experience with Azure DevOps and Azure cloud services.

  • Proven experience in designing, deploying, and managing Azure-based cloud solutions.

  • Proficiency in Azure DevOps, including YAML pipelines with a focus on module reusability, Git, and CI/CD automation.

  • Strong knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Bicep templates.

  • Knowledge of Azure PaaS and SaaS services.

  • Experience with PowerShell scripting for automation and deployment.

  • Knowledge of networking technologies, including TCP/IP, DNS, and VPN.

  • Excellent problem-solving and communication skills.

  • Professional fluency in English, both written and verbal.

  • A sustainable mindset!


Our offer

Working at GreenFlux means working in a dynamic, innovative, and informal organization, where new ideas and developments move quickly, and each day brings new opportunities and challenges.

We are proud to have an international and inclusive team, so we also offer Visa sponsorship and relocation compensation to expats. We support a hybrid working culture but also have a new and modern office with all the amenities.

Join our revolution in mobility and energy and you will have the chance to actively contribute to a sustainable future!

title: Secondary School Biology Specialist
description:
Responsibilities

· Plan and deliver engaging Biology lessons aligned with Singapore MOE secondary syllabus.

· Assess students’ learning needs and customise lesson plans to maximise learning outcomes.

· Teach scientific concepts in Biology, and/or lower-secondary Science according to the tutor’s expertise.

· Develop students’ understanding of scientific inquiry, experimental methods, data analysis, and practical applications.

· Guide students in answering structured, application-based, and calculation questions accurately.

· Mark assignments, monitor student progress, and provide timely, constructive feedback.

· Prepare students for school assessments, practical assessments, and national examinations.

· Develop students’ curiosity, critical thinking, scientific reasoning, and problem-solving skills.

· Create a positive, engaging, and disciplined learning environment.

· Communicate professionally with parents regarding students’ academic progress.

· Develop teaching resources, lesson materials, and practical learning activities.

· Uphold ATOP Academy’s teaching standards, values, and commitment to academic excellence.

Requirements

· GCE A-Level, Diploma, Degree, or equivalent qualification in Education, Science, Engineering, or a related field.

· Good command of spoken and written English.

· Strong subject knowledge in Biology, and/or lower-secondary Science.

· Familiarity with Singapore MOE Secondary Science syllabus and assessment requirements.

· Passion for teaching and supporting secondary school students.

· Strong communication, interpersonal, and classroom management skills.

· Responsible, patient, enthusiastic, and committed to student success.

· Prior teaching or tutoring experience is an advantage but not required.

Interested applicants are invited to submit their resume. Thank you.

title: Concept Artist
description:
Youtooz creates designer toys for the heroes and role models of the internet â Youtubers, Instagram influencers, celebrities, athletes, rappers, and more. Our mission is to bring the people you already love to watch and keep up with on the internet in real life, on your desk at work, your shelf in your bedroom or even in your living room for everyone to see. Youtooz are incredibly personal and unique to each creator and customer and comes with a variety of unique perks.

Concept Artists at Youtooz will create the aesthetic and look for our product. They are responsible for taking a content creator or popular IP and turning them into a 2D concept that fits the brandâs aesthetic guidelines and accurately personifies the creator.

PORTFOLIOS USING AI WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED
