title: TAR Coordinator
description:
The Role

You will be supporting Turnaround events for a particular asset and has the responsibility to coordinate planning and field execution to ensure turnaround activities are executed safely, efficiently and in accordance with approved premises, work processes, and standards. This includes interface management with all Adura disciplines and execution contractors.

To coordinate with the work preparer/planners/schedulers during the planning phase and work closely with field Coordinators, Contractors, Adura Operations, Project and Discipline Engineering Groups.

Scope of role includes all aspects of turnaround planning and performance, incl. preparation planning and scheduling, shutdown, decontamination, maintenance, project execution, commissioning, and restart; and lookbacks for learnings and future planning.

Our Clients and Projects

Designing the future. Transforming the world.

Our Operations team delivers a wide range of services, including maintenance, turnaround (TAR) execution, and asset support across both onshore and offshore environments. This area of the business offers diverse opportunities to develop your skills while working across a varied portfolio of energy assets.

Our current portfolio supports multiple operators across the North Sea, covering everything from day-to-day maintenance activities and shutdown delivery through to late-life asset support and decommissioning. This breadth of work provides valuable exposure to different phases of the asset lifecycle, allowing you to build well-rounded experience in a dynamic and safety-critical industry.

At the heart of our success is our people. We are proud of our high-performing team, built on a strong “one team” culture where collaboration, accountability, and safety are always prioritised. You’ll be supported by experienced colleagues who are committed to working together to achieve safe, efficient, and high-quality outcomes.

This is an exciting time to join our team. If you’re looking to be part of a supportive environment where your contribution is valued and your development is encouraged, Wood offers the opportunity to grow your career while playing a key role in delivering critical energy projects.

What we can offer

  • Meaningful and interesting projects delivered to leaders of industry across renewables and emerging energy sectors
  • Flexible working arrangements that balance client, team and individual needs offering hybrid and remote working where relevant –
  • Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion; we are an organisation actively committed to diversity and inclusion across our business with employee networks committed to giving all employees a voice
  • Competitive salary with regular salary reviews to ensure we are rewarding at the right level in line with the market
  • Flexible benefits package; inclusive of 33 days annual leave (including public holidays), generous contributory pension scheme, private medical cover, 4x base salary life insurance; all of these and more that can be adapted to suit your own lifestyle
  • Commitment to continued professional development; development plans that are tailored to your individual needs and interests

Turnaround Planning and Execution:

  • Accountability to deliver effective Turnaround within the area to achieve targeted budget, scope and schedule.
  • Leads work scope collection, scope challenge and selection.
  • Supports the development of cost estimates and budget.
  • Coordinate the detailed scope and execution plan for all scope in the area: maintenance, plant changes, capital projects
  • Responsible for the coordination of the scope change process after scope freeze
  • Reviews work packages by work preparer / Planner / Schedulers to ensure quality and completeness.
  • Assists Turnaround Event Manager in the development of the turnaround organization plan.
  • Coordinates optimization activities including Reviews, Schedule Optimizations, resource leveling etc.
  • Works closely with operations to ensure proper integration of turnaround activities.
  • Directs field activities during turnaround execution; this includes participation in shift handover meetings working with all the Asset Maintenance Teams, Contractors, Asset Operations, Project and Discipline Engineering Groups and daily report out on progress
  • Supports archiving of turnaround files and records, financial closeout and Lessons Learned records
  • Supports event close-out and documentation

What makes you remarkable?

At Wood, we are committed to equal opportunities and welcome all talented individuals to consider joining our team. So even if you don't match every statement below but feel you have some of the experience, knowledge or skills needed for this role, we encourage you to apply. It will take all of us working together to deliver solutions to the world’s most critical challenges.

Expected:

  • Offshore apprenticeship/experience or degree qualified in a relevant subject with excellent knowledge of offshore asset operations, maintenance, planning and execution
  • Supervisory/management experience required
  • Extensive experience in turnarounds and maintenance, with experience as a field coordinator with accountability for delivering various scope of turnaround work involving a variety of technical disciplines preferred
  • Has experience with Management of Change process, budgeting, contract and procurement management
  • The Turnaround Coordinator shall have good understanding of various technical disciplines including of the Technical Authority process and assurance requirement
  • Asset operations and maintenance knowledge as well as experience of offshore environments

title: Automobilverkäufer/in (m/w/d)
description:
Du willst Autos verkaufen und richtig Geld verdienen? Dann zeig, was du kannst.


Bei Kraska Automobile bekommst du keine Verkäuferstelle zum Absitzen.

Du bekommst die Chance, mit Leistung, Disziplin und echter Kundenbegeisterung ein außergewöhnliches Einkommen zu erreichen.

Unser Portfolio gibt dir dafür die richtige Bühne: hochwertige Gebrauchtwagen, attraktive EU-Reimporte und emotionale Sportwagen verschiedener Marken und Klassen. Du verkaufst nicht jeden Tag das Gleiche – du findest für unterschiedliche Kunden die passende automobile Lösung.

Für starke Finanzierungslösungen stehen dir Bank11, BDK und Santander zur Seite. Operativ halten wir dir den Rücken frei:
  • Die Disposition bereitet Fahrzeuge und Probefahrten vor.
  • Die Abwicklung übernimmt nach dem Abschluss bis zur Auslieferung.
  • Du konzentrierst dich auf Beratung, Finanzierung und Abschluss.
Wir liefern Fahrzeuge, Leads, Prozesse und Unterstützung. Du machst daraus Abschlüsse.

Der Kunde muss von dir begeistert sein.


Nicht nur zufrieden. Begeistert.

Du hörst dem Kunden verdammt noch mal zu. Du verstehst, was er braucht, welches Fahrzeug zu seinem Leben passt und welche Finanzierung ihm einen echten Mehrwert bietet.

Ob Gebrauchtwagen, EU-Reimport oder Sportwagen: Du kennst dein Produkt, erklärst die Vorteile verständlich und gibst dem Kunden Sicherheit bei seiner Entscheidung.

Darauf kommt es an:
  • Du bearbeitest jeden Lead schnell, diszipliniert und konsequent.
  • Du kennst Fahrzeuge, Ausstattungen und Finanzierungsprodukte im Schlaf.
  • Du fasst aktiv nach und lässt keine echte Verkaufschance versanden.
  • Du präsentierst nicht nur Fahrzeugdaten, sondern den konkreten Nutzen für den Kunden.
  • Du hältst jede Zusage ein und übernimmst Verantwortung bis zum Abschluss.
  • Du behandelst jeden Kunden offen, fair und mit demselben Respekt.
Kein Druckverkauf. Keine Märchen. Ehrliche Beratung, klare Abschlüsse und ein starkes Ergebnis für beide Seiten: WIN-WIN.

Leistung ist eine Entscheidung.


Wir suchen keinen Schlüsselübergeber, keinen Prospektverteiler und niemanden, der auf Laufkundschaft wartet.

Wir suchen einen Auto-Enthusiasten mit Energie, Empathie und Abschlusswillen.

Langjährige Autohauserfahrung ist nicht nötig. Verkaufserfahrung ist willkommen – entscheidend sind deine Haltung, deine Lernbereitschaft und deine Disziplin.

Du passt zu uns, wenn du:
  • für Autos und Verkauf brennst,
  • Menschen wirklich zuhörst und Vertrauen aufbaust,
  • diszipliniert arbeitest und Zusagen einhältst,
  • verbindlich nachfasst und klar nach dem Abschluss fragst,
  • offen für verschiedene Marken, Fahrzeugklassen und Kundenbedürfnisse bist,
  • Feedback annimmst und Gelerntes konsequent umsetzt,
  • im Team gewinnen willst, ohne dich hinter dem Team zu verstecken.
Hunger, Disziplin und Verbindlichkeit musst du mitbringen. Produktwissen und Prozesse bringen wir dir bei.

Wir liefern die Voraussetzungen. Du lieferst die Leistung.


Du bekommst kein leeres Motivationsgerede, sondern ein Umfeld, in dem du erfolgreich verkaufen kannst:

  • intensive Einarbeitung und ein klares Sales-Onboarding,
  • regelmäßiges Training durch externe Powerseller,
  • ein vielseitiges und emotionales Fahrzeugportfolio,
  • starke Finanzierungspartner und stabile Verkaufsprozesse,
  • Unterstützung durch Disposition und Abwicklung,
  • ein transparentes und leistungsorientiertes Provisionssystem.
In vergleichbaren, leistungsstarken Autohäusern können Top-Verkäufer 150.000 bis 250.000 Euro im Jahr erreichen. Das ist kein garantiertes Gehalt und kein Geschenk. Dein tatsächliches Einkommen hängt von deiner Verkaufsleistung und den geltenden Provisionsbedingungen ab.

Wer durchschnittlich arbeitet, verdient durchschnittlich. Wer Leads diszipliniert bearbeitet, Kunden begeistert und Abschlüsse macht, soll richtig gut verdienen.

Wir versprechen dir nicht, dass es bequem wird. Wir geben dir den Rahmen um erfolgreich zu sein.

Bereit für Vollgas?


Dann bewirb dich jetzt bei Kraska Automobile.

title: Traineeship Subsidieadviseur Energie & Duurzaamheid
description:
• Je hebt een brede affiniteit voor techniek en kunt technische concepten gemakkelijk eigen maken. Of het nu gaat om procestechnologie, milieutechniek, energiesystemen of duurzaamheidsberekeningen, jij bent nieuwsgierig en wilt er meer over te weten komen.
  • Je bent communicatief vaardig en kunt de behoeften van de klanten begrijpen en vertalen naar mogelijkheden door complexe informatie over subsidies op de juiste manier over te brengen. Het opbouwen en onderhouden van sterke relaties is cruciaal.
  • Je hebt kennis van zaken en bent commercieel ingesteld, wat betekent dat je begrijpt wat de business drijft en hoe jouw bijdrage de financiële haalbaarheid van een project kan beïnvloeden.

In deze functie volg je een traineeship waarin je twee jaar lang wordt begeleid in je professionele en persoonlijke ontwikkeling. Binnen het Gi3 team krijg je de kans je breed te oriënteren en mee te kijken met experts uit verschillende vakgebieden.

Natuurlijk ga je op zoek naar de vraag áchter de vraag en wil je elke dag nieuwe dingen ontdekken en leren. Bovenal durf je jezelf te zijn. De ware jij. Met je eigen sterke punten, blik op de wereld en persoonlijkheid. Verder heb jij als Subsidieadviseur Energie & Duurzaamheid:

  • Een afgeronde wo-opleiding in een technische richting zoals Science, Business & Innovation, Technische Bestuurskunde, Mechanical Engineering of vergelijkbaar;
  • 0 tot 2 jaar werkervaring;
  • Een uitstekende beheersing van zowel de Nederlandse als de Engelse taal in woord en geschrift.

Je bent de brug tussen bedrijven en hun groene ambities. Je bedient een divers klantenbestand en begeleidt toonaangevende bedrijven bij het realiseren van hun duurzame initiatieven door hen te ondersteunen in het verkrijgen van subsidies. Als Trainee Subsidieadviseur Energie & Duurzaamheid word je in het gehele proces betrokken, van het onderhouden van klantrelaties tot het identificeren van passende subsidies en het opstellen van een succesvolle subsidieaanvraag.

title: Sr. Client Account Manager | Nordics (CPG)
description:
About Pinterest:

Millions of people around the world come to our platform to find creative ideas, dream about new possibilities and plan for memories that will last a lifetime. At Pinterest, we’re on a mission to bring everyone the inspiration to create a life they love, and that starts with the people behind the product.

Discover a career where you ignite innovation for millions, transform passion into growth opportunities, celebrate each other’s unique experiences and embrace the flexibility to do your best work. Creating a career you love? It’s Possible.

At Pinterest, AI isn't just a feature, it's a powerful partner that augments our creativity and amplifies our impact, and we’re looking for candidates who are excited to be a part of that. To get a complete picture of your experience and abilities, we’ll explore your foundational skills and how you collaborate with AI.

Through our interview process, what matters most is that you can always explain your approach, showing us not just what you know, but how you think. You can read more about our AI interview philosophy and how we use AI in our recruiting process here.

As a Sr. Client Account Manager you will manage a portfolio of Nordic CPG Enterprise Brands. You will drive growth with high‑potential, performance, and brand‑focused advertisers. You will harness the power of AI-driven solutions and data intelligence to optimize client outcomes and elevate the account management experience. You will provide strategic direction to drive growth for your clients on Pinterest, helping them realize the opportunity available to expand their business. You will be part of a team culture that values collaboration, innovation and achieving great results. If this sounds like fun, we’d love to hear from you!

What you’ll do:

  • Build and nurture relationships with our advertising partners; proactively share performance, optimizations, updates, and opportunities.
  • Ensure that our partners’ objectives and requirements are met by working together with our internal teams (Measurement & Insights, Product Marketing, Creative Strategy, etc).
  • Gain a deep understanding of our partners’ business goals and turn data into compelling stories and actionable insights to help them succeed.
  • Specifically own the “how” conversation with advertisers to advise on media strategies across targeting, bidding models, creative, measurement, etc.
  • Become an expert at Pinterest campaign best practices, enabling yourself to be the resource for performance related opportunities. Ensure that campaign setup is optimal. Interpret and communicate campaign results so that the customer sees impact. Evaluate if a digital media campaign is successful and how to troubleshoot. Understand how to drive results, optimize campaigns and deliver performance updates to partners. Ensure the performance metrics that were agreed upon are being met, and take action to course correct.
  • Leverage AI-driven tools and workflows to streamline campaign management, identify emerging trends, and recommend optimizations at scale.
  • Analyze campaign data using AI-powered insights platforms, translating findings into actionable strategies for client success.
  • Actively engage in client meetings to understand their key objectives and challenges. Present and explain complex solutions in a clear and concise manner to help partners achieve their objectives. Handle resistance, complaints, or objections from partners professionally and patiently. Cross sell and upsell opportunities that contribute to the team’s ability to exceed revenue targets.
  • Understand complex processes and products, distilling them down into a digestible format for our partners’, Provide partners with guidance and expertise on product, service best practices and features. Be highly knowledgeable about all relevant Pinterest ad product features and capable of clearly explaining their benefits to a customer. Maintain up-to-date education and fluency on evolving product roadmap and iterations. Efficiently interfaces with appropriate cross-functional teams to answer product-related questions or challenges when needed.
  • Continuously evaluate and adopt AI technologies to enhance account management capabilities and client service.

What we’re looking for:

  • Digital advertising sales experience within the CPG category, managing high value relationships, driving strategic learning agendas and owning client communication.
  • Analytical with a strong performance marketing background.
  • Excited about using insights to create robust creative marketing campaigns that align with trends and moments.
  • Comfortable managing complex client relationships.
  • Able to manage large workloads while remaining a proactive, solutions‑oriented problem solver.
  • Hands-on experience integrating AI or automation into routine workflow processes, and a curiosity for adopting new AI technologies.
  • Familiarity with AI-powered marketing, analytics, and reporting tools for scalable account management.
  • Track record of compelling data-backed storytelling and recommendations.
  • Expert in managing forecasts and achieving goals.
  • Strong communication skills in person, on the phone, and through email.
  • A team player who’s able to work in a fast-paced environment with technical and non-technical teams at scale.
  • Expertise in digital advertising and media sales including campaign optimization, analytical problem solving, and product/platform knowledge.
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field such as Business, or equivalent experience.

Relocation Statement:

  • This position is not eligible for relocation assistance. Visit our PinFlex page to learn more about our working model.

In-Office Requirement Statement:

  • We let the type of work you do guide the collaboration style. That means we're not always working in an office, but we continue to gather for key moments of collaboration and connection.
  • This role will need to be in the office for in-person collaboration 1-2 times per week and therefore needs to be in a commutable distance from one of the following offices; Dublin.



#LI-HYBRID

#LI-SL2

At Pinterest we believe the workplace should be equitable, inclusive, and inspiring for every employee. In an effort to provide greater transparency, we are sharing the base salary range for this position. The position is also eligible for equity and incentive compensation. Final salary is based on a number of factors including location, travel, relevant prior experience, or particular skills and expertise.

Information regarding the culture at Pinterest and benefits available for this position can be found here.

Location based applicants
728 000 kr—1 346 800 kr SEK

Our Commitment to Inclusion:

Pinterest is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. We want to have the best qualified people in every job. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion or religious creed, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, status as a protected veteran, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information or characteristics (or those of a family member) or any other consideration made unlawful by applicable federal, state or local laws. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. If you require a medical or religious accommodation during the job application process, please complete this form for support.
By submitting this application, I certify that all information submitted in my application and throughout the hiring process is true, accurate, and complete to the best of my knowledge. I understand that any false statement, omission, or misrepresentation may disqualify me from employment consideration or result in termination if discovered after hire.

title: Product Manager - Global Football & Rugby
description:
At Hudl, we build great teams. We hire the best of the best to ensure you’re working with people you can constantly learn from. You’re trusted to get your work done your way while testing the limits of what’s possible and what’s next. We work hard to provide a culture where everyone feels supported, and our employees feel it—their votes helped us become one of Newsweek's Top 100 Global Most Loved Workplaces.

We think of ourselves as the team behind the team, supporting the lifelong impact sports can have: the lessons in teamwork and dedication; the influence of inspiring coaches; and the opportunities to reach new heights. That’s why we help teams from all over the world see their game differently. Our products make it easier for coaches and athletes at any level to capture video, analyze data, share highlights and more.

Ready to join us?

Your Role

We’re looking for a Product Manager to join our Global Football & Rugby product group. You’ll work on Hudl Wyscout and Hudl Statsbomb, our comprehensive analysis and scouting tools trusted by Global Football coaches and scouts worldwide. You’ll help us scale user engagement across international markets, and deliver products that empower our customers to make efficient, impactful decisions to help them win.

This is a hands-on product role and we’re looking for someone with high energy to match our big ambitions in this space.

As a Product Manager, you’ll:

  • Focus on delivering value to our users. You’ll understand the biggest user pain points, and scope how those can be solved in a way that benefits from Hudl’s existing product suite. You’ll lead key investments, with a heavy focus on delivering a product experience that customers love.
  • Advocate for the product. You’ll develop a deep understanding of our customers and business needs, building trusting relationships with customers and senior internal stakeholders.
  • Foster collaboration. You’ll determine what success looks like and motivate your squad, ensuring all are pulling in the same direction. You’ll also work with Marketing, Sales and Customer Support to create go-to-market plans.

For this role, we're currently only considering candidates who live within a commuting distance of our office in London.

Must-Haves

  • Experience. You’ve spent more than two years in product management, and you have a track record of shipping valuable customer-facing software.
  • A communicator. You’re comfortable presenting and can effectively share your product roadmap with stakeholders. You can get buy-in on your ideas and can motivate others toward a common goal.
  • Naturally curious. You never stop learning—you’re eager for knowledge and new ideas because that improves the way you work. You love building prototypes, testing, learning and evolving.
  • AI workflow experience. You have experience using AI tools and building AI-powered workflows into products to improve customer outcomes and reduce operational burden.
  • Data-driven. You have a passion for using data and insights to drive high quality decision-making, but also know when to take calculated leaps of faith.
  • Sport knowledge. You are passionate about the sport of Global Football, and are keen to learn more.
  • Collaborative. You seek to understand the strengths of those around you and have made each team you’ve worked on better. You relish tackling hard problems alongside your squad and building great relationships with teams.
  • Ambitious and energetic. You’re willing to roll your sleeves up and get hands-on when necessary to make an impact. You’re ambitious and keen to develop your long-term career at Hudl.

Nice-to-Haves

  • Commercially-minded. You have good business sense and know how to balance user needs with business goals.
  • A strong negotiator. You’re able to communicate tradeoffs and generate buy-in on a solution that’s best for everyone.
  • Market connections. You have either coached or participated in Global Football at a competitive level, and you can bring industry context and connections to the role.

Our Role

  • Champion work-life harmony. We’ll give you the flexibility you need in your work life (e.g., flexible vacation time above any required statutory leave, company-wide holidays and timeout (meeting-free) days, remote work options and more) so you can enjoy your personal life too.
  • Guarantee autonomy. We have an open, honest culture and we trust our people from day one. Your team will support you, but you’ll own your work and have the agency to try new ideas.
  • Encourage career growth. We’re lifelong learners who encourage professional development. We’ll give you tons of resources and opportunities to keep growing.
  • Provide an environment to help you succeed. We've invested in our offices, designing incredible spaces with our employees in mind. But whether you’re at the office or working remotely, we’ll provide you the tech you need to do your best work.
  • Support your wellbeing. Depending on location, we offer medical and retirement benefits for employees—but no matter where you’re located, we have resources like our Employee Assistance Program and employee resource groups to support your mental health.

Compensation

The base salary range for this role is displayed below—starting salaries will typically fall near the middle of this range.

We make compensation decisions based on an individual's experience, skills and education in line with our internal pay equity practices.

Base Salary Range
£51,000—£86,000 GBP

Inclusion at Hudl

Hudl is an equal opportunity employer. Through our actions, behaviors and attitude, we’ll create an environment where everyone, no matter their differences, feels like they belong.

We offer resources to ensure our employees feel safe bringing their authentic selves to work, including employee resource groups and communities. But we recognize there’s ongoing work to be done, which is why we track our efforts and commitments in annual inclusion reports.

We also know imposter syndrome is real and the confidence gap can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply—we’d love to hear from you.

Privacy Policy

Hudl Applicant and Candidate Privacy Policy

title: Deputy Manager, Production Center (Overseas Custom Factory Management)
description:
Key Responsibilities

Business Management

1. Identify and Evaluate Overseas Contract Manufacturing Partners

  • Conduct market research on lubricant manufacturing facilities across Europe, Africa, and the Middle East.
  • Evaluate potential contract manufacturers based on production capabilities, operational standards, certifications, and Quality, Health, Safety & Environment (QHSE) performance.
  • Recommend suitable manufacturing partners to support business requirements.

2. Supplier Qualification and Contract Manufacturing Management

  • Assess contract manufacturing proposals based on cost, delivery performance, quality, and operational efficiency.
  • Coordinate supplier evaluations, commercial negotiations, and tender activities where applicable.
  • Manage supplier onboarding, including confidentiality agreements, product verification, label approval, and contract execution.
  • Support the implementation of new contract manufacturing projects.

3. Business Development and Process Improvement

  • Identify opportunities to expand contract manufacturing capabilities, including new product categories and packaging formats.
  • Coordinate product validation, label updates, and contract renewals.
  • Review and improve manufacturing and operational processes to enhance efficiency and service quality.

4. Business Planning and Operations

  • Develop annual business plans and objectives for overseas contract manufacturing operations.
  • Coordinate the execution of business plans and monitor project progress.
  • Prepare operational reports and business performance updates.
  • Manage day-to-day coordination with internal stakeholders and external manufacturing partners.

Risk Management

  • Develop and maintain quality inspection standards for raw materials and finished products used in contract manufacturing.
  • Establish technical requirements covering packaging, warehousing, logistics, and product quality.
  • Monitor operational risks and implement appropriate controls throughout the manufacturing process.
  • Standardize supplier qualification, order management, and commercial approval processes to ensure compliance with company policies and applicable regulatory requirements.

Health, Safety & Environment (HSE)

  • Comply with the Company's Health, Safety & Environment (HSE) policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
  • Promote workplace safety and support continuous improvement in HSE performance across contract manufacturing operations.

Other Responsibilities

  • Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance operational efficiency and business performance.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to achieve departmental and organisational objectives.
  • Perform other duties as assigned by management.

Job Requirements

  • Master's Degree or higher in Engineering, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Business, Supply Chain Management, or a related discipline.
  • At least 8 years of relevant experience in lubricants and grease manufacturing, production operations, technical services, quality management, contract manufacturing, OEM operations, or related industries.
  • Experience in supplier management, contract manufacturing, OEM/ODM operations, or international manufacturing projects is preferred.
  • Strong analytical, project management, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Excellent communication and cross-functional coordination skills.
  • Professional certification or relevant engineering/economics qualification will be an advantage.

title: Renewable Energy Co-Founder / Head of Growth (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

title: FULL-TIME FLOOR MANAGER — LATIN GEISHA
description:
Latin Geisha is looking for a passionate and experienced Floor Manager to join our team!

We are a vibrant restaurant in the heart of Vesterbro, bringing together Latin American energy, Asian influences, great food, cocktails, wine, and a lot of personality.

We are looking for someone who understands hospitality, loves creating memorable experiences for guests, and enjoys being part of a fast-paced, energetic restaurant environment.

As our Floor Manager, you will play an important role in leading the floor team and ensuring that every service runs smoothly — from the moment our guests arrive until the end of their experience.

Your responsibilities will include:

• Leading and supporting the floor team during daily service
• Ensuring high standards of hospitality and guest experience
• Managing reservations and coordinating the flow of the restaurant
• Making sure service runs smoothly and efficiently
• Supporting, motivating, and developing the team
• Maintaining and improving our service standards
• Taking an active interest in our wine program and contributing to the development and creation of our wine menu
• Helping create a fun, energetic, and professional atmosphere for both guests and the team

We are looking for someone who:

• Has previous experience in a supervisory or management role within hospitality
• Has strong communication and leadership skills
• Is passionate about creating great guest experiences
• Has an interest in wine and is excited to learn and contribute to our wine program
• Is organized, proactive, and able to remain calm under pressure
• Enjoys working in a fast-paced and energetic environment
• Is passionate about hospitality and enjoys being part of a team
• Has the personality and energy to match the spirit of Latin Geisha!

If you think you could be the right person to join the Latin Geisha family, we would love to hear from you!

title: Gaming Manager - Ramsgate Hotel
description:
Gaming Manager | Ramsgate Hotel

Base Salary $79,600 + Super + Bonus Scheme + Group wide discounts

We’re on the lookout for a Gaming Manager to help run our gaming room and keep it the friendly, welcoming spot our locals love.

Our modern, stylish gaming room features 32 of the latest machines, full SA Lotteries facilities, and a dedicated Gaming Bar. We pride ourselves on delivering a clean, welcoming, and premium experience for our guests, driven by a friendly and attentive team focused on high-quality customer service.

A day in the life of a Gaming Manager:

  • Know the regulars by name and make them feel right at home
  • Work closely with the Publican to keep the room running smoothly, safely, and within all the rules
  • Share your gaming know-how and help your team be the best in the biz
  • Bring the good vibes, great service, and friendly smiles every shift

Inherent requirements:

  • This is a hands-on role that involves manual handling and lifting as part of regular duties (e.g. moving stock, kegs, and deliveries). Applicants must have the ability to perform physical tasks safely and efficiently.

About you:

  • You’ve been in the gaming room world for a while, as an attendant, supervisor, or manager
  • You’ve learned from great managers and are ready to step up
  • You’ve got the confidence, pride, and people skills to be the face of the gaming room
  • You understand compliance and safe gaming inside out
  • You’ve got a current RSA and RSG/RCG as per state requirements

If you’re all about great service, happy players, and running a top-notch gaming room – we’d love to chat.

The benefits are good too!

Grow your career within ALH Hotels and the wider Endeavour Group network. An exclusive discount card for ALH Hotels, BWS, Dan Murphy’s and other Endeavour Group brands. Through our partnership with Woolworths, you'll also enjoy discounts at Woolies supermarkets and BIG W. Endeavour Wellbeing Support; our EAP service offers critical incident support, career, conflict, nutrition and lifestyle, money, family, legal and employee assistance.

In line with our ongoing commitment to responsibility, candidates will be required to complete Employee Due Diligence checks which may include a National Police Check as part of the recruitment process.

#WeAreTogetherCreators #ComeAsYouAre #DreamBig #FeelTheEnergy #LeaveYourMark #ALH #EndeavourGroup

Our Talent Team and Hiring Leaders kindly request no unsolicited resumes or approaches from Recruitment Agencies. Endeavour Group is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

title: Accountant, Commodities (Contract)
description:
• Drive financial reporting and controls within a global commodities business
  • Gain exposure to derivatives accounting, controls and reporting

About Our Client

Our client is a leading multinational agribusiness and food company with a significant global footprint spanning dozens of countries across the Americas, Europe, Asia and Africa. As one of the world's largest participants in agricultural supply chains, the organisation connects producers and consumers through extensive commodity sourcing, processing and distribution operations.

With thousands of employees globally and a strong focus on operational excellence, digital transformation and financial governance, the company offers exceptional career development opportunities within a dynamic and international environment.

Job Description

  • Support the execution and coordination of monthly financial reporting and month-end close activities
  • Prepare balance sheet reviews, account analysis and commentary on key financial movements
  • Perform and review reconciliations relating to financial instruments, intercompany transactions, banking activities and trade balances
  • Investigate and resolve discrepancies through close collaboration with internal stakeholders and regional teams
  • Maintain the integrity, completeness and accuracy of financial data across reporting systems
  • Support internal control, audit and SOX compliance activities through timely preparation of documentation and supporting evidence
  • Partner with finance and shared services teams across multiple regions to ensure accurate and timely deliverables

The Successful Applicant

  • Degree in Accounting, Finance or a recognised professional accounting qualification
  • Min 5 years of accounting, financial reporting, controlling and/or audit experience
  • Min 5 years of experience handling financial instruments, derivatives accounting and related accounting treatments
  • Hands-on experience with month-end close, balance sheet reconciliations and financial reporting processes
  • Strong knowledge of SAP and enterprise financial systems
  • Familiarity with US GAAP and financial control frameworks
  • Experience supporting audits, internal controls and compliance-related activities

What's on Offer

This opportunity offers the chance to join a globally established organisation with strong financial governance and a collaborative international culture.

title: Performance Marketing Manager (m/w/d)
description:
Be(come) the Experience – Steuere Kampagnen, die wirklich performen.

Wir sind Kapten & Son. Mit über 250 Kaptens arbeiten wir täglich daran, Europas führende D2C Lifestyle Brand zu werden. Unsere Produkte begleiten Menschen bei ihren alltäglichen Erlebnissen – und du sorgst dafür, dass unsere Brand zur richtigen Zeit, auf dem richtigen Kanal, mit der richtigen Botschaft sichtbar ist und messbar wächst.

Start: 01.09.2026

Deine Kapten Experience umfasst

  • Performance-Strategien entwickeln und vorantreiben: Du verantwortest die Entwicklung, Steuerung und kontinuierliche Optimierung unserer Performance-Marketing-Strategien, mit echtem Eigenantrieb und klarem Blick auf Wirkung.

  • Kampagnen zum Leben erwecken: Du planst, setzt um und wertest Kampagnen über Meta Ads, TikTok Ads, Pinterest Ads, YouTube und Display Retargeting aus, kanalübergreifend, datenbasiert und immer mit dem Ziel, zu performen.

  • Budgets eigenverantwortlich steuern: Du übernimmst die Planung und Steuerung unserer Marketingbudgets und setzt sie dort ein, wo sie den größten Impact erzielen.

  • KPIs analysieren und Potenziale heben: Du hast relevante Kennzahlen wie ROAS, CPA, CAC, Conversion Rate, CTR und CPC im Blick, leitest konkrete Optimierungsmaßnahmen ab und setzt sie konsequent um.

  • Testen, lernen, optimieren: Du konzipierst und wertest A/B-Tests für Creatives und Kampagnen aus und bringst so unsere Performance kontinuierlich voran.

  • Interdisziplinär zusammenarbeiten und sichtbar machen: Als zentrale Schnittstelle arbeitest du eng mit Campaign, Country, Content, Design, Webentwicklung und E-Commerce zusammen und präsentierst deine Ergebnisse in regelmäßigen Reportings.

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium oder Ausbildung im Bereich BWL, Marketing oder einer vergleichbaren Fachrichtung

  • 3–5 Jahre Berufserfahrung im Performance Marketing, idealerweise im D2C- oder E-Commerce-Umfeld

  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse im Meta Ads Manager sowie Erfahrung mit weiteren Paid-Kanälen wie TikTok, Pinterest, YouTube oder Display Retargeting

  • Du interpretierst KPIs souverän, denkst datenbasiert und leitest daraus klare Handlungsempfehlungen ab

  • Strukturierte, detailorientierte und kommunikative Arbeitsweise – du behältst auch in dynamischen Phasen den Überblick

  • Gute Deutsch- sowie Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift

Was dich bei uns erwartet

  • Flexibilität: Gleitzeit + zwei Tage Remote pro Woche

  • Workation: Bis zu zwei Wochen im Jahr von deinem europäischen Lieblingsort

  • Unser HQ am Rhein: Modernes Office mit Barista, subventioniertem Lunch und Blick aufs Wasser

  • Crew Love: Regelmäßige Team-Events und Kreativschmieden

  • Wachstum: Persönliche Entwicklungsangebote und quartalsweise Feedback-Gespräche

  • Perks: Urban Sports Club, Corporate Benefits, Mitarbeiterrabatte

  • Volunteer Day: Ein bezahlter Tag für dein Ehrenamt

Ready to join the crew?

Ein Kapten by heart zu sein bedeutet für uns, füreinander da zu sein, mit Leidenschaft zu handeln und Performance-Marketing als echten Hebel für Wachstum zu verstehen. Wenn du Lust hast, Kampagnen zu gestalten, die messbar wirken und unsere Brand täglich sichtbarer machen – freut sich Carolin auf deine Nachricht.

Be the experience.

Mehr über uns als Arbeitgeber erfährst du auf @kaptenandson.career

title: Head of Sales Japan
description:
Sell what you love. For us and millions of users across the globe, that’s Spotify. Join the Sales team and you’ll build the relationships that help grow our business in existing markets and beyond. We don’t just sell creative solutions to our clients and partners, we help to shape them; using our expert knowledge of ad products, sales channels and the industry to impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts.

We’re looking for someone with an established record of sales leadership and revenue generation with a deep interest and passion for online media, music and innovation. You will be responsible for advertising efforts and lead a team of hardworking and consultative sales leaders within a defined region.
We want someone who has a passion for building and growing businesses, developing relationships externally and internally, is well versed in public speaking and engaged within local regions.

What You'll Do

  • Define the Japan sales growth strategy; meet and exceed revenue and targets
  • Handle sales forecasts accurately, set budgets and goals for the sales team
  • Engage with key clients on a senior level, and deliver extraordinary consultative customer service
  • Hire, develop and actively coach sales talent & managers within the region. Analyze, validate and update performance to increase growth
  • Implement/lead/oversee client events, social engagements, and sponsorships based on local market dynamics and broad business knowledge
  • Represent the Spotify Brand at client and agency summits through public speaking, panel moderation, and keynotes
  • Maintain high levels of communication and visibility with management, cross-functional teams, and business partners internally and externally
  • Collaborate with key cross-functional partners, including music & podcasts teams to collectively grow the Spotify business in Japan

    • Who You Are

  • You have 15+ years of advertising / marketing sales experience including at least 10 years in sales leadership which also includes 5+ years of larger staff management in the Japan market
  • Highly motivated, organized and entrepreneurial, you are not afraid to roll up your sleeves and build with the team
  • You are an inspiring leader who recognizes the value of working as a team and gets fulfillment from growing others
  • You have mastered the fundamentals of sales management and leadership including forecasting, client research, pitch and presentations skills, bottoms up planning, and meeting reports
  • You have strong agency & brand relationships, with an existing network of senior level contacts in media and marketing
  • Great communication, consultative selling, problem solving and negotiating skills and optimally position Spotify's ad solutions through creative storytelling
  • Strategically use data and insights to inform client investment strategy
  • You have a deep understanding of the digital ecosystem and trends around digital, programmatic, audio & video best practices, opportunities, and challenges
  • Understanding and expertise in account and strategic planning, and media buying

    • Where You'll Be

  • This role will be based in Tokyo, Japan
  • We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home. We ask that you come in 2-3 times per week
    • Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
    At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

title: Property and Casualty Underwriter
description:
about the company

Our client is a leading insurance corporation with a strong and growing foothold in the Asia-Pacific region. Known for our financial stability, innovative risk solutions, and commitment to excellence, they pride themselves on delivering exceptional value to our commercial clients and fostering a dynamic workplace for our employees.

about the job

As a Property and Casualty (P&C) Underwriter, you will be responsible for evaluating, pricing, and negotiating commercial property and casualty risks. Your day-to-day duties will involve analyzing complex risk data, ensuring profitable portfolio growth, and reviewing policy wordings. You will also play a key role in the market by building and maintaining strong relationships with brokers and distribution partners to drive both new business and successful renewals.

about the manager/team

You will be joining a highly collaborative, fast-paced, and experienced underwriting team. Reporting directly to the Head of Commercial Lines, you will work in a supportive environment that values continuous learning, mentorship, and open communication. The team is dedicated to sharing market insights and helping each other succeed.

skills and experience required

You should possess a Bachelor's degree with at least 3 to 5 years of proven experience in Property and Casualty underwriting within the commercial insurance sector. Strong analytical skills, a deep understanding of current market trends, and excellent negotiation abilities are essential. Exceptional interpersonal skills for broker management are a must, and professional insurance qualifications (such as ACII, CGI, or equivalent) will be highly advantageous.

To apply online please use the 'apply' function, alternatively you may contact Josh Goh(EA: 94C3609/R1104689)

title: Specialist, Settlement Services
description:
JOB SUMMARY / INTRODUCTION

  • Responsible for daily monitoring and processing of Settlement processes covering Domestic and Regional and International Market for equities, mutual funds, fixed income, hedge fund, structured funds, ETF, Local and International Securities Lending and Borrowing, Local Market Short Selling and ensuring highest quality of client service delivery as per international standards.
    Responsible for handling extremely complex settlement transaction across Local, Regional, International Markets. Processing responsibilities include the receipt of client, market / sub custodian instructions. Validating the correctness of all instructions. Ensuring the matching and settlement of instructions in timely manner across all markets.
    Communicate with many entities Clients, Counter parties, Markets, Sub Custodians, Regulators, Companies, Brokers, Paying Agents, Issuers covering Local, Regional, Global Markets, related to settlement process, support Due Diligence visits linking to Settlement process.
    The incumbent is responsible for adhering to established quality control measures and procedures.
    Responsible to provide the first level training for new specialists and will assist specialists in handling inquiries.
    Handle the cash managements for the client to facilitate the smooth settlements across all markets.
    Active participation in understanding the Operating module, including Securities Lending and Borrowing transactions, that are large in value and complex in nature, ensuring their successful settlement processing, avoiding loss and penalties to the bank and clients that could cause the bank Reputational, Financial and Operational Risk.
    Current offering of Department: Position Management, Security/Cash Settlement, Corporate Actions, Reporting, Invoicing, Reconciliation, Swift, Market Security Movement, FX Management, Cash Management, Project Support, Proxy Services, Tax Reclaims, Income Management. Planned expansion into support of Securities Lending, Client Short Selling, Network Management Support, Claim Management, and other services.
    Specialist will participate in section related projects such as system upgrade / changes, processing enhancements and off hours system testing.
    Active participation in implementation of Settlement processing related to new clients SLA and SLD, Sub Custodian SLA, New Functions, Products, Market Rules Regulations and Procedures with relevant controls to provide highest level of client delivery.
    Cope with market changes and subsequent upgradation of internal processes and to identify any potential risk and to create the appropriate controls.
    Responsible to coordinate and work with various internal departments and external entitles and concerned persons as an integral part related to participation in Projects in order to Re-engineer the process for excellence client delivery and reduce manual activities.
    Responsible for the end-to-end Settlements processing in accordance with regulatory and compliance requirements, with an increased focus on accurate and timely execution to enhance the client experience.
  • KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Responsible for daily monitoring and processing of Settlement processes covering Domestic, Regional and International Markets for products like equities mutual funds, fixed income, hedge fund, structured funds, ETF Local and International Securities Lending and Borrowing, Local Market Short Selling and ensuring highest quality of client service delivery as per international standards.
  • Responsible for handling extremely complex settlement transaction across Local, Regional, International Markets. Processing responsibilities include the receipt of client, market / sub custodian instructions. Validating the correctness of all instructions. Ensuring the matching and settlement of instructions in timely manner across all markets.
  • Ensure to track and respond to internal and external clients' queries by providing clear detailed Settlement information in line with the relevant market rules and regulations and Settlement best practice.
  • Responsible for communicating with various stakeholders like Markets, Sub Custodians, Agents, Companies, Depositories, Brokers, Issuers to ensure smooth accurate timely Settlement processing.
  • Ensure that all trade related statuses reports/Instructions are communicated to all stake holders in timely manners.
  • Responsible to have excellent knowledge related to Settlements swift messages and usages of the same.
  • Demonstrates reliability by taking necessary actions to continuously meet required deadlines and goals.
  • Articulates information clearly and presents information effectively and confidently when working with others.
  • Convince others by making a strong case, bringing others along to their viewpoint; maintains strong, trusting relationships while at the same time is comfortable challenging ideas.
  • Participation in section related projects, system changes and upgrades and new client’s requirements and implementation of sub custodian / client SLA
    Review processes and suggest and implement changes to enhance client satisfaction and reduce manual intervention and reduce overall cost.
  • Cash / FX Settlement: Review and validate all transactions inputted by the officer / another specialist. Escalate all mismatches to VP. Validate all data entered into the system and forward to VP for review. On settlement date of transaction ensure transaction settled and all appropriate accounting entries executed. Escalate any settlement issues / discrepancies to VP Validate and Sign any vouchers that need to be passed for FX / Cash execution for review by the VP
    Position / Cash Management: Review clients’ positions and holdings daily and reconcile positions with sub-custodians and market to ensure there are no discrepancies. Escalate any discrepancies to VP.
  • Daily review of client’s cash positions to ensure all clients have appropriate funding and escalating to VP and Middle Office any potential over drafts or if client is at risk of being insufficient.
  • SWIFTS: Validate swift instructions inputted by officer / another specialist into the system for VP to Authorize. Second Level of SWIFT generation process
    Reconciliation: Second level of reconciliation ensuring all appropriate checks are done to ensure there are no discrepancies and escalating any issues to the VP.
  • Ad Hoc Requests: Supporting the VP as required for business-as-usual matters and projects and initiatives
    Risk Indicators: Responsible to ensure controls and procedures are being adhered to by the section. Monitor all KPIs, KRIs, Capacity Planning. Submit any incidents and issues to VP for review. Monitoring transactions to ensure they are being processed according to Compliance / Audit standards (requirements)
    Follow all relevant departmental policies, processes, standard operating procedures, and instructions so that work is carried out in a controlled and consistent manner.
  • Demonstrate compliance to organization’s values and ethics at all times to support the establishment of a value drive culture within the bank
    Contribute to the identification of opportunities for continuous improvement and sustainability of systems, processes and practices considering global standards, productivity improvement and cost reduction.
  • Assist in the preparation of timely and accurate statements and reports to meet department requirements, policies and quality standards
    Participation on section related projects, systems upgrades / changes, new client requirements, new product functions related to corporate actions
    Understanding and active participation in Projects requirements and system upgrades and changes.
  • Ensure accurate and timely processing of client and client impacting instructions. Reconciling to confirm appropriate settlement of all transactions (FX, Cash, Securities, Corporate Action, Tax Reclaims). Volumes are significant, and as this is a new an up-and-coming business the volumes and complexity of the products and services being offered are increasing exponentially.
  • Managing transactions that are large in value and complex in nature, ensuring their successful settlement, avoiding loss and penalties to the bank and clients that could cause the bank Reputational, Financial and Operational Risk.
  • University Graduate / bachelor’s degree (or 5 years related work experience).
  • 5 + years related experience in security services and / or back-office operations e.g. managing securities settlements/ corporate actions.
  • Solid understanding of security services and operations and accounting activity related to security services.
  • Ability to work under pressure, to priorities and to manage several tasks at the same time.
  • Team oriented and ability to work under pressure.
  • Ability to achieve business objectives without compromising controls and risk parameters established.
  • Strong execution skills in a multi-tasking mode and follow-up effectively.
  • Self-starter with excellent attitude, communication and interpersonal skills.
  • High level of accuracy & attention to detail and excellent team working skills.
    Preferred / Beneficial

  • Specific experience in Securities related functions e.g. Settlement, Client Services, Corporate Actions, Data Maintenance & Billing or Custody Account Opening will be an added advantage.

    REQUIREMENTS / QUALIFICATIONS

  • University Graduate / bachelor’s degree (or 5 years related work experience).
  • 5 + years related experience in security services and / or back-office operations e.g. managing securities settlements/ corporate actions.
  • Solid understanding of security services and operations and accounting activity related to security services.
  • Ability to work under pressure, to priorities and to manage several tasks at the same time.
  • Team oriented and ability to work under pressure.
  • Ability to achieve business objectives without compromising controls and risk parameters established.
  • Strong execution skills in a multi-tasking mode and follow-up effectively.
  • Self-starter with excellent attitude, communication and interpersonal skills.
  • High level of accuracy & attention to detail and excellent team working skills.
    Preferred / Beneficial
  • Specific experience in Securities related functions e.g. Settlement, Client Services, Corporate Actions, Data Maintenance & Billing or Custody Account Opening will be an added advantage.

description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

  • You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Cybersecurity

  • You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management

  • You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs

  • You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding

  • You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively

  • You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate

  • You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues

  • You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: Engineer I Mechanical Engineering IX
description:
Job Description for Engineer – Mechanical

Job Description:

To Design and Develop Outdoor Enclosures in sheet metal domain for Telecommunication Products. He / She will be working on New product Development projects, VA/VE Projects & Quality Improvement projects as required. He / She will take responsibility of project execution from end to end and deliver as per customer need along with assistance from Engineer, Sr. Engineer or Lead Engineer as required. He / She needs to Estimate, Plan and execute projects along with other team members to meet deadlines as per agreement. He / She will be in coordination with Sr. Engineer/ Asst. or Deputy Manager/Manager for projects on Design and Development. Person should independently handle Analytical calculations and actively participate in Design Reviews. Being part of Global Engineering Function, he / she is expected to maintain desired performance (quality & schedule adherence) levels set by organization and report as per defined communication plan.

Job Specification:

  • Diploma/ Bachelor degree in Mechanical Engineering
  • Minimum 3 years of experience in New Product Design Is must
  • Excellent command on Creo into sheet metal domain is must.
  • Experience on Part & Assembly Modelling, Drafting and Engineering Change Orders is required.
  • Experience in handling large Creo CAD assemblies and manage modifications is desirable.
  • Hands on experience on any PDM/PLM tools like Oracle PLM, Team Center, Windchill is required
  • Knowledge of material selection & Sheet metal manufacturing processes is required.
  • Experience on design of Telecom/Electrical/Electronic Enclosure Product is preferable.
  • Experience on DFMEA & DFMA of Product assembly processes is desirable.
  • Experience in Value Engineering and Value Analysis projects is added advantage.
  • Self-motivated, capable of working with minimal supervision and timely manner.
  • Handle projects or Project tasks independently as needed.
  • Good Interpersonal skills and ability to work in team environment.
  • Ability to communicate effectively in both written and spoken English.
  • A willingness to learn is critical.

title: Full Stack Developer
description:
Who are we?

At BCE Global Tech we are on a mission to modernize global connectivity, one connection at a time. We aim to build the highway to the future of communications, media and entertainment, determined to emerge as a powerhouse within the technology landscape in India team in Bengaluru.
We bring ambitions to life through design thinking that bridges the gaps between people, devices and beyond, fostering unprecedented customer satisfaction through technology.
Our core values support a customer-centric approach and the harnessing of cutting-edge technology to provide business outcomes with positive societal impact. Guided by innovation and a commitment to progress, we’re shaping a brighter future for the generations of today and tomorrow.
If you would like to be a part of a team of thought-leaders pioneering advancements in 5G, MEC, IoT and cloud-native architecture, we’d love to hear from you

Requirements

We are seeking a talented and experienced Senior Java Developer to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining scalable backend systems and microservices while leveraging cutting-edge technologies.

Key Responsibilities:

· Design and implement robust and efficient microservices using Spring Boot.

· Architect scalable systems based on microservices architecture principles.

· Utilize containerization tools such as Docker, Kubernetes, and OpenShift to package and deploy applications.

· Collaborate with DevOps teams to leverage cloud platforms including AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure.

· Manage and optimize both relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL) and NoSQL databases (MongoDB, Cassandra).

· Develop asynchronous communication systems using RabbitMQ or Apache Kafka.

· Create and maintain APIs, including REST, GraphQL, and gRPC, for seamless integration across platforms.

· Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality solutions on time.


Required Skills and Qualifications:

· Proven expertise in Java programming and backend development with 9 to 14 years experience.

· Strong experience with Spring Boot for building microservices and RESTful APIs.

· In-depth knowledge of microservices architecture and its implementation.

· Hands-on experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and OpenShift.

· Proficiency in cloud platforms like AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure.

· Solid understanding of relational and NoSQL databases, including MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and Cassandra.

· Experience with messaging systems such as RabbitMQ or Apache Kafka.

· Knowledge of API development technologies, including REST, GraphQL, and gRPC



Benefits

Benefits

What We Offer:

Competitive salaries and comprehensive health benefits
Flexible work hours and remote work options
Professional development and training opportunities
A supportive and inclusive work environment

title: Senior Test Consultant (Nederlands sprekend)
description:
Als Senior Test Consultant bij Netcompany werk je samen met je projectteam om verschillende applicaties gestructureerd en succesvol te testen, zowel handmatig als automatisch. Het is van belang dat je informatie makkelijk opneemt, je een helicopterview hebt, maar ook oog voor detail hebt. Geen enkele applicatie is hetzelfde.

Wat ga je doen?

  • Opstellen en (laten) uitvoeren van testplannen.
  • Vastleggen en beheren van bevindingen.
  • Coördineren en/of schrijven van geautomatiseerde tests in Selenium (C#), afhankelijk van het project.
  • Rapporteren over de testvoortgang aan de projectleider of testmanager.
  • Begeleiden van gebruikers bij de klant en ondersteunen op het gebied van testen.
  • Proactief meedenken met onze klanten over oplossingsrichtingen voor het testproces.

Wat breng je mee?

Wij zoeken collega’s met de volgende vaardigheden en competenties:

  • Een bachelor diploma, bij voorkeur in de richting van IT.
  • TMAP en/of ISTQB Foundation gecertificeerd.
  • Minimaal 3 jaar relevante werkervaring en ervaring met het opzetten van een (geautomatiseerd) testtraject voor een of meerdere projecten.
  • Klant- en oplossingsgerichtheid.
  • Vermogen om zowel zelfstandig als in teamverband te werken.
  • Sterke analytische vaardigheden, kwaliteitsgerichtheid en nauwkeurigheid.
  • Ervaring met het begeleiden en/of aansturen van andere testers.
  • Beheersing van de Nederlandse en Engelse taal in woord en geschrift.

Word onderdeel van onze inclusieve community

Bij Netcompany stimuleren we een open en inclusieve werkomgeving, gebaseerd op samenwerking, vertrouwen en respect. Wij vinden het belangrijk dat iedereen zich gezien, gehoord en gewaardeerd voelt — ongeacht achtergrond, identiteit of ervaring.

Je werkt samen met collega’s uit verschillende landen en disciplines en kunt deelnemen aan diverse sociale en professionele activiteiten om je netwerk uit te breiden.

Wat wij bieden?

    • Afhankelijk van je werkervaring een bruto maandsalaris tussen de € 4.375,- en € 5.815,- op basis van een fulltime dienstverband.
    • 26 vakantiedagen en 8% vakantiegeld.
    • Een pensioenregeling waarbij Netcompany twee derde van de premie voor haar rekening neemt.
    • Volledige vergoeding van reiskosten met het openbaar vervoer of €0,25 per kilometer bij reizen met de auto.
    • Een thuiswerkvergoeding van €2,45 (netto) per dag.
    • De mogelijkheid om tot vijf vakantiedagen te kopen of te verkopen.
    • Een flexibele feestdagenregeling die culturele en religieuze diversiteit ondersteunt.
    • Uitgebreide ontwikkelmogelijkheden via de Netcompany Academy , waaronder een vijfdaags Client Engagement Seminar in Kopenhagen.
    • Mogelijkheden om maatschappelijke impact te maken via JINC-projecten.
    • Een laptop en mobiele telefoon van de zaak.
    • Een fietsregeling en korting op sportschoolabonnementen.
    • Dagelijks premium snacks op kantoor, met altijd koffie, thee en vers fruit beschikbaar.
    • Diverse sociale activiteiten, waaronder evenementen georganiseerd door AfterDark en jaarlijkse zomer- en winterfeesten.

    Solliciteer bij ons

    Geïnteresseerd? Upload jouw CV en (optioneel) motivatiebrief hier!

    Als je nog vragen hebt, aarzel dan niet om contact op te nemen met Ankita Chadha via +31 611 431 235 of stuur je CV naar recruitment.nl@netcompany.com.

    Wij zijn een werkgever die zich inzet voor mensen met een beperking en we creëren een inclusieve en diverse omgeving waarin elk individu welkom is. Onze sollicitatieprocessen zijn gebaseerd op individuele behoeftes. Als je tijdens het sollicitatiegesprek behoefte hebt aan extra ondersteuning, stuur dan een e-mail naar recruitment.nl@netcompany.com. We helpen je met plezier.

    Alle gekwalificeerde kandidaten worden aangemoedigd om te solliciteren, ongeacht geslacht, genderexpressie, seksuele geaardheid, beperkingen, leeftijd, religieuze overtuigingen, politieke opvattingen, nationaliteit, etnische achtergrond of sociale afkomst. #LI-MN1

    Onderzoek laat zien dat veel talentvolle mensen pas solliciteren als zij aan alle functie-eisen voldoen.

    Spreekt deze rol je aan, maar voldoe je niet aan alles?

    Neem gerust contact op met een van onze recruiters.

title: Werkstudent*in (m/w/d) Commercial Litigation
