title: Assistant Outlet Manager (Food & Beverage)
description:
Company Description

Paradox Singapore is an urban resort that offers guests a sophisticated yet playful blend of traditional refinement and modern elegance. Located in the vibrant river district of Clarke Quay, the hotel is perfect for those looking for a stylish home base during business travel, or a quiet sanctuary to relax and unwind. Our comfortable rooms and spacious suites feature picturesque views of the city's skyline and are equipped with thoughtful amenities for laid-back style without any fuss.

Role Description

This is a full-time on-site role for Assistant Outlet Manager (Food & Beverage) who plays a vital role in the smooth daily functioning of the hotel by coordinating and supervising operational departments including Food & Beverage, Front Office and Security. Act as a key support to the Senior Operations Manager / Operations Manager, this role combines hands-on involvement, supervisory leadership, and a keen eye for details to ensure all aspects of the guest experience meet the highest standards.

Designed for individuals with a passion for hospitality and service, this position offers a strong foundation in hotel operations and is ideal for those aspiring to become future hospitality leaders.

Responsibilities:

  • Handle daily operations across Food & Beverage (F&B), Front Office, and Security departments to ensure seamless guest experiences and operational efficiency.
  • Oversee service delivery, team performance, hygiene standards, and setup for events, meetings, and group functions
  • Coordinate closely with Culinary and F&B teams to maintain timely communication, outlet readiness, inventory control, and presentation standards.
  • Manage check-in/check-out, Service Centre operations, VIP arrivals, and personalized requests, ensuring accuracy and hospitality excellence.
  • Monitor lobby presentation, team grooming, and coordinate concierge functions such as transportation, tours, and special arrangements.
  • Address and resolve guest complaints or special request promptly, escalating when necessary.
  • Supervise security shift activities, ensuring all patrol, surveillance, and emergency procedures are followed in line with safety protocols.
  • Ensure security teams are briefed on VIPs, special events, and high-risk zones; participate in drills and support incident response.
  • Review and act on incident reports, collaborate with relevant teams during emergencies, and assist with enforcing safety and evacuation procedures.
  • Prepare and maintain accurate operational reports and logs for management review.
  • Ensure full compliance with Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), brand guidelines, and health & safety regulations.
  • Identify service gaps and recommend workflow improvements, supporting the implementation of new initiatives to enhance efficiency and guest satisfaction.
  • Contribute to the training and onboarding of new employees through effective On-the-Job Training (OJT) and continuous support.
  • Perform other duties as assigned by management.

title: Sales Manager
description:
OVERVIEW

Experience a place of energy, passion, and excitement. A place where the joy of discovery and uncommon artistry blend to create exhilarating buying experiences—for true beauty enthusiasts. At Ulta Beauty, we’re transforming the world one shade, one lash, one cut at a time. Because beauty is powerful.

If you seek greater purpose—a place of vision, mission, and lived values—where voices are heard, contributions valued and recognized and growth opportunities abound, consider Ulta Beauty. Nowhere else are the possibilities quite this beautiful.

GENERAL SUMMARY & SCOPE

The Sales Manager (SM) is responsible for leading through Ulta Beauty’s mission, vision, and values in order to develop a high-performing team that consistently delivers top-line sales growth across all categories. The SM leads a team of Beauty Advisors, Prestige Beauty Advisors, and Lead Cashiers and is accountable for all aspects of the retail business with the exception of boutiques, including sales, service, and operational process. The SM is accountable for Net Promoter Score (NPS) and delivering an exceptional guest experience. This leader drives the business through a focus on performance (NPS, sales and in-store events), people (guest service and associate training), and process (standard operating procedures and compliance standards). This position requires a drive for results, a passion for the beauty industry, exceptional guest service, the aptitude to learn and teach extensive product knowledge, and the ability to lead, influence, and develop their team.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES (*Essential Job Functions)

The SM is a champion of Ulta Beauty’s mission, vision, and values, and should demonstrate them skillfully and consistently by performing the following duties and responsibilities of the position (as well as all other projects/duties as assigned):

Performance

  • Promote a culture of accountability to meet or exceed Ulta Beauty’s goals related to retail and service sales, guest loyalty (including credit), payroll, omni-channel, and retail shrink as set by Ulta Beauty for the store.
  • Drive company profitability by identifying underperforming metrics and developing strategies that leverage company programs, tools, and resources through operational excellence to deliver top-line sales growth and improve business.
  • Leverage store forecast and payroll budgets to support with store scheduling needs and communicate the needs to the GM to maximize productivity, achieve sales and payroll goals, and complete workload on time, and within the payroll budget.
  • Plan and execute in-store events that deliver an unrivaled guest experience, drive in-store and brand partner collaboration, and deliver on sales and payroll goals.
  • Review, analyze and react to Ulta Beauty’s financial and operational reporting, including store visit and audit results, regularly and in a timely manner.
  • Perform makeup applications, skincare analysis, and product demonstrations with guests to drive sales and guest service experience.
  • Take the initiative to stay informed regarding new and existing industry trends, products, and services during work time and be knowledgeable about the ingredients and benefits of these trends, products, and services to better serve guests.
  • Maintain prompt, regular attendance as the SM and hold store associates accountable to the Ulta Beauty attendance policy.

People

  • Attract, hire, and retain a diverse team of top talent, and efficiently address any staffing needs and open positions for the store.
  • Build a highly engaged team at the store that embodies the Ulta Beauty brand through effective collaboration, open and honest communication, and prioritization of work.
  • Create an inclusive environment that inspires and encourages the growth and engagement of associates.
  • Support all aspects of associate professional development, including training, providing individualized competency-based feedback and coaching, performance reviews, succession planning, and individual development plans to enable continuous development and drive sales performance.
  • Regularly participate in ongoing training, including brand and category education, to enable continuous professional development and drive sales performance.
  • Ensure direct reports regularly participate in ongoing training, including brand and category education, to enable continuous professional development and drive sales performance.
  • Establish professional brand partner relationships that foster a shared interest in collaboratively delivering on sales and service goals.
  • Model a culture of teamwork and guest services excellence while working alongside the leadership team by establishing priorities, providing clear direction and support, and sharing best practices.
  • Execute the Guest Engagement Leader program flawlessly through business ownership, associate coaching and leading a helpful and friendly guest experience to drive business results across all Annual Performance Goals (APGs) metrics for the store.

Process

  • Be knowledgeable of and ensure compliance with Ulta Beauty’s policies, procedures, and standards for the store.
  • Adhere to and enforce Ulta Beauty’s dress code.
  • Use the company’s scheduling tool as directed to adjust schedules and manage attendance in-the-moment during manager-on-duty shifts to ensure accurate schedules and reporting.
  • Use the company’s task management tool as directed to prioritize and execute store workload, including product and marketing resets, pricing updates, inventory related tasks, cleaning, and replenishment.
  • Protect company assets and minimize loss by ensuring all store standards and operating procedures are met, including workplace safety, inventory control, cash management, and loss prevention.
  • Execute day-to-day inventory control processes (e.g., known theft, damages, hazardous waste, inventory adjustments, product returns) and complete theft reporting as necessary.
  • Support continuous improvement by influencing the adoption of company initiatives and technology, communicating expectations, and ensuring the execution of all store tasks.
  • Utilize company programs, tools, and resources to drive store improvements.
  • Regularly communicate and provide feedback to field leadership on business trends and opportunities, events, operational challenges, merchandise needs, and competitive landscape for their store.

JOB QUALIFICATIONS

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree is preferred

Experience

  • 2-3+ years of fast-paced, retail management, or other relevant work experience
  • Financial management: success with driving top-line sales, interpreting reporting data, managing payroll budgets, and controlling expense
  • Retail management: proven ability with monitoring inventory levels, achieving operational excellence, driving a service culture, and executing merchandising directives
  • Leadership management: experience with attracting, developing, and motivating top talent, swiftly adapting to change and leading others through the change-curve, developing collaborative relationships with others, and leading and influencing a team

Skills

  • Proficient with basic technology (e.g., Point of Sale system, Microsoft Office programs, and Apple devices)
  • Excellent written and verbal communication
  • Strong collaboration and interpersonal skills
  • Strong organizational skills to manage multiple tasks
  • Ability to react under pressure, use good judgment in ambiguous situations, and be flexible/adaptable

SPECIAL POSITION REQUIREMENTS

  • Work a flexible, full-time schedule to include days, evenings, weekends, and holidays
  • Attend corporate business meetings and conferences

WORKING CONDITIONS

  • Continuous mobility throughout the store during shift, including twisting
  • Frequent bending, pulling, pushing, stooping, reaching, and twisting during shift
  • Frequent lifting and/or moving up to 25 lbs. during shift
  • Continuous coordination and manipulation of objects during shift

If an associate has a disability that prevents them from performing an essential function of the job, the Company will engage in the interactive process with the associate to determine whether there is a reasonable accommodation that will enable the associate to perform the essential functions of the job.




The pay range for this position is $28.00 - $35.00 / Hour with the opportunity for eligible associates to earn additional compensation pursuant to the Company’s bonus plan. Exact pay will be based on factors including, but not limited to relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, level, shift, geographic location, and business and organizational needs. Full-time positions are eligible for paid time off, health, dental, vision, life and disability benefits. Part-time positions are eligible for dental, vision, life, and disability benefits. For additional information concerning our benefits, visit our Benefits and Career Development page: https://learn.bswift.com/ulta

ABOUT

At Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ: ULTA), the possibilities are beautiful. Ulta Beauty is the largest North American beauty retailer and the premier beauty destination for cosmetics, fragrance, skin care products, hair care products and salon services. We bring possibilities to life through the power of beauty each and every day in our stores and online with more than 25,000 products from approximately 500 well-established and emerging beauty brands across all categories and price points, including Ulta Beauty’s own private label. Ulta Beauty also offers a full-service salon in every store featuring—hair, skin, brow, and make-up services.

We will consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with arrest records, conviction records, or other criminal histories, in a manner consistent with the requirements of any applicable state and local laws, including the City of Los Angeles’ Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, and the New York City Fair Chance Act.

title: UAE National Document Controller
description:

This is a full-time onsite position in Sharjah, UAE.

Wood is currently recruiting for a Graduate Document Controller (Emirati) to join its UAE business unit focusing on supporting project teams by ensuring all project documents are accurately controlled, stored, distributed, and maintained in accordance with company and client procedures.

This is a full-time onsite position in Sharjah, UAE.

#Wood #UAE #Sharjah #Graduate Document Controller

The role

This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced Graduate Document Controller to join our UAE Business Unit.

Our Clients and Projects

Designing the future. Transforming the world.

Wood is an integral part of operations in Iraq, and we are continuing to grow our footprint across the country. With a 90-year heritage, 10 major contracts and over 850 employees, we have the skills, expertise and asset knowledge to support clients to deliver energy security and decarbonisation ambitions.

Wood excels in supporting our clients to deliver complex upstream and midstream oil and gas projects in Iraq delivering maintenance, modifications and engineering solutions. We are committed to decarbonising existing assets, producing cleaner energy and developing a sustainable national workforce, ensuring the long-term growth of the region.

What we can offer

  • Meaningful and interesting projects Delivered to high profile clients and complex projects in Iraq
  • Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion; we are an organisation actively committed to diversity and inclusion across our business with employee networks committed to giving all employees a voice
  • Competitive salary with regular salary reviews to ensure we are rewarding at the right level in line with the market
  • Commitment to continued professional development; development plans that are tailored to your individual needs and interests
  • Global connections: join experts around the world who are at the leading edge of our industry, shaping the standards of our profession
  • Medical Insurance: Ensures inpatient and outpatient medical care is available should you require treatment.
  • Group Life and Personal Accident Insurance: The company provides Life Assurance cover
  • The Employee Share Plan: Gives you the opportunity to purchase Wood shares and receive a match from the company
  • Long Service Award: Wood values the commitment and loyalty of our people. Celebrating Long Service provides an opportunity for the business to demonstrate their appreciation of the hard work, dedication and many years’ services of our employees at 5-year service milestones

Job Purpose

The Graduate Document Controller will support project teams by ensuring all project documents are accurately controlled, stored, distributed, and maintained in accordance with company and client procedures. Training will be provided.

Key Objectives

  • Assist with the registration, filing, and distribution of project documents in line with company procedures.
  • Maintain accurate records of incoming and outgoing documentation.
  • Ensure documents are correctly version‑controlled and archived.
  • Support the setup and maintenance of project document registers.
  • Support communication between project teams, vendors, and clients regarding documentation.
  • Assist in tracking document status and following up on outstanding submissions.
  • Support meeting documentation, including distribution of minutes and action lists.
  • Upload, manage, and retrieve documents from the Electronic Document Management System (EDMS).
  • Ensure all documentation complies with project quality and audit requirements.
  • Assist with audits by providing requested documentation and records.
  • Learn document control best practices, project workflows, and system usage.
  • Actively seek feedback and demonstrate continuous improvement

What makes you remarkable?

At Wood, we are committed to equal opportunities and welcome all talented individuals to consider joining our team. So even if you don't match every statement below but feel you have some of the experience, knowledge or skills needed for this role, we encourage you to apply. It will take all of us working together to deliver solutions to the world’s most critical challenges.

Expected:

  • Bachelor’s degree or Diploma in: Business Administration / Information Management / Engineering / IT or a related field
  • Fresh graduate or up to 1 year of experience (internship experience is an advantage)
  • Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
  • Exposure to document management systems is an advantage but not required

What We Offer

  • Entry‑level role designed for UAE National graduates
  • Structured training and on‑the‑job mentoring
  • Exposure to professional project and engineering environments
  • Clear career development path in Document Control / Project Administration
  • Supportive, inclusive, and professional work culture

title: Werkstudent Gezondheid, Innovatie & Duurzaamheid
description:
• affiniteit met de gezondheidszorg (life science & healthcare), danwel met duurzaamheid (energie/transport) en/of innovatie;
  • analyseren van verschillende industrie thema’s voor onze klanten, zodat daar geschikte subsidiemogelijkheden aan gekoppeld kunnen worden;
  • meeschrijven aan artikelen en aan het opstellen van presentaties voor klanten;
  • identificeren van energie efficiënte processen en de daarbij horende financiële / fiscale voordelen;
  • analyseren van R&D van bedrijven, om aan de hand van die informatie subsidieaanvragen te schrijven;
  • ondersteunen in business development, targeting en project management voor het GI3 team.

Natuurlijk ga je door tot de oplossing er is en maak je weleens fouten, maar hier haal je jouw leerpunten uit. Bovenal durf je jezelf te zijn. De ware jij. Met je eigen sterke punten, blik op de wereld en persoonlijkheid. Verder heb je als Werkstudent Gezondheid, Innovatie & Duurzaamheid:

  • Minimaal de eerste 2 jaar van jouw wo-opleiding afgerond. Hiervoor zijn meerdere studieachtergronden mogelijk, zoals: Fiscaal Recht/Economie, ICT, Life Science, Biotechnologie, Biochemistry, Innovation, Technology, Engineering, of een andere technische achtergrond;
  • Persoonlijke groei doorgemaakt buiten je studie om (denk aan bijbaan, sport, vrijwilligerswerk of bestuur);
  • Goede communicatieve vaardigheden en ben je commercieel vaardig (vertalen van de klantwens);
  • Een uitstekende beheersing van de Nederlandse en Engelse taal, in woord en geschrift;
  • En ben je 3 dagen per week beschikbaar.

Als Werkstudent Gezondheid, Innovatie & Duurzaamheid word je volledig onderdeel van het team en ondersteun je jouw collega’s bij hun werkzaamheden. Hierbij help je bedrijven grip te krijgen op de fiscale subsidies voor gezondheid, duurzaamheid of innovatie. Deze functie is een uitgelezen kans om te zien of wij jouw toekomstige werkgever zijn. Na je werkstudentschap kan je jouw scriptie schrijven bij ons, of start je na het afstuderen in het Evolve Traineeship.

title: Projektleiter (m/w/d) TGA im Hochbau
description:
Unser Mandant ist ein erfolgreiches und wirtschaftlich stark aufgestelltes Bauunternehmen mit langjähriger Erfahrung in der Realisierung anspruchsvoller Hochbauprojekte. Das Unternehmen steht für höchste Qualitätsstandards, innovative Lösungen und eine partnerschaftliche Zusammenarbeit mit Kunden und Projektbeteiligten. Moderne Strukturen, eine nachhaltige Unternehmensentwicklung und eine ausgezeichnete Auftragslage bieten optimale Voraussetzungen für eine langfristige berufliche Perspektive. Zur Verstärkung des Teams suchen wir einen Projektleiter (m/w/d) TGA im Hochbau, der technische Gebäudeausrüstungsprojekte eigenverantwortlich steuert und aktiv zum Erfolg anspruchsvoller Bauvorhaben beiträgt.
  • Eigenverantwortliche Leitung und Steuerung der TGA-Gewerke in anspruchsvollen Hochbauprojekten
  • Koordination und Abstimmung mit Bauherren, Fachplanern, Nachunternehmern und internen Projektteams
  • Sicherstellung der termin-, kosten- und qualitätsgerechten Umsetzung aller TGA-Leistungen
  • Technische Prüfung, Steuerung und Optimierung der Planungs- und Ausführungsprozesse
  • Begleitung der Projekte von der Planungsphase bis zur erfolgreichen Inbetriebnahme und Übergabe
  • Abgeschlossenes Studium oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation im Bereich Versorgungstechnik, Gebäudetechnik, Elektrotechnik oder TGA
  • Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in der Projektleitung oder Bauleitung im Bereich Technische Gebäudeausrüstung
  • Fundierte Kenntnisse in der Planung und Umsetzung anspruchsvoller TGA-Projekte im Hochbau
  • Strukturierte, selbstständige und wirtschaftliche Arbeitsweise sowie ausgeprägtes Organisationsgeschick
  • Kommunikationsstärke, Teamfähigkeit und ein souveränes Auftreten gegenüber allen Projektbeteiligten
  • Unbefristete Festanstellung in einem wirtschaftlich erfolgreichen und zukunftssicheren Unternehmen
  • Attraktive und leistungsgerechte Vergütung mit zusätzlichen Benefits
  • Verantwortungsvolle Projekte mit hohem Gestaltungsspielraum und Eigenverantwortung
  • Individuelle Weiterbildungs- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten für Ihre fachliche und persönliche Karriere
  • Moderne Arbeitsmittel sowie digitalisierte Projekt- und Arbeitsprozesse
  • Langfristige Perspektive in einem motivierten und professionellen Team
Sie möchten anspruchsvolle TGA-Projekte im Hochbau eigenverantwortlich steuern und Teil eines erfolgreichen Unternehmens werden? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung unter Angabe Ihres frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermins. Nach Eingang Ihrer Unterlagen setzen wir uns zeitnah mit Ihnen in Verbindung und informieren Sie transparent über die nächsten Schritte im Bewerbungsprozess.

title: Board Validation and Test Engineer
description:
About us:
Aeva’s mission is to bring the next wave of perception to a broad range of applications from automated driving to industrial robotics, consumer electronics, consumer health, security, and beyond. Aeva is transforming autonomy with its groundbreaking sensing and perception technology that integrates all key LiDAR components onto a silicon photonics chip in a compact module. Aeva 4D LiDAR sensors uniquely detect instant velocity in addition to 3D position, allowing autonomous devices like vehicles and robots to make more intelligent and safe decisions.
Role Overview:
We are looking for an experienced Board Validation and Test Engineer to join our test engineering team. This role, you will drive board DFT, design validation test platforms and high-volume ICT & FCT bring up for all Aeva’s product lines.

What you'll be doing:

  • Drive PCBA testability (DFT) from schematic and functional reviews for high-volume manufacturing. Work with electrical design team to assess test point and boundary scan coverage, and other DFT techniques into board designs to enhance test coverage and fault detection.
  • Develop test platforms for initial board bring up and low volume prototype and engineering validation builds. Write test methods and extract metrics using python programming for these test platforms.
  • Assess contract manufacturing capabilities and define the requirements for in-circuit test (ICT) and functional test (FCT) for high volume manufacturing.
  • Drive board yield fallout investigations by isolating intermittent, marginal, and low-yield failures using systematic debug methods including debug of power integrity, clocks, resets, boot sequences, and mixed-signal paths.
  • Correlate ICT/FCT failures with schematic intent, layout, and silicon performance.
  • Use advanced instrumentation (scopes, logic analyzers, current probes, thermal tools) to capture elusive failures.
  • Create debug playbooks, DFT guidelines, and failure isolation documentation.
  • Collaborate with engineering and manufacturing teams to ensure on time delivery with optimum test coverage at lowest cost.
  • Document test strategies, procedures, and results for internal reference and compliance purposes.
  • Provide end-to-end support through entire product life cycle from NPI (New Product Introduction) to high volume manufacturing.

    • What you'll have:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Electrical/Electronics Engineering or equivalent experience
  • 7+ years of hands-on experience in board bring up and validation
  • DFT experience and strong practical knowledge for ICT and FCT
  • Expert ability to read and critique schematics and PCB layouts
  • Deep understanding of analog, digital, power, and mixed-signal systems and boundary scan (JTAG)
  • Proven track record of improving yield through DFT and failure analysis learnings
  • Hands-on experience in Python programming for test automation
  • Experience with data analysis using tools such as JMP and dashboards
  • Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to troubleshoot complex technical issues
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams
  • Experience working with CMs worldwide
  • Excellent verbal and written communication and presentation skills and capable of making clear conclusions and recommendations

    • What's in it for you:

  • Be part of an early start-up that's backed by industry-leading venture capitalists
  • Very competitive compensation and meaningful equity!
  • Exceptional benefits: Medical, Dental, Vision, and more!
  • 401k matching
  • Unlimited PTO: We care about results, not punching timecards
  • Paid lunches, ping pong tournaments, and fun team off-sites!
    • Aeva is an Equal Opportunity Employer including protected Veterans and individuals with disabilities.

title: Anlagenmechaniker für Sanitär-, Heizungs- und Klima-technik (m/w/d) oder Gas- und Wasserinstallateur (m/w/d)
description:
Die Beschäftigungs- und Qualifizierungsgesellschaft Werkhof gem. GmbH in Hagen ist ein kommunaler Beschäftigungs- und Qualifizierungsträger mit vielfältigen Aktivitäten im Geltungsbereich des SGB II und SGB III. Unsere Schwerpunkte liegen in der Begleitung und Qualifizierung von Langzeitarbeitslosen sowie Jugendlichen im Übergang Schule-Beruf und Menschen mit gesundheitlichen Einschränkungen. Im Rahmen unserer Arbeitsmarktmaßnahmen betreiben wir unter anderem mehrere Sozialkaufhäuser, die den Teilnehmenden eine niederschwellige Arbeitsalltagserfahrung sowie wiederkehrende Tagesstruktur und eine sinnvolle Beschäftigung bieten.

Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams im Werkhof Hagen suchen wir einen engagierten Anlagenmechaniker für Sanitär-, Heizungs- und Klimatechnik (m/w/d) oder Gas- und Wasserinstallateur (m/w/d). Es handelt sich um eine zunächst befristete Stelle mit einem von mindestens 25 Wochenstunden.

Deine Aufgabenschwerpunkte

  • Durchführung von Sanitärinstallationen sowie Wartungs- und Reparaturarbeiten

  • Installation, Instandhaltung und Erneuerung von Wasser- und Abwasserleitungen

  • Montage und Reparatur von sanitären Einrichtungen

  • Durchführung von Kleinreparaturen und Instandsetzungsarbeiten in unseren Einrichtungen

  • Fehlersuche sowie Behebung von Störungen an sanitären Anlagen

  • Dokumentation der ausgeführten Arbeiten

  • Unterstützung bei Umbau-, Renovierungs- und Modernisierungsmaßnahmen

  • Einhaltung der geltenden Sicherheits-, Qualitäts- und Hygienestandards

Dein Profil

  • Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung als Anlagenmechaniker SHK (m/w/d), Gas- und Wasserinstallateur (m/w/d) oder vergleichbare Qualifikation

  • Berufserfahrung im Bereich Sanitär-, Heizungs- und Klimatechnik

  • Selbstständige, zuverlässige, saubere und sorgfältige Arbeitsweise

  • Handwerkliches Geschick sowie technisches Verständnis

  • Improvisationstalent bei Arbeiten im Bestandsbau

  • Teamfähigkeit, Flexibilität und Verantwortungsbewusstsein

  • Eigenständige und sorgfältige Arbeitsweise sowie Planungs- und Organisationsgeschick

  • Freundliches, souveränes und serviceorientiertes Auftreten

Wir bieten

  • Eine abwechslungsreiche Tätigkeit in einem engagierten Team unterschiedlicher Professionen

  • Freiraum zur Gestaltung und Strukturierung des eigenen Arbeitsalltags

  • Eine familiäre Atmosphäre mit niedrigen Hierarchien

  • Umfangreiches Benefit-Programm mit tollen Rabatten

  • Betriebsrestaurant

  • 30 Urlaubstage im Jahr bei einer 5-Tage-Woche, dienstfreie Tage: 24.12. und 31.12.

Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

Bei Rückfragen zum Bewerbermanagement wenden Sie sich an:
Hagener Versorgungs- und Verkehrs-GmbH,
Personalmanagement - Karina Schäfer, Am Pfannenofen 5, 58097 Hagen
Tel.-Nr. 02331/208-258

Werkhof gem. GmbH,

Malermeisterin - Mandy Baltzer

title: Personal Assistant - Leading Global Bank
description:
• Opportunity to join a Leading Global Bank

title: Consultant (m/w/d) Business Process Management
description:
Akkodis ist ein global führendes Unternehmen in Engineering, IT und F&E. Mit unserem 360°-Angebot unterstützen wir Kunden in einer dynamischen Technologie- und Wettbewerbswelt dabei, eine nachhaltige und smarte Zukunft zu gestalten. Unsere 50.000 visionären Köpfe weltweit treiben Innovation mit Ideenreichtum und Einsatzkraft voran - für eine intelligentere Welt von morgen.

Kundendetails

Für unseren renommierten Kunden aus der Luftfahrtbranche suchen wir Verstärkung für unser Team in Hamburg - zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt.

Stellenbeschreibung

  • Modellieren (Visualisieren) von Prozessen der verschiedenen Functional Areas innerhalb der Kunden Group im entsprechenden Prozessmodellierungstool unter Berücksichtigung der Process Excellence-Methodik
  • Unterstützen der kontinuierlichen Verbesserung bestehender Prozesse in Zusammenarbeit und Abstimmung mit dem jeweiligen Process Architect
  • Abstimmen von modellierten Prozessinhalten und zugehörigen Dokumenten mit dem verantwortlichen Process Owner beziehungsweise Process Architect, moderieren von Prozess Design Workshops
  • Initiieren von Prozessverbesserungen/-anpassungen des Process Excellence-Prozesses beziehungsweise des Process Excellence-IT-Tools
  • Zusammenarbeit und Kommunikation im Einklang mit dem Prozessmanagementrollen-Konzept (FAR+)
  • Multiplikator der Process Excellence-Methodik/Berater im Kontext des Process Excellence-Prozesses innerhalb der Kunden Group, beantworten von Nutzerfragen
  • Sonderaufgaben wahrnehmen, Projekte bearbeiten und begleiten, Fachbereiche beraten

Profil

  • Abgeschlossenes Hoch-/Fachhochschulstudium oder gleichwertige Fähigkeiten und Kenntnisse
  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse der erforderlichen Teile des integrierten Managementsystems bzw. Bereitschaft zur Einarbeitung in die Methoden und Konzepte
  • Methodenkenntnisse des Business Process Management (BPM) und entsprechender Systeme
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse (verhandlungssicher)
  • Sehr gute EDV-Kenntnisse (MS-Office), sehr gute Kenntnisse und Beherrschung der für die Prozessdokumentation erforderlichen Arbeitsmittel wie Modellierungstool, Dokumentenmanagementtool sowie Projektmanagement
  • Sehr gut ausgeprägtes konzeptionelles und analytisches Denkvermögen, Fähigkeiten sich schnell in neue Sachverhalte einarbeiten zu können sowie Initiative, Engagement und Kreativität
  • Sprachliche und didaktische Fähigkeiten, um komplexe betriebliche Prozesse/Dokumente zielgruppengerecht, klar und präzise darstellen zu können
  • Sicheres Auftreten, Überzeugungs- und Durchsetzungsvermögen, hohe Belastbarkeit
  • Ausgeprägte Planungs- Koordinations- und Dispositionsvermögen; Entscheidungsfähigkeit
  • Bereitschaft zu Einsätzen im In- und Ausland, zu berufsbezogener Weiterbildungen und ggf. zu einer psychologischen Eignungsuntersuchung

Das Angebot

  • Es besteht die Möglichkeit zum Teil im Homeoffice /remote zu arbeiten und somit von den Vorteilen hybrider Arbeit zu profitieren (flexibles Arbeiten)
  • Teamspirit und Diversität
  • Work-Life-Balance
  • Attraktive Vergütung
  • Sozialleistungen
  • Vielseitige Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten
  • Team- und Sportevents
  • Globales Netzwerk
  • Attraktives Mitarbeiterempfehlungsprogramm

Die Benefits können je nach Position und Standort variieren. Eine Übersicht über unsere Benefits befindet sich auf unserer Webseite unterhalb Karriere und Benefits.

title: Performance Analyst
company: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
description:
Bristol, Darlington, London, Manchester, Wolverhampton

Job Summary

We are MHCLG

Here at the Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government (MHCLG), we work on things that make a real difference to peopleâs lives. Whether it's through the homes we live in, the work of our local councils, or the communities weâre all part of, our work is at the top of the political agenda. We have ambitious and far-reaching outcomes to achieve, so thereâs never been a more exciting time to join us.

This Performance Analyst role will be part of our Elections Digital team at MHCLG. Itâs an exciting time to improve and expand our services to meet usersâ needs.

Our Services Include

  • Register to vote
  • Apply for a proxy vote
  • Apply for a postal vote
  • Apply for photo ID to vote (called a âVoter Authority Certificateâ)
  • And a product we have built for electoral teams in Local Authorities to support electoral administration.

We have lots to do. We must continue to improve our existing services to meet the needs of our users, especially those of disabled and other marginalised users. We must also think strategically about the future, understanding what problems still exist and champion user-centred ways of working to approach these issues.

You will be embedded in the Operations team, providing statistics and analysis for an existing portfolio of high-profile online services. Moreover, a substantial part of your responsibilities will be working with the Product and Design teams in Elections Digital, who are busy developing entirely new and exciting online services to improve voter registration as part of the Electoral Resilience & Democratic Engagement Programme.â¯â¯

This role will involve working closely with the development team on supporting user research and various analytics for services in various stages of development, and also performing analytics and continuous improvement to our existing services.â¯â¯

The role will also focus on supporting improvements to our digital compliance, making sure that we have proportionate data governance across the operational portfolio of live services that is aligned with MHCLG and data governance frameworks.

Find out more about our Digital teams and what they are working on through our MHCLG Digital blog. Please note that MHCLG do not offer visa sponsorship and applicants will need a valid visa for the duration of your employment.

â¯

Job Description

As a Performance Analyst, you'll:â¯â¯â¯

  • Be responsible for the performance analysis in your given area and help the product team measure and assess outcomes: for example, what good looks like and how it will be measured
  • Help to plan and ensure project delivery, finding opportunities to share information with the product team, community colleagues and others. Also to integrate the use of data analytics across the different phases of product of the product lifecycle
  • Be adaptable in applying analysis techniques to new contexts, especially when acting as the sole representative for performance analysts on a project
  • Be able to work on data governance artifacts with the wider team, including identifying documentary requirements, and then taking responsibility as a subject matter expert to deliver against these requirements
  • Be responsible for the accuracy and quality of data and analysis and how they are used, including putting robust quality assurance in place
  • Be engaged with, and actively contribute to, the performance analysis community

As a Performance Analyst, you'll:â¯â¯â¯

  • Be responsible for the performance analysis in your given area and help the product team measure and assess outcomes: for example, what good looks like and how it will be measured
  • Help to plan and ensure project delivery, finding opportunities to share information with the product team, community colleagues and others. Also to integrate the use of data analytics across the different phases of product of the product lifecycle
  • Be adaptable in applying analysis techniques to new contexts, especially when acting as the sole representative for performance analysts on a project
  • Be able to work on data governance artifacts with the wider team, including identifying documentary requirements, and then taking responsibility as a subject matter expert to deliver against these requirements
  • Be responsible for the accuracy and quality of data and analysis and how they are used, including putting robust quality assurance in place
  • Be engaged with, and actively contribute to, the performance analysis community

Person specification

As a Performance Analyst, You'll Have

  • Knowledge on how to use the most appropriate medium to visualise data to tell compelling and actionable stories relevant for business goals. You can present, communicate and disseminate analysis and recommendations effectively, appropriately and with high impact
  • Knowledge on how to enable the team to develop a performance framework including KPIs or a service or product. You can provide the intelligence to support the formulation of both short-term and long-term strategic plans. You understand the different phases of product delivery and can plan and run the analysis for these. You can contribute to decision-making throughout the product life cycle
  • Skills to develop subject matter expertise in an area, and then apply this to support the development of comprehensive and robust governance frameworks. You can challenge incumbent ways of thinking, and will take full ownership of driving forward solutions to ensure compliance and clear transparent oversight
  • Ability to understand and identify who users are and what their needs are, based on evidence. You put users first and can manage competing priorities. You understand the importance of analysis to user-centred design and know how to collaborate with professions that are focused on user-centred design (for example, user research, user experience, content, design, business analysis)
  • Knowledge about quality assurance techniques including those used by other analytical professions, and be comfortable deploying these as relevant. You can identify the right data sources and then validate and understand how to interpret the results. You know how to ensure that data is fit for purpose

title: Plant Head - Sulphuric Acid  & ETP
description:
SAP & ETP Operations Management

  • Lead end-to-end operations of Sulphuric Acid Plant and ETP.
  • Ensure achievement of production and treatment targets.
  • Maintain stable and efficient operation of:
    • Gas Cleaning Plant
    • Contact Section
    • Acid Absorption Section
    • Acid Storage & Dispatch Systems
    • ETP, Neutralization & Water Recovery Systems
    • ZLD Systems (where applicable)
  • Optimize plant throughput, conversion efficiency, and recovery rates.
  • Ensure maximum plant availability and reliability.

Environment Compliance

  • Ensure adherence to:
    • CPCB norms
    • GPCB norms
    • Environmental Clearance conditions
    • Consent to Operate requirements
  • Ensure compliance related to:
    • SO₂ emissions
    • Acid mist emissions
    • Stack monitoring
    • Water discharge standards
    • Hazardous waste handling
  • Drive zero environmental non-compliance.

Process Excellence & Optimization

  • Improve:
    • Sulphur burning efficiency
    • SO₂ to SO₃ conversion efficiency
    • Sulphuric acid quality
    • Utility consumption
    • Water recovery
    • Energy efficiency
  • Lead process debottlenecking initiatives.
  • Implement Lean, Six Sigma and continuous improvement projects.

Safety & Risk Management

  • Champion Process Safety Management (PSM).
  • Ensure compliance with:
    • SOPs
    • PTW System
    • LOTO
    • HIRA
    • MOC
    • Emergency Response Procedures
  • Conduct incident investigations and implement corrective actions.
  • Build a strong safety culture across operations teams.

Maintenance Coordination

  • Work closely with Maintenance teams for:
    • Preventive Maintenance
    • Predictive Maintenance
    • Shutdown Planning
    • Turnaround Management
  • Drive improvement in equipment reliability.
  • Reduce unplanned downtime.

Water & Effluent Management

  • Ensure efficient operation of:
    • ETP
    • Recycle Systems
    • Neutralization Systems
    • Sludge Handling Facilities
    • Water Recovery Systems
  • Monitor water balance and recycling performance.
  • Promote water conservation initiatives.

Team Leadership

  • Lead a multidisciplinary team of:
    • Shift In-Charges
    • Engineers
    • Operators
    • Technicians
    • Contract Workforce
  • Develop competency and succession plans.
  • Drive performance and employee engagement.

Budget & Cost Control

  • Prepare annual operating budget.
  • Monitor:
    • Chemical consumption
    • Utility consumption
    • Maintenance expenses
    • Spares inventory
  • Drive cost optimization without compromising safety or quality.

Projects & Expansion

  • Support:
    • Capacity enhancement projects
    • Debottlenecking initiatives
    • Environmental improvement projects
    • Technology upgrades
  • Participate in commissioning and performance guarantee tests.

Education Qualification:

B.E./B.Tech in Chemical Engineering

M.Tech Chemical / Environmental Engineering preferred

Experiences:

18–25 years of experience in process industries, focused can be Sulphuric Acid Plant Operations, Gas Cleaning Systems, Environmental Systems, ETP / Wastewater Treatment Operations

title: Médecin interne en médecine interne pour la chirurgie orthopédique – 100%
description:
En tant que médecin interne en orthopédie, avec activité dédiée à la médecine péri-opératoire, vous contribuez à la prise en charge globale des patientes et patients hospitalisés dans le service d’orthopédie. Dans ce contexte, vous intervenez dans les cinq unités de ce service dont vous retrouverez le détail grâce au lien suivant : Chirurgie orthopédique et traumatologie de l'appareil moteur. Ce poste s’adresse notamment à des médecins souhaitant développer leurs compétences en médecine interne dans un environnement chirurgical et interdisciplinaire. L’activité est organisée principalement du lundi au vendredi, selon les besoins et l’organisation du service.

Sous la supervision de médecins cadres, vous assurez le suivi médical quotidien des patientes et patients qui vous sont confiés. Votre activité comprend l’optimisation préopératoire, la prise en charge médicale durant l’hospitalisation et le suivi postopératoire, ainsi que la prévention, l’identification et le traitement des complications péri-opératoires.

Vous prenez en charge l’ensemble des problématiques rencontrées à l’étage, notamment l’antalgie, la gestion des traitements anticoagulants, les troubles hydro-électrolytiques, les anomalies biologiques courantes, les comorbidités aiguës ou chroniques et l’organisation du projet de sortie. Vous coordonnez ces prises en charge avec les équipes d’orthopédie, d’anesthésiologie, de soins infirmiers, de physiothérapie et les autres spécialistes impliqués.

Vous participez aux visites médicales, aux discussions interdisciplinaires, à la tenue du dossier médical et à l’amélioration continue de la qualité et de la sécurité des soins. Vous développez votre raisonnement clinique en étant confronté ou confrontée à des situations péri-opératoires variées, parfois complexes, tout en bénéficiant d’une supervision de proximité.

Au sein d’un environnement universitaire et pluridisciplinaire, vous participez également à l’encadrement des médecins stagiaires et avez accès aux formations théoriques et pratiques proposées par les services de médecine interne générale et d’orthopédie.

À ce titre, vous poursuivez votre formation postgraduée conformément aux exigences FMH. L’année effectuée est reconnue comme une année de formation postgraduée en orthopédie et peut être prise en compte dans le cursus menant au titre de spécialiste FMH en médecine interne générale.

Vous êtes titulaire du diplôme fédéral de médecin ou disposez d’une formation jugée équivalente par la Commission des professions médicales de l’Office fédéral de la santé publique (MEBEKO) et êtes au bénéfice d’une première expérience en tant que médecin interne en médecine interne. Vous faites preuve d’un intérêt marqué pour le domaine de la chirurgie orthopédique.

Vous avez une parfaite maîtrise de la langue française (niveau C1 requis) et une très bonne capacité d'expression écrite et orale.

Possédant d’excellentes compétences à communiquer en équipe pluridisciplinaire et avec la patientèle, vous êtes une personne dynamique, flexible et engagée. De plus, vous avez démontré de fortes capacités d’adaptation.

Vous pouvez travailler tant en horaires de journée, qu’en horaires de nuit et de week-end.

  • Entrée en fonction : 01.11.2026 ou à convenir
  • Nombre de postes : 2
  • Taux d’activité : 100%
  • Classe de fonction : 20
  • Contrat : CDD de 12 mois, puis renouvelable en CDI
  • Délai de candidature : 21.08.2026
  • Demande de renseignement : Dr. C. Larpin, Médecin chef de clinique, christophe.larpin@hug.ch

Votre dossier de candidature doit comporter une lettre démontrant votre motivation, votre curriculum vitae, les notes du Bachelor et du Master et si à disposition les notes du diplôme fédéral médical, les rapports de stage ainsi que les lettres de recommandation à disposition. Retrouvez ces informations grâce au lien suivant : https://www.hug.ch/chirurgie/formation-chirurgie.

Cette annonce s'adresse indistinctement aux femmes et aux hommes.

Souhaitant s’engager dans la lutte contre le chômage, les HUG encouragent les candidatures qui proviennent de l’Office cantonal de l’emploi.

Seules les candidatures soumises via la plateforme de recrutement sont prises en compte. Les candidatures papier et mail ne seront pas traitées.

title: Bars Manager
location: Sharm El-Sheikh, South Sinai Governorate, eg
description:

2.8 Consolidate the relationships between departmental employees.
2.9 Ensure that preparations are made before service for special occasions and control the preparations.

To provide minimum consumption currently used all stuffs and equipment (paper, printed out etc.).

title: (Senior) Communicatieadviseur
description:
Jij weet hoe je collega’s meeneemt, verbindt en in beweging krijgt. Je hebt vernieuwende ideeën om medewerkers te betrekken en te behouden, en je voelt feilloos aan wat nodig is om organisatiebrede projecten en veranderopgaven goed te laten landen.

Ter uitbreiding van ons team Marketing & Communicatie zijn wij op zoek naar een (senior) communicatieadviseur. Word jij enthousiast van een rol waarin interne communicatie, medewerkerbeleving en verandercommunicatie samenkomen? Dan staat de koffie of thee klaar voor een kennismaking!

Jouw rol als communicatieadviseur bij Prisma

Als (senior) communicatieadviseur werk je binnen de afdeling Mens & Organisatie in het team Marketing, Communicatie & Recruitment. Samen met een grafisch vormgever, contentontwikkelaar, online marketeer, (strategisch) communicatieadviseurs en onze recruiters werk je aan heldere, betekenisvolle communicatie voor én met onze collega’s.

Je werkt intensief samen met collega’s van afdeling Mens & Organisatie (o.a. duurzame inzetbaarheid en medewerker behoud), projectleiders van de beleidsagenda, teamleiders en het management. Jij wordt de communicatiesparringpartner bij uiteenlopende organisatie- en verandertrajecten. Daarbij ben je één van de gezichten van communicatie binnen Prisma en draag je bij aan een sterke, warme en goed geïnformeerde organisatiecultuur. Met jouw enthousiasme, organisatiesensitiviteit en verbindende persoonlijkheid weet je medewerkers te bereiken én te raken.

Je schakelt moeiteloos tussen strategisch adviseren en praktisch uitvoeren. Je begeleidt projectleiders en managers bij verandertrajecten, ontwikkelt interne campagnes, schrijft krachtige teksten en organiseert bijeenkomsten die echt effect hebben. Je werkt niet alleen aan informatievoorziening, maar vooral aan dialoog, betrokkenheid en behoud van medewerkers. Je ziet kansen, neemt initiatief en brengt structuur, ook wanneer er veel tegelijk speelt.

Wie ben jij?

Met passie en plezier vertaal jij de visie van Prisma naar betekenisvolle communicatie. Je denkt vanuit mogelijkheden en hecht waarde aan echt contact. Je staat stevig in jouw schoenen, kent jouw talenten en valkuilen en werkt graag samen. Je durft door te vragen, denkt vooruit en bent niet bang om feedback te geven of te ontvangen.

Daarnaast:

  • Heb je een afgeronde hbo-opleiding, bij voorkeur in de richting van communicatie.

  • Heb je aantoonbare ervaring met interne communicatie en/of verandercommunicatie.

  • Heb je ervaring met projectmatig werken en weet je hoe je communicatie inzet binnen verandertrajecten.

  • Kun je communicatieplannen schrijven en vertalen naar duidelijke boodschappen en passende middelen.

  • Ben je creatief, organisatiesensitief, flexibel en initiatiefrijk.

  • Adviseer je overtuigend en communiceert helder, zowel mondeling als schriftelijk.

  • Ben je een teamspeler en een echte verbinder.

Wat bieden wij?

Onze medewerkers zijn het hart van onze organisatie. En daar zijn wij enorm trots op!

Daarom bieden wij:

  • Contract: jaarcontract van 24 - 28 uur met intentie tot verlenging voor onbepaalde tijd.

  • Salaris: €3.551 - €5.200 bruto per maand op fulltime basis [FWG 55 CAO gehandicaptenzorg].

  • Extra’s: 8,33% eindejaarsuitkering, balansverlof-uren, pensioenregeling en scholingsmogelijkheden, fisc free (fiscaal voordeel op uitjes, telefoon/tablet,fiets of sporten)

  • Kom je in dienst bij Prisma en ken jij iemand die goed bij ons past? Draag een nieuwe collega aan en ontvang een aanbrengbonus van €500,- na het succesvol doorlopen van de proeftijd.

Enthousiast geworden?

Zie jij jezelf al werken bij Prisma als (senior) communicatieadviseur? Wacht dan niet langer en solliciteer op een manier die bij jou past. De eerste gesprekken vinden plaats op 24 augustus in de middag en 25 augustus in de ochtend.

Heb je vragen? Neem gerust contact op met Nicolette De Wit, Teamleider Marketing, Communicatie & Recruitment via 06 - 51726344.

Over Prisma

Bij Prisma bouwen we samen aan een samenleving waarin iedereen ertoe doet en meedoet. Wij ondersteunen mensen met een verstandelijke beperking (en/of andere zorgvragen) om het beste uit zichzelf en hun leven te halen. Of het nu gaat om wonen, werken, dagbesteding of vrije tijd: de eigen regie en de dromen van onze cliënten staan altijd centraal. Als medewerker bij Prisma krijg je de ruimte om écht het verschil te maken, jezelf te ontwikkelen en samen met betrokken collega's te bouwen aan betekenisvolle zorg.

Acquisitie naar aanleiding van deze vacature wordt niet op prijs gesteld.

title: Restaurant Manager – Italian Restaurant - Italian National
description:
• Oversee the daily operation of the Italian restaurant, ensuring smooth and efficient service.
  • Maintain Fairmont luxury service standards at all times.
  • Conduct daily briefings with the restaurant team.
  • Monitor restaurant appearance, cleanliness, and ambiance.
  • Ensure all opening and closing procedures are completed accurately.
  • Coordinate with the kitchen team to ensure timely and high-quality food service.
  • Supervise reservations, seating arrangements, and guest flow.
  • Deliver exceptional and personalized guest service.
  • Welcome and engage with guests during service.
  • Handle guest complaints promptly and professionally.
  • Build strong guest relationships and encourage repeat business.
  • Ensure all guest feedback is followed up and appropriate corrective actions are implemented.
  • Promote Fairmont's "Turning Moments into Special Memories" philosophy.
  • Recruit, train, coach, and develop restaurant colleagues.
  • Conduct performance evaluations and provide continuous feedback.
  • Prepare staff schedules based on business demand.
  • Ensure compliance with grooming, hygiene, and uniform standards.
  • Foster teamwork, employee engagement, and a positive working environment.
  • Conduct daily training sessions to improve product knowledge and service standards.
  • Achieve departmental revenue and profitability targets.
  • Monitor daily sales performance.
  • Control labor costs through effective scheduling.
  • Manage operating expenses and maximize productivity.
  • Review financial reports and identify improvement opportunities.
  • Maintain high standards of food presentation and beverage service.
  • Collaborate with the Chef de Cuisine on menu planning and seasonal promotions.
  • Ensure authentic Italian dining experiences are consistently delivered.
  • Monitor portion control and service consistency.
  • Support wine and beverage promotions to increase revenue.
  • Monitor restaurant inventory and operating supplies.
  • Control breakage, spoilage, and waste.
  • Ensure proper stock ordering and storage procedures.
  • Conduct regular inventory counts.
  • Maintain cost-effective operations while preserving service quality.
  • Ensure compliance with HACCP, food safety, hygiene, and sanitation standards.
  • Maintain a clean and safe working environment.
  • Ensure compliance with local municipality regulations and hotel policies.
  • Conduct regular health and safety inspections.
  • Report maintenance issues promptly.

  • Diploma or Bachelor's Degree in Hospitality Management, Hotel Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
  • Minimum 3–5 years of experience as a Restaurant Manager in a luxury hotel or fine-dining Italian restaurant.
  • Strong leadership and people management skills.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
  • Sound knowledge of Italian cuisine, wines, and restaurant operations.
  • Strong financial and analytical skills.
  • Knowledge of HACCP and food safety regulations.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office and restaurant POS systems.
  • Fluent in English; Italian language skills are an advantage.

title: Business Improvement Manager
description:
The Business Improvement Manager will be responsible for supporting the business core teams in initiating and implementing improvement projects and initiatives across all elements of warehouse and transport operations, as well as supporting functions.

Working hours: Monday to Friday, 08.00-17.00

We are flexible for this role to be based at any of our sites across the country: Kent (Teynham), Evesham, Washington, Hilsea, Newton Abbot or Broxburn.

Key Duties of a Business Improvement Manager:

  • Co-ordinate and work with site senior teams to identify improvement opportunities, which have a positive impact on operational and business performance across quality, cost, service and safety elements.
  • Identify, agree and deliver improvement initiative objectives and timescales with site & regional leadership teams (Including as appropriate, local C.I. Champions).
  • Produce clear operational improvement proposals, change plans and communicate to the relevant stakeholders as required, ensuring consideration for the cost benefit and complexity of delivering the change.
  • Liaise with key stakeholders and subject matter experts to ensure the creation of fully considered and objective proposals and manage effective change processes where necessary.
  • Ensuring plans and teams maintain structure, focus and deliver on the identified improvement projects for the region.
  • Ensure savings generated through improvement initiatives are quantified and validated by Finance.
  • Complete in depth and complex analysis to identify current performance and/or warehouse/transport trends used to support any improvements.
  • Support the operational improvement implementation process; ensuring relevant operational teams have support and coaching throughout the change cycle.
  • Utilise business central support teams including IT expertise to develop potential new ways of working as part of a depot / business improvement initiative.
  • Complete analytical activity to measure potential and delivered benefits of any operational improvement proposal at either site, regional or business level ensuring capture of actual vs expected value.
  • Provide regular communication updates on the progress of improvement initiatives at either depot, regional or business level.
  • Act as a business control point to identify best practice and facilitate information sharing across the business, ensuring “lessons learned” are identified and shared.
  • Ongoing collaboration - sharing best practice and generating ideas for future projects across the FW business, as well as wider Chill Division peers
  • Support and evolve tools and techniques to improve the business continuous improvement agenda.
  • Logistics/Industry experience is highly desirable
  • Ability to apply Project Management and LEAN Methodologies to support the delivery of any given improvement initiative to completion.
  • High level of communication and influencing skills required to ensure business stakeholders deliver the required savings and productivity initiatives.
  • A proven track record managing project change within warehouse/transport operations.
  • Ability to stay focused and proactively prioritise initiatives.
  • Flexibility within the role to be able to contribute to a variety of areas within the operation.
  • Able to demonstrate a high level of attention to detail.
  • A strong operational warehousing/transport background.
  • Able to work to tight deadlines.
  • High level of expertise in MS Word, Excel, Power Point and Outlook.
  • Literate in warehouse/transport management systems
  • A proactive self-starter with the ability to be work remotely.
  • Should demonstrate the drive and desire to progress.
  • Excellent time management and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong interpersonal, communication skills and the capability to interact at local to Senior levels.

As part of our drive to make Fowler Welch a great place to work. We are proud to be an inclusive and diverse organisation where we are committed to employee development and recognising success for hard working performers.

Our dedicated learning and development programmes are open to every employee to give you the opportunity to shape your own future within logistics and continue to work in an environment where team culture thrives.

Our People are the driving force behind our success, which is why we offer a wide range of benefits which include:

  • Annual Leave – Competitive holiday entitlement of 25 days plus the normal bank bank holidays.
  • Company Bonus – We do our best work to succeed together. When we achieve our goals you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.
  • Private Medical Cover – This gives you peace of mind, you have choice, flexibility and speed of access to the most clinically appropriate care via Bupa.
  • Pension scheme – we want colleagues to enjoy a comfortable retirements so we offer a great contribution of 4.5% employee and 8% employer.
  • Life Assurance - x3 your annual salary.
  • Wellness – Via our Employee Assistance Programme we offer immediate access to a confidential telephone counselling and legal information service that operates 24 hours a days, 365 days a year.
  • Eye Care Vouchers – We can provide you with substantial savings with free eye tests and discounts on prescription glasses.
  • Reward & Recognition – We recognise that employees have gone the extra mile via Employee of the month and year, special recognition and long service awards.
  • Everyday discounts - via our benefit platform you will have access to over 50 retailer discounts for everyday savings!

If you meet the requirements for the above role and are looking for your next career opportunity please apply now and become a part of our #WinningTeam!

title: Logistics Co-Founder / CFO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

  • You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management

  • You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: R&D Specialist | Oil & Gas | Water Treatment {hkhdv}
company: THE SUPREME HR ADVISORY PTE. LTD.
description:
R&D Specialist

Working hours: Monday to Friday (8am to 5pm)

· Ph.D in Chemistry or Chemical Engineering with more than 5 years of experiences in the area of specialty chemical manufacturing/applications, oil & gas or water treatment industry.

title: Engineer I Mechanical Engineering IX
description:
Mechanical Design Engineer – Sustaining Engineering
Location: Pilsen, CZ


Position Summary
As a Mechanical Design Engineer – Sustaining at Vertiv, you will be responsible for supporting, maintaining, and continuously improving existing products in production. This role requires strong technical ownership, practical problem‑solving, and the ability to work independently with limited supervision while collaborating with manufacturing, quality, supply chain, and customer‑facing teams.

You will focus on resolving design, manufacturing, quality, and field issues while driving cost, quality, and reliability improvements across released products.
Responsibilities
• Support released products through ongoing sustaining engineering activities, including issue resolution, design updates, and product improvements.
• Investigate and resolve manufacturing, quality, and field issues using structured root‑cause analysis techniques.
• Develop and release updated 3D models, drawings, and documentation using PTC Creo.
• Create, review, and execute engineering change orders (ECOs) to address product corrections, cost reductions, and manufacturability improvements.
• Coordinate with manufacturing engineering, suppliers, quality, and operations to implement changes effectively.
• Support cost‑reduction initiatives, part standardization, and component substitutions.
• Maintain BOM accuracy, drawings, and product data within ERP and PLM systems.
• Apply lessons learned from issues and field feedback to prevent recurrence and improve product robustness.
• Balance responsiveness to production and customer needs with disciplined engineering change control.

Qualifications

Minimum Requirements:
• Associate degree in mechanical engineering or equivalent work experience.
• 3+ years of mechanical engineering experience, preferably in sustaining, product development, manufacturing engineering, or production support roles.
• Proficiency in 3D CAD Creo preferred.
• Ability to work independently with limited supervision in a production environment.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills.
• Experience designing and supporting sheet metal enclosures and assemblies.
• Working knowledge of laser cutting, punch press, press brake, welding, and powder coating processes.
• Experience supporting high‑volume or configurable products in production.
• Experience with ERP systems such as Oracle PD Cloud or Microsoft Dynamics.
• Experience with PLM tools such as Autodesk Vault, Windchill, or Agile.
• Understanding of applicable regulatory, compliance, and industry standards.

What Will Make You Successful?
• Strong ownership and accountability for existing products.
• Practical, hands‑on problem‑solving mindset.
• Ability to prioritize and respond quickly to production and customer issues.
• Collaborative approach across manufacturing, quality, and supply chain teams.
• Continuous improvement mindset focused on reliability and cost.
Vertiv Core Principles & Behaviors

Our Core Principles: Safety. Integrity. Respect. Teamwork. Diversity & Inclusion.
Our Strategic Priorities: Customer Focus. Operational Excellence. High‑Performance Culture. Innovation. Financial Strength.
Our Behaviors: Own It. Act With Urgency. Foster a Customer‑First Mindset. Think Big and Execute. Lead by Example. Drive Continuous Improvement. Learn and Seek Out Development.


#LI-MŠ1

title: Kaufmännischer Mitarbeiter (m/w/d) Faktura / Qualitätsmanagement
description:
• Du erledigst die Abrechnung der Kurierunternehmer und Erstellung der entsprechenden Gutschriften
  • Die Prüfung und Zahlungsfreigabe von Eingangsrechnungen nationaler und internationaler GO! Partnerfirmen sowie sonstiger Leistungserbringer (z.B. Spezial-Transportaufträge) liegt in deinem Verantwortungsbereich
  • In deiner Funktion bist du mit der Erstellung von Ausgangsrechnungen und Gutschriften vertraut
  • Des weiteren bist du für die Bearbeitung des Mahnwesen verantwortlich
  • Die Reklamationsbearbeitung obliegt dir
  • Die Bearbeitung von Eingangsmails und Bewertung zur etwaigen Weiterberechnung von Extrakosten runden deine Aufgabe ab. Dazu gehört ebenfalls die Berücksichtigung von Besonderheiten bei der Kundenabrechnung
  • Unterstützung bei der Vorarbeit für die interne und externe Auditierung
  • Einhaltung und Überwachung der Qualitätsrichtlinien
  • Kaufmännische Berufserfahrung oder eine Tätigkeit in der Logistik können von Vorteil sein, sind aber kein Must-have
  • deine Arbeitsweise ist sowohl organisiert als auch professionell und du behältst auch in schwierigen Situationen den Überblick
  • Fließende Deutschkenntnisse sind Grundvoraussetzung
  • Ein sympathisches und dynamisches Auftreten ist uns genauso wichtig, wie eine kundenorientierte Denk- und- Arbeitsweise
  • du beherrschst die gängigen MS-Office Anwendungen
  • erste Erfahrung und Kenntnis im Bereich Qualitätsmanagement von Vorteil
  • unbefristeter Arbeitsvertrag. Die Schichten werden rechtzeitig bekanntgegeben.
  • Sicherheit: Eine zukunftssichere Branche, die von Wachstum geprägt ist. Eine unbefristete Anstellung in unserem Unternehmen mit über 40-jähriger Tradition
  • Herausfordernde Aufgabe: Bei uns hast du eine herausfordernde Aufgabe in einem wachstumsorientierten Umfeld mit relevanter Verantwortung für Themen und Projekte
  • Attraktives Arbeitsumfeld: Dich erwartet ein attraktives Arbeitsumfeld nah am Management mit Raum für eigene Ideen, flachen Hierarchien und kurzen Entscheidungswegen
  • Tolles Team: Du bist nicht alleine und wirst von einem erfahrenen Team eingearbeitet und stets begleitet, denn wir sind Alles außer_gewöhnlich, bei uns wird TEAMWORK gelebt
  • Weiterbildung: Starte mit uns durch. Wir fördern deine persönliche und berufliche Weiterentwicklung
  • Vergütung: Ein attraktives Gehaltspaket
  • Selbstverständlichkeiten: wöchentlich frisches Obst, Kaffee und Wasser
  • Mitarbeiter-Benefits: zum Beispiel Arbeitgeberzuschuss zum Bike oder E-Bike-Leasing, Firmeneigener Parkplatz

title: Senior Produktmanager
description:
• Du übernimmst die End-to-End-Verantwortung für unsere Wealth Advisory Platform – von der Produktidee über Discovery und Delivery bis hin zur erfolgreichen Einführung und kontinuierlichen Weiterentwicklung.
  • Gemeinsam mit Kunden, Wealth Managern, internen Fachexperten und Stakeholdern entwickelst du ein tiefes Kundenverständnis und übersetzt Erkenntnisse in eine überzeugende Produktvision sowie nachhaltige Lösungen.
  • Mit einem klaren Blick auf Kundennutzen und Unternehmensziele entwickelst, priorisierst und verantwortest du die Produkt-Roadmap und bringst Initiativen strukturiert durch den gesamten Produktentwicklungsprozess.
  • Präzise Anforderungen, User Stories und Akzeptanzkriterien schaffen für Engineering und Design die Grundlage, um gemeinsam hochwertige Lösungen zu entwickeln.
  • Als Sparringspartner für Engineering und Design triffst du fundierte Produktentscheidungen, löst Zielkonflikte pragmatisch und sorgst dafür, dass Qualität und Time-to-Market gleichermaßen im Fokus bleiben.
  • Regulatorische Compliance ist für dich fester Bestandteil moderner Produktentwicklung – Anforderungen wie GDPR, DORA oder der EU AI Act denkst du von Beginn an mit.
  • Mithilfe relevanter Produktdaten definierst du Erfolgskennzahlen, analysierst Nutzerverhalten und leitest daraus Maßnahmen für die kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung unserer Plattform ab.
  • In enger Zusammenarbeit mit internationalen und cross-funktionalen Teams innerhalb der Harvest-Gruppe sorgst du für eine nahtlose Zusammenarbeit über Unternehmensgrenzen hinweg.
  • Ob Kunden, Geschäftsführung oder interne Stakeholder – deine Kommunikation schafft Transparenz, Vertrauen und Orientierung auf allen Ebenen.
  • Dein oberstes Ziel ist die erfolgreiche Weiterentwicklung der Wealth Advisory Platform bei gleichzeitig reibungsloser Kundenlieferung.
  • Mehr als fünf Jahre Erfahrung im Produktmanagement von B2B-SaaS-Produkten – idealerweise im WealthTech-, FinTech- oder einem anderen regulierten Umfeld.
  • Einen nachweisbaren Track Record bei der Entwicklung und erfolgreichen Einführung digitaler Produkte über den gesamten Produktlebenszyklus hinweg.
  • Leadership-Erfahrung ist für diese Position zwingend erforderlich – du verstehst es, Teams zu führen, Menschen zu begeistern und Entscheidungen voranzutreiben.
  • Du fühlst dich in internationalen sowie cross-funktionalen und cross-kulturellen Arbeitsumfeldern zuhause und bringst unterschiedliche Perspektiven erfolgreich zusammen.
  • Agile Produktentwicklung, Product Discovery, Priorisierung und das Formulieren klarer Anforderungen gehören zu deinem Handwerkszeug.
  • Ausgeprägte Analysefähigkeiten sowie ein datengetriebener und kundenorientierter Arbeitsstil zeichnen dich aus.
  • Auch in komplexen oder dynamischen Situationen bewahrst du den Überblick und entwickelst pragmatische Lösungen.
  • Mit deiner Kommunikationsstärke überzeugst du Entwicklerteams ebenso wie Executive Management und Kunden.
Wir sind ein etabliertes Produktunternehmen mit dem Spirit eines Startups. Dich erwarten Teamgeist, schnelle Entscheidungen und echte Freiheit, dein Arbeitsumfeld zu gestalten. Flexible Arbeitszeiten, Weiterbildungsbudgets und attraktive Benefits geben dir Raum für persönliche Entwicklung, Karrieremöglichkeiten und ein erfülltes Leben. Verhandlungssichere Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse
Zusätzliche Sprachkenntnisse wie Italienisch, Französisch oder Schweizerdeutsch nice-to-have Julian Eder

title: Marketing Manager
description:
Cloudfresh ⛅️ is a Global Google Cloud Premier Partner, Zendesk Premier Partner, Asana Solutions Partner, GitLab Select Partner, Hubspot Platinum Partner, Miro Solutions Partner, Okta & Microsoft Partner.

Since 2017, we’ve been implementing, migrating, integrating, administering, and auditing cloud solutions everybody knows—all the way from PoC and training to ongoing support and GenAI guidance, always with the customer’s IT setup in mind.

Businesses get to capture the value of compelling products that span cloud computing, location and mapping, borderless collaboration, peerless customer service, and DevSecOps.

We’re currently looking for a Marketing Manager to join our team. In this role, you’ll help shape and manage marketing efforts across various channels—supporting key business goals, generating leads, strengthening our brand, and building meaningful connections. Your work will play an important role in how we communicate, grow, and deliver value. We’re excited to meet someone who’s ready to make a real impact 💚

Requirements

Minimum of 3 years of experience in a marketing manager role, ideally within the B2B space.
2+ years of experience in the SaaS industry.
Proven ability to plan and execute campaigns across various channels, and/or develop content and shape content strategies.
Solid understanding of lead generation, brand awareness, content creation, event execution, and other key B2B marketing activities.
Experience working with CRM systems such as HubSpot or similar platforms.
Familiarity with advertising tools including Google Ads, Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and a basic grasp of Google Analytics.
Upper-intermediate to advanced English proficiency, both written and spoken.
A proactive mindset with a collaborative, solution-oriented approach.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams.
Responsibilities

Collaborate with the team to plan and execute multi-channel marketing campaigns that drive pipeline growth and strengthen brand awareness.
Develop and launch initiatives that promote the company’s products and services across key channels, including social media, email, digital media, paid search, SEO, and others.
Monitor campaign performance, test new approaches, and continuously optimize to support business goals.
Identify and explore new platforms and touchpoints to engage target audiences across the entire funnel—from awareness to conversion.
Build and nurture strong relationships with partners and clients through joint marketing activities such as special projects, events, success stories, co-branded content, and other.
Would be a plus

Proven writing skills with experience in the technology and software sectors, specifically within a B2B context.
Work conditions

Advance with Top Cloud Brands: Build your cloud marketing expertise with Google Cloud, Asana, GitLab, Zendesk, HubSpot, Miro, Okta, and Microsoft.
Expand Your Reach: Lead meaningful campaigns across EMEA, making an impact in diverse and dynamic markets.
Make a Real Difference: Drive smart, results-focused strategies that truly resonate with our clients.
Grow with a Supportive Team: Join a team that celebrates your wins and encourages your growth every step of the way.
Experiment & Innovate Freely: Bring your ideas to life in a culture that values creativity and is open to new approaches.
Earn What You Deserve: Enjoy a competitive salary and a transparent bonus system tied to your results.
Flexible Remote Schedule: Work flexibly remote or join us for a coffee in Kyiv or Prague offices.
Comprehensive Leave: 24 vacation days, bank holidays, and unlimited sick leave.
Continuous Learning: Access sponsored educational opportunities such as courses, conferences, meet-ups, company-sponsored English learning groups to keep your skills sharp and relevant.

When applying to this position, you consent to the processing of your personal data by CLOUDFRESH for the purposes necessary to conduct the recruitment process, in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council of April 27, 2016 (GDPR).
Additionally, you agree that CLOUDFRESH may process your personal data for future recruitment processes. You can withdraw this consent at any time.
