title: Sr DFT -  Verification
description:
WHAT YOU DO AT AMD CHANGES EVERYTHING

At AMD, our mission is to build great products that accelerate next-generation computing experiences—from AI and data centers, to PCs, gaming and embedded systems. Grounded in a culture of innovation and collaboration, we believe real progress comes from bold ideas, human ingenuity and a shared passion to create something extraordinary. When you join AMD, you’ll discover the real differentiator is our culture. We push the limits of innovation to solve the world’s most important challenges—striving for execution excellence, while being direct, humble, collaborative, and inclusive of diverse perspectives. Join us as we shape the future of AI and beyond. Together, we advance your career.




SENIOR SILICON DESIGN ENGINEER

THE ROLE:

The focus of this role is to plan, build, and execute DFT verification of new and existing features for AMD’s graphics processor IP, resulting in no bugs in the final design for AMD's next generation Zen-architecture based CPU cores

THE PERSON:

You have a passion for modern, complex processor architecture, digital design, and verification in general. You are a team player who has excellent communication skills and experience collaborating with other engineers located in different sites/timezones. You have strong analytical and problem-solving skills and are willing to learn and ready to take on problems.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Develop and execute pre-silicon verification testplans for DFT features of the next generation Zen-architecture based CPU Cores
  • Develop directed and random verification tests to fully validate DFT functionality
  • Verify DFT design blocks and subsystems (such as JTAG/1500/1687, MBIST, Fuse, Clocks, Resets, etc.) using complex SV or C++ verification environments. Construct System Verilog and/or C/C++ models and test sequence libraries for simulation.
  • Debug regression test failures to expose specification and implementation issues. Identify and address areas of concern to meet design quality objectives.
  • Collaborate with architects and hardware engineers, and firmware engineers to understand the new features to be verified
  • Estimate the time required to write the new feature tests and any required changes to the test environment
  • Debug test failures to determine the root cause; work with RTL and firmware engineers to resolve design defects and correct any test issues
  • Review functional and code coverage metrics – modify or add tests or constrain random tests to meet the coverage requirements
  • Post silicon ATE and System level debug support of the test patterns delivered

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:

  • Proficient in IP/SoC level ASIC verification
  • Proficient in debugging firmware and RTL code using simulation tools
  • Proficient in using UVM testbenches and working in Linux and Windows environments
  • Experienced with Verilog, System Verilog, C, and C++
  • Strong background in the C++ language, preferably on Linux with exposure to Windows platform
  • Good understanding and hands-on experience in the UVM concepts and SystemVerilog language
  • Developing UVM based verification frameworks and testbenches, processes and flows
  • CPU architecture knowledge is desirable
  • Automating workflows in a distributed compute environment.
  • Scripting language experience: Perl, Ruby, Makefile, shell preferred.
  • DFT knowledge on Memory BIST, Logic BIST, Scan, ATPG is highly desirable

ACADEMIC CREDENTIALS:

    • Bachelors or Masters degree in Electronics & Communication Engineering/Computer Engineering/Electrical Engineering

#LI-RR1

#LI-Hybrid




Benefits offered are described: AMD benefits at a glance.

AMD does not accept unsolicited resumes from headhunters, recruitment agencies, or fee-based recruitment services. AMD and its subsidiaries are equal opportunity, inclusive employers and will consider all applicants without regard to age, ancestry, color, marital status, medical condition, mental or physical disability, national origin, race, religion, political and/or third-party affiliation, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates and will accommodate applicants’ needs under the respective laws throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

AMD may use Artificial Intelligence to help screen, assess or select applicants for this position. AMD’s “Responsible AI Policy” is available here.

title: Team Member – Process Engineering (Instrumentation)
description:
• You will be responsible for recognizing potential hazards during critical maintenance and coordinating safety requirements through work permits, LOTO procedures, and JSA reviews to ensure 100% compliance.
  • You will be responsible for handling the maintenance, calibration, and installation of instruments like, Temperature, pressure, flow, level, humidity sensors, Load cells, speed sensors, encoders, Proximity, photoelectric, and IR sensors, IPC, HMI, PLC and control panels, ensuring accuracy and reliability in plant operations.
  • Identify and rectify instrument‑related issues such as signal fluctuation, false alarms, interlock failures, and sensor misalignment and Ensure all instruments are mechanically healthy, properly mounted, protected, and operational.
  • You will be responsible for planning and executing preventive, predictive, and shutdown maintenance activities, maintaining good housekeeping, and managing critical spare availability efficiently.
  • You will be responsible for supporting automation systems to minimize downtime, implementing control system backups, and identifying opportunities for new areas of automation.
  • You will be responsible for managing Preventive Maintenance activities as per schedule, maintaining accurate records, identifying discrepancies, and ensuring compliance with ISO, cGMP, and statutory requirements.
  • You will be responsible for troubleshooting instrumentation issues, coordinating with engineering teams, and issuing new instruments to user departments for seamless operations.
  • You Will be responsible to handle and Idea About Process Equipment like, RMG, FBD, Blenders, Octagonal mixers, Tablet compression machines, Capsule filling machines, Coating machines, Packing equipment and Support production during routine operation, changeovers, and breakdown conditions to avoid process interruptions.
  • Responsible for planning, executing, and supporting PLC and SCADA upgradation activities for manufacturing equipment.
  • Responsible for participating in TPM activities by identifying, reporting, and supporting closure of instrumentation‑related abnormalities and Responsible for performing root cause analysis (RCA) for instrumentation failures and implementing corrective actions.
  • You will be responsible for initiating purchase requisitions, inspecting received spares, and overseeing contractor work related to fabrication and insulation, while executing annual maintenance plans.
  • You will be responsible for identifying process abnormalities, implementing lean tools such as 5S, Kaizen, and Autonomous Maintenance, and coordinating small project modifications with plant leads.

Diploma / B.E. / B.Tech in Electrical, Electronics, or Instrumentation Engineering from a recognized institution.

Exp : 3 to 8 Years


Benefits Offered
At Dr. Reddy’s we actively help to catalyse your career growth and professional development through personalised learning programs.
The benefits you will enjoy at Dr. Reddy’s are on par with the best industry standards. They include, among other things and other essential equipment, joining & relocation support, family support (Maternity & Paternity benefits), learning and development opportunities, medical coverage for yourself and your family, life coverage for yourself.

Our Work Culture
Ask any employee at Dr. Reddy’s why they come to work every day and they’ll say, because Good Health Can’t Wait. This is our credo as well as the guiding principle behind all our actions. We see healthcare solutions not only as scientific formulations, but as a means to help patients lead healthier lives, and we’re always attuned to the new and the next to empower people to stay fit. And to do this, we foster a culture of empathy and dynamism. People are at the core of our journey over the last few decades. They have been supported by an enabling environment that buoys individual ability while fostering teamwork and shared success. We believe that when people with diverse skills are bound together by a common purpose and value system, they can make magic.
For more details, please visit our career website at https://careers.drreddys.com/#!/

title: CHIEF COOK
description:
Responsible for food preparation and cooking to standard operating procedures.

Ensure smooth operations and allocation of duties within the kitchen.

Ensure that all orders are fulfilled in a timely manner and to expected standards.

Attend to kitchen-related duties stock- checking and inventory and ordering.

Ensure good hygiene and housekeeping standards within the kitchen.

Ensure food is stored, prepare and presented in a safe and hygiene manner.

Maintain strong relationships with key clients and wholesale buyers.

Willingness to work night shift and split shift.

Willingness to work extra hours or weekend if needed.

title: Plant Head - Strategic Precious Metals and Minor Metals
description:
Plant Operations Management

  • Lead daily plant operations to achieve production volume, throughput, recovery, metal yield, plant availability, and dispatch targets as per business plan.
  • Optimize process parameters across smelting, refining, leaching, electrowinning/electrolysis, crystallization, casting, and allied processes based on feed composition and plant conditions.
  • Monitor shift-wise performance, identify bottlenecks, minimize unplanned downtime, and ensure timely troubleshooting of process deviations.
  • Ensure effective coordination between production, maintenance, quality, stores, utilities, logistics, and commercial teams for smooth plant operations.
  • Review and strengthen SOPs, operating discipline, shift handover practices, daily management systems, and production reporting mechanisms.
  • Drive continuous improvement in OEE, equipment reliability, manpower productivity, material flow, and process stability.

Safety, Health and Environment

  • Drive a zero-harm and LTI-free culture across the plant by implementing robust safety systems, behavior-based safety practices, and regular shopfloor engagement.
  • Ensure compliance with applicable statutory requirements including Factories Act, environmental regulations, hazardous waste handling rules, safety standards, and internal EHS guidelines.
  • Oversee management of effluent, emissions, fumes, dust, slag, hazardous material storage, and waste disposal in coordination with EHS and environment teams.
  • Lead incident investigations, root cause analysis, corrective and preventive actions, emergency preparedness, mock drills, and safety audits.
  • Promote disciplined usage of PPE, permit-to-work systems, HIRA/JSA, SOP compliance, and contractor safety management.

Quality, Metallurgy & Process Excellence

  • Ensure achievement of product quality standards including metal purity, impurity control, physical quality, casting quality, and customer specification compliance.
  • Work closely with laboratory, R&D, and process engineering teams to improve yield, recovery, impurity removal, energy efficiency, and process robustness.
  • Review process data, assay results, mass balance, heat balance, and quality trends to identify improvement opportunities.
  • Reduce rework, rejection, customer complaints, off-spec production, and process variability through structured corrective actions.
  • Support new product development, process trials, technology improvements, and scale-up initiatives for minor metals and by-products.

High Value Project

  • Conceive, evaluate, and drive high-value projects related to recovery, processing, and value maximization of minor metals in the copper value chain, including lead (Pb), zinc (Zn), chlorine (Cl), rhodium (Rh), bismuth, selenium, tellurium, and platinum group metals (PGMs).

Cost, Budgeting & CAPEX

  • Own plant OPEX budgets and drive cost optimization across power, fuel, chemicals, consumables, refractory, electrodes, maintenance, manpower, and logistics.
  • Track cost per ton, conversion cost, specific energy consumption, consumable usage, productivity, and variance against budget.
  • Plan and execute plant shutdowns, debottlenecking projects, modification jobs, and CAPEX initiatives within approved cost, quality, safety, and timeline parameters.
  • Support technical evaluation of vendors, contractors, spares, equipment, consumables, and service contracts in coordination with procurement and commercial teams.
  • Identify value engineering opportunities and drive initiatives for capacity enhancement, yield improvement, waste reduction, and cost competitiveness.

People Leadership & Capability Building

  • Lead, guide, and develop teams across production, shift operations, process, quality, maintenance coordination, and support functions.
  • Build a high-performance work culture focused on safety, accountability, operational discipline, problem-solving, and continuous improvement.
  • Develop succession plans for critical roles and ensure capability building of engineers, supervisors, operators, and shift in-charges.
  • Drive training on SOPs, safety practices, process fundamentals, equipment operation, emergency response, and quality requirements.
  • Ensure effective manpower planning, shift rosters, contractor deployment, productivity tracking, and employee engagement across the plant

Systems, Compliance & Continuous Improvement

  • Implement and sustain systems such as TPM, 5S, Kaizen, Lean Manufacturing, Daily Management, ISO systems, and audit compliance.
  • Coordinate with regulatory bodies, auditors, internal stakeholders, and cross-functional teams for statutory, quality, safety, and environmental compliance.
  • Ensure accurate production reporting, SAP transactions, MIS dashboards, inventory control, and documentation for audit readiness.
  • Leverage DCS/SCADA, process data, digital dashboards, and analytics to improve decision-making and operational visibility.
  • Ensure reliability of all critical equipment through asset optimization and timely corrective actions.
  • Drive process optimization initiatives to improve plant efficiency, productivity, cost effectiveness, and operational stability.

Education Qualification:

  • B.E./B.Tech in Metallurgy, Chemical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related discipline. M.Tech/M.E. in Metallurgy, Chemical, Materials Science, or Process Engineering will be preferred.

Experiences:

  • 18 - 20 years of relevant experience in metals, non-ferrous metals, minor metals, smelting, refining, electrolysis/electrowinning, chemical processing, or continuous/process plant operations.
  • 10-15yrs of experience in handling plant at highest level

title: BUNKER CLERK
description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented Bunker Clerk to support bunker tanker operations in Singapore by managing accurate documentation, ensuring regulatory compliance, and coordinating with vessel crews, customers, and port authorities.

Responsibilities

  • Manage accurate documentation to support bunker tanker operations and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements
  • Coordinate with vessel crew and cargo officers to verify bunker quantities and oversee delivery processes
  • Liaise with customers and port authorities to serve as the primary contact for operational and documentation matters
  • Assist in maintaining compliance with Maritime and Port Authority (MPA) regulations by keeping accurate records and preparing reports
  • Work collaboratively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment to support smooth bunkering activities

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Prior experience in maritime operations or clerical support for bunker tanker activities
  • Familiarity with Maritime and Port Authority (MPA) regulations and bunkering documentation
  • Strong organizational skills applied to managing documentation and operational tasks
  • Attention to detail in verifying quantities and maintaining accurate records

title: Recepcionista - Esplugues
description:
La Vostra Llar és un grup de residències amb més de 30 anys d’experiència en el sector. Gestionem actualment 12 centres i la nostra voluntat és continuar creixent amb el mateix entusiasme, mantenint la nostra filosofia i valors.


Actualment, estem buscant un/a recepcionista pel nostre centre d'Esplugues (Barcelona).


Oferim:

  • Contracte temporal (suplència del 10/08 al 26/08)

  • Horari: de dilluns a divendres de 10hrs a 19hrs.

  • Sou: 17.094€ bruts/any (proporcional a la jornada).


Funcions:

  • Atenció telefònica i presencial a les visites.

  • Control i gestió de la documentació.

  • Arxiu.

  • Ús d’eines informàtiques (nivell mig-alt).

  • Altres tasques administratives pròpies del lloc.


Requisits:

  • Formació en administració.

  • Incorporació immediata.

  • Es valorarà experiència prèvia en l’àmbit de la gerontologia.


Beneficis:

  • Àpats a càrrec de l’empresa.

  • Formació contínua

  • Integració a un equip multidisciplinari i en creixement constant.

title: Senior Software Engineer Frontend (m/w/d) - Angular
description:
Unsere CPQ Software wird von Unternehmen im Maschinen- und Anlagenbau genutzt, um komplexe Produkte zu konfigurieren und abzubilden.
**Ein großer Teil dieser Logik lebt im Frontend**: Modellierungsprozesse, Abhängigkeiten, Validierungen, Visualisierungen. **Das, was Du hier entwickelst, bestimmt direkt, wie effizient und zuverlässig unsere Kunden jeden Tag arbeiten.**
Es geht nicht darum, "hübsche Oberflächen" umzusetzen. Sondern um ein **zentrales Werkzeug, das produktionskritische Entscheidungen unterstützt**.
Dafür suchen wir jemanden, der architekturelle Entscheidungen ganzheitlich denkt und die Weiterentwicklung unserer Frontend-Architektur aktiv prägt.

Deine Rolle im Modeler Tools Team

Als Senior Software Engineer Frontend (m/w/d) arbeitest Du in einem agilen Team an einem der zentralen Werkzeuge unserer gesamten Plattform:

Die **Modeler Tools sind das Herzstück**, mit dem unsere Kunden ihre Produktstrukturen, Regeln und Konfigurationslogiken modellieren.

Dein Beitrag im Frontend hat direkten Einfluss auf sämtliche CPQ-Projekte – und damit auf die Effizienz und Zuverlässigkeit des Produkts, mit dem unsere Kunden täglich arbeiten.

In dieser Rolle übernimmst Du eine **zentrale Verantwortung** **für die Weiterentwicklung unseres Frontends**. Du bringst Deine Expertise und Erfahrungswerte ein, um unsere Angular-Architektur langfristig zu gestalten und weiterzuentwickeln.

### Genau das macht Deine Aufgaben wirkungsvoll

  • Verantwortung für die **Weiterentwicklung und Qualität der Angular-Frontend-Komponenten**
  • Treffen fundierter **Architekturentscheidungen** sowie aktive Weiterentwicklung des Frontend-Tech-Stacks
  • Ganzheitliche Analyse neuer Anforderungen und Ableitung **nachhaltiger technischer Lösungen sowie effizienter Schnittstellendesigns**
  • Sicherstellung von **Performance und Skalierbarkeit** auch in komplexen Anwendungsszenarien

### Was Dich auszeichnet

  • Mehrjährige, fundierte Erfahrung (ca. 10+ Jahre) in der Frontend-Entwicklung
  • Tiefes Verständnis von Angular, TypeScript und komplexen Frontend-Architekturen
  • Erfahrung in der Gestaltung skalierbarer und wartbarer Systeme
  • Sehr gutes Verständnis von Schnittstellen und Systemzusammenhängen
  • Lösungs- und systemorientiertes Denken - nicht ausschließlich featurebezogen
  • Fließende Deutschkenntnisse sowie sehr gutes technisches Englisch

### Warum diese Rolle für Dich interessant sein könnte

  • Du **gestaltest aktiv** Architektur, Technologien und Engineering-Standards
  • Du arbeitest an einem System mit **hoher fachlicher und technischer Komplexität**
  • Du bringst Deine Erfahrung in ein Umfeld ein, das **Qualität und Ownership** wertschätzt
  • Du stehst eng mit dem Backend und anderen Stakeholdern im **Austausch**, um Lösungen **ganzheitlich z**u verbessern
  • Deine technischen Entscheidungen haben **langfristigen Einfluss**
  • Du arbeitest mit **modernen Technologien**, ohne technologischem Selbstzweck hinterherzulaufen

**Wenn Du gerne in komplexen Frontend-Landschaften arbeitest und Systeme bauen willst, die Jahre halten, dann passt diese Rolle gut zu Dir.**

title: Business Analyst
description:
Mayflower is a technology company building large-scale entertainment products for a global audience. Our platforms serve millions of users worldwide and rank among the top-50 most visited websites globally. For more than 10 years, we’ve been creating reliable, high-performance solutions at the intersection of product, engineering, and entertainment.

We are looking for a Business/System Analyst to join a team for our Growth stream.

Job Responsibilities

Requirements Elicitation & Specification

  • Gather, structure, and validate requirements collaboratively with Product, Engineering, Design, and QA.

  • Translate product hypotheses and business goals into clear, testable artifacts such as user stories, epics, acceptance criteria, and business rules.

  • Ensure task descriptions are clear and detailed for development and QA, including scenarios, parameters, constraints, and necessary checks.

System Analysis & Architecture

  • Dive deep into technical details, demonstrating an understanding of data structures, APIs, integrations, system constraints, and microservice architecture to communicate with engineers on their level.

  • Participate in architectural analysis alongside developers and architects.

  • Deeply detail technical tasks to reduce the workload on architects and developers.

Requirements Lifecycle & Delivery Support

  • Maintain requirements throughout the delivery cycle, managing changes and documenting decisions and assumptions.

  • Support developers and QA engineers in clarifying context and validating shipped features against the intended requirements.

  • Serve as the primary bridge between Product and Engineering, driving alignment on scope, risks, dependencies, and constraints.

Knowledge Base Management & AI Preparation

  • Create and maintain a clear, structured, and searchable knowledge base detailing product behavior, business logic, APIs, data, admin pages, and system relationships.

  • Actively eliminate product knowledge gaps by extracting missing information from the Product, Dev, and QA teams and documenting it to prevent recurring questions.

  • Update documentation immediately following releases to accurately reflect the actual system behavior.

Prepare knowledge for LLMs:

  • Detail architectural and business logic so it can be effectively used to build a vector knowledge base for LLMs. Organize documentation so that AI can accurately answer system queries, and continually refine the knowledge base to address gaps the AI cannot answer.

  • Utilize AI tools (like ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude) to accelerate your daily tasks—such as drafting requirements, checking consistency, and generating scenarios—while applying rigorous verification and accountability for the output's correctness.

You’ll thrive here if you have:

  • 5+ years working as a Business or System Analyst in product development, closely collaborating with cross-functional delivery teams.

  • The ability to understand complex existing product and architectural logic quickly, identify system risks, and translate them into usable documentation for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

  • Demonstrated ability to structure ambiguous problems, define business rules, and produce detailed, clear, and testable requirements.

  • Exceptional, structured writing skills with the ability to create documentation that is concise, consistent, and easy to navigate over time.

  • Practical, hands-on experience using modern AI/LLM tools in day-to-day analysis work (beyond basic prompting) to increase output, as well as an understanding of how to structure data for LLM vector databases.

  • The ability to influence without authority, facilitate discussions, and drive decisions in a fast-paced environment with shifting priorities.

  • Upper-Intermediate (B2) or higher in English, as documentation and discussions are conducted in English.

Conditions

We know that great talent deserves great conditions, so here’s what you can expect when joining us:

  • EU-based employment contract and a 3-year Cyprus work visa with full support for your relocation and visa processes, including assistance for your family.

  • EU passport. Opportunity to obtain an EU passport after 4 years of residence.

  • Full relocation package: flights to Limassol for you and your family, a company-covered apartment for the first month, and full relocation support to make your move smooth and hassle-free.

  • Transparent performance reviews twice a year, with bonus opportunities and salary adjustments.

  • Private medical insurance for you and your family.

  • Corporate mobile plan (unlimited in Cyprus with roaming included).

  • Mindfulness & well-being support, including psychological assistance with 50% coverage.

  • 50% coverage of school and kindergarten fees for your children.

  • Fully covered sports benefits, and also access to in-house electric scooters and bike rentals.

  • Provident fund (Cyprus): a long-term savings plan co-funded by you and the Company together (available after probation) that grows throughout your time with us in Cyprus.

  • Professional growth support: language courses, conferences, training programs, and coaching.

  • Peer recognition program: receive and share kudos for great work.

  • A fully equipped office in Limassol’s city center, with everything you need for deep work and collaboration.

  • Free breakfasts and lunches in the office and an in-house coffee bar with high-quality drinks and a health bar stocked with nutritious snacks.

  • A strong engineering culture: international teams, corporate events, team buildings, and hackathons — because great work happens in great communities.

Benefits are provided in accordance with the company’s policies and eligibility criteria.

Recruitment process

  • HR interview (40 min);

  • Technical interview (1 hour);

  • Test task;

  • Final interview (1 hour);

  • Recommendations check;

  • Job Offer.

title: Spüler:in (m/w/d) in Vollzeit
description:
Werde Teil der Bellezza Familie - wir freuen uns auf Dich!

Du bist zuverlässig, packst gerne mit an und arbeitest gerne im Team? Dann bist Du bei uns genau richtig!

Komm zur Bellezza Family und werde Teil eines motivierten Küchenteams, das jeden Tag mit Leidenschaft und Qualität für unvergessliche Genussmomente sorgt. Als Spüler / Mitarbeiter Spülküche (m/w/d) bist Du ein unverzichtbarer Teil unseres Teams und trägst maßgeblich dazu bei, dass unsere Küche reibungslos funktioniert.

Warum mit uns?

  • Ein Arbeitsplatz, auf den Du stolz sein kannst

  • Ein modernes, stylisches Restaurant mitten in Hamburg

  • Ein engagiertes und motiviertes Team mit echter Leidenschaft

  • Flache Hierarchien und Kommunikation auf Augenhöhe

  • Raum für Deine Ideen und Deine persönliche Entwicklung

  • Eine faire und zeitgemäße Vergütung

  • Flexible Dienstplanung

  • Wellpass (EGYM Wellpass) für Deine Gesundheit und Fitness

  • persönliches Geburtstagsgeschenk

  • Langfristige Perspektiven und echte Aufstiegsmöglichkeiten

Bei uns zählt nicht nur, was Du kannst – sondern auch, wer Du bist.

Deine Aufgaben

  • Reinigung von Geschirr, Besteck, Töpfen, Pfannen und Küchengeräten

  • Bedienung und Pflege der Spülmaschinen sowie des gesamten Spülbereichs

  • Sicherstellung von Sauberkeit und Ordnung in der Küche und im Back-of-House-Bereich

  • Unterstützung bei der Vorbereitung (Mise en Place) und einfachen Küchenhilfsarbeiten

  • Mithilfe bei der Warenannahme sowie beim Verräumen und Lagern der Waren

  • Einhaltung aller Hygiene- und HACCP-Standards

  • Unterstützung des Küchenteams bei allgemeinen Reinigungs- und Aufräumarbeiten

  • Sorgfältiger und verantwortungsbewusster Umgang mit Arbeitsmitteln und Geräten

  • Zuverlässige Erledigung der übertragenen Aufgaben und aktive Zusammenarbeit im Team

Das bringst du mit

  • Zuverlässigkeit und eine sorgfältige Arbeitsweise

  • Teamgeist und Einsatzbereitschaft

  • Belastbarkeit und Freude an körperlicher Arbeit

  • Verantwortungsbewusstsein und Ordnungssinn

  • Erste Erfahrung in der Gastronomie ist wünschenswert, jedoch keine Voraussetzung – auch Quereinsteiger sind herzlich willkommen

Warum mit der Bellezza Group?

Weil wir daran glauben, dass großartige Gastronomie nur mit großartigen Menschen funktioniert.

Wir möchten ein Arbeitsumfeld schaffen, in dem man gerne zur Arbeit kommt, sich weiterentwickeln kann und gemeinsam Erfolge feiert.

Bei uns bist Du keine Personalnummer, sondern Teil einer Familie, die zusammenhält.

Klingt nach Dir?

Dann freuen wir uns darauf, Dich kennenzulernen!

Sende Deine Bewerbungsunterlagen mit Deinem frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermin.

title: Maritime Tech Co-Founder / CSO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

  • You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Maritime Tech

  • You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management

  • You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs

  • You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding

  • You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively

  • You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate

  • You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues

  • You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: Commercial Automobile BtoB (H/F)
description:
Ton objectif ? Jouer un rôle crucial dans le développement exponentiel de notre marketplace !

  • Tu développes nos ventes BtoB par téléphone auprès de nos clients sur toute la France. La clef du succès ? Proposer les meilleurs deals aux professionnels sur la plateforme, et surtout, ne rien lâcher !
  • Tu as un discours commercial impactant permettant de présenter efficacement tous les bénéfices de nos services innovants
  • Tu analyses, identifies et cibles nos futurs partenaires BtoB (marchands, concessions automobiles, etc.) à partir de fichiers fournis par l’entreprise et tu crées de nouveaux canaux de vente pour AUTO1.com
  • Tu présentes les solutions pour les financements à nos partenaires
  • Tu pilotes l’intégralité de notre démarche (formation des partenaires, fidélisation…)
  • Tu guideras les clients tout au long du processus d'achat de véhicules sur notre plateforme, en répondant à toutes leurs questions. Une fois l'achat effectué, tu les aideras pour toute question ou problème éventuel.
  • Tu es passionné par le monde automobile et souhaite rejoindre un groupe solide, innovant et en développement constant ?
  • Tu es un compétiteur dans l’âme animé par le goût du challenge ?
  • Tu es à l’aise au téléphone, tu aimes la prospection et tu sais convaincre ?
  • Tu as une bonne capacité d’écoute et d’adaptabilité ?
  • Tu as au moins une expérience commerciale réussie ?

Convaincu que ce poste est fait pour toi ?

  • Échange avec Rosa, Chargée de recrutement, pour connaître tes aspirations et voir si nous avons la même vision pour avancer ensemble
  • Mise en situation et debrief avec ton manager, pour partager tes compétences en lien avec le poste à pourvoir

Le tout en CDI !

Prêt(e) à embarquer dans l’aventure AUTO1 Group et à révolutionner l’univers des véhicules d’occasion ? On a hâte de te rencontrer !

Choose Challenge. Choose Pace. Choose Growth.

* Engagée dans une démarche inclusive ayant pour priorité de garantir l'égalité des chances dans l'accès et le maintien dans l'emploi, AUTO1 Group, développe un environnement de travail ouvert à tous et à toutes sans distinction.

Le ou la candidat(e) retenu(e) bénéficiera des dispositions de rémunération applicables à sa catégorie de poste en vertu de la convention collective en vigueur (Automobile, IDCC 1090) notamment en matière de primes conventionnelles et de tout autre avantage prévu, sous réserve de remplir les conditions d'éligibilité requises.

title: SITE ENGINEER
description:
Job Responsibilities:

- Coordinate and supervise construction site activities.

- Liaise with RE/RTO, subcontractors, consultants and supplies.

- Ensure all works are carried out in accordance with approved drawings and specifications.

- Conduct site inspections to ensure quality workmanship and compliance.

- Assist in planning work schedules and sequencing of site activities.

- Attend site meetings, prepare minutes and follow up site issue.

- Maintain proper documentation including daily reports, drawings and site records.

title: Project Coordinator - 12 month contract
description:
About the Role

Reporting to the Program Manager, the PMO Coordinator will play a critical role in supporting the successful delivery of our Cloud program across North America. Acting as a central point of coordination between regional technology teams, program stakeholders and delivery partners, you will help drive governance, planning, communication and execution activities across multiple workstreams.

This is an opportunity to contribute to a high profile enterprise transformation program while gaining exposure to global stakeholders, modern cloud technologies and large-scale program delivery practices.

This role will be based in Sydney (Mascot), Melbourne (CBD) or Port Kembla.

Key Responsibilities

  • Coordinate regional workshops, planning sessions and project activities across North America
  • Support resource planning and coordination of assessment and migration activities
  • Monitor actions, milestones and deliverables to ensure timely progress and escalation of risks or issues
  • Maintain governance artefacts including RAID logs, status reporting and decision registers
  • Support program communications, stakeholder engagement and change management activities
  • Manage project documentation and information repositories
  • Coordinate onboarding and offboarding of program resources
  • Work closely with Microsoft, DXC, Lab3 and internal technology teams to support successful delivery outcomes

About You

You are a highly organised and proactive project professional with strong coordination, communication and stakeholder management skills. You thrive in fast paced environments, enjoy bringing structure to complex initiatives and can effectively build relationships across technical and business teams.

What You'll Bring

  • Experience in project coordination or program support roles within large or complex projects
  • Strong organisational, administration and communication skills
  • Experience tracking risks, issues, actions and dependencies
  • Exposure to project governance, reporting and change management practices
  • Ability to influence, collaborate and work effectively with diverse stakeholders
  • Experience working on enterprise technology or transformation programs will be highly regarded
  • Knowledge of project delivery frameworks such as PMBOK, Prince2 or MSP is advantageous

The BlueScope Difference

A career at BlueScope provides the opportunity to make a difference – to you, to your team, to our customers, to transforming products, processes and brands, and to an organisation intent on shaping a better built environment for all our communities. At BlueScope, you will:

  • make an impact both within the organisation and in the broader community
  • create and deliver innovative products and solutions that shape the world we live in
  • grow on a path of continuous learning and development
  • enjoy the sense of belonging that comes from working with people who genuinely look out for one another

We’re dedicated to supporting our people keeping one another safe, rewarding successes, and helping individuals grow. We foster workplace flexibility and an inclusive work environment that welcomes people of all backgrounds. We’re committed to climate action and to helping shape a better future for all our communities; we are committed to Our Purpose, Creating Strength.

Come and discover us at bluescope.com/life at bluescope/. Together we can make a world of difference.

title: Porgera | Assistant - Scaffolder - Scaffolding
description:
Assistant Scaffolder - Scaffolding

About New Porgera

New Porgera Limited (NPL) is a leading mining company dedicated to sustainable practices and community engagement in Papua New Guinea. As the operator of the Porgera Gold Mine, we focus on extracting high-quality mineral resources while prioritizing the well-being of our employees, the environment, and the local communities. Our commitment to safety, innovation, and responsible mining ensures that we not only contribute to the economy but also support the development and prosperity of the regions we operate in. At NPL, we strive to create a positive impact, fostering strong relationships with stakeholders and promoting sustainable growth for future generations.

New Porgera Limited, a leading name in the mining industry, is currently seeking a skilled and experienced Assistant Scaffolder to assist the Scaffolders in handling scaffolds within the Fixed Plant Maintenance Department. The successful candidate will play a vital role in ensuring the safe and efficient setup and dismantling of scaffolding structures within our mining site.

In this role, you will:

  • Supporting the work of the scaffolder

  • Carrying, declaring and clearing scaffolding material

  • Tidies up the workplace or house keeping

  • Perform routine inspections and maintenance on scaffolding equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards.

  • Assist in the construction, assembly, maintaining and dismantling of tubular scaffolding

  • Adhere to all safety protocols, guidelines, and regulations during scaffolding operations.

  • Identifying the risks in their work area and using the correct controls.

  • Reporting to the Supervisor immediately any actual or potential unsafe conditions or activities, mishaps, incidents, accidents, injuries or illnesses in the work place.

  • Using personal protective clothing or equipment as provided.

  • Maintain a high level of health, safety and environmental awareness and comply with all relevant NPL policies & Procedures.

About You

To succeed in this role, the ideal candidate will possess the following qualifications and experiences:

  • Minimum of Grade 10 or above Education level

  • Proven experience as a Scaffolding in building complex and hanging, preferably in a mining or heavy equipment environment.

  • Strong understanding of scaffolding techniques and safety procedures.

  • Ability to interpret and follow technical manuals and specifications.

  • Physical fitness and ability to work in various weather conditions.

  • Safety hazard awareness in JHA or JSA, Zero Tasol, Take 5, FLRA, Risk Assessments

  • Knowledge of local building codes and regulations

  • Excellent communication and teamwork skills.

Mobilization Requirement

This is a PNG National Award position, a site-based role on a Walk in Walk out arrangement with rotation of 20 days on and 8 days off. Must successfully pass a pre-employment medical including drug and alcohol screening. (This will be arranged only for successful candidates who progress after the interview stage) and have an up-to-date Police Clearance Certificate (acquired within the last 6 months). Must a be Local Porgeran (Cat 1A – SML, Cat 1B –LMP or Cat 2 – Other Porgera) candidate and available to start immediately. Females are encouraged to apply.

Join us at New Porgera Limited and be part of a forward-thinking team dedicated to excellence!

Note: This advert will be open until 31st of July 2026. If you do not hear from NPL within the next seven (7) days following the closing date, please consider your application unsuccessful for this opportunity.
