title: Gearbox Specialist
description:
About Business:

Adani Group: Adani Group is a diversified organisation in India comprising 10 publicly traded companies. It has created a world class logistics and utility infrastructure portfolio that has a pan-India presence. Adani Group is headquartered in Ahmedabad, in the state of Gujarat, India. Over the years, Adani Group has positioned itself to be the market leader in its logistics and energy businesses focusing on large scale infrastructure development in India with O&M practices benchmarked to global standards. With four IG rated businesses, it is the only Infrastructure Investment Grade issuer in India.

Wind Turbine Generator (WTG): Adani Wind Turbine Generators (WTG) is a leading player in the renewable energy sector, specializing in the design, manufacturing, and installation of high-performance wind turbine systems. With a commitment to sustainability, Adani WTGs deliver efficient and reliable energy solutions, contributing to clean power generation across global markets.

Job Purpose:

The Mechanical Drive Train Engineer is responsible for executing the design, testing, and validation of mechanical components within the drive train system, such as main shafts, bearings, and couplings. Utilizing CAD tools (e.g., CATIA, NX) and basic FEA techniques, the Engineer ensures that designs meet structural, thermal, and fatigue requirements. This role supports testing activities, collects and analyzes performance data, and provides hands-on assistance during component assembly and site troubleshooting. The Engineer also participates in root cause analysis of component failures, material selection, and compliance checks against industry standards. By contributing to continuous improvement initiatives, the Mechanical Drive Train Engineer helps optimize designs and manufacturing processes, enhancing the overall reliability and performance of wind turbine systems.

Design Execution and Drafting

  • Execute the design of mechanical drive train components, including main shafts, bearings, gearboxes, and couplings, using CAD software (e.g., CATIA, NX).
  • Ensure designs comply with stress, fatigue, and thermal requirements, conducting basic FEA to validate design integrity, particularly for high-load components like main bearings.

Component Testing and Validation

  • Participate in component testing, including fatigue testing, gear meshing analysis, and noise/vibration assessments to validate the performance of critical components like gearboxes and couplings.
  • Collect and analyze data during testing phases, identifying discrepancies between expected and actual performance and suggesting corrective actions.

Technical Support in Manufacturing

  • Provide hands-on support during the manufacturing and assembly of drive train components, resolving engineering challenges related to fit, alignment, and part integration, especially for gearboxes and shafts.
  • Assist in the development of assembly instructions, ensuring accurate alignment of drive train components for optimal performance.

Problem Solving and Site Troubleshooting

  • Assist in site troubleshooting and diagnostics, applying engineering principles to resolve performance issues with components like main shafts, gearboxes, and bearings.
  • Conduct initial root cause analysis on-site, documenting findings and recommending design or process changes to prevent recurrence.

Material Selection and Analysis

  • Evaluate material properties and select appropriate materials for drive train components, considering factors such as load, wear resistance, and thermal conductivity, particularly for high-stress components.
  • Assist in material testing and analysis, including hardness testing, tensile testing, and metallurgical evaluations.

Compliance and Standards Adherence

  • Ensure all engineering work aligns with relevant industry standards, including ISO, ANSI, and company-specific design guidelines.
  • Maintain awareness of the latest standards and regulatory changes, updating designs and processes as required.

Adherence to Policies and Ethical Considerations

  • Adhere to organizational policies and ethical standards in all engineering activities, ensuring transparency and integrity in design, testing, and manufacturing processes.
  • Participate in training on corporate policies, ethics, and compliance to maintain high professional standards.

Continuous Improvement and Learning

  • Participate in continuous improvement initiatives, suggesting enhancements to design methodologies, testing protocols, and production processes.
  • Engage in professional development activities, including training on new engineering tools, industry trends, and emerging technologies in drive train design.

Key Stakeholders - Internal:

  • Head - Mechanical Engineering
  • Lead - Mechanical Drive Train
  • Manufacturing Teams
  • QA Teams

Key Stakeholders - External:

  • Material Testing Laboratories

Educational Background:

  • BE in Mechanical Engineering or related discipline.

Work Experience:

  • 3-5 years’ experience in executing component design, testing, and on-site troubleshooting of mechanical systems in turbine operations.

Industry Knowledge:

  • Understanding of CAD tools, material properties, and initial failure analysis, focusing on main shafts, bearings, and gearboxes.

title: Chef ou Cheffe de clinique avec titre de spécialité pour une spécialisation mains - 100%
description:
En tant que cheffe ou chef de clinique titulaire du titre de spécialiste en chirurgie orthopédique, vous rejoignez l’unité de chirurgie de la main dans le cadre d’une spécialisation. L’unité assure les urgences 24h/24 de la main et du poignet pour tout le canton de Genève (traitement des plaies, fractures du poignet ou des doigts, etc.) ainsi que les consultations dédiées pour différentes pathologies telles que l’arthrose de la main, le syndrome du canal (ou tunnel) carpien, la tendinite, etc.

Dans cette fonction, vous assurez une activité clinique complète, incluant les interventions chirurgicales et la prise en charge des patientes et patients intrahospitalier. Vous contribuez également activement aux projets de l’unité.

Vous faites preuve de rigueur, de sens clinique et d’une grande capacité d’analyse dans la prise en charge de situations parfois complexes, y compris dans la gestion des complications à court terme. Grâce à votre sens de l’organisation et à votre capacité à prioriser, vous assurez efficacement vos activités cliniques tout en répondant aux sollicitations multiples (patients et patientes, téléphones, correspondances, réunions).

Vous évoluez dans un environnement exigeant, à l’interface des soins, de l’enseignement et de la recherche. À ce titre, vous collaborez étroitement avec l’ensemble de l’équipe médico-soignante et veillez à une communication claire, fluide et respectueuse, tant avec vos collègues qu’avec les patientes et patients, en adaptant votre discours à leur compréhension.

En vous appuyant sur votre esprit d’équipe, vous faites preuve de solidarité face aux imprévus et aux charges de travail élevées. En tant que cheffe ou chef de clinique, vous participez activement à la supervision et à la formation des médecins internes, en adoptant une posture de mentorat et en partageant votre expertise.

Vous prenez part aux gardes de l’unité de chirurgie de la main.

L’activité clinique s’inscrit dans un parcours de formation postgraduée structuré et exigeant, visant la spécialisation en chirurgie de la main. Cet environnement offre des opportunités de développement reconnues, incluant des formations pratiques et théoriques de haut niveau, notamment à travers des collaborations avec la SFITS (Swiss Foundation for Innovation and Training in Surgery).

Vous êtes titulaire du diplôme fédéral de médecin ou disposez d’une formation jugée équivalente par la Commission des professions médicales de l’Office fédéral de la santé publique (MEBEKO).

Vous êtes au bénéfice d’un titre FMH en chirurgie orthopédique ou FMH en chirurgie de la main, ou vous avez un titre jugé équivalent reconnu par la MEBEKO et êtes en possession du droit de pratique cantonal ou êtes en mesure de l'obtenir avant votre entrée en fonction auprès du médecin cantonal de Genève.

Vous justifiez d'une expérience confirmée en chirurgie de la main vous permettant d’être autonome et souhaitez vous spécialiser en chirurgie de la main. Votre expérience préalable est réalisée idéalement dans un environnement universitaire avec un haut volume d’activité.

Pour les personnes non francophones, le niveau français C1 au test parlé et écrit est requis.

Vous avez d’excellentes compétences à communiquer en équipe pluridisciplinaire et avec la patientèle, vous êtes une personne dynamique, flexible, engagée et faites preuve de discrétion. De plus, vous avez démontré de fortes capacités d’adaptation.

Vous pouvez travailler tant en horaires de journée, qu’en horaires de nuit et de week-end.

  • Entrée en fonction : à convenir
  • Nombre de poste : 1
  • Taux d’activité : 100%
  • Classe de fonction : 24
  • Contrat : CDI
  • Délai de candidature : 21.08.2026
  • Demande de renseignement : Mme C. Bandi, secrétaire du médecin-chef, carole.bandi@hug.ch

Votre dossier de candidature doit comporter une lettre démontrant votre motivation, votre curriculum vitae, les copies des diplômes et attestations requis pour le poste et les 2 derniers certificats de travail.

Cette annonce s'adresse indistinctement aux femmes et aux hommes.

Souhaitant s’engager dans la lutte contre le chômage, les HUG encouragent les candidatures qui proviennent de l’Office cantonal de l’emploi.

Seules les candidatures soumises via la plateforme de recrutement sont prises en compte. Les candidatures papier et mail ne seront pas traitées.

title: Commis 2 - Pan Asian
description:
We are seeking an enthusiastic and detail-oriented Commis 2 to join our culinary team at our establishment in Visakhapatnam, India. In this role, you will work alongside our experienced kitchen brigade to prepare and present authentic Pan Asian cuisine to the highest standards. As a Commis 2, you will support senior chefs whilst developing your culinary expertise in a dynamic, fast-paced kitchen environment. This is an excellent opportunity to advance your culinary career within a professional organisation that values precision, collaboration, and excellence.

  • Prepare and cook Pan Asian dishes according to established recipes and presentation standards, maintaining consistency and quality • Assist senior chefs with food preparation, including chopping, slicing, and marinating ingredients with precision and efficiency • Maintain a clean and organised workstation, adhering to all food safety and hygiene regulations at all times
  • Manage mise en place effectively to ensure smooth kitchen operations during service periods • Operate kitchen equipment, including woks and specialised Asian cooking apparatus, safely and competently
  • Support the kitchen brigade in a collaborative manner, responding promptly to instructions and requests • Monitor stock levels and communicate with senior chefs regarding ingredient requirements • Contribute to the development of new Pan Asian dishes and menu items under guidance • Maintain time keeping standards and demonstrate reliability and commitment to the team • Assist with kitchen cleaning and maintenance to uphold organisational standards
  • Proficiency in Pan Asian cooking techniques, including Chinese, Thai, Vietnamese, Japanese, Korean, and Malaysian cuisines • Strong knife skills and food preparation techniques • Comprehensive knowledge of food safety, hygiene, and sanitation standards • Excellent organisational and time management abilities • Ability to work efficiently in a high-pressure kitchen environment
  • Strong people skills and ability to work collaboratively within a kitchen brigade • Attention to detail and commitment to maintaining quality standards • Physical capability to stand for extended periods and lift heavy items safely
  • Desirable experience as a Commis Chef or equivalent kitchen position • Desirable knowledge of Asian ingredients, flavour profiles, and culinary traditions • Desirable experience with wok cooking and specialised Asian cooking methods • Desirable awareness of food costing and portion control • Desirable completion of culinary training, apprenticeship, or formal culinary programme

Votre équipe et votre environnement de travail :
En quelques mots, présentez l’équipe, l’établissement ou l’environnement de travail qui reflète la culture de l'équipe
Note : Vous pouvez inclure ici les spécificités locales ou les critères légaux, tels que le permis de travail.
Notre Engagement Diversité & Inclusion :
Nous sommes une entreprise inclusive et notre ambition est d’attirer, de recruter, et de promouvoir la diversité des talents.

title: Chef / Cheffe de Projet Industrie
description:
Votre équipe :

En intégrant l’établissement du Wavre (Belgique), vous rejoignez une équipe dynamique et ambitieuse, où chaque projet est une opportunité de monter en compétences et d’évoluer dans un environnement stimulant.

Prêt(e) à relever le défi avec nous ?

Vos missions :

En tant que Chef de projet / Cheffe de Projet, vous interviendrez en études et maîtrise d’œuvre pour la réalisation de projets industriels en tous corps d’état avec une expérience réussie en gestion de projets dans l’industrie des procédés.

Vous intervenez sur des projets d'installations nouvelles ou d'amélioration d'unités existantes.

Dans ce cadre vos missions consisteront à :

  • Gérer les projets (planification, choix et validation des orientations techniques du projet, achats sur les projets en maîtrise d’œuvre, suivi financier des projets devis, offres, …)
  • Etre l’interlocuteur de proximité des clients
  • Manager des projets et des équipes d’études
  • Rédiger des notes techniques, des estimations financières et des plannings des projets
  • Participer activement à la conception des projets
  • Réaliser les bilans d’affaires
  • Etre garant de la qualité et de la politique HSE

Vos atouts :

De formation Bac+5 Ingénieur généraliste ou niveau équivalent, vous avez acquis une expérience de 10 ans minimum en gestion de projet ingénierie ou EPC taille de projet jusqu’à 50 000 heures, en ingénierie industrielle dans le secteur de la pétrochimie, du raffinage, de la pharmacie, ou d’autres industries de process.

Vous maîtrisez les techniques des technologies industrielles. Vous possédez une véritable culture industrielle et vous avez une capacité d’anticipation sur des problématiques Technique/Budget/Planning.

La maîtrise de l'Anglais est un plus dans le cadre de ce poste.

L’application des procédures Qualité ISO 9001 et VCA sont votre quotidien. Veiller à la sécurité sur les sites et/ou chez nos clients fait partie de vos priorités.

Vous maitrisez les outils informatique tels les outils de gestions de projet (plan d’exécution projet, planning,….)

Vous ne travaillez jamais seul, un bon esprit d’équipe, une capacité à prendre du recul dans vos missions, une grande curiosité, un sens du partage et d’entraide avec vos collègues seront vos atouts pour réussir.

Travailler sur les projets d'Artelia vous permet d’enrichir et développer continuellement vos compétences. Nos parcours de formation structurés (par spécialités ou métiers) seront adaptés aux personnes curieuses et désireuses de progresser dans leur domaine.

Nous vous offrons :

  • Une entreprise sociale et citoyenne
  • Une organisation du travail modulable
  • Une politique de partage des résultats
  • Des opportunités de carrière et un accompagnement de vos challenges professionnels

Notre process de recrutement :

Mieux se connaître pour se choisir, c’est ce que nous vous proposons lors des 2 étapes de notre processus de recrutement :

  • Echange téléphonique / Entretien avec un recruteur pour mieux cerner vos attentes et orienter votre candidature. L’objectif est de vous présenter notre groupe, les perspectives de carrière, les contours du poste envisagé, et surtout comprendre qui vous êtes, vos aspirations, vos motivations, votre projet professionnel
  • Entretien avec un manager opérationnel : il vous présentera plus en détail les missions, ce qu’il attend de vous, le mode de fonctionnement de l’équipe, les projets.

#LI-CA1

title: Senior Product Manager, Fraud and Compliance
description:
About Remote

Remote is solving modern organizations’ biggest challenge – navigating global employment compliantly with ease. We make it possible for businesses of all sizes to recruit, pay, and manage international teams. With our core values at heart and future focused work culture, our team works tirelessly on ambitious problems, asynchronously, around the world. You can find Remoters working from 6 different continents (Antarctica left to go!) and all of our positions are fully remote.

With Innovation as one of the core values, we have built Automation and AI capabilities into the requirements for every role.

We encourage every member of the Remote team to bring their talents, experiences and culture to the table to help us build the best-in-class HR platform.
If you are energetic, curious, motivated and ambitious, be part of our world. Apply now and define the future of work!

What this job can offer you

This is an exciting time to join Remote and make a personal difference in the global employment space as a Senior Product Manager, Fraud and Compliance, joining the Fintech area.

As Remote grows and handles larger transaction volumes across countries, so does our exposure to fraud, credit risk, and compliance risk. This role owns the systems and strategies that protect Remote and its customers — from onboarding verification and screening, to credit risk assessment and reserve management, to fraud detection and enforcement across the full customer lifecycle.

You'll drive the evolution of our risk and compliance capabilities from reactive, rule-based controls toward proactive, automated systems that identify risk signals early and scale without proportional increases in manual effort. This is a high-autonomy, high-stakes role. You'll report to the Director of Product and work closely with Risk, Compliance, Treasury, Legal, Collections, and Engineering.

What you bring

  • Proficiency in Cursor and/or Claude Code is required. You must be able to autonomously build, debug, and ship functional code and prototypes — not just write specs.
  • AI fluency. You have a point of view on how AI and automation can transform risk operations — from pattern detection to anomaly scoring to compliance workflows.
  • Seasoned Product Manager, with a track record on platform, API, or products for operational teams.
  • Platform mindset. You build systems others can extend. Scalability means growing the business without proportional increases in manual work or complexity.
  • Strong UX sensibilities. Risk and compliance tooling doesn't have to be painful. You care about the admin experience as much as the customer-facing one.
  • Outstanding communication and collaboration skills. You partner effectively with compliance, engineering, design, treasury, and legal — and you lead by example on async workflows.
  • Data-driven decision-making. Comfortable querying data and working with visualisation tools, you let insights drive your prioritisation.
  • Fast learner with a growth mindset. You're humble, eager to learn, and get up to speed quickly in new territory, then pass on what you pick up so the whole team levels up.
  • Great judgment - making strategic (what to prioritize) and tactical (feature scope) decisions.
  • Language: Business-level proficiency writing and speaking English.
  • Domain advantage (nice to have): Experience with risk/compliance platforms and third-party screening or fraud detection tooling.

Key Responsibilities

  • Own credit risk assessment and reserve management: Drive the strategy for how Remote evaluates, scores, and mitigates credit risk. Evolve the underlying models and tooling to be smarter, faster, and more automated over time.
  • Fraud detection and prevention: Own fraud controls, freeze mechanisms, and dispute workflows. Build proactive strategies that detect and prevent fraud — not just react to it.
  • Compliance automation: Own end-to-end onboarding compliance — KYB verification, AML screening, and background checks. Drive automation to reduce manual effort while maintaining regulatory defensibility.
  • Build flexible, platform-oriented systems: Prefer solutions that operations teams can configure and extend without engineering involvement. Remove engineering as a bottleneck for responding to new risk and compliance patterns.
  • Collections and enforcement: Partner with Collections and Treasury on risk reserves, payment blocks, and enforcement actions. Own the admin and customer-facing surfaces that support this.
  • Ship iteratively at pace: Lead a product team delivering high throughput and high ROI. Advance key KPIs around automation rates, false-positive reduction, and risk accuracy.
  • Stakeholder management across risk, ops, and finance. Translate complex risk trade-offs into clear product decisions. Align with Compliance Operations, Treasury, Legal, and senior leadership.

Practicals

  • You'll report to: Director of Product
  • Location: For this position we welcome everyone to apply;
  • Start date: As soon as possible

Application process

  1. AI interview
  2. Interview with recruiter
  3. Interview with future manager
  4. Live session
  5. Team interview
  6. Bar Raiser Interview
  7. Interview with CTO
  8. Offer + Prior employment verification check

Remote's Total Rewards philosophy is to ensure fair, unbiased compensation and fair equity pay along with competitive benefits in all locations in which we operate. We do not agree to or encourage cheap-labor practices and therefore we ensure to pay above in-location rates. We hope to inspire other companies to support global talent-hiring and bring local wealth to developing countries.

At first glance our salary bands seem quite wide - here is some context. At Remote we have international operations and a globally distributed workforce. We use geo ranges to consider geographic pay differentials as part of our global compensation strategy to remain competitive in various markets while we hiring globally.

Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location, and our job titles may span more than one career level. The actual base pay for the successful candidate in this role is dependent upon many factors such as location, transferable or job-related skills, work experience, relevant training, business needs, and market demands. The base salary range may be subject to change.

At Remote, we foster internal mobility as a key element of our culture of employee growth and development, supported by a compensation philosophy that guarantees pay equity and fairness. Therefore, all compensation changes associated with an internal move will be reviewed by the Total Rewards & People Enablement team on a case by case basis.

The annual salary range for this full-time position is
$149,750—$168,500 USD

Benefits

Our full benefits & perks are explained in our handbook at remote.com/r/benefits. As a global company, each country works differently, but some benefits/perks are for all Remoters:
  • work from anywhere
  • flexible paid time off
  • flexible working hours (we are async)
  • 16 weeks paid parental leave
  • mental health support services
  • stock options
  • learning budget
  • home office budget & IT equipment
  • budget for local in-person social events or co-working spaces

How you’ll plan your day (and life)

We work async at Remote which means you can plan your schedule around your life (and not around meetings). Read more at remote.com/async.

You will be empowered to take ownership and be proactive. When in doubt you will default to action instead of waiting. Your life-work balance is important and you will be encouraged to put yourself and your family first, and fit work around your needs.

If that sounds like something you want, apply now!

How to apply

  1. Please fill out the form below and upload your CV with a PDF format.
  2. We kindly ask you to submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use here at Remote.
  3. If you don’t have an up to date CV but you are still interested in talking to us, please feel free to add a copy of your LinkedIn profile instead.

Not only do we encourage folks from all ethnic groups, genders, sexuality, age, abilities, disability status and any other under-represented group to apply, but we prioritize a sense of belonging. We have 4 ERGs (Women, Disability, Queer, Minorities in Tech) who meet regularly with the People team. During your interviews and beyond, we ask & encourage anybody who needs an accommodation to request one from their recruiter.

We will ask you to voluntarily tell us your pronouns at interview stage, and you will have the option to answer our anonymous demographic questionnaire when you apply below. As an equal employment opportunity employer it’s important to us that our workforce reflects people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences and this data will help us to stay accountable. We thank you for providing this data, if you chose to.

At Remote, we embrace AI as a valuable tool while prioritizing human creativity and authenticity. We look forward to meeting candidates who balance innovation with genuine expertise and experience. To learn more about Remote's AI guidelines check see here.

Please note we accept applications on an ongoing basis.

title: Finance Compliance Associate
description:
Привіт!
Ми OBRIO — команда, яка завжди хоче більшого: від себе, від життя, від роботи. Якщо тобі теж замало «достатньо добре» — ти вже розумієш, про що ми. Тут сильні люди притягують сильних — і разом ми розвиваємо компанію, що будує продукт №1 у ніші spiritual wellness — Nebula, бренд із 70M+ користувачів.

З вісьмох ентузіастів ми виросли у команду з 300+ професіоналів, які створюють рішення, що впливають на життя людей у всьому світі.
OBRIO — Lovemark brand з найвищим eNPS за версією Forbes.
За 7+ років наш продукт Nebula виріс з ідеї у платформу і став частиною життя мільйонів користувачів у понад 60 країнах.
На платформі працюють 1500+ експертів. Ми створили складну операційну модель і щодня обробляємо тисячі запитів від користувачів по всьому світу.
Амбіція OBRIO — стати першим українським юнікорном у ніші spirituality.

Зараз ми шукаємо Finance Compliance Associate.
Ти будуватимеш Finance & Compliance-напрямок, який допомагає компанії масштабуватися без втрати прозорості й контролю. Не просто готуватимеш звітність, а вдосконалюватимеш процеси, координуватимеш взаємодію з локальними адміністраторами й аудиторами та забезпечуватимеш відповідність фінансових операцій і внутрішніх процесів вимогам різних юрисдикцій.
У команді ти матимеш можливість попрацювати з Finance, Legal і Compliance-напрямками, а також долучатися до запуску нових юрисдикцій і розвитку фінансових процесів.
А твоїм безпосереднім менеджером буде Галина Стадницька, Head of Finance.

Які виклики тебе чекають?
Побудувати глибоку експертизу у Finance & Compliance, працюючи з фінансовими процесами та регуляторними вимогами в різних юрисдикціях.
Взяти ownership над ключовими процесами: від щоденної операційної роботи до їх автоматизації, стандартизації та масштабування.
Працювати з критично важливими фінансовими даними й безпосередньо впливати на прозорість, контроль і стійкість фінансової функції компанії.

Твій вплив:
У розвитку фінансової й compliance-функції компанії через прозору та своєчасну звітність.
У забезпеченні відповідності фінансових процесів вимогам різних юрисдикцій і успішному проходженні зовнішніх аудитів.
У трансформації Finance & Compliance-процесів через автоматизацію, стандартизацію та пошук ефективніших рішень.
У побудові Finance & Compliance-процесів для нових юрисдикцій, підтримуючи подальше масштабування бізнесу.

Що для нас важливо?
Маєш 3–5 років досвіду в аудиті, бухгалтерському обліку, фінансовому аналізі або суміжній сфері та добре розумієш, як працюють фінансова звітність і Finance & Compliance-процеси.
Впевнено працюєш в Excel. Досвід із VBA або SQL буде перевагою.
Маєш ступінь бакалавра або магістра в економіці, фінансах, бізнесі або суміжній галузі.
Володієш англійською на рівні Upper-Intermediate або вище — достатньому для роботи із документацією, локальними адміністраторами й аудиторами.
Любиш брати ownership: не чекаєш готових рішень, а самостійно рухаєш задачі від ідеї до результату.
Уважний(-а) до деталей, відповідально працюєш із фінансовими даними та цінуєш точність у роботі.
Умієш будувати партнерську комунікацію з різними стейкхолдерами — від внутрішніх команд до зовнішніх аудиторів.
Ти впізнаєш себе в цінностях OBRIO — GREAT
AI — вже частина того, як ти працюєш. У нас працюють з Claude · Cursor · CodeRabbit · OpenRouter · AWS Bedrock · NotebookLM · N8N · Gemini · OpenAI · HeyGen · Arcads · Krea · Runway · Kling · ElevenLabs

Що ти отримаєш в OBRIO?
Свободу у форматі роботи — можливість працювати з офісу в Києві, Львові, Варшаві або remote, з підтримкою надкомфортних умов у трьох містах.
Сильне середовище для зростання — 75% команди формують Middle та Senior спеціалісти, тож ти швидко зростаєш через складні задачі та сильних колег.
Підтримку фізичного та ментального ресурсу — участь в OBRIO Wellness Club або покриття індивідуальних занять спортом та Spiritual Allowance для твого балансу (дивись, як це працює в нас).
AI Workation — до 5 робочих днів на квартал, які ти можеш повністю присвятити створенню масштабованих AI-інструментів.
Час на відновлення — 20 оплачуваних днів відпустки, необмежені лікарняні та можливість sabbatical leave. Щоб досягати якнайкращих результатів потрібно якнайкраще почуватись.
Причетність до соціально важливих ініціатив — участь у благодійних проєктах, волонтерстві та підтримці мобілізованих колег і їхніх сімей. Один з наших проєктів OBRIO Innovation Space x КПІ нещодавно номінований на DOU Awards.
Інвестиції у твій професійний розвиток — окремий бюджет на навчання, менторство та участь у топових українських і міжнародних конференціях (IT Arena, DOU Days, Web Summit, TikTok & Meta Summits).
Круті колаборації — об'єднуємось з українськими брендами (СЕНС, Matcher, Syndicat, KURAZH) і даруємо стильний OBRIO-мерч не лише з нагоди святкових дат.

Інтро-кол з TA Specialist → Інтерв'ю з менеджером → Оцінка навичок → Бар-рейзинг / Фінальне інтерв'ю → Job Offer

У тестових завданнях є блок на використання AI — при оцінці ми спираємось на 4D Framework від Anthropic. Для нас важливе критичне й усвідомлене використання AI-інструментів.

Do Big. Be Great.

title: Parts Interpreter
description:
We have a great opportunity for a motivated Salesperson/ Parts Interpreter to join our experienced and friendly team in Caloundra. This role is perfect for someone who thrives on delivering outstanding customer service and enjoys being part of a fast‑paced, hands‑on environment.

You’ll be supporting our loyal customer base and working closely with a knowledgeable team who are passionate about what they do. Whether you bring prior Auto Parts experience or a genuine interest in learning the industry, you’ll hit the ground running with training and support behind you.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Inbound phone & online sales processing
  • In-store customer service
  • Order & receipt incoming stock
  • Process & pick purchase orders
  • Showroom merchandising, pricing & maintenance
  • Exceptional communication skills
  • A customer service champion
  • Experience or a passion for auto parts
  • Well presented
  • Great phone manner
  • Ability to influence & negotiate
  • Ability to translate technical problems to simple solutions
  • Background in European vehicles would be an advantage

Why join Bapcor?

At Bapcor, we focus on what matters most for our customers, team members and the communities we serve. We go the extra mile to get the job done and always live our values.

In joining our team, you can access:

  • Diverse career opportunities within the Bapcor Group
  • Discounts across Bapcor products and services, including our Retail and Trade brands
  • Industry leading policies to support your work/ life balance (gender neutral parental leave, purchase leave, volunteer leave etc…)
  • Paid birthday leave
  • Wellbeing support
  • Novated leasing and more

We pride ourselves on our inclusive work environment, and encourage applications from people of all ages, cultures, gender identity, abilities and backgrounds, providing a recruitment experience that’s fair, free from bias and accessible. If you need to inquire about reasonable accommodation for the application process, please email careers@bapcor.com

While we understand that recruitment agencies would love to offer their support, we kindly request all agencies to refrain from contacting us regarding this job posting. All applications should be submitted directly by candidates.

title: カスタマーサクセス・デプロイメントストラテジスト・Decarb Venture Studio, Mobility Service
description:
ウーブン・バイ・トヨタについて
ウーブン・バイ・トヨタは、トヨタの100年に一度のモビリティ・カンパニーへの変革に寄与していきます。「自分以外の誰かのために」発明を続けたという歴史からインスピレーションを受けた私たちのミッションーそれは、モビリティの定義を拡げ、そしてモビリティが社会に貢献する方法を拡張し、人を想うイノベーションで、モビリティの常識に挑み続けることです。

私たちは4つの柱を中心に活動しています：自動運転・先進運転支援システム技術のAD/ADAS、SDV（ソフトウェア定義車両）のための車両ソフトウェア生産プラットフォームであるArene、モビリティのテストコースであるWoven City、そして協業基盤を支えるデジタル・インフラストラクチャのCloud & AIです。また、ビジネスに不可欠な機能がこれらのチームの実装を下支えし、一丸となって「事故ゼロ社会」と「幸せの量産」の実現に向けて取り組んでいます。

=========================================================================

チームについて

トヨタは「移動とは何か」の再定義に取り組んでいます。人・モノ・情報・エネルギーの移動を進化させることで、新しいモビリティの開発に挑戦しています。Woven Cityは、A Living Laboratory™（生きた実験都市）、Human-Centered（人間中心）、Ever Evolving City™（常に進化し続ける都市）という3つのコアコンセプトを軸に、「すべての人の幸福の実現」という目的を目指すモビリティの実証の場です。
Woven Cityには未来のモビリティへの想いを共有する多様な仲間が集まります。Inventors（インベンターズ／発明家）、住民、ビジターは、ヒト・モビリティ・社会インフラが連携する街の形をしたテストコースで、生活をしながら様々なプロダクトやサービスを実証します。

Decarb Venture Studioは、Woven by Toyotaにおいて複数の新規サービスや事業を継続的に創出する組織です。私たちのミッションは「脱炭素の民主化」です。脱炭素を限られた専門家だけの業務から、あらゆる組織と人が手軽に参加できる実践的な活動へ広げることを目指しています。スタートアップのような裁量・スピード・創造性と、トヨタの事業規模・信頼・現実世界での検証機会を両立します。机上の仮説だけでプロダクトをつくるのではなく、複雑な実オペレーションから学び、再現可能なパターンを抽出し、トヨタの外にも広く展開できる汎用ソフトウェアへ昇華します。最初のサービスはPoCを終え、ベータ開発へ移行しています。電力・ガス・油・水など、顧客設備のインフラデータを統合するB2Bアプリケーションです。最先端のIoT、AI、ブロックチェーン、クラウド技術を活用してデータ基盤を構築すると同時に、産業用ソフトウェアであってもコンシューマーサービスのように直感的なUXを追求します。
Decarb Venture Studioは一つのプロダクトで終わりません。最初のサービスを有償化・スケールさせながら、顧客探索、PoC、プロトタイプを通じて次の事業の萌芽を継続的に生み出します。チームには国籍・言語・性別の多様性がある一方で、トヨタの文化に対する尊重も大切にしています。
働き方はハイブリッド（オフィスもしくはリモート）となります。今後の状況によっては、トヨタ自動車もしくはトヨタグループへの出張が発生する場合があります。
Woven Cityの詳細はこちらをご覧ください： https://www.woven-city.global/

求める人材

複雑な顧客環境に入り込み、現場観察、業務設計、プロダクト思考、導入、カスタマーサクセスを自在に行き来できる方を求めています。
顧客が語る内容だけでなく、実際にどのように仕事が行われているかを理解し、プロダクトが価値を生み出す上での業務・組織・行動上の障壁を特定します。
初回導入から継続利用までの道筋を設計します。顧客探索、成功条件、データ・センサ準備、オンボーディング、運用フロー、教育、サポート、価値測定、利用拡大までを一貫して担います。
一般的なアカウントマネジメントではありません。顧客価値を現場で実証し、個別顧客から得た知見を、再現可能なプロダクトとGo-To-Marketモデルへ変換する、ハンズオンな事業創出の役割です。

業務内容

  • 顧客の現実を理解する：現場調査を通じて業務・課題・機会を把握する
  • 導入を設計する：導入・業務設計・オンボーディング・運用準備をリードする
  • 利用定着を実現する：活用促進・カスタマーサクセス・継続改善を推進する
  • 再現性ある事業へ昇華する：顧客の学びを製品・Go-To-Market・運用モデルへ反映する
  • 次の機会を発見する：新たな顧客課題を探索し、次の事業機会を検証する

    • 必須条件

  • 顧客探索：B2Bソフトウェアにおいて、顧客探索、デプロイメント、導入、またはカスタマーサクセスを主導した経験
  • 複雑な業務の理解：複雑な顧客オペレーションを、再現可能な業務フローおよび製品要件へ変換できること
  • デプロイメントリーダーシップ：計画立案からオンボーディング、利用定着、運用移管まで、デプロイメント全体に責任を持った経験
  • 分析力と部門横断の推進力：高い分析力を有し、部門横断チームとともにPMFを推進した経験
  • コミュニケーションとオーナーシップ：文脈やニュアンスを理解できるネイティブレベル日本語力、チーム内で情報授受できる程度のビジネスレベル英語力、および不確実性の高いスタートアップ型の環境で主体的に成果を出せること

    • 歓迎条件

  • 産業ソフトウェア、製造業、エネルギー、Climate Tech、IoT、センサ導入、OT、施設運用に関する経験
  • 初期スタートアップ、創業期チーム、Venture Studio、コンサルティングチーム、新規事業組織などで、運用モデルをゼロから構築した経験
  • プロダクト分析と定性リサーチを活用し、オンボーディング、アクティベーション、継続率、顧客成果を改善した経験
  • 価格、支払意思、有料転換、エンタープライズソフトウェアの価値仮説を検証した経験
  • プレイブック、パートナーイネーブルメント、導入事例、教育資料、スケール可能なCSプロセスを構築した経験
  • データ連携、API、クラウドシステム、IoT機器、セキュリティ制約、導入リスクをエンジニアリングチームと議論できる技術的理解
  • 日本および海外のチーム、顧客、パートナーを横断して業務を進めた経験
    • =========================================================================
    注意事項
    ・通常、すべての面接はGoogle Meetで実施いたします。
    ・現在募集中の求人票は英語版と日本語版の両方で掲載しております。つきましては、いずれか一方のみにご応募いただきますようお願い申し上げます。
    ・ご応募の際には、できるだけ英文レジュメのご提出をお願いしておりますが、何らかの理由で日本語職務経歴書をご提出いただいた場合、ポジションによっては選考過程で英文レジュメの提出をお願いすることがございますので、あらかじめご了承ください。

    待遇・福利厚生
    ・経験に基づく業界水準に見合った給与
    ・勤務時間 ：フレキシブルな勤務時間
    ・年次有給休暇：年間20日（初年度は入社月により日数が異なる）
    ・私傷病休暇：年間6日（初年度は入社月により日数が異なる）
    ・休日：土日、祝日、その他当社が定めた日
    ・社会保険：健康保険、厚生年金保険、労災保険、雇用保険、介護保険
    ・住宅手当
    ・退職金制度
    ・レンタカーサポート
    ・社内研修制度（ソフトウェア学習・語学学習）

    私たちのコミットメント
    ・当社は機会均等な雇用を実現し、多様性を尊重しています。
    ・お預かりした個人情報は、採用および入社手続きにのみ使用いたします。詳細については、個人情報規約をご覧ください。

title: Key Account Manager F/H
description:
Nous recherchons pour notre équipe Développement Europe, un Responsable Grands Comptes (Key Account Manager - KAM) F/H dans le cadre d'un CDI situé à Paris Montparnasse qui viendra renforcer une équipe composée de 5 commerciaux grands comptes.

Il est attendu du KAM :

  • Gestion des partenaires en portefeuille sur la zone Italie principalement (captives, financiers indépendants, constructeurs automobiles, sociétés de leasing, …).
  • Être l’interlocuteur privilégié de nos clients grands comptes dans la gestion des différents accords nationaux :
    • Animer et représenter Covéa Affinity auprès de nos clients,
    • Prospecter des nouveaux clients en fonction des cibles stratégiques,
    • Développer et fidéliser un portefeuille de clients grands comptes sur l’ensemble de l’offre,
    • Entretenir, fidéliser, développer et animer un réseau local d’apporteurs bien positionnés auprès de nos cibles,
    • Accompagner et conseiller les clients/prospects dans l'étude de leurs besoins,
    • Assurer le suivi de la bonne exécution des contrats afin de maximiser la satisfaction client,
    • Répondre aux appels d’offres en local et participer aux appels d’offres pan européens,
    • Préparer, animer et suivre les réunions qualité, les comités de suivi, les comités de projet, pilotage etc. de votre portefeuille
    • Participer aux travaux et projets internes : conduite des projets commerciaux, reportings…

Déplacements prévus en Italie (Milan - Turin - Rome) : 2 fois par mois en moyenne

Profil :

  • Italien et français courant, anglais apprécié
  • Connaissance du milieu automotive
  • Profil commercial et gestion de partenariats grands comptes
  • Expérience a minima de 5 ans sur un poste similaire

Vos qualités :

  • Sens de l'écoute,
  • Capacité à transformer des besoin détectés en opportunités,
  • Capacité à gérer des négociations complexes,
  • capacité à décliner le plan d'actions stratégiques en actions concrètes, culture du résultats
  • Esprit d'équipe et d'entraide

Les avantages qui font la différence :

  • Salaire Fixe + Bonus
  • 13ème mois et la prime de vacances mensualisés,
  • Primes d’Intéressement / de Participation liées au groupe Covéa,
  • Formation et accompagnement riche et complet,
  • Télétravail possible jusqu’à 45 jours par an (mis en œuvre selon votre degré d’autonomie),
  • Mutuelle avantageuse + régime sur complémentaire santé (prise en charge employeur 60%),
  • De nombreux avantages CE (chèque vacances, voyages, …),
  • Forfait jours : 200/an + 31 jours de congés payés + différentes formules de RTT possibles,

title: Robotics Co-Founder / Head of Product (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

  • You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Robotics

  • You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management

  • You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs

  • You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding

  • You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively

  • You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate

  • You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues

  • You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: Room Attendant - Night
description:
Pullman at Sydney Olympic Park is seeking reliable and enthusiastic night shift Room Attendants to join our Housekeeping Team. Reporting directly to the Executive Housekeeper, you will play an important role in ensuring our guests enjoy a comfortable, welcoming and memorable stay.

This is a casual night position, working 5 days per week from 6:30pm to 8:30pm.

As a night shift Room Attendant, you will be responsible for maintaining the cleanliness, presentation and comfort of our guest rooms. You will carry out evening housekeeping duties, including turndown service, while ensuring all areas meet Pullman brand standards.

What you’ll be doing:

  • Service and refresh guest rooms to the highest hotel standards, ensuring there is no history of pre occupation.
  • Carry out evening turndown service.
  • Report maintenance issues and repair requirements promptly and as per hotel procedure.
  • Maintain housekeeping storerooms and trolleys in a clean, organised and well stocked condition
  • Complete deep cleaning tasks as required
  • Follow hotel procedure for lost property
  • Ensure the security of guest rooms, keys and hotel property at all times
  • Safely use and store cleaning chemicals in accordance with WHS requirements

We're looking for someone who is trustworthy, dependable and takes pride in delivering quality work every shift.

To be successful in this role, you will have:

  • Previous housekeeping, room attendant or cleaning experience in a hotel or similar environment.
  • Excellent attention to detail and strong time management skills.
  • A positive attitude and the ability to work independently.
  • A high level of trustworthiness, honesty and reliability
  • Strong communication skills with fluent spoken English.
  • Comfortable with the physical aspects of the role including tasks such as walking, lifting, pushing, pulling, bending and kneeling.
  • Flexibility to work evenings, weekends, public holidays and major event periods when required
  • Full Australian working rights

Why work for Accor?
We are far more than a worldwide leader. We welcome you as you are and you can find a job and brand that matches your personality. We support you to grow and learn every day, making sure that work brings purpose to your life, so that during your journey with us, you can continue to explore Accor’s limitless possibilities. With 5400+ Hotels across 110+ countries, the opportunities to grow your career are endless!

  • ALL Heartist Membership - Take advantage of discounts on accommodation and food & beverage worldwide!
  • Family & Friends Discounts - Available on a range of accommodation, services and events for those who mean the most to you
  • Accor Live Limitless (ALL) Loyalty Program - Earn status & reward points on your worldwide stays
  • Learn Your Way - Support your career development with access to Accor’s industry leading training platforms, Accor Academy and Typsy
  • Build Your Network - With more than 300,000 Accor professionals globally sharing the same guest passion, you have the ability to grow your hospitality network worldwide

Why Join the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct?

  • Be part of a collaborative, multi-brand precinct team in a dynamic location.
  • Enjoy exclusive staff benefits such as $5 duty meals, complimentary coffee and free parking on non major event days.
  • Be part of a vibrant, high energy precinct with Sydney's biggest events right on your doorstep.

title: Senior Analyst Reconciliation / Financial Controls
description:
Це ключова роль у напрямку фінансового контролю, аудиту розрахунків та виявлення фінансових ризиків. Спеціаліст відповідатиме за аналіз розбіжностей у платіжній інфраструктурі, пошук їх першопричин, контроль коректності фінансових потоків і побудову ефективної системи reconciliation.

Вимоги
Вища освіта (фінанси, економіка, бухгалтерський облік або аудит).
Досвід роботи від 5 років у напрямках Reconciliation, Financial Control або Internal Audit.
Мінімум 2–3 роки досвіду у FinTech, Payments, P2P, Crypto або High-Risk Acquiring.
Практичний досвід аналізу та розслідування фінансових розбіжностей, а не лише підготовки звітності.
Глибоке розуміння процесів P2P, механіки формування трейдерської маржі та життєвого циклу платежів (Authorization → Clearing → Settlement → Reconciliation).
Сильна база в бухгалтерському обліку: подвійний запис, проводки, звірки, міжкомпанійні розрахунки.
Просунутий рівень Excel (зведені таблиці, складні формули, аналіз великих масивів даних).
Досвід роботи з великими обсягами фінансових даних.
Аналітичне мислення, уважність до деталей, незалежність у прийнятті рішень.
Буде перевагою:
Досвід роботи з криптовалютними продуктами, PSP, Forex, Gambling або Betting.
Досвід виявлення фінансових маніпуляцій або шахрайських схем.
Робота з BI-інструментами (Power BI, Tableau, Metabase).
Сертифікації ACCA, CIMA, CPA або CIA.
Обов'язки
Проведення аудиту фінансових розрахунків, P2P-потоків та трейдерської маржі.
Контроль коректності взаєморозрахунків між процесингом, банками, PSP, EMI, криптогаманцями та внутрішніми системами.
Аналіз і розслідування фінансових розбіжностей, визначення їх першопричин та оцінка впливу на бізнес.
Виявлення фінансових аномалій, потенційних маніпуляцій і ризикових операцій.
Контроль комісій, взаємозаліків, settlement-процесів, залишків на рахунках і collateral.
Розробка контрольних механізмів, автоматизація reconciliation-процесів та налаштування системи алертів.
Підготовка аналітичної звітності, рекомендацій щодо усунення системних ризиків та підвищення ефективності фінансового контролю.

title: TechBio Co-Founder / CFO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

description:
Die GBB Gesellschaft für berufliche Bildung mbH sucht einen/eine Psychologen*in für unser einzigartiges Projekt "Startklar"!
Unser Projekt zielt darauf ab, junge Erwachsene, die soziale oder psychische Schwierigkeiten haben, durch kreative-Projekte zu erreichen und zu unterstützen. Gemeinsam entwickeln wir nachhaltige Projekte und fördern die Selbstwirksamkeit und Integration unserer Teilnehmer*innen.

Deine Benefits

  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten, Sabbatical oder Jobsharing möglich
  • Familienfreundliche Regelungen bei erkrankten Kindern (Ausgleichszahlung von Kinderkrankengeld zu Nettoentgeld bis zu 10 Tage im Jahr)
  • Internes Gehaltsband: Steigendes Gehalt bei fortdauernder Betriebszugehörigkeit
  • Wir übernehmen einen Großteil deiner Urban Sports Club Flatrate

  • Regelmäßige Supervision

  • Entwicklungs- und Karrieremöglichkeiten
  • Viele Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten, flache Hierarchien
  • Corporate Benefits - Zugang
  • Teamevents, wie Sommerfest und Teamtage
  • Hunde sind willkommen
  • Gehaltsband auch für Annerkennungsjahr
  • Firmeneigene Betriebssozialarbeiterin

Deine Aufgaben
  • Aufsuchende und mobile Sozialarbeit mit jungen Erwachsenen, die soziale oder psychische Schwierigkeiten haben
  • Intensive Beziehungsarbeit zur Unterstützung der Teilnehmer*innen in ihrer individuellen Lebenssituation
  • Sozialpädagogische Diagnostik und Entwicklung individueller Hilfepläne
  • Hilfe zur Selbsthilfe – Empowerment der Teilnehmer*innen, um ihre beruflichen und persönlichen Ziele zu erreichen
  • Verweisberatung und Vermittlung an weiterführende Unterstützungsangebote
  • Organisation und Durchführung von nachhaltigen und kreativen Projekten
  • Gestaltung und Leitung von sozialpädagogischen Gruppenangeboten
Abgeschlossenes Ausbildung in einem der folgenden Fächer:
  • Masterabschluss und mind. 1 Jahr Berufserfahrung
  • Empathie und Professionalität bei der Kontaktaufnahme mit jungen Erwachsenen in schwierigen Lebenslagen
  • Wertneutralität und Verbindlichkeit
  • Motivationsfähigkeit und Fähigkeit zur Selbstmotivation
  • Flexibilität und Gestaltungswille
  • Bereitschaft zur engen Zusammenarbeit mit Netzwerkpartner*innen, Ämtern und Behörden
  • Interkulturelle Kompetenzen und Sensibilität im Umgang mit jungen Erwachsenen mit Migrationshintergrund
Warum Wir?

Unser Ziel ist es die Lebensqualität unserer Adressat*innen zu verbessern. Wir bieten ihnen professionelle und individuelle Unterstützung, um ein selbstbestimmtes Leben zu fördern und ihre vorhandenen Ressourcen und Talente zu stärken.

Dies gelingt uns durch vielfältige Hilfsangebote und die hervorragende Arbeit unserer multiprofessionellen Teams aus Sozialpädagog *innen, Coaches, Anleiter *innen und Psycholog *innen.
In unserem Arbeitsumfeld schaffen wir Raum für Mitgestaltung und kreative Lösungen und legen Wert auf flache Hierarchien, eine positive Fehlerkultur und vertrauensvolle Teamarbeit.

Wenn du Freude daran hast, andere Menschen zu unterstützen und Teil eines engagierten und multiprofessionellen Team sein möchtest, das nachhaltig positive Veränderungen bewirkt, dann bist du bei uns genau richtig!

Gehalt: ab 3.540 € pro Monat

Haben wir dein Interesse geweckt? Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung!

title: Principal Data Scientist - RPL
description:
About the Role
We’re looking for a Principal Data Scientist to lead the science behind our voucher and incentive programs across food delivery, ride-hailing, and logistics. Our promotional spend reaches millions of consumers weekly, and every rupiah is allocated by models you’ll help design.
This is a hands-on technical leadership role. You’ll set the scientific direction for causal modeling across the incentives portfolio, mentor senior data scientists, and partner closely with product, engineering, and business teams to move key efficiency metrics while growing transactions and retention.
If you're excited about proving what's actually incremental — and setting the causal bar for an org that moves real money on your answers — this role is for you.

What You Will Do

  • Own the end-to-end modelling and estimation behind promotion optimisation — elasticity, heterogeneous treatment effects, budget-constrained allocation, and incrementality — from problem framing to production.
  • Advance our causal inference stack: experiment and quasi-experiment design (geo/switchback tests, holdouts, diff-in-diff, synthetic control), debiasing observational data, and variance reduction — raising the bar on experimentation rigor across the team.
  • Design and productionize heterogeneous treatment effect (uplift) models at scale (tens of millions of users), with honest offline evaluation (uplift/Qini curves, policy-value estimation).
  • Formulate and solve budget-constrained allocation under fairness and dynamic business constraints — from LP/MILP to greedy or Lagrangian methods where they scale better.
  • Mentor and technically guide a team of data scientists; set standards for model evaluation, documentation, and scientific review.
  • Partner with business to turn model outputs into budget decisions, and communicate tradeoffs (subsidy efficiency vs. growth) clearly.

    • What You Will Need

  • 8+ years in data science or ML, with 3+ years focused on causal inference or uplift modeling in production settings
  • Deep expertise in heterogeneous treatment effect estimation, with hands-on production experience in several of: meta-learners (S/T/X/R), causal forests, DR-learner, or deep uplift architectures
  • Hands-on experience optimizing promotions, pricing, or marketing incentives with evolving constraints and measurable business outcomes
  • Strong grounding in experimentation and observational causal methods — propensity weighting, instrumental variables, synthetic control, difference-in-differences
  • Experience with constrained optimization (MILP, Lagrangian methods, etc.) applied to resource allocation
  • Proficiency in Python and SQL; shipping models to production with engineering partners
  • Track record of technical leadership at principal/staff level: setting technical direction for a team, reviewing high-stakes analyses, and influencing roadmaps and partner teams without direct authority\
  • Experience in marketplaces, ride-hailing, food delivery, e-commerce, or fintech

    • Nice to Have

  • Familiarity with off-policy evaluation, bandits, or reinforcement learning for sequential incentive decisions
  • Publications or open-source contributions in causal ML (e.g., work building on EconML, CausalML, or the uplift literature)
  • Experience operating across multiple markets/geographies in Southeast Asia

    • About the Team

    You'll join the Budget Efficiency Data Science team, a group of data scientists focused on making Gojek's promotions and incentives spend as effective as possible across our core services. We combine machine learning, causal inference, and optimization to make sure our budget drives real, measurable impact for the business and our customers. We value rigor, collaboration, and turning complex problems into decisions that leaders can act on. Our mission is to make every rupiah of investment count — and we're excited for you to be part of it.
    About GoTo Group
    GoTo is the largest digital ecosystem in Indonesia. GoTo's mission is to 'empower progress' by offering technology infrastructure and solutions that help everyone to access and thrive in the digital economy.
    The GoTo ecosystem provides a wide range of services, including mobility, delivery, payments, financial services, and technology solutions for merchants. The ecosystem also provides e-commerce services through Tokopedia and banking services through its partnership with Bank Jago.

    About Gojek
    Gojek is Southeast Asia’s leading on-demand platform and pioneer of the multi-service ecosystem with over 2.5 million driver partners across the regions offering a wide range of services such as transportation, food delivery, logistics and more. With its mission to create impact at scale, Gojek is committed to resolving consumer problems and raising standards of living by connecting consumers to the best providers of goods and services in the market.

    About GoTo Financial
    GoTo Financial accelerates financial inclusion through its leading financial services and merchants solutions. Its consumer services include GoPay and GoPayLater and serve businesses of all sizes through Midtrans, Moka, GoBiz Plus, GoBiz, and Selly. With its trusted and inclusive ecosystem of products, GoTo Financial is open to new growth opportunities and aims to empower everyone to Make It Happen, Make It Together, Make It Last.

    GoTo and its business units, including Gojek and GoToFinancial ("GoTo") only post job opportunities on our official channels on our respective company websites and on LinkedIn. GoTo is not liable for any job postings or job offers that did not originate from us. You should conduct your own due diligence to prevent being victims of any fake job scams, if they did not originate from GoTo's official recruitment channels.



    #LI-HYBRID

title: Senior Employee Relations Advisor
description:
• Love what you do and leave your mark
  • Support our leaders as a trusted partner, navigating complex workplace matters
  • Be based in either Sydney, or Melbourne

As a Senior Employee Relations Advisor within the Team Services team, you will act as a trusted partner and escalation point for Endeavour Group leaders navigating complex workplace matters.

In this role, you will lead end-to-end grievance investigations and translate employment legislation and internal policies into robust, commercially pragmatic recommendations.

Beyond delivering expert, day-to-day case management, you will play a pivotal role in shaping our wider People strategy by championing continuous improvement projects and coaching a dedicated team of ER Advisors to elevate our collective capability.

Sound good? Read on.
Here's a snapshot of the responsibilities:

  • Execute the day-to-day operational delivery of employee relations case management and lead end-to-end grievance investigation services across several regions.
  • Deliver strong, supportive leadership across ER team members within the Team Services team, providing coaching and development support to junior ER advisors.
  • Represent the business in claims across external jurisdictions (such as the Fair Work Commission and Human Rights Commissions) and in interactions with external employee representative bodies like the SDA.
  • Support and guide the business with industrial disputes and workplace issues outside of standard bargaining processes.
  • Develop and deliver performance management training programs to emerging leaders across the business.
  • Interpret and deliver data insights, trends, and strategic opportunities to Senior Operations stakeholders.
  • Support business-wide projects and strategic initiatives, including bargaining, compliance, and employment governance.

Now let’s talk about you:

  • 5-7 years of experience in Human Resources or Employee Relations.
  • Demonstrable experience managing complex HR/ER matters and a proven track record of leading a high-performing team of professionals.
  • You possess sound knowledge and deep understanding of relevant ER legislation across different jurisdictions and its practical application within company policy.
  • Demonstrate a clear capacity to provide commercial, risk-managed, yet fast and simple HR/ER solutions to Line Managers and business units.
  • You hold excellent negotiation and influencing skills to engage collaboratively with a wide range of internal and external stakeholders and customers.
  • Tertiary qualifications in HR, ER, Employment Law, or a relevant field (and/or equivalent significant practical experience).
  • Previous experience leading large teams in service delivery environments is highly regarded.

The benefits are good too!

  • An exclusive discount card for our ALH Hotels pubs & accommodation, BWS, Dan Murphy’s and other Endeavour Group brands. Through our partnership with Woolworths, you'll also enjoy discounts at Woolies supermarkets and BIG W
  • As a valued member of the team, your health and wellbeing is our top priority. You will have access to a range of free services to help you live well and support your physical, mental and financial wellbeing
  • Endeavour Group is full of opportunities - use our dedicated learning and development options to grow an idea, yourself, and your career. This is just the start, so dream big
  • Find out more about our range of benefits and discounts here

At Endeavour, we value being a workplace where everyone’s welcome - if you meet a number of the requirements (and not all), we encourage you to apply.

We are together creators

With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, Endeavour Group is big on sociability. Together we create the moments that bring millions of people together. And together we have more fun, create more opportunities, and score a lot more goals. We’re serious about creating a safe, inclusive and fun place to rock up to where equal opportunity is key, and flexibility is part of how we roll.

We’re all about creating a more sociable future - for our customers and each other. If this job excites you - and you’re close-enough on the requirements, reach out, we’d love to hear from you.

You can learn more about working with us on LinkedIn or at endeavourgroupcareers.com.au.

Our Talent Team and Hiring Leaders kindly request no unsolicited resumes or approaches from Recruitment Agencies. Endeavour Group is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

#WeAreTogetherCreators #ComeAsYouAre #DreamBig #FeelTheEnergy #LeaveYourMark #EndeavourGroup

title: Data Scientist
description:
Data Science UA is a service company with deep expertise in AI and Data Science. Our story began in 2016 with the first Data Science UA Conference in Kyiv, and since then we've built one of the largest AI communities in Europe.

About the role:
We are looking for a Data Scientist to design and build data-driven and machine-learning capabilities for clients products - a global leader in Advertising Resource Management.
You will work with Product, Engineering, and domain experts to translate business questions into analytically sound agentic solutions. The role covers the full data science lifecycle: problem definition, data exploration, experimentation, modelling, evaluation, and production-readiness assessment.

Responsibilities:
- Agentic Solutions & AI Orchestration (Core Focus)
- Autonomous Workflow Design: Architect, build, and deploy autonomous and semi-autonomous AI agents (using modern LLM orchestration, tool-use, and multi-agent frameworks) to automate complex, multi-step media management workflows.
- Tool-Using Agents: Develop agents capable of interacting directly with client's internal tools and external APIs (e.g., DSPs, ad servers, ERPs) to execute actions, run diagnostics, and fetch real-time campaign data.
- Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) Guardrails: Design robust evaluation, permissioning, and safety frameworks to ensure agentic decisions—especially those involving live budget allocations or financial adjustments—operate safely with appropriate human oversight.
- Media Planning & Predictive Intelligence
- Agent-Driven Media Planning: Build predictive frameworks (e.g., MMM, reach & frequency forecasting) and feed them into autonomous planning agents that can generate, critique, and optimize cross-channel media plans automatically.
- Scenario Simulation: Develop models that allow AI agents to simulate "what-if" budget scenarios, channel mixes, and KPI trade-offs to present optimized strategies to media planners.
- Autonomous Buying & In-Flight Optimization
- Real-Time Execution Agents: Create autonomous optimization agents that continuously monitor in-flight campaign performance, automatically reallocating budgets, adjusting pacing, and executing "next-best-action" optimizations.
- Multi-Platform Analytics: Model performance trends across programmatic platforms, direct publisher buys, and walled gardens to feed real-time decision loops for buying agents.
- Agentic Financial Reconciliation & Audit
- Automated Audit Agents: Build autonomous auditing agents that cross-reference planned spend, ad server delivery logs, publisher invoices, and contract terms to detect discrepancies and flag billing leakage.
- Anomaly & Leakage Detection: Design predictive algorithms for automated discrepancy resolution, overspend prevention, and financial clearing integrations (e.g., SAP, ERPs).
- Technical Leadership & Agent Governance
- Agent Observability & Evaluation: Establish team standards for agent benchmarking, prompt/tool evaluation, latency optimization, and failure mode analysis in production environments.
- Mentorship & Strategy: Mentor data scientists on modern LLM architectures, agentic design patterns (RAG, multi-agent systems, tool execution), and production deployment best practices.

Requirements:
- Agentic AI & Modern LLM Architecture
- Agentic Frameworks: 3+ years of Data Science / ML experience, with hands-on expertise building and deploying production-grade agentic architectures using frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, or LlamaIndex.
- Tool Use & Function Calling: Proven experience implementing function calling, tool use, structured outputs (e.g., Pydantic/Instructor), and multi-agent coordination.
- LLM Engineering & RAG: Deep practical understanding of prompt engineering, fine- tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), context management, and vector database architectures (e.g., Qdrant, Pinecone, Milvus, PGVector).
- AI Guardrails & HITL: Experience designing safety mechanisms, rate/cost controls, human-in-the-loop (HITL) approval gates, and deterministic fallbacks for autonomous agent execution.
- Machine Learning, Statistics & Optimization
- Classical ML & Optimization: Proficiency in supervised/unsupervised ML (Scikit- learn, XGBoost/LightGBM) and mathematical optimization (e.g., SciPy, Pyomo, Google OR-Tools) for resource allocation, pricing, and budget pacing algorithms.
- Time Series & Anomaly Detection: Demonstrated experience building forecasting models (Prophet, ARIMA, NeuralProphet) and anomaly detection frameworks for automated financial/data audit workflows.

Will be a plus:
- Master’s degree or PhD in a quantitative field.
- Experience in advertising, marketing technology, or media optimisation.
- Experience with causal inference, uplift modelling, or marketing measurement.
- Familiarity with AWS, ETL processes, Docker, CI/CD, and model-monitoring tools.

title: Steuerberater (m/w/d) in Schopp, auf Wunsch mit Partnerperspektive, gesucht - mindestens 90.000€
description:
TaxTalente ist die Nr. 1 Job-Matching Plattform für Jobsuchende in der Steuerberatung.

Sie suchen eine positive berufliche Veränderung? Sie möchten den nächsten Schritt in Ihrer Karriere gehen und streben möglicherweise sogar eine Partnerschaft in einer zukunftsorientierten Kanzlei an? Sie möchten endlich wieder in einem positiven Arbeitsumfeld arbeiten, in dem Sie finanziell und persönlich wertgeschätzt werden? Sie suchen nach Tätigkeiten, die Sie auch wirklich weiterbringen?

Sofern Sie sich in mindestens einem der genannten Punkte wiederfinden, sollten Sie sich bei uns melden. Wir haben die attraktivsten Arbeitgeber der Steuerberatungsbranche in unserem Netzwerk und bringen zusammen, was zusammengehört. Grenzenlose Entwicklungschancen mit Partnermöglichkeiten, vielschichtige Tätigkeitsbereiche, Gehälter wie in der Industrie und vieles mehr – melden Sie sich bei uns und wir unterstützen Sie auf Ihrem weiteren Karriereweg!

Derzeit suchen wir nach einem Steuerberater (m/w/d) für eine unserer Partner-Kanzleien in Schopp

Bei Rückfragen kannst du dich gerne jederzeit (auch nach deinem Feierabend) direkt telefonisch an Herrn Becker-Koch über folgende Nummer wenden: 015901431130

Aufgaben

Wir bringen zusammen, was zusammengehört. Dabei spielt das passende Tätigkeitsprofil natürlich eine entscheidende Rolle. Bei unserer Partnerkanzlei können Sie sich Ihren Aufgabenbereich auf Basis Ihrer persönlichen Stärken und Schwächen selbst zusammenstellen.

  • Erstellung und Review von Jahresabschlüssen
  • Erstellung von Steuererklärungen für Personen- und Kapitalgesellschaften
  • Steuerliche Sonderberatungsprojekte auf Basis individueller QualifikationenB. in den Bereichen der Erbschaftsteuer oder des Umwandlungssteuerrechts
  • Begleitung von Betriebsprüfungen
  • Teamleitung und Personalverantwortung (optional)
  • Weitere individuell wählbare Tätigkeitsbereiche, die Ihnen Freude bringen

Qualifikation

Am Ende sollte es für beide Seiten passen. Deshalb wäre es gut, wenn Sie sich in den folgenden Punkten wiederfinden.

  • Kommunikative Art und Freude am Austausch mit Mandanten
  • Sorgfältige, effiziente und gewissenhafte Arbeitsweise
  • Offen für eine digitale Arbeitsweise
  • Führungsverständnis und Verantwortungsbewusstsein gegenüber Mitarbeitern (optional)
  • Ein erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Steuerberaterexamen
  • Leidenschaft für steuerliche Themen

Benefits

Sie haben genug von 0815 Benefits, von denen Sie nicht wirklich profitieren? Dann sollten Sie einen Blick auf die nachfolgenden Benefits werfen.

  • Außerordentliche Vergütung von 90.000 € bis 130.000 € / Jahr, auf Wunsch auch Partnerperspektive
  • Flexible Gestaltung der Arbeitszeiten sowie die Option auf Home-Office
  • Nettolohnoptimierung mit monatlichen 50,00€ Sachbezugsgutscheinen
  • Umfangreiche Unterstützung bei Weiterbildungsmaßnahmen wie z.B. Fachberater Qualifikationen
  • Modernste Ausstattung mit digitalen Prozessen
  • Starker Teamzusammenhalt und regelmäßige Teamevents

Schnell, unkompliziert und effizient Ihren Traumjob finden – ein Versprechen an Sie, dass wir aufgrund unseres selektiven, breit gefächerten und deutschlandweiten Kanzleinetzwerks halten werden. lhre Vorteile mit uns auf einen Blick:

  • Sie führen keine unsinnigen Bewerbungsgespräche mehr, bei denen Sie während des Bewerbungsprozesses merken, dass es (bspw. wegen des Gehalts) sowieso nicht passt - wir klären vor Ihrem Bewerbungsgespräch all Ihre mit uns besprochenen Kriterien mit der Kanzlei ab.
  • Wir führen Ihre Gehaltsverhandlungen und kennen die Gehaltsstrukturen der Branche dabei bestens.
  • 100% Anonymität – solange Sie uns nicht das „Go“ geben und einem Kennenlernen mit Ihrem neuen potentiellen Arbeitgeber zustimmen, erfährt niemand von Ihrem Namen oder Ihren bisherigen Arbeitgebern – Ihre Ihnen wichtigen Kriterien können wir auch zunächst anonym mit unseren Partnerkanzleien klären.
  • Attraktive Kanzleien – die Kanzleien in unserem breit gefächerten Kanzleinetzwerk sind selektiv ausgewählt und heben sich klar von “normalen” Kanzleien ab (z.B. hinsichtlich der Vergütung, Benefits, Hierarchien, Digitalisierungsgrad, Ausstattung etc.).
  • Und das Beste: Unser gesamter Service ist unverbindlich und völlig kostenfrei für Sie.

title: Aprio PH - US Estimator (Cost Segregation)
description:

Aprio has 30 U.S. office locations, one in the Philippines and more than 2,100 team members that speak 60+ languages across the globe. Discover a top-rated culture, vast growth opportunities and your next big career move with Aprio.

title: Database Security Consultant
description:
About Capco

Capco is a global technology and business consultancy focused on the financial services and energy sectors. We combine deep industry expertise with innovative thinking to help our clients transform their businesses, modernize their technology landscapes, and deliver exceptional customer experiences.

At Capco, you will work in an entrepreneurial environment where collaboration, innovation, and continuous learning are part of our DNA. Our consultants contribute to high-impact transformation programs across leading financial institutions.

About the Role

Capco is looking for a Database Security Consultant to strengthen our cybersecurity and technology consulting capabilities.

In this role, you will help clients protect critical databases and data platforms across on-premises, cloud and hybrid environments. You will work on topics such as database hardening, identity and access management, encryption, monitoring and secure-by-design engineering.

You combine a solid technical foundation with a consulting mindset. You enjoy analysing security challenges, translating risks into practical improvements and working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. You are motivated to further deepen your expertise in database and cloud security.

What You’ll Do

  • Assess and improve the security of SQL, NoSQL and cloud-native database platforms.
  • Implement security standards for hardening, configuration, patching and access management.
  • Strengthen access controls based on role-based access and least-privilege principles.
  • Support the secure management of service accounts, secrets, certificates and cryptographic keys.
  • Contribute to encryption, database logging, auditing and security monitoring.
  • Integrate database security events with SIEM and SOC platforms.
  • Embed security into provisioning, deployment, backup, recovery and data migration processes.
  • Collaborate with database administrators, platform teams, cloud architects and security specialists.
  • Translate security risks into clear and practical recommendations.

What You’ll Bring

  • A first proven professional experience in cybersecurity, database engineering, cloud security, platform security or a related field.
  • Hands-on experience with SQL and/or NoSQL database technologies.
  • Experience working in on-premises, cloud or hybrid environments.
  • Knowledge of at least one cloud platform, such as Microsoft Azure, AWS or Google Cloud.
  • Practical knowledge of identity and access management, least privilege, encryption, logging or monitoring.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving and communication skills.
  • A proactive, collaborative and delivery-focused mindset.
  • Professional proficiency in English.

Knowledge of Dutch is a strong advantage.

Why Join Capco?

  • Work on cybersecurity, data and cloud transformation initiatives at leading financial institutions.
  • Join a global consultancy with an entrepreneurial culture and a strong focus on innovation.
  • Collaborate with experts across cybersecurity, data, cloud, architecture and technology.
  • Access continuous learning, certifications and career development opportunities.
  • Enjoy a flexible hybrid working model and a people-first environment.
  • Benefit from an attractive remuneration package reflecting your experience, including a company car or green mobility budget, net allowances, an end-of-year bonus, health insurance, meal vouchers, a laptop, and a mobile phone with subscription.

Be Yourself at Work

We are committed to creating an inclusive environment where everyone is encouraged to be themselves.

We value different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives, and believe that diversity strengthens our teams and improves the solutions we deliver for our clients.

Join Capco and develop your career at the intersection of cybersecurity, data and technology.

title: Senior Java Developer (m/w/d) - Konstanz, Berlin oder remote
description:
Du bist überzeugt, dass innovative Technologie und Software gesellschaftlich relevante Themen unterstützt, verändert und vorantreibt? Das sind wir auch!

Unsere Kund*innen sind vielfältig. Unsere Software Projekte ebenso.
Wir lassen die großen Visionen unserer Kund*innen Wirklichkeit werden und helfen ihnen dabei die Digitalisierung in Deutschland voranzutreiben. Dabei setzen wir auf unsere Begeisterung für Software-Engineering, Open Source und agiles Mindset.

  • Du denkst dich mit Begeisterung in die fachlichen Fragestellungen unserer Kund*innen ein und entwickelst hochqualitative und skalierbare Softwarelösungen.
  • Du implementierst User Stories in Java, optimierst bestehenden Code und bringst dein tiefgreifendes Verständnis für Clean Code Praktiken ein.
  • Du entwickelst und optimierst kontinuierlich automatisierte Testszenarien, um eine hohe Qualität des Codes zu gewährleisten.
  • Du führst Code Reviews durch, arbeitest im Pair Programming und förderst aktiv den Know-how-Transfer im Team.
  • Du treibst die Weiterentwicklung und Optimierung der im Team etablierten Continuous Integration / Continuous Delivery Pipeline voran, um Effizienz und Qualität nachhaltig zu maximieren.
  • Ein abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Informatik oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation mit mehrjähriger Berufserfahrung in der Softwareentwicklung
  • Umfassende Expertise in der Entwicklung komplexer Anwendungen unter Einsatz aktueller Java-Frameworks wie Spring Boot
  • Erfahrung mit Kubernetes oder Docker in einer Cloud Umgebung
  • Gute Kenntnisse in Containerisierungstechnologien und -praktiken sowie Erfahrung mit CI/CD Werkzeugen wie Jenkins oder GitLab
  • Lust auf agiles Arbeiten, hohe Eigeninitiative und eine starke Motivation, dich kontinuierlich mit neuen Technologien und Trends auseinanderzusetzen.
  • Praktische Erfahrung im Einsatz von KI-Tools in der Softwareentwicklung und die Fähigkeit, diese gezielt und kritisch in den Entwicklungsalltag zu integrieren.
  • Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1) und Kommunikationsfähigkeiten, um effektiv im Team und mit Kund*innen zu interagieren.
  • Du arbeitest mit namhaften Kund*innen an gesellschaftlich relevanten und innovativen Projekten.
  • Du bist Teil eines selbstorganisierten Teams im agilen Umfeld mit hoher Eigenverantwortung.
  • Durch das breite Technologiespektrum und die Vielfältigkeit unserer Projekte und Kund*innen hast du die Möglichkeit, dich stetig weiterzuentwickeln.
  • Du trittst in ein wachsendes Unternehmen mit langfristiger Perspektive ein.
  • Um optimale Arbeitsbedingungen zu ermöglichen, setzen wir uns für dich und deine Familie ein.
  • Umfangreiche Benefits runden unser Angebot ab.
