title: Research Fellow (Physics)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS Career Portal. Please note your application will only be processed if you apply via NUS Career Portal.

NUS Career Portal link https://careers.nus.edu.sg/job/Research-Fellow-%28Physics%29/33827-en_GB/?st=7452C4667885822BFC190B3D2741F0E5B1E6FBEB

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Job Description

The successful candidate will work with Zhang Chun on computational studies of thermoelectric effects at nanoscale under a project on “ab initio modelling of thermoelectric effects in materials: Beyond the linear response regime”.


The main responsibilities of the position include:

  • Design a new ab initio method for modelling nanoscale thermoelectric effects.
  • Investigate thermoelectric effects in experimentally fabricated nanoscale materials with the proposed ab initio method.

Qualifications

PhD in Physics, Chemistry or Materials Science.


Skills:
Experienced in parallel coding; Expert in DFT-based computational theories and software; Familiar with nonequilibrium modelling of quantum systems.


Experience:
Published first-author papers in main-stream journals in computational condensed matter physics, materials physics or quantum chemistry.

title: Distribution Sales Manager – Africa
description:
Distribution Sales Manager – Africa

Location: Nairobi, Kenya (with frequent regional travel)
Industry: IT Distribution – Imaging, Printing & Visual Technology
Salary: KES 550,000 per month, plus benefits
Reports to: Regional Commercial Leadership

About the Role

Gap Recruitment Services Limited is recruiting a Distribution Sales Manager - Africa on behalf of a well-established IT distribution business representing leading global principals in printing, imaging, and visual technology across East and West Africa.

This is a senior commercial leadership role for a hands-on operator who can own the numbers. The successful candidate will take full responsibility for sales performance across all product lines, build and manage distributor and reseller channels across multiple territories, lead in-country sales teams, and deliver against revenue, margin, and KPI targets.

We are looking for a commercially aggressive, analytically strong sales leader with a proven record of growing technology distribution business in African markets.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and drive regional sales growth across East and West Africa
  • Own performance across all product categories, including hardware, consumables, and media
  • Develop and expand distributor, dealer, and reseller networks in assigned markets
  • Oversee in-country sales teams and coordinate closely with Dubai commercial operations
  • Deliver revenue targets, gross margin objectives, and agreed commercial KPIs
  • Prepare accurate sales forecasts, budgets, and performance reports
  • Gather and analyse competitor activity, pricing movements, and market opportunities
  • Support vendor and principal growth plans, and execute market expansion strategies
  • Maintain strong commercial discipline over receivables, stock movement, and profitability

Qualifications & Experience

  • Minimum 8 years' senior sales experience in IT distribution, printing and imaging, office automation, or related technology hardware
  • Hands-on category exposure across one or more of the following:
  • Inkjet, laser, dot-matrix, and ink-tank printing systems
  • Large-format, label, and production/commercial print
  • Projection, display, and visual solutions
  • Photographic imaging, photo-finishing, and digital imaging equipment
  • Scanning, document capture, and multifunction devices
  • Consumables, inks, toners, and print media
  • Track record built with globally recognized Japanese or international imaging and printing principals direct principal-facing or authorized-distributor experience strongly preferred
  • Strong regional or multi-country commercial management experience
  • Proven success in channel development and distributor management
  • Solid command of sales forecasting, budgeting, P&L management, and KPI reporting
  • Proven leadership ability, with experience managing and developing sales teams
  • Highly analytical, performance-driven, and commercially assertive
  • Willingness and availability to travel frequently across the region

Closing Date;

Interested candidates who meet the above criteria are invited to send their CV to Before Friday 31st July, 2026

title: Senior Employee Relations Advisor
description:
• Love what you do and leave your mark
  • Support our leaders as a trusted partner, navigating complex workplace matters
  • Be based in either Sydney, or Melbourne

As a Senior Employee Relations Advisor within the Team Services team, you will act as a trusted partner and escalation point for Endeavour Group leaders navigating complex workplace matters.

In this role, you will lead end-to-end grievance investigations and translate employment legislation and internal policies into robust, commercially pragmatic recommendations.

Beyond delivering expert, day-to-day case management, you will play a pivotal role in shaping our wider People strategy by championing continuous improvement projects and coaching a dedicated team of ER Advisors to elevate our collective capability.

Sound good? Read on.
Here's a snapshot of the responsibilities:

  • Execute the day-to-day operational delivery of employee relations case management and lead end-to-end grievance investigation services across several regions.
  • Deliver strong, supportive leadership across ER team members within the Team Services team, providing coaching and development support to junior ER advisors.
  • Represent the business in claims across external jurisdictions (such as the Fair Work Commission and Human Rights Commissions) and in interactions with external employee representative bodies like the SDA.
  • Support and guide the business with industrial disputes and workplace issues outside of standard bargaining processes.
  • Develop and deliver performance management training programs to emerging leaders across the business.
  • Interpret and deliver data insights, trends, and strategic opportunities to Senior Operations stakeholders.
  • Support business-wide projects and strategic initiatives, including bargaining, compliance, and employment governance.

Now let’s talk about you:

  • 5-7 years of experience in Human Resources or Employee Relations.
  • Demonstrable experience managing complex HR/ER matters and a proven track record of leading a high-performing team of professionals.
  • You possess sound knowledge and deep understanding of relevant ER legislation across different jurisdictions and its practical application within company policy.
  • Demonstrate a clear capacity to provide commercial, risk-managed, yet fast and simple HR/ER solutions to Line Managers and business units.
  • You hold excellent negotiation and influencing skills to engage collaboratively with a wide range of internal and external stakeholders and customers.
  • Tertiary qualifications in HR, ER, Employment Law, or a relevant field (and/or equivalent significant practical experience).
  • Previous experience leading large teams in service delivery environments is highly regarded.

The benefits are good too!

  • An exclusive discount card for our ALH Hotels pubs & accommodation, BWS, Dan Murphy’s and other Endeavour Group brands. Through our partnership with Woolworths, you'll also enjoy discounts at Woolies supermarkets and BIG W
  • As a valued member of the team, your health and wellbeing is our top priority. You will have access to a range of free services to help you live well and support your physical, mental and financial wellbeing
  • Endeavour Group is full of opportunities - use our dedicated learning and development options to grow an idea, yourself, and your career. This is just the start, so dream big
  • Find out more about our range of benefits and discounts here

At Endeavour, we value being a workplace where everyone’s welcome - if you meet a number of the requirements (and not all), we encourage you to apply.

We are together creators

With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, Endeavour Group is big on sociability. Together we create the moments that bring millions of people together. And together we have more fun, create more opportunities, and score a lot more goals. We’re serious about creating a safe, inclusive and fun place to rock up to where equal opportunity is key, and flexibility is part of how we roll.

We’re all about creating a more sociable future - for our customers and each other. If this job excites you - and you’re close-enough on the requirements, reach out, we’d love to hear from you.

You can learn more about working with us on LinkedIn or at endeavourgroupcareers.com.au.

Our Talent Team and Hiring Leaders kindly request no unsolicited resumes or approaches from Recruitment Agencies. Endeavour Group is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

#WeAreTogetherCreators #ComeAsYouAre #DreamBig #FeelTheEnergy #LeaveYourMark #EndeavourGroup

title: Assistant Sourcing Manager
description:
Are you a commercially minded procurement professional looking to take the next step in your career?

As an Assistant Sourcing Manager within our Central Group Procurement team, you will play a pivotal role in identifying, delivering and embedding value across the Culina Group through structured sourcing activities. This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing procurement function where you'll gain exposure to a diverse range of indirect spend categories, partner with senior stakeholders, and contribute to strategic procurement initiatives.

Reporting to the Head of Sourcing, you'll be responsible for managing sourcing projects from initial business requirement through to implementation and supplier handover. For the right candidate, this role offers a clear pathway for progression within a dynamic and ambitious procurement team.

Working within allocated spend categories, you will follow our structured seven-stage sourcing methodology to deliver sustainable commercial value:

  • Capture and understand business requirements and stakeholder needs.
  • Analyse requirements against existing suppliers and incumbent capabilities.
  • Assess market opportunities and identify potential solutions.
  • Develop tender documentation in collaboration with key stakeholders.
  • Run tender processes and negotiate best-value outcomes for the Culina Group.
  • Implement sourcing outcomes and provide effective handovers to Supplier Relationship Managers.
  • Continually review spend activity to identify opportunities for cost optimisation, innovation, sustainability improvements and revenue generation.

You'll also:

  • Support the creation and implementation of strategic sourcing and category plans.
  • Build strong relationships with stakeholders at all levels across the organisation.
  • Lead sourcing projects within your category areas to deliver best-in-class procurement outcomes.
  • Contribute to supplier performance, business resilience and risk management objectives.
  • Work collaboratively with Supplier Relationship Management teams and subject matter experts.
  • Identify and help build a pipeline of procurement opportunities that support continuous improvement and value creation.
  • Proven experience within an Indirect Procurement, Strategic Sourcing, Purchasing or Buying role.
  • Demonstrable success delivering value through competitive tendering, negotiation and supplier management.
  • Experience creating professional tender documentation, including output-based specifications and performance measures/KPIs.
  • Strong project management skills with the ability to manage multiple sourcing projects simultaneously.
  • A structured and analytical approach to supplier evaluation, including the use of weighted scorecards and evaluation criteria.
  • Understanding of negotiation strategies and commercial decision-making.
  • Experience supporting or leading contract drafting, negotiation and execution, working alongside legal teams where appropriate.
  • Strong Excel, data analysis and eProcurement system skills.
  • Knowledge of Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) methodologies and cost-benefit analysis.
  • Commercially focused with a track record of identifying savings and value-add opportunities.
  • Excellent communication, influencing and stakeholder management skills.
  • Experience contributing to category strategies and sourcing plans that balance performance, risk and long-term value.

Desirable

  • Experience working with large framework agreements and structured call-off processes.
  • CIPS Level 4 qualification (or working towards) or equivalent procurement qualification.
  • Experience within logistics, supply chain, transport or operationally focused environments.

As part of our drive to make Culina Group a great place to work. We are proud to be an inclusive and diverse organisation where we are committed to employee development and recognising success for hard working performers.

Our dedicated learning and development programmes are open to every employee to give you the opportunity to shape your own future within logistics and continue to work in an environment where team culture thrives.

Our People are the driving force behind our success, which is why we offer a wide range of benefits which include:

  • Annual Leave – Competitive holiday entitlement of 24 days + 8 Bank Holidays
  • Pension scheme – we want colleagues to enjoy a comfortable retirements so we offer a great contribution of 4% employee and 4% employer
  • Life Assurance - x2 your annual salary
  • Wellness – Via our Employee Assistance Programme we offer immediate access to a confidential telephone counselling and legal information service that operates 24 hours a days, 365 days a year
  • Eye Care Vouchers – We can provide you with substantial savings with free eye tests and discounts on prescription glasses
  • Reward & Recognition – We recognise that employees have gone the extra mile via Employee of the month and year, special recognition and long service awards.
  • Everyday discounts - via our benefit platform you will have access to over 50 retailer discounts for everyday savings!

If you meet the requirements for the above role and are looking for your next career opportunity please apply now and become a part of our #WinningTeam!

title: Calculator koeltechniek
description:
Ben jij een Calculator Koeltechniek die technische ontwerpen weet te vertalen naar betrouwbare en haalbare calculaties? Bij Frimex in Moordrecht bereken je koeltechnische projecten voor bekende opdrachtgevers binnen de retail en hospitality en leg je de financiële basis voor een succesvolle uitvoering.

Je werkt aan afwisselende projecten voor onder andere supermarkten, hotels en casino’s. Daarbij krijg je veel zelfstandigheid, werk je nauw samen met collega’s uit verschillende disciplines en zie je jouw calculaties daadwerkelijk terug in de praktijk.

Wat ga je doen

Als Calculator Koeltechniek beoordeel je aanvragen, tekeningen en technische omschrijvingen en vertaal je deze naar een complete kostprijsberekening. Je kijkt niet alleen naar de cijfers, maar denkt ook mee over technisch slimme, uitvoerbare en commerciële oplossingen.

Je werkzaamheden bestaan onder andere uit:

  • Opstellen van calculaties en kostprijsbegrotingen voor koeltechnische projecten

  • Bestuderen van tekeningen, bestekken en technische specificaties

  • Bepalen van benodigde materialen, uren, materieel en werkzaamheden

  • Opvragen, beoordelen en vergelijken van offertes van leveranciers en onderaannemers

  • Signaleren van technische en financiële risico’s binnen aanvragen

  • Meedenken over alternatieven en optimalisaties die projecten slimmer of efficiënter maken

  • Overdragen van goedgekeurde calculaties aan projectleiding en werkvoorbereiding

Dankzij jouw nauwkeurigheid en technisch inzicht kunnen projecten realistisch worden aangeboden en goed voorbereid van start gaan.

Wat wij je bieden

  • Je ontvangt een passend salaris, afhankelijk van jouw kennis, werkervaring en opleiding

  • Veel zelfstandigheid en ruimte om mee te denken over technische en commerciële oplossingen

  • 26 vakantiedagen en 6,5 ADV-dagen

  • Een laptop en telefoon van de zaak

  • Volop opleidings- en doorgroeimogelijkheden binnen Frimex en de VDK Groep

  • Werken aan afwisselende projecten voor bekende opdrachtgevers binnen retail en hospitality

  • Daarnaast profiteer je van diverse extra voordelen, zoals personeelskortingen, leuke evenementen en andere aantrekkelijke extra's

Wat neem je mee

  • Mbo 4- of hbo-werk- en denkniveau in de richting van koeltechniek, werktuigbouwkunde of installatietechniek

  • Ervaring met calculatie, werkvoorbereiding of engineering binnen de koel- of installatietechniek

  • Technisch en cijfermatig inzicht en het vermogen om nauwkeurig te werken

  • Goede communicatieve vaardigheden en een praktische, commerciële instelling

  • Ervaring met calculatiesoftware is mooi meegenomen, maar geen harde eis

Heb je veel technische kennis vanuit de uitvoering en wil je de stap maken naar een functie als calculator? Ook dan komen we graag met je in contact.

Frimex

Frimex is al meer dan 50 jaar specialist in koeltechniek, klimaattechniek, elektrotechniek en grootkeukentechniek. Met ruim 350 medewerkers realiseren en onderhouden we technische installaties voor toonaangevende opdrachtgevers binnen de retail, hospitality en utiliteit.

Wat Frimex onderscheidt:

  • Afwisselende projecten voor bekende opdrachtgevers zoals Albert Heijn, Jumbo en Holland Casino

  • Korte lijnen en nauwe samenwerking tussen calculatie, engineering en projectuitvoering

  • Een eigen opleidingscentrum met volop mogelijkheden om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen

Frimex en de VDK Groep

Frimex maakt onderdeel uit van VDK Installatiegroep: een organisatie met meer dan 115 zelfstandige installatiebedrijven in Nederland. Hierdoor profiteer je van de zekerheid, opleidingen en doorgroeimogelijkheden van een grote organisatie, terwijl je werkt binnen de informele en betrokken sfeer van Frimex.

Heb je interesse?

Bel of app dan met Tim de Leeuw, Recruitment Business Partner van de VDK Groep.

06-83275613

t.deleeuw@vdkgroep.com

title: Ausbildung 2027 - Rohrleitungsbauer (m/w/d)
company: Ing. Ludwig Freitag Elektro-GmbH & Co. KG
description:
Energie ist unsere Stärke! Die Ing. Ludwig Freitag Elektro-GmbH & Co. KG ist Teil der Freitag Gruppe - dem größten familiengeführten Energiedienstleister Deutschlands. Die Freitag Gruppe, die auf eine über 100-jährige Geschichte zurückblickt, umfasst derzeit 28 unabhängige und europaweit agierende Unternehmen mit über 3000 Mitarbeitern. Im Bereich Medienversorgung und Telekommunikation steht sie für moderne Versorgungstechnik, solides Handwerk, Zuverlässigkeit und Qualität.

Energiegeladen und bereit für Neues? Die Ing. Ludwig Freitag Elektro-GmbH & Co. KG freut sich auf Deine Verstärkung für die Einsatzorte Ingolstadt.

Ausbildungsstart: 01.09.2027

  • Hohe Übernahmechance nach erfolgreich abgeschlossener Ausbildung
  • Sicherer Arbeitsplatz in einem mittelständischen und systemrelevanten Unternehmen
  • Vielfältige Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten
  • Corporate Benefits - Attraktive Mitarbeiterrabatte, Firmenfitness mit EGYM Wellpass mit Zugang zu über 7.000 Sporteinrichtungen
  • Abwechslungsreiche und verantwortungsvolle Aufgaben

Deine Aufgaben:

  • Herstellung und Inbetriebnahme von Rohrleitungssystemen für die Gas-, Wasser- und Wärmeversorgung
  • Bearbeitung von Metall- und Kunststoffrohrsystemen durch Schweißen, Kleben und Fügen
  • Herstellung von Hausanschlussleitungen einschließlich fachgerechter Dokumentation

  • Teamfähigkeit, Zuverlässigkeit, Sorgfalt und Verantwortungsbewusstsein

In dieser Stellenanzeige werden Personenbezeichnungen zugunsten einer besseren Lesbarkeit vereinheitlicht und beziehen sich gleichermaßen auf alle Geschlechter.
Uns ist wichtig, wer Du bist. Herkunft, Geschlecht, Alter, Behinderung, sexuelle Orientierung oder religiöse Überzeugung spielen für uns keine Rolle. Wir stehen für Vielfalt, Chancengleichheit und ein respektvolles Miteinander und freuen uns auf Deine Bewerbung!

title: Aide-soignant H/F
description:
Se révéler chez Colisée !

En rejoignant notre équipe, attendez vous à :

  • vous sentir épanoui, plein d’énergie le matin en vous levant,
  • des journées nouvelles et sans routine aux côtés des résidents et des autres membres de l'équipe,
  • un contrat de 35 heures à durée indéterminée,
  • 1 week-end travaillé sur 2
  • des perspectives de carrière et des formations pour vous révéler,
  • un salaire stimulant, avec SEGUR et reprise d’ancienneté.

Le talent, c'est vous !

Vous êtes Aide-soignant H/F et aimez prendre soin des résidents.

  • Vous êtes un élément clé au cœur de la Communauté Colisée.
  • Vous accompagnez les résidents dans leur vie quotidienne : lever, hygiène, confort, aide à la prise de repas, accompagnements, coucher). Nos chambres sont équipées de rails de transfert afin de faciliter votre confort et celui des Mentors.
  • Vous placez les résidents au cœur de votre attention : aucun petit détail sur leurs préférences ne vous échappe.
  • Vous offrez a nos ainés un soutien permanent afin de favoriser l'indépendance des gestes du quotidien
  • Vous partagez des moments complices avec eux et leur apportez les meilleures marques d’attentions.

Devenez Coliséen et faites partie d’une Communauté de Talents au service des résidents !

Nous ne voyons pas que votre CV en vous !

Parce que les résidents débordent d’énergie et de curiosité, vous serez choisi et reconnu pour votre personnalité et vos singularités !

Vous êtes diplômé d’état Aide-soignant et vous vous reconnaissez dans nos valeurs CORE (COhésion, Respect, Engagement).

Nous considérons chaque Talent et mettons tout en œuvre pour qu’ils puissent se révéler.

Cela passe à la fois par l’écoute, la transparence et un dialogue social continu, mais aussi par les opportunités de formation, avec notamment notre plateforme CORE Academy.

Tous nos postes sont ouverts aux personnes en situation de handicap. Parce que la diversité est une force, Colisée s'engage dans l'inclusion et la non-discrimination pour garantir l'égalité des chances.

title: Senior Project Manager
description:
Growe welcomes those who are excited to:

  • Manage the full project lifecycle, from scoping and planning to execution and delivery;

  • Collaborate closely with engineers, product managers, QA, and design teams;

  • Proactively engage with parallel technical teams to ensure alignment and efficient dependency management;

  • Ensure the stability and uptime of owned products and services;

  • Take ownership of proactively improving the quality, performance, and reliability of the product/service;

  • Track and manage key performance metrics, including: Uptime Rate, Development Deviation (normal deviation), Defect Detection Rate, Error Monitoring, Delivery Rate;

  • Ensure effective prioritisation, sprint planning, and tracking of deliverables;

  • Mitigate risks, remove blockers, and maintain transparency with stakeholders;

  • Identify opportunities and drive continuous improvements within the team: processes, communication, and delivery practices.

We need your professional experience:

  • 5+ years of experience in project management within a technology-focused company or digital product environment;

  • Strong understanding of software development life cycles, Agile/Scrum;

  • Hands-on experience managing cross-functional technical teams (e.g., backend, frontend, DevOps);

  • Strong track record of delivering complex projects in environments with strict reliability and uptime requirements;

  • Proven ability to work with data-driven KPIs and apply them to improve delivery and operational excellence;

  • Experience working in industries with fast release cycles, live systems, and customer-facing platforms;

  • Familiarity with incident management, root cause analysis, and risk mitigation in production;

  • Upper-intermediate level of English (written and spoken);

  • Experience with iGaming will be a plus.

We appreciate if you have those personal features:

  • Collaborative and assertive: able to work across multiple stakeholders, push for the right outcomes, and manage conflicts constructively;

  • Proactive and ownership-driven: doesn't wait to be told what to do, takes initiative to spot issues and act on them;

  • System thinker: able to connect dots across multiple teams, processes, and technical domains;

  • Resilient: capable of making decisions and communicating clearly during incidents or uncertainty;

  • Curious and fast learner: keen to understand complex technical systems and domain-specific nuances (e.g., gambling platforms, compliance);

  • Data-minded: comfortable working with metrics and turning insights into actions.

We are seeking those who align with our core values:

  • GROWE TOGETHER: Our team is our main asset. We work together and support each other to achieve our common goals;

  • DRIVE RESULT OVER PROCESS: We set ambitious, clear, measurable goals in line with our strategy and driving Growe to success;

  • BE READY FOR CHANGE: We see challenges as opportunities to grow and evolve. We adapt today to win tomorrow.

title: Ehrenamtliche*r / Übungsleiter*in (d/w/m) für unsere Werkstatt für Menschen mit Behinderung (WfbM)
description:
Sie möchten sich sozial einbringen, wertvolle Erfahrungen sammeln und Menschen mit Behinderung bei ihrer Arbeit unterstützen? Wir suchen für unsere WfbM Ehrenamtliche, die Lust haben sich zu engagieren und in einem sinnstiftenden Umfeld helfen möchten.

Wir suchen für folgende 2 Bereiche:

1) Betreuungsaufgaben im Werkstattalltag

Sie begleiten unsere Werkstatt Beschäftigten im Arbeitsalltag und unterstützen bei folgenden Aufgaben:

  • Arbeitsunterstützung: Sie unterstützen Werkstattbeschäftigte bei der Erledigung ihrer täglichen Arbeitsaufgaben.

  • Begleitung: Sie helfen bei arbeitsbegleitenden Angeboten und Maßnahmen.

  • Pflege & Hilfe: Sie unterstützen bei einfachen Tätigkeiten der Grundpflege im Alltag.

2) Ehrenamtliche Vertrauensperson des Werkstattrates

Als Vertrauensperson stehen Sie dem Werkstattrat (der Interessenvertretung der Beschäftigten) mit Rat und Tat zur Seite.

Ihre Aufgaben:

  • Organisation: Sie unterstützen bei den organisatorischen Aufgaben der Vertrauenspersonen.

  • Veranstaltungen: Sie begleiten die Vertrauenspersonen zu Terminen und Events.

  • Sitzungen: Sie nehmen unterstützend an den Sitzungen des Werkstattrates teil.

☐ Herzblut: Spaß an der Arbeit mit Menschen und eine positive Ausstrahlung.

☐ Zuverlässigkeit: Du bist verbindlich und man kann sich auf dich verlassen.

☐ Das gewisse Extra (kein Muss!): Eine Übungsleiter-Lizenz (z.B. C-Lizenz) oder pädagogische Vorerfahrung ist toll, aber keine Voraussetzung.


Aus datenschutzrechtlichen Gründen können wir Bewerbungen ausschließlich über unser Karriereportal verarbeiten.

Hört sich gut an? Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

title: Copy of Site EHS Head (API)
description:
Job Summary

This role is for a Safety, Health & Environment (SHE) Compliance Manager responsible for driving regulatory adherence, operational safety, and environmental stewardship across CTO 5 at Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories. The position requires a seasoned professional with deep expertise in SHE policy implementation, risk assessment, hazardous waste management, and regulatory coordination. As a key enabler of sustainable operations, this role will oversee compliance with Pollution Control Board (PCB) regulations, manage Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP) performance, and ensure alignment with OSHA 18001 and ISO standards.

Roles & Responsibilities

  • You will be responsible for implementing SHE policies, conducting risk assessments, and monitoring hazardous waste reduction to ensure a safe and compliant work environment.
  • You will be responsible for organizing safety committee meetings, conducting equipment inspections, and performing internal audits to uphold safety standards and drive continuous improvement.
  • You will be responsible for guiding cross-functional teams in facilities compliance, investigating incidents, and initiating Hazard and Operability (HAZOP) studies to mitigate operational risks.
  • You will be responsible for facilitating SHE audits, coordinating with auditors, preparing audit presentations, and maintaining OSHA 18001 and ISO 14001 documentation and manuals.
  • You will be responsible for assessing environmental footprint, monitoring emissions and hazardous waste, and overseeing Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP) operations in compliance with PCB regulations.
  • You will be responsible for preparing Consent for Establishment (CFE) and Consent for Operation (CFO), securing necessary permissions for WWRF operations, and ensuring proper waste handling and disposal.
  • You will be responsible for conducting mock drills, identifying training needs, developing training modules, and facilitating workshops to build awareness and capability in SHE practices.
  • You will be responsible for collaborating with Human Resources for manpower planning, recruitment, performance appraisals, and coaching, while identifying and retaining high-potential talent.

Qualifications, Skills & Experience Educational Qualification

  • Graduation in Chemical Engineering mandatory.
  • Post Graduation in Environmental Management/Sustainability is preferred.
  • Certification in NEBOSH IGC, NEBOSH PSM, ISO 14001 & ISO 45001 or equivalent is preferred.

Minimum Work Experience

  • 15+ years of experience.

Skills & Attributes Functional Skills

  • Accident Analysis
  • Emergency Response Planning
  • Energy Efficiency Improvement
  • Environmental Impact Assessments
  • Fire Safety
  • Functional Safety Assessment
  • Safety Monitoring
  • Waste Management

Resident Skills

  • Accident Prevention
  • Compliance Management
  • Data Analysis
  • Hazard Management
  • Vendor Management

Behavioural Skills

  • Strategic Leadership & Accountability – Demonstrates ownership of SHE outcomes by proactively driving compliance, managing risks, and aligning operations with global standards like ISO and OSHA. Leads cross-functional teams and influences decision-making to ensure consistent safety performance.
  • Resilience & Crisis Management – Remains composed and decisive during audits, incidents, and regulatory challenges. Anticipates risks, conducts thorough investigations, and implements corrective actions to prevent recurrence.
  • People-Centric Development & Engagement – Fosters a culture of safety through coaching, mentoring, and capability building. Collaborates with HR and creates recognition programs to inspire teams to embrace the DREAM Zero Harm philosophy.

Critical Exposures

  • 8+ years of experience in Safety, Health, and Environmental roles or manufacturing operations within pharmaceutical, chemical or manufacturing industries is desired.
  • Experience with safety frameworks such as HAZOP, behavior-based safety (BBS), and incident investigation systems is desired.
  • Exposure to SHE audit systems, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 (OSHA 18001) compliance documentation, and regulatory certifications is preferred.
  • Experience with hazardous and chemical waste handling infrastructure, legal permit management, and regulatory registers under PCB norms is desired.
  • Experience in Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP) operations, environmental monitoring systems, and statutory consent processes (CFE/CFO) is desired.

About the Department
Global Manufacturing Organisation (GMO)
At Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, we are dedicated to making a meaningful impact on global healthcare through precision manufacturing and innovation. With a legacy of excellence, we are a leading force in the pharmaceutical industry.
We operate 19 state-of-the-art manufacturing plants across Hyderabad, Vizag, Baddi, Mexico, Shreveport, and Mirfield, comprising 8 OSD facilities, 3 Injectables facilities, and 8 API facilities.
Benchmarking manufacturing processes and continuous operational excellence are at the core of our capability to deliver quality medicines to our patients in 66 countries. We manufacture a portfolio of complex APIs and 1,150+ drug master files across key therapy areas such as Oncology, Cardio-vascular, Central Nervous System and Anti-Diabetes. he World Economic Forum has recognised our largest manufacturing facility in Bachupally, Hyderabad, as part of its Global Lighthouse Network. We aspire to be the most efficient pharma operations in the world. Our productivity improvement and digitalisation efforts are key to staying competitive, meeting business imperatives, and meeting our ambitious ESG goals. Building such ‘factories of the future’ is integral to innovation and to build healthcare of the future.

Benefits Offered

At Dr. Reddy’s we actively help to catalyse your career growth and professional development through personalised learning programs.

The benefits you will enjoy at Dr. Reddy’s are on par with the best industry standards. They include, among other things and other essential equipment, joining & relocation support, family support (Maternity & Paternity benefits), learning and development opportunities, medical coverage for yourself and your family, life coverage for yourself.

Our Work Culture

Ask any employee at Dr. Reddy’s why they come to work every day and they’ll say, because Good Health Can’t Wait. This is our credo as well as the guiding principle behind all our actions. We see healthcare solutions not only as scientific formulations, but as a means to help patients lead healthier lives, and we’re always attuned to the new and the next to empower people to stay fit. And to do this, we foster a culture of empathy and dynamism. People are at the core of our journey over the last few decades. They have been supported by an enabling environment that buoys individual ability while fostering teamwork and shared success. We believe that when people with diverse skills are bound together by a common purpose and value system, they can make magic.

title: GovTech Co-Founder / CMO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)

We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

title: Financial Controller, Family Office
description:
Reporting To: Chief Financial Officer and Senior Stakeholders

Role Overview

A privately held investment office is seeking an experienced and highly discreet Financial Controller to oversee its finance and accounting operations.

The successful candidate will be responsible for financial reporting, multi-entity consolidation, investment accounting, treasury management, tax compliance and internal controls across a portfolio of companies, trusts and investment vehicles.

This is a hands-on role requiring strong technical accounting capabilities, excellent attention to detail and the ability to translate complex financial and investment information into clear, decision-useful reporting.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the preparation of accurate monthly, quarterly and annual financial statements and consolidated management reports across multiple companies, trusts and investment vehicles.

Manage group consolidation activities, including intercompany reconciliations, consolidation adjustments, eliminations, supporting schedules and variance analysis against budgets and prior periods.

Maintain complete accounting records in Xero, including journals, accruals, prepayments, accounts payable, accounts receivable, fixed assets and bank, credit card, investment and foreign exchange reconciliations.

Maintain comprehensive investment records across listed securities, managed funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate and cash instruments, including cost bases, acquisitions, disposals, valuations, income distributions, capital calls and commitments.

Reconcile investment records against information provided by banks, custodians, brokers, fund administrators and investment managers.

Prepare portfolio and management reporting covering investment performance, asset allocation, net worth, liquidity, cash runway and foreign exchange exposure.

Monitor cash positions, funding requirements and liquidity across entities and currencies, and prepare cash flow forecasts for operating and investment obligations.

Manage foreign exchange accounting, including exposure monitoring, revaluation schedules and reporting of realised and unrealised gains and losses.

Coordinate intercompany funding, payment approvals, cash transfers and other treasury and cash management arrangements.

Manage the annual audit process and coordinate corporate tax, GST, statutory filings and regulatory reporting with external auditors, tax advisers, corporate secretaries and other professional service providers.

Maintain tax records, provisions, deferred tax positions, withholding tax schedules and investment cost-base information across relevant entities and jurisdictions.

Support tax, accounting and structuring considerations relating to new investments, entity formations and cross-border transactions.

Monitor and support compliance with applicable ACRA, IRAS and MAS requirements, including Section 13O or Section 13U substance conditions and reporting obligations where relevant.

Develop and maintain accounting policies, internal controls, approval workflows, segregation of duties and payment authorisation procedures.

Act as the primary finance contact for banks, custodians, investment managers, trustees, brokers, fund administrators, legal advisers and corporate service providers.

Prepare financial and supporting documentation for board meetings, trustee resolutions, investment approvals and senior stakeholder reviews.

Provide clear and timely financial insights to the CFO, Principal and other senior stakeholders, translating complex financial and investment data into practical decision-making information.

Identify and implement improvements to closing, reporting, reconciliation, data management and finance workflows, including automation opportunities and system integrations.

Maintain standard operating procedures and ensure finance processes remain accurate, scalable, well controlled and audit-ready as new entities, structures and asset classes are introduced.

Handle all financial, investment and personal information with the highest level of discretion and confidentiality.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or a related discipline.
  • Professional accounting qualification such as CA, CPA or ACCA.
  • At least 8-10 years of relevant accounting and financial reporting experience.
  • Strong experience in multi-entity consolidation and management reporting.
  • Exposure to investment accounting, portfolio reporting, private wealth, fund management or family office structures.
  • Strong hands-on experience with Xero is essential.
  • Advanced Excel skills, including consolidation and reporting models.
  • Strong knowledge of foreign exchange accounting and revaluation.
  • Familiarity with Singapore accounting, taxation and regulatory requirements.
  • Experience supporting Section 13O or Section 13U structures would be highly advantageous.
  • Previous Big Four experience combined with relevant in-house exposure would be an advantage.
  • Experience with portfolio or wealth-reporting systems would be beneficial.


Personal Attributes

  • Meticulous, dependable and highly discreet.
  • Strong analytical and reconciliation capabilities.
  • Able to communicate complex financial information clearly.
  • Comfortable working directly with senior stakeholders.
  • Proactive and able to operate independently.
  • Strong organisational and deadline-management skills.
  • Able to manage multiple entities, priorities and reporting requirements simultaneously.

All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence. Personal data provided will be used for recruitment purposes only.

Please apply with your CV in word document format. We regret toinform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. For other positions related to real estate or otheralternative investments, please go to https://careers.tri-cap.com.sg/.

title: Alternance en Vente H/F
description:
Venez composer nos équipes de choc des boulangeries Ange et préparez votre Diplôme en Vente.

Vous suivrez un Titre Professionnel en alternance au sein de notre boulangerie ANGE sur une durée de 1 à 2 ans

Votre formation :

Les techniques de commercialisation et de vente de produits ou de services en boulangerie.
Les connaissances sur les circuits de distribution, sur les modes d'approvisionnement, les procédures de stockage des marchandises et en missions ça donne quoi ?

  • Accueillir, conseiller et orienter les clients sur nos produits de boulangerie, snacking et pâtisserie.

  • Assurer la vente, le service et l’encaissement avec sourire et professionnalisme.

  • Participer à la mise en rayon, au réapprovisionnement et à la présentation des produits en vitrines.

  • Veiller à la propreté et à l'organisation de l’espace de vente et de restauration.

  • Contribuer aux actions commerciales et à l’animation de la boutique.

.
Vous deviendrez un(e) véritable ambassadeur/ambassadrice de notre enseigne.

Vous avez le sens de la satisfaction et du contact client ainsi que l'envie de rejoindre un concept ambitieux au sein d'une entreprise performante ?
Vous êtes reconnu(e) pour votre dynamisme et votre esprit d'équipe ?

Dans le cadre de votre cursus en alternance, nous saurons vous apporter une vraie valeur ajoutée sur la pratique liée à vos études et à l'amour du terrain.

Et l'organisation chez Ange, ça donne quoi ?
Afin de permettre une bonne rotation des plannings de chaque membre de l'équipe, vous alternez des prises de poste en ouverture, journée et fermeture en horaire continue.
Compte tenu des horaires et de l'accès à notre boulangerie, il est impératif d'être autonome dans vos déplacements.

Profil requis :

Le Titre Professionnel est accessible à partir du niveau Bac

Le diplôme est obtenu en un ou deux an

Type de contrat :

Formation en Alternance

Titre visé : Titre Professionnel de Conseiller de Vente

Vous partagez nos valeurs et souhaitez rejoindre un groupe en plein développement qui offre des possibilités d'évolution ?
Vous souhaitez rejoindre notre équipe de choc ? Rencontrons-nous !



Et Devenez un Ange, Gardien du bon !

Réseau de plus de 300 boulangeries, nous sommes implantés en France, au Canada, à la Réunion… et d'autres nouveaux pays nous tendent les bras.
Notre ambition est d’étendre significativement notre nombre de boutiques. Un objectif motivant pour lequel nous avons besoin de talents motivés !

Depuis 2008, le réseau des boulangeries ANGE s'est hissé au 2ème rang de son secteur et le premier en franchise. Et nous ne comptons nous arrêter là !

title: 🪵 Kan du se skævt træ... uden selv at blive det? Så mangler vi jer!
description:
Vi søger flere tømrere, der kan deres håndværk, trives med serviceopgaver og ikke går hjem, før kunden står tilbage med et smil.

Hos os kommer I ikke til at stå på den samme byggeplads i månedsvis. Til gengæld får I en afvekslende hverdag med spændende opgaver hos både private og erhverv – og hvis I samtidig har flair for snedkerarbejde, er det et kæmpe plus.

Vi har eksisteret siden 1952, og selvom vi er en mindre virksomhed, har vi gennem årtier opbygget et stærkt ry for kvalitet, ordentlighed og godt håndværk. Derfor bliver vi ved med at få spændende opgaver fra private, virksomheder, boligforeninger, forsikringsselskaber og offentlige kunder over hele Storkøbenhavn samt Nord- og Midtsjælland.


🔨 Hvad kommer I til at lave?

  • Service- og spjældopgaver.

  • Reparationer og vedligeholdelse.

  • Mindre renoveringer.

  • Finish- og snedkerarbejde, hvor detaljerne tæller.

👷 Vi leder efter flere, som:

  • Er uddannede tømrere.

  • Har kørekort B.

  • Er servicemindede og professionelle over for kunderne.

  • Er mødestabile og tager ansvar.

  • Er gode kollegaer.

  • Gerne har erfaring med snedkerarbejde.

🚐 Det får I hos os:

  • Firmabil.

  • Løn efter kvalifikationer.

  • Overenskomst og ordentlige vilkår.

  • Frihed under ansvar.

  • En arbejdsplads, hvor kvalitet betyder mere end stopuret.

  • Dygtige kollegaer og en uformel omgangstone.

📩 Har I værktøjskassen klar? Så vil vi gerne høre fra jer!

Send en ansøgning til olga@entreprisegruppen.com eller ring på 60 59 74 29.

Vi ansætter løbende, så vent ikke på, at tommestokken bliver længere – søg allerede i dag! 🔨😄

title: Aprio PH - Senior Accounting Associate, E-Commerce
description:
Work with a nationally ranked CPA and advisory firm that is passionate for what's next. Aprio has 30 U.S. office locations, one in the Philippines and more than 2,100 team members that speak 60+ languages across the globe. By bringing together proven expertise, deep understanding, and strategic foresight for fast-growing industries, Aprio ensures clients are prepared for wherever life or business may take them. Discover a top-rated culture, vast growth opportunities and your next big career move with Aprio.

Join Aprio's Outsourcing team and you will help clients maximize their opportunities. Aprio is a progressive, fast-growing firm looking for a Senior Accounting Associate to join their dynamic team.

Position Responsibilities:

  • Reviews the correctness and completeness of the weekly work, which includes bills processing and bank transaction coding, and monthly work prepared by the Associate Accountant.
  • Ensures all weekly and monthly responsibilities of the Associate accountant are being done diligently.
  • Performs initial and/or final review of monthly and/or quarterly management reports.
  • Provide Accounting and Technical support to Accounting Associate(s); train or guide the Accounting Associate(s) where needed.
  • Handles new and complicated tasks, client onboarding, clean up and catch-up work, seasonal tasks (such as 1099 and audit support), and creation or maintenance of accounting work papers.
  • Preparation of process documents and works together with the Manager on process improvements.
  • Attend to Manager/Project Manager/Client’s questions, concerns, or issues.
  • Work with the Manager on people management – team’s capacity, performance evaluation, staff concerns, interviews for new hire, employee onboarding, etc.
  • In the absence of the Associate, Senior Accounting Associate assumes the responsibility of the Associate.
  • In the absence of the Manager, can perform as the leader of the team.

    • Qualifications Needed:

  • Bachelor’s Degree: A degree in accounting is required.
  • CPA License: Holding a CPA license is advantageous.
  • Experience: Minimum of 3 years (preferably in the BPO industry)
  • Software Proficiency: Required experience with XERO, QBO, NetSuite, and BILL.
  • Client Experience: Preference for candidates with experience managing E-commerce clients.
  • Communication Skills: Exceptional communication skills with a proven ability to thrive in a hybrid team setting, adept at using platforms such as Teams and email to collaborate with colleagues. The role involves extensive communication, as it involves direct engagement with clients.
  • Leadership Skills: Demonstrated experience in leading projects or teams, mentoring junior staff, and managing client relationships.
  • Problem-Solving Abilities: Strong critical thinking and problem-solving skills to address technical and interpersonal challenges effectively.
  • Client Management: Proven ability to manage multiple clients successfully.
  • Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment.
    • Perks/Benefits we offer for full-time team members:
    - Wellness program
    - HMO coverage
    - Rewards and Recognition program
    - Free shuttle service (provided by CDC | for onsite employees)
    - Free lunch meal (For onsite employees)
    - On-demand learning classes
    - Discretionary time off and Holidays
    - Performance-based salary increase

title: Verkoper (13-32 uur)
description:
Join America Today – A Brand inspired by the American lifestyle!

Jaren terug benoemde een van onze founders dat een klant lekker moet kunnen winkelen bij America Today zonder dat de verkoper een kauwgom kauwende secreet is die je afsnauwt in de store. Daar zijn we het eigenlijk nogsteeds mee eens. Ben jij niet dat secreet maar wel een energieke verkoper die graag iedere klant zich thuis laat voelen en geniet jij ervan om iedereen met een goed gevoel de store uit te laten gaan? Lees snel verder want wij zijn op zoek naar jou!

Wat doe je nu eigenlijk als verkoper?

Nieuwe collectie uitpakken, de store stylen, paskamers netjes houden en zorgen dat alles er top uitziet. Achter de kassa sta jij met een glimlach klaar om elke klant met een goed gevoel de deur uit te laten gaan.

Als gezicht van de store zorg je dat klanten zich welkom voelen. Of je nu helpt bij het zoeken van de perfecte jeans, advies geeft over een hoodie of een praatje maakt, jij maakt winkelen bij America today een fijne ervaring.

Servicegericht werken geeft je energie. Je hebt interesse in mode en vindt het leuk om klanten mee te nemen in het verhaal achter onze collectie.

Een goede outfit is belangrijk, maar de beleving eromheen maakt het compleet!

Zie jij dit helemaal zitten dan lees je hier wat je krijgt van ons:

  • een all-in uurloon van €17,54 wanneer je 21 jaar of ouder bent (20 jaar: €14,03, 19 jaar: €10,52, 18 jaar: €8,78, 17 jaar: €6,93, 16 jaar: €6,05).

    Een all-in uurloon betekent dat jouw vakantietoeslag en opgebouwde vakantie-uren iedere maand direct uitbetaald worden en je er dus niet op hoeft te wachten

  • je krijgt 150% betaald op zondag en 200% op feestdagen

  • 100% vergoeding obv openbaar vervoer, met een paar regels hieraan verbonden

  • altijd 20% korting en unieke shopping avonden met 40% korting

  • nog veel meer, kijk maar door hier te klikken: Benefits store

Join Today?

Helemaal enthousiast? Solliciteer dan direct of neem contact met ons op voor meer informatie over deze vacature!

Nog nooit van America Today gehoord? Wat gek😉! Lees hier wie we zijn, wat we belangrijk vinden en feitjes waar je waarschijnlijk niets mee gaat doen.

title: Senior Officer - Commercial
description:
About Business:

Adani Group: Adani Group is a diversified organisation in India comprising 10 publicly traded companies. It has created a world class logistics and utility infrastructure portfolio that has a pan-India presence. Adani Group is headquartered in Ahmedabad, in the state of Gujarat, India. Over the years, Adani Group has positioned itself to be the market leader in its logistics and energy businesses focusing on large scale infrastructure development in India with O&M practices benchmarked to global standards. With four IG rated businesses, it is the only Infrastructure Investment Grade issuer in India.

Job Purpose: This position is responsible for providing exceptional service to customers, addressing inquiries, resolving issues, and ensuring a positive customer experience.

Accounting Executive

Financial Accounting and Control

  • Maintain clear and organized ledgers, ensuring accurate and up-to-date financial records
  • Set up provisions and maintain financial book hygiene to align with accounting standards and regulations
  • Monitor and manage the ageing of cargo inventory to optimize inventory control and ensure timely actions
  • Process refunds and manage short closures in accordance with contractual agreements
  • Maintain Delivery Orders (DOs), books, credit and debit notes
  • Re-validate Delivery Orders (DOs) as needed to support smooth financial transactions
  • Keep meticulous records of financial transactions related to port activities for transparent reporting
  • Implement strategies to effectively manage debtors, minimize outstanding receivables, and ensure financial stability
  • Monitor and control financial exposure related to mark-to-market (M2M) activities, mitigating risks
  • Address and manage items noted in Letters of Credit (LCs) to maintain compliance with LC terms
  • Accurately account for quality-related penalty premiums in financial records
  • Calculate and manage interest charges, plot rent, and financial adjustments, adhering to contractual terms
  • Champion IRM Registration amongst existing customers and ensure new orders are done through IRM (as mandated)

  • Review existing processes and systems for identifying process/system improvements, identify new initiatives, automation, and digitization opportunities.

Customer Query Handling and Financial Reconciliation

  • Respond promptly and accurately to customer queries related to financial transactions, ensuring customer satisfaction and clarity
  • Perform regular sales reconciliation to verify the accuracy of financial records, identifying and resolving discrepancies
  • Process debit and credit notes associated with sales transactions to maintain complete and accurate financial documentation
  • Follow compliance with Tax Collection at Source (TCS) regulations under Section 27C of the Income Tax Act to meet legal requirements
  • Accurately record and manage customer payments in the financial system to maintain up-to-date financial records
  • Track records received from external sources and input them into the IRM (Information Resource Management) system
  • Prepare and manage PIS (Packed in slip) records
  • Manage delivery orders, financial books, debit, and credit notes
  • Process ledger entries such as LC (Letter of Credit) clearing
  • Manage port reconciliations, provisions, and entries

Digitisation and Automation

Support comprehensive digitization strategies to optimise operational efficiency

Innovate and implement automation solutions to support overall organisational goals / strategy

Key Stakeholders Internal:

  • Finance and accounting
  • Marketing
  • Operations
  • Logistics

Key Stakeholders External:

Customers

Suppliers/ Vendors

Education Qualification:

  • Post-Graduation (Finance)

Work Experience (Range of years):

  • 2-5 Years of experience

Preferred Industry

  • Coal Trading, Commodity Trading

title: Chef
description:

The Chef is responsible for preparing and cooking high-quality Western cuisine, ensuring consistency in food presentation, maintaining kitchen hygiene, and supporting the smooth operation of the kitchen.

Responsibilities

  • Assist in daily food preparation and mise en place to support kitchen readiness

title: Staff Manufacturing Systems Engineer (AI & Automation)
description:
WHAT YOU DO AT AMD CHANGES EVERYTHING

At AMD, our mission is to build great products that accelerate next-generation computing experiences—from AI and data centers, to PCs, gaming and embedded systems. Grounded in a culture of innovation and collaboration, we believe real progress comes from bold ideas, human ingenuity and a shared passion to create something extraordinary. When you join AMD, you’ll discover the real differentiator is our culture. We push the limits of innovation to solve the world’s most important challenges—striving for execution excellence, while being direct, humble, collaborative, and inclusive of diverse perspectives. Join us as we shape the future of AI and beyond. Together, we advance your career.




THE ROLE:

We are seeking a Staff Agentic AI Automation Engineer to design and deliver AI-powered automation solutions that enhance manufacturing and enterprise operations. This role will focus on transforming manual, repetitive, and complex business workflows into intelligent digital processes by leveraging AI agents, automation platforms, data integration, and enterprise system connectivity.

THE PERSON:

You're a highly motivated team player with a strong development background, problem solving mentality, excellent communication skills, ability to prioritize tasks along with willingness to learn and adapt. Excellent teamwork skills and capable of working independently.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Identify business processes across manufacturing, engineering, and enterprise operations that can be improved through AI and automation.
  • Design and deploy AI agents that can analyze data, support decision-making, and execute actions across connected systems.
  • Build automation workflows that integrate applications, APIs, data sources, and enterprise platforms such as MES, ServiceNow, SharePoint, and reporting systems.
  • Develop scalable approaches for agent orchestration, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement.
  • Use operational data, logs, reports, and documents to enable predictive insights, root cause analysis, and smarter process execution.
  • Partner with cross-functional teams to translate business challenges into practical AI-driven solutions.
  • Track impact through measurable outcomes such as cycle time reduction, productivity gains, error reduction, and improved process efficiency.Develop and support enterprise applications at expert level and drive timely resolution.

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:

  • 8-12 years of experience in software development, automation, systems engineering, or enterprise application integration.
  • Experience in semiconductor, manufacturing, lab, or enterprise operations environments.
  • Exposure to MES, test systems, reporting platforms, or operational data workflows.
  • Familiarity with AI agent frameworks, enterprise copilots, or emerging Agentic AI tools.
  • Understanding of cloud-based integration, data pipelines, or analytics platforms.
  • Ability to evaluate new technologies and apply them to real business use cases.

ACADEMIC CREDENTIALS:

  • Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Computer Science or related field strongly preferred.

LOCATION:

Penang, Malaysia

title: 🔥 Er du certifikatsvejser? Vi søger flere til fjernvarmeprojekter på Sjælland 🔥

✔ Er uddannet smed eller har en tilsvarende faglig baggrund

✔ Arbejder selvstændigt og leverer et højt kvalitetsniveau

Vi tilbyder:

💰 Løn efter kvalifikationer og erfaring

😄 Kort fortalt

Hvis du er typen, der går op i ordentlige svejsninger, gyldige certifikater og et højt fagligt niveau, får du her muligheden for at arbejde på spændende fjernvarmeprojekter sammen med kollegaer, der deler den samme tilgang til håndværket.

Lyder det spændende, så tøv ikke med at sende dit CV på mail eller ringe på telefon – vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

title: Tesztautomatizáló / tesztfejlesztő

Its focus is on financial services providers, telecommunications companies, the automotive industry and energy service providers. Over 400 employees in 6 subsidiaries work together to ensure state of the art solutions for our clients.

We are looking for new colleagues in Qualysoft teams for diverse projects providing continuous learning opportunities. Our common goal is to provide honesty, development and a stable background while getting to know the latest technologies. We are waiting for your application for the position below!

title: Program Manager – Strategic Account (Israel)
description:
Please note this role can be based within EMEA but with Frequent travel to Israel

The Program Manager – Strategic Account (Israel) is Vertiv's dedicated program leader for a key strategic customer account, primarily based in Israel. This role serves as the Single Point of Contact (SPOC) for all Vertiv deliverables into the account, operating within an established governance model built on the principle of "One Customer • One Owner • One Process."

The position bridges internal Business Unit coordination and external customer governance, blending account management with operational coordination to ensure seamless delivery from implementation through commissioning and lifecycle handover.

Operating under a Hypercare Principle - where the Business Unit owns the outcome and Quality owns the system - this role is the connective tissue that ensures accountability, drives proactive resolution, and guarantees communication across all workstreams.

Key Responsibilities:

Account Ownership & Single Point of Contact

  • Act as the single dedicated point of contact for senior customer stakeholders across all Vertiv deliverables
  • Own end-to-end accountability for all Israel-based deployments and programmes
  • Represent Vertiv across all customer engagement forums, governance reviews, and executive briefings
  • Build and maintain trusted senior-level relationships with customer leadership - becoming the recognised face of Vertiv delivery
  • Deputise for EMEA leadership where requested to oversee and resolve deployment issues
  • Drive a culture of accountability and ownership across all internal teams engaged on the account

Internal Coordination & Governance

  • Coordinate across all Vertiv Business Units to ensure aligned delivery per the established governance model
  • Lead internal alignment meetings ensuring all BUs deliver against commitments and timelines
  • Implement and maintain the governance framework including Root Cause Analysis (RCA), 8D problem-solving, KPI tracking, and CAPA verification
  • Drive Quality Governance adherence - ensuring BU recovery ownership while maintaining systemic process compliance
  • Manage cross-functional resource planning, prioritisation, and de-confliction across regions

  • Collaborate with regional delivery teams to optimise coverage, share best practices, and ensure consistency of service across geographies

Proactive Resolution & Risk Management

  • Identify risks early and drive pre-emptive resolution before escalation to the customer
  • Manage escalations with urgency, coordinating internal resources for rapid resolution
  • Anticipate customer needs and address potential gaps before they materialise
  • Foster a culture where issues are surfaced early and resolved collaboratively - never hidden or deferred

Communication Guarantee

  • Develop and execute a comprehensive communication strategy for the account
  • Guarantee structured, timely, and transparent communication with customer and internal stakeholders
  • Deliver regular governance reports, progress updates, and status briefings
  • Ensure no communication gaps between BUs and customer - act as the single funnel of information
  • Maintain a clear documentation trail of decisions, actions, and commitments

Continuous Improvement & Operational Excellence

  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives across the programme, challenging the status quo
  • Identify opportunities to improve delivery efficiency, reduce cycle times, and enhance customer experience
  • Leverage cross-regional learnings and best practices to elevate account performance
  • Champion process adherence and standardisation aligned to Vertiv operational frameworks
  • Actively contribute to the evolution of Vertiv's strategic account delivery model

Business Compliance

All activities must comply with Vertiv's policies on Health & Safety, Environmental standards, International Trade Compliance, data protection, and applicable local regulatory requirements.

Key Interactions

Internal: Service & Technical Support, Thermal Business Unit, Power Business Unit, Infrastructure Solutions Business Unit, Operations & Supply Chain, Sales, Regional Delivery Teams, EMEA Management, Factories, Quality Governance

External: Senior customer stakeholders, Consultants, Contractors, End Users

Required Qualifications

  • Relevant technical qualification (HND minimum) in mechanical or electrical engineering field
  • Minimum 5 years of program/contract management experience in a complex delivery environment
  • Project Management certification (Prince2, PMP, or equivalent) preferred
  • Experience in critical infrastructure, data centre, or power solutions industry
  • Proven track record as a dedicated single point of contact for major/strategic accounts
  • Demonstrated ability to manage and influence senior stakeholders without direct authority
  • Strong knowledge of governance frameworks and quality management methodologies
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills (English required; Hebrew advantageous)
  • Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities concurrently
  • Self-motivated with the ability to operate autonomously with minimal supervision
  • Valid driving license
  • Willingness to travel within EMEA as required

Preferred Experience

  • International program management experience across multiple geographies
  • Six Sigma or Lean certification
  • Experience managing programmes with budgets exceeding $5M
  • Multi-site and multi-region programme coordination
  • Vendor and contract management experience
  • Experience working within or alongside account management structures
  • Background in cross-functional coordination across engineering, operations, and commercial teams

Working Conditions

  • Based in Israel or EMEA-based with regular travel to Israel (frequency to be agreed)
  • International travel required occasionally with overnight stays
  • Office, data centre, and construction site environments

Vertiv Core Principles

Safety • Integrity • Respect • Teamwork • Diversity & Inclusion

Strategic Priorities

Customer Focus • Operational Excellence • High-Performance Culture • Innovation • Financial Strength

Behaviours

Own It • Act With Urgency • Foster a Customer-First Mindset • Think Big and Execute • Lead by Example • Drive Continuous Improvement • Learn and Seek Out Development • Promote Transparent & Open Communication

About Vertiv

Vertiv (NYSE: VRT) brings together hardware, software, analytics, and ongoing services to enable its customers' vital applications to run continuously, perform optimally, and grow with their business needs. Vertiv solves the most important challenges facing today's data centres, communication networks, and commercial and industrial facilities with a portfolio of power, cooling, and IT infrastructure solutions and services that extends from the cloud to the edge of the network. Headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, USA, Vertiv employs approximately 27,000 people and does business in more than 130 countries.

Vertiv is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. We promote equal opportunities for all with respect to hiring, terms of employment, mobility, training, compensation, and occupational health, without discrimination as to age, race, colour, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy status, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, national origin, disability, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

title: Supplier Quality Engineer - Electronics
description:
Who we are

You enjoy creating and innovating. You never stop striving for better. You take responsibility and you get results. You love being part of a team. Above all, you want your work to matter: Welcome to our world! At Sonova we create sense by bringing sound to life. Our innovative hearing care solutions help millions of people enjoy life’s unforgettable moments.

We offer exceptional career opportunities through market-leading brands from consumer to medical, products and services that keep pushing hearing care forward, and a culture where you can quickly belong and perform at your best.

If you want the freedom to explore, opportunities to grow, and make positive change on people lives through your work, this is the place for you.

Supplier Quality Engineer – Electronics

This position is to Support the SQM-Team in supplier quality improvement and harmonization activities, utilizing quality tools, problem-solving methodologies and coordinating resolution supplier quality issues and escalations.

Develop, maintain, monitor and ensure good application of supplier performance control requirements to increase the transparency of cost to poor quality and ensure compliance to applicable regulations

More about the role:

  • Support the SQM team in supplier quality improvement and harmonization activities, utilizing quality tools and problem-solving methodologies to resolve supplier quality issues and escalations.
  • Develop, maintain, monitor, and ensure the good application of supplier performance control requirements to increase the transparency of cost to poor quality and ensure compliance with applicable regulations.
  • Support supplier quality assurance and control activities, monitoring performance indicators to ensure compliance with quality requirements and international regulations.
  • Act as a point of contact for supplier quality concerns, collaborating with Sonova locations and suppliers globally.
  • Process supplier nonconformities as part of supplier control activities and ensure the implementation of SQM requirements in terms of containment actions, root cause investigations, and preventative measures.
  • Evaluate the performance and capability of our suppliers and services, developing and implementing measures to ensure a high level of supply chain stability.
  • Enforce quality requirements with suppliers and organize quality meetings and escalations.
  • Process 8D/SCAR reports and coordinate all related activities with the support of the respective specialist departments.
  • Coordinate and follow-up on corrective and preventative actions and support in supplier audits and new supplier activities, including part and process qualification and validation.

More about you:

  • Education: A graduate degree in engineering, science, quality, or another technical field, or 8+ years of work experience in supplier quality management.
  • Regulatory Knowledge: In-depth knowledge of ISO 13485 and ISO 9001. Knowledge of QSR 820, MDSAP, and MDR is a plus.
  • Experience: Minimum of 6 years of experience in a medical device manufacturing environment in a quality-related role, with practical experience in supplier quality management and contract manufacturing.
  • Technical Expertise: Profound knowledge of electronic commodities, including batteries, PCBs, chargers, and electro-mechanics, and related production technologies for electronic components. Experience with 8D/SCAR methodologies, quality reports, improvement programs, and supplier audits is essential.
  • Personal Attributes: You are self-confident, well-organized, reliable, and pay close attention to detail. You are open and communicative and perform well in teams.
  • Leadership: Familiarity with navigating changing environments, driving continuous process improvements, and setting priorities from a global harmonization perspective.
  • Language & IT: Fluent English skills (written and spoken) are mandatory. Vietnamese or German language skills are a plus. Advanced MS Office skills are required, and programming or AI tools knowledge is beneficial.
  • Travel: You are willing to travel (approx. 30% of the time), primarily within the APAC region.

More about what we offer:

As one of the world’s leading hearing care providers headquartered in Switzerland, we’re committed to building an inclusive culture. We want to create an environment where you can balance a successful career with your commitments and interests outside of work, through our flexible hybrid working model. We offer a wide range of training opportunities for both your professional and personal development, and there are exceptional growth opportunities with individual development plans.

Sonova is an equal opportunity employer.

We team up. We grow talent. We collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds to win with the best team in the marketplace. We guarantee every person equal treatment in regard to employment and opportunity for employment, regardless of a candidate's ethnic or national origin, religion, sexual orientation or marital status, gender, genetic identity, age, disability or any other legally protected status.

Interested candidates are invited to apply directly through the following link:

https://jobs.sonova.com/job-invite/164429/

Alternatively, please visit the Sonova company website, select “Careers” and search for “Singapore” to view and apply for the position.

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Sonova is an equal opportunity employer.

title: AgriTech Co-Founder / Head of Sales (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

title: Occupational Therapists - WCAN
