title: Servicemedewerker
description:
Ben jij klantgericht, houd je van regelen en krijg je energie van mensen helpen? Dan zijn wij op zoek naar jou!

Bij Whoon vinden we goede service minstens zo belangrijk als het bouwen van mooie woningen. Ook nadat een woning is opgeleverd, willen we dat onze kopers en huurders op ons kunnen rekenen. Daarom zoeken we een enthousiaste Servicemedewerker die het eerste aanspreekpunt is voor vragen, servicemeldingen en garantieaanvragen.

In deze functie zorg jij ervoor dat klanten zich gehoord voelen. Je beantwoordt vragen, houdt iedereen op de hoogte van de voortgang en zorgt ervoor dat meldingen op de juiste plek terechtkomen. Dankzij jouw overzicht en communicatie kunnen jouw collega's zich richten op de inhoudelijke afhandeling van service- en garantiemeldingen.

Wat ga je doen?

Geen dag is hetzelfde. Je hebt veel contact met kopers, huurders, collega's en onderaannemers. Je schakelt snel, houdt overzicht en zorgt ervoor dat iedereen weet waar hij aan toe is.

Jouw werkzaamheden bestaan onder andere uit:

  • Je bent het eerste aanspreekpunt voor klanten via telefoon, e-mail en WhatsApp.

  • Je beantwoordt vragen over servicemeldingen en garantieaanvragen of zoekt de juiste informatie op.

  • Je controleert de actuele status van meldingen in ons 4PS-systeem en koppelt deze terug aan de klant.

  • Je neemt contact op met onderaannemers om de voortgang van werkzaamheden op te vragen en acties op te volgen.

  • Je ondersteunt je collega's bij het plannen van afspraken met kopers, huurders en onderaannemers.

  • Je verwerkt contactmomenten en acties zorgvuldig in het systeem.

  • Je verzorgt diverse administratieve werkzaamheden, zoals het aanmaken van meterkaststickers.

  • Je pakt proactief ondersteunende werkzaamheden op en helpt waar nodig binnen het serviceteam.

  • Samen met je collega's ben je de back-up voor het 24/7-callcenter.

Het team

Je komt terecht in een betrokken en informeel serviceteam dat bestaat uit een Servicecoördinator, twee Servicemedewerkers en twee Servicemonteurs. Samen zorgen jullie ervoor dat servicemeldingen snel en zorgvuldig worden afgehandeld.

Je staat er niet alleen voor. Vanaf je eerste werkdag krijg je een uitgebreid inwerkprogramma en leren ervaren collega's je stap voor stap de werkzaamheden en onze systemen kennen. Vragen stellen moedigen we juist aan; samenwerken staat bij ons centraal.

Wie ben jij?

Je vindt het leuk om mensen te helpen en bent sterk in communiceren. Je blijft vriendelijk, ook wanneer iemand een klacht of vraag heeft, en je weet hoofd- en bijzaken goed van elkaar te onderscheiden. Je bent nauwkeurig, schakelt gemakkelijk tussen verschillende werkzaamheden en houdt overzicht.

Daarnaast vragen wij:

  • Minimaal mbo werk- en denkniveau.

  • Bij voorkeur enige ervaring in een klantgerichte of administratieve functie. Heb je die nog niet, maar denk je dat deze functie goed bij je past? Dan nodigen we je ook van harte uit om te reageren.

  • Je bent minimaal 20-28 uur per week beschikbaar

  • Je bent communicatief vaardig, zowel mondeling als schriftelijk.

  • Je werkt nauwkeurig en voelt je verantwoordelijk voor een goede dienstverlening

En dit bieden we jou?

In het nieuwe kantoor, gelegen in hartje Den Bosch, staat een moderne werkplek voor je klaar voorzien van een laptop en sta-/zitbureau. Whoon biedt jou een professionele werkomgeving waarin je in staat wordt gesteld om het beste uit jezelf te halen. Wij hebben aandacht voor jou én je ambities.

Daarnaast kun jij rekenen op de volgende voorwaarden:

  • Een salaris wat meegroeit met jouw ontwikkeling

  • Vergoeding op je aanvullende zorgverzekering;

  • 43 (!) verlofdagen (25 vakantiedagen, 15 roostervrijedagen en 3 kortverzuimdagen o.b.v. een fulltime dienstverband);

  • Zeer goede pensioenregeling met werkgeversbijdrage;

  • Je eigen laptop en een fijne werkplek met een sta-/zitbureau;

  • Een gedreven team met jaarlijks meerdere leuke personeelsactiviteiten;

  • Uitstekend bereikbaar, op slechts 10 minuten lopen van NS Station Den Bosch;

Welkom!

Bij Whoon houden we van doorpakken. Niet door te haasten, maar door slim te organiseren en strak samen te werken. We schakelen snel, houden de lijnen kort en zorgen dat er écht iets gebeurt. Dat voel je in alles. Op kantoor, op de bouwplaats en hoe we met elkaar omgaan. Hier kun je meters maken. In je werk én in je ontwikkeling.

Je kent ons misschien als Van Wanrooij Bouw & Ontwikkeling; sinds kort zijn we Whoon. Een andere naam, maar we blijven wie we zijn: nuchtere aanpakkers met verstand van zaken. Wat ook blijft, is onze vertrouwde werkwijze: doordacht, efficiënt en met oog voor kwaliteit. Omdat Whoon onderdeel is van Heijmans, hebben we ook nog eens alle voordelen van een grote speler. Samen komen we verder!

Weet jij het verschil te maken?

Voor meer informatie over de functie kun je contact opnemen met Carlijn van den Broek (Corporate Recruiter) via 06 57 47 93 45. Of stel je vraag via WhatsApp. Wil je direct solliciteren? Dat kan via onderstaande button!

title: Senior adviseur drinkwater
description:
Ontwerp slimme oplossingen en realiseer impactvolle verandering

‘Help jij onze drinkwatervoorziening toekomstbestending te maken? Als Adviseur Drinkwater kijk je verder dan de eerste technische vraag, breng je oorzaken, belangen en oplossingsrichtingen bij elkaar en geef je richting aan keuzes in aanpak en in ontwerp. Wil jij jouw ervaring inzetten op het snijvlak van techniek en advies? Dan maken we graag kennis met je.’

- Kris Spierings - teammanager Drinkwater

Werken bij Sweco betekent dat je onderdeel wordt van het grootste architecten- en ingenieursnetwerk van Europa. Binnen team Drinkwater van de business unit Watertechnologie werk je aan drinkwatervraagstukken die steeds urgenter worden. De vraag naar schoon en voldoende drinkwater groeit, terwijl productielocaties, pompstations en installaties moeten worden vernieuwd en uitgebreid.. Je werkt vooral voor drinkwaterbedrijven en soms voor industriële klanten of waterschappen. Daarbij schakel je regelmatig met procestechnologen, werktuigbouwkundigen, civiel specialisten, elektrotechnici en projectleiders.

Als senior adviseur drinkwater breng jij overzicht in complexe klantvragen. Je vertaalt ambities naar een heldere probleemdefinitie en verkent welke technische oplossingsrichtingen passen. Ook denk je mee over uitbreidingen, renovaties, nieuwe pompstations en technologiekeuzes voor drinkwaterproductie. Je voert gesprekken met opdrachtgevers, haalt de vraag achter de vraag op en helpt assetmanagers en technische teams aan klantzijde om keuzes goed te onderbouwen. Daarbij combineer je inhoudelijke kennis met adviesvaardigheid: je analyseert, stelt scherpe vragen, brengt disciplines samen en geeft richting aan het vervolg.

Bij Sweco krijg je de ruimte om je verder te ontwikkelen als inhoudelijk expert en adviseur. Je werkt aan projecten die maatschappelijk relevant zijn: jouw adviezen dragen eraan bij dat er ook in de toekomst voldoende schoon drinkwater uit de kraan komt. De schaalgrootte van Sweco geeft je toegang tot veel kennis, verschillende disciplines en collega’s die vanuit hun eigen expertise meedenken. Tegelijkertijd speel je zelf een belangrijke rol in de ontwikkeling van het team. Je draagt jouw kennis over, begeleidt minder ervaren collega’s en helpt team Drinkwater verder groeien in techniek, analyse en integraal advies. Wil jij jouw ervaring inzetten voor drinkwateroplossingen die ertoe doen? Dan gaan we graag met je in gesprek.

[sweco-recruitee-vacancy-summary]

Dit is wat we jou bieden

Bij Sweco zit je goed. Dit mag je verwachten van een werkgever met het World-class Workplace label. Om te beginnen ontvang je een goed salaris en maar liefst 32 vakantiedagen. Daarnaast bieden we een thuiswerkvergoeding, vakantietoeslag, een circulaire thuiswerkplek en een uitzonderlijk goede pensioenregeling.

Daarnaast krijg je bij Sweco veel regie, vrijheid en verantwoordelijkheid. Over hoeveel je werkt, waar je werkt, hoe je werkt en wanneer je werkt. Binnen ons arbeidsvoorwaardenpakket heb je de keuze om te kiezen op welke manier jij reist. We stimuleren duurzame en actieve keuzes en belonen deze extra. Ook krijg je direct toegang tot onze eigen AI-omgeving SwecoGPT, tot de Sweco Academy voor zeer ruime ontwikkelmogelijkheden, en tot ons vitaliteitsprogramma gericht op leefstijl en sport.

Naast uitdagend werk, support en middelen pakken we bij Sweco ook graag uit als het gaat om ontspanning en lol. We willen graag dat je met veel plezier naar je werk gaat. Denk aan een tweedaags event voor alle nieuwe collega’s, de dagelijkse Fika’s op kantoor, events op jouw vestiging, en activiteiten vanuit Jong Sweco en de lokale personeelsverenigingen.

Solliciteer op deze functie

Enthousiast geworden? Solliciteer dan direct via de button ‘solliciteer’. We kijken uit naar je cv en motivatie!
Neem bij inhoudelijke vragen of interesse in Sweco contact op met Kris Spierings via +31 6 55 38 56 77 of kris.spierings@sweco.nl. Voor Informatie over de sollicitatieprocedure kun je contact opnemen met Bert aan den Toorn via +31 6 39 44 05 98 of bert.aandentoorn@sweco.nl.


  • Een afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding in werktuigbouwkunde, civiele techniek, bouwkunde, procestechnologie of vergelijkbaar

  • 5 -10 jaar ervaring met advisering binnen deze of een vergelijkbare branche

  • Kennis van drinkwaterinstallaties, productielocaties, pompstations en de vraagstukken die spelen bij drinkwaterbedrijven.

  • Je brengt complexe klantvragen terug tot een heldere probleemanalyse en vertaalt die naar een passende aanpak, oplossingsrichting en advies.

  • Je werkt zelfstandig, schakelt makkelijk met opdrachtgevers en collega’s en vindt het vanzelfsprekend om jouw kennis actief over te dragen aan minder ervaren collega’s.

title: Corporate Legal Counsel
description:
This is Adyen

Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft - making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.

For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.

Corporate Legal Counsel

As Corporate Legal Counsel within Corporate Legal’s Advisory team, you will primarily be working on matters relevant to Adyen’s public listing in the Netherlands. You will also help the team facilitate our major corporate projects such as, capital markets enquiries, complex corporate projects and corporate development.

This is a strategic role that is important to sound corporate governance practices and continued sustainable growth. The role requires someone effective at cross collaboration, who excels at decision making in a fast-moving complex environment. Knowledge of and comfort with the Market Abuse Regulation is imperative to success. The counsel will drive discussions and decisioning on disclosure matters as well as advise on public filings and equity matters. The role forms part of Adyen’s larger corporate practice which covers corporate governance, litigation and disputes and corporate development. The corporate legal team is currently centered in Amsterdam and the United States but Adyen is a global company with offices, entities, and boards all over the world.

What you'll do:

  • Review and advise on public filings as well as other disclosure documentation.
  • Providing legal support and internal training on other corporate law matters as required, such as Mergers & Acquisitions, corporate development and other corporate legal initiatives.
  • Manage insider trading and conflict of interest inquiries, procedures, and policies.
  • Ensure compliance with the Market Abuse Regulation.
  • Support the disclosure committee, driving strategic disclosure reviews and determinations.
  • Partner with Investor Relations and Communications on disclosure matters.
  • Advise on equity structures and incentive plans in partnership with HR and Rewards teams.
  • Consult with regulators and financial authorities such as the AFM.
  • Keep abreast of developments in relevant Dutch and European corporate legal matters.

What you bring:

  • An attorney with minimum of 4+ years of corporate legal experience, either as a corporate attorney or in-house corporate counsel at a publicly listed company in an international setting.
  • Strong knowledge of the Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) and related Dutch and European regulations.
  • Familiarity with equity compensation structures.
  • Experience interfacing with the AFM, supervisory boards, and management boards.
  • Excellent analytical, communication, and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to structure and prioritize in a high volume, fast-paced, complex environment.
  • Can work independently as well as collaboratively, gathering appropriate input and making clear, well-reasoned decisions.
  • You are available to work from our Amsterdam office and travel to other offices periodically.

Our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion commitments

Our unique approach is a product of our diverse perspectives. This diversity of backgrounds and cultures is essential in helping us maintain our momentum. Our business and technical challenges are unique, and we need as many different voices as possible to join us in solving them - voices like yours. No matter who you are or where you’re from, we welcome you to be your true self at Adyen.

Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Does this sound like you? If so, Adyen encourages you to reconsider and apply. We look forward to your application!

What’s next?

Ensuring a smooth and enjoyable candidate experience is critical for us. We aim to get back to you regarding your application within 5 business days. Our interview process tends to take about 4 weeks to complete, but may fluctuate depending on the role. Learn more about our hiring process here. Don’t be afraid to let us know if you need more flexibility.

This role is based out of our Amsterdam office. We are an office-first company and value in-person collaboration; we do not offer remote-only roles.

title: Laser Manufacturing Engineer
company: ELECTRO SCIENTIFIC INDUSTRIES SINGAPORE PTE. LTD
description:
A Day in Your Life at MKS:

As a Laser Manufacturing Engineer at MKS | ESI, you will partner with the Manufacturing to establish and improve the efficiency of the Laser Production lines. In this role, you will report to the Manufacturing Engineering Manager.



You Will Make an Impact By:

  • Assist in the development and implementation of manufacturing processes and procedures to improve efficiency and product quality
  • Tasks include qualifying active and passive optical components, developing and benchmarking system performance models, developing and implementing test procedures, and evaluating and selecting appropriate test instrumentation
  • Support production activities by troubleshooting issues and providing technical assistance to manufacturing technicians
  • Maintain and update manufacturing documentation, including work instructions, process flows, and standard operating procedures (SOPs)
  • Participate in continuous improvement initiatives, such as Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma, to enhance production processes
  • Ensure compliance with safety regulations and company policies, promoting a safe and productive work environment
  • Develop New Laser Product lines and improve production cycle time
  • Design and/or modify production lines for new customization or configuration
  • Maintain Manufacturing documentation and execute Engineering Changes for implementation.
  • Perform Failure Analysis on RMAs and Production issues.
  • Collaborate with other stakeholders to address technical issues and resolution
  • Support qualification of Optical components or setup for LEAN initiatives.


Skills You Bring:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Physics, or related field.
  • 3+ years of experience in laser processing, manufacturing engineering.
  • Experience with laser systems (e.g., UV, CO₂, fiber lasers), optics, and motion control systems.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a data-driven approach.
  • Familiarity with statistical process control (SPC), DOE, and Lean Six Sigma methodologies.
  • Excellent communication and teamwork skills.
  • Experience in a cleanroom or semiconductor manufacturing environment is a plus.

title: Physical Design Engineer
description:
Company Overview

JDI is a Venture Catalyst driving forward the future of innovation. We empower innovative companies to build, grow, and scale across Southeast Asia.
1) Tech JDI - Developing great tech solutions
2) Talent JDI – Growing human capital
3) Launch JDI – Accessing new markets
4) Capital JDI - Raising venture capital
5) Venture JDI - Building new ventures
Our clients range from fast-scaling start-ups to MNCS as well as Unicorns from industries such as e-commerce, cybersecurity, Fintech, F&B, Edtech, etc.

Job Purpose

You will be responsible for the physical implementation of large-scale, high-performance ASICs ranging from 100M to 400M gates. This role involves taking complex designs from RTL/Netlist through to GDSII, ensuring all timing, power, and area (PPA) goals are met using advanced process nodes (7nm down to 2nm).

Key Responsibilities

Full-Flow Implementation: Execute the complete physical design flow, including Floorplanning, Power Grid Design, Placement, Clock Tree Synthesis (CTS), and Routing.

Sign-off Verification: Perform physical verification (DRC/LVS/ERC) and Static Timing Analysis (STA) to ensure the design meets manufacturing requirements.

Architecture Optimization: Work closely with front-end teams to implement CPU/DSP architectures, translating complex algorithms into optimized physical layouts.

Advanced Node Management: Address challenges specific to 5nm, 3nm, and 2nm processes, such as multi-patterning, electromigration, and IR drop.

Automation: Develop and maintain scripts to automate EDA tool flows and improve design productivity.

Requirements

  • Programming: High proficiency in Python, Tcl, and Shell scripting.

  • Architecture Knowledge: Basic understanding of CPU/DSP data paths to better optimize tool placement algorithms.

  • Environment: Advanced UNIX/Linux administration skills (managing disk space, memory, and LSF/Grid jobs).

  • Degree: A Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering (EE), Electronic Engineering, Computer Engineering (CE), or a closely related technical field is the mandatory baseline.

title: Head of Finance
description:
About us

i6 provides the world’s most advanced end-to-end Aviation fuel management technology - enabling operational efficiency, transparency, and sustainability for airlines, fuel service providers, and suppliers.

Our cloud-based solutions digitise the entire aviation fuel lifecycle through real-time fuel inventory and into-plane management platforms, patented electronic refuelling technology, and advanced fuel analytics and insights.

With the adoption of our technology, our customers have been able to reduce thousands of tonnes of CO2 and benefited from millions in cost savings.

Role at i6 are primarily remote however for this role we require you to be able to come into our London office once a week.

Your new role

As the Head of Finance at i6 you will run the finance function day-to-day, owning financial control, reporting and the month-end close, and supporting the modernisation of the group's finance systems, processes and controls. You will report to the CFO, turning financial strategy into disciplined delivery, building a well-controlled and resilient function that scales with the business, and play a central role in preparing i6 for a successful exit. This is a hands-on senior role for someone who is equally comfortable owning the detail and building the team and systems around them.

What you will do

Financial Control, Reporting & Planning:

  • Own the month-end close, delivering accurate, well-reviewed accounts on a reliable timetable.

  • Own the group consolidation across multiple entities, including intercompany elimination.

  • Own the calculation and reporting of ARR, moving it from a manual exercise to an automated, reconciled output.

  • Support board-level reporting packs, KPIs and dashboards.

  • Maintain the general ledger, chart of accounts and tracking categories in Xero, with costs booked to the right entity.

  • Support revenue recognition across licence, transaction, professional services and hardware.

  • Support delivery of the annual budget and rolling forecast, measuring budget-vs-actuals, and maintain rolling cash-flow forecasting.

Systems, Process & Controls:

  • Support modernisation of the finance systems (PO/approvals, month-end, ARR, budgeting and reporting).

  • Design and document finance processes end-to-end, ensuring cross-cover to remove single points of failure.

  • Embed clear segregation of duties and review steps across payroll, month-end, and payments.

  • Partner with Procurement on the roll-out and running of the PO system, and drive cost discipline so spend is justified and delivers value.

Statutory, Tax, Treasury & Compliance:

  • Support accurate, compliant and timely year-end statutory accounts, and keep the function audit and diligence ready.

  • Support corporation tax, VAT and payroll compliance across all entities, coordinating outsourced partners.

  • Manage day-to-day cash, banking and liquidity, and the group's currency exposure.

Team & Exit Readiness:

  • Manage, develop and grow the finance team.

  • Support exit preparation: data room, quality-of-earnings, ARR substantiation and buyer due diligence.

Who you are

We want to hear from you if you have the below:

  • As a guide a Head of Finance at i6 should have 10-12+ years of relevant experience.

  • Fully qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA / CIMA).

  • Strong financial-control and reporting background, ideally including a Financial Controller or Head of Finance role in a growing business.

  • Proven ownership of the month-end close, group consolidation, and statutory accounts.

  • Experience designing and embedding financial controls and segregation of duties.

  • Hands-on and comfortable owning the detail in a lean team while building it out.

  • Strong financial-modelling skills.

You will be a great fit for this role if in addition to the above you have:

  • SaaS or recurring-revenue experience, with a good understanding of ARR and revenue recognition policies.

  • Track record of implementing or upgrading finance systems, automating and documenting processes.

  • Familiarity with Xero and related tools.

  • Multi-entity and international experience.

  • Experience supporting a funding round, or exit / due-diligence process.

  • Familiarity with the aviation or B2B software sectors.

A bit more about us

  • We’ve recently raised our Series B funding.

  • We are a remote first company with an office in Manchester and we will have an office in London at the end of 2026/early 2027. Each team has its own norms; the Finance Team will aim to meet in person once a week in the London office.

  • We aim for all of the company to come together for a day once a quarter.

  • Our benefits include: 25 days annual leave + your birthday day off, private healthcare and 5% pension contribution.

#LI-DP1

title: Sys/Test Validation Eng.
description:
ADVANCE YOUR CAREER. ADVANCE THE WORLD.

At AMD, we believe technology can change lives for the better. It can heal us, entertain us, and make us more connected, productive, and understanding of the world around us. And we’re looking for talent who feel the same: people who want to leave the planet better than they found it, those who don’t shy away from humanity’s challenges but are determined to help solve them.

AMD is powering the next generation of supercomputing, high-performance computing, cloud, and AI. Whether you’re designing next-gen processors, enabling AI breakthroughs, or creating go-to-market plans, every role at AMD contributes to something bigger — technology that moves the world forward.




THE ROLE:

We are looking for a Systems Test Validation Engineer with hands‑on experience in server BIOS and BMC validation, focusing on L10‑level system qualification.
In this role, you will execute L10 system test cases, verify BIOS and BMC functionality against release criteria, and identify, reproduce, and debug firmware‑related issues found during validation.
You will work directly with firmware developers, hardware developers, and board design teams to triage L10 failures track issues to resolution and confirm fixes through re‑validation. The role involves using platform‑level debug tools and system environments to ensure BIOS and BMC issues are addressed before release and do not result in customer‑visible quality escapes.

THE PERSON:

As a Systems Test Validation Engineer, you will deliver our next generation of system tests for our products. In this high visibility position, your systems engineering expertise will be necessary to find and resolve silicon platform issues.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Execute system‑level test cases to validate server BIOS and BMC functionality
  • Validate BIOS and BMC behavior across boot, power, reset, and management flows on server platforms
  • Analyze, reproduce, and document system‑level failures found during L10 validation
  • Work with firmware developers, hardware developers, and board design teams to debug and resolve BIOS/BMC issues
  • Support improvements to L10 validation coverage and test procedures based on defect findings
  • Assist in maintaining and enhancing test scripts or basic automation used in system validation

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE:

  • Experience in post‑silicon system validation or L10 system testing on server platforms
  • Basic familiarity with BIOS or BMC firmware validation, bring‑up, or board‑level testing
  • Ability to debug system‑level issues involving BIOS, BMC, firmware, or hardware interactions
  • General understanding of server platform architecture (CPU, memory, chipset)
  • Experience using scripting languages (Python, shell, etc.) for test or debug support
  • Interest in server hardware, firmware, and platform validation
  • Comfortable working with firmware, hardware, and board development teams to resolve issues
  • Effective communication skills working across large multi-functional HW, SW, architecture teams

ACADEMIC CREDENTIALS:

  • Bachelor’s or Master's degree in electrical engineering, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, or an equivalent

#LI-JW2




Benefits offered are described: AMD benefits at a glance.

AMD does not accept unsolicited resumes from headhunters, recruitment agencies, or fee-based recruitment services. AMD and its subsidiaries are equal opportunity, inclusive employers and will consider all applicants without regard to age, ancestry, color, marital status, medical condition, mental or physical disability, national origin, race, religion, political and/or third-party affiliation, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates and will accommodate applicants’ needs under the respective laws throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

AMD may use Artificial Intelligence to help screen, assess or select applicants for this position. AMD’s “Responsible AI Policy” is available here.

This posting is for an existing vacancy.

title: Neuwagenverkäufer/in EU-Reimporte (m/w/d)
description:
Du willst im Verkauf richtig Geld verdienen? Dann zeig, was du kannst.


Bei Kraska Automobile bekommst du keine Verkäuferstelle zum Absitzen. Du bekommst die Chance, mit Leistung, Disziplin und echter Kundenbegeisterung ein außergewöhnliches Einkommen zu erreichen.

Du verkaufst attraktive Neuwagen und EU-Reimporte verschiedener Marken – mit starken Ausstattungen, überzeugenden Preisvorteilen und kurzen Entscheidungswegen.

Für passende Finanzierungslösungen stehen dir Bank11, BDK und Santander zur Seite.

Und wir halten dir operativ den Rücken frei:
  • Die Disposition bereitet Fahrzeuge und Probefahrten vor.
  • Die Abwicklung übernimmt nach dem Abschluss die komplette Verkaufsnachbereitung bis zur Auslieferung.
  • Du konzentrierst dich auf Beratung, Finanzierung und Abschluss.
Wir liefern Produkte, Leads, Prozesse und Unterstützung. Du machst daraus Abschlüsse.

Der Kunde muss von dir begeistert sein.


Ein EU-Reimport ist für viele Kunden erklärungsbedürftig.

Genau hier kommst du ins Spiel: Du schaffst Klarheit, baust Vertrauen auf und erklärst verständlich, warum der Kunde bei uns einen hervorragend ausgestatteten Neuwagen zu attraktiven Konditionen bekommt.

Du hörst dem Kunden verdammt noch mal zu. Du findest heraus, welches Fahrzeug zu seinem Leben passt, welche Ausstattung er wirklich braucht und welche Finanzierung ihm einen echten Mehrwert bietet.

Darauf kommt es an:
  • Du bearbeitest jeden Lead schnell, diszipliniert und konsequent.
  • Du erklärst Modelle, Ausstattungen, Herkunft und Vorteile unserer EU-Reimporte sicher und transparent.
  • Du fasst aktiv nach und lässt keine echte Verkaufschance versanden.
  • Du kennst Fahrzeuge, Verfügbarkeiten und Finanzierungsprodukte im Schlaf.
  • Du hältst jede Zusage ein und übernimmst Verantwortung bis zum Abschluss.
  • Du behandelst jeden Kunden offen, fair und mit demselben Respekt.
Keine Märchen. Kein Druckverkauf. Klare Antworten, ehrliche Beratung und ein Kunde, der von dir begeistert ist.

Leistung ist eine Entscheidung.


Wir suchen keinen Prospektverteiler und niemanden, der auf Laufkundschaft wartet. Wir suchen einen Auto-Enthusiasten mit Energie, Empathie und Abschlusswillen.

Langjährige Autohauserfahrung ist nicht nötig. Erfahrung im Neuwagenverkauf ist willkommen – entscheidend sind aber deine Haltung, deine Lernbereitschaft und deine Disziplin.

Du passt zu uns, wenn du:
  • für Neuwagen, Technik und unterschiedliche Marken brennst,
  • komplexe Unterschiede einfach und überzeugend erklären kannst,
  • Menschen wirklich zuhörst und Vertrauen aufbaust,
  • diszipliniert arbeitest und Zusagen konsequent einhältst,
  • verbindlich nachfasst und klar nach dem Abschluss fragst,
  • Feedback annimmst und Gelerntes sofort umsetzt,
  • im Team gewinnen willst, ohne dich hinter dem Team zu verstecken.
Hunger, Disziplin und Verbindlichkeit musst du mitbringen. Produktwissen und Prozesse lernst du bei uns.

Wir liefern die Voraussetzungen. Du lieferst die Leistung.


Du bekommst kein leeres Motivationsgerede, sondern ein Umfeld, in dem du erfolgreich Neuwagen verkaufen kannst:

  • intensive Einarbeitung in EU-Reimporte, Modelle und Ausstattungen,
  • klares Sales-Onboarding und regelmäßiges Training durch externe Powerseller,
  • attraktive Neuwagen verschiedener Marken,
  • starke Finanzierungspartner und stabile Verkaufsprozesse,
  • Unterstützung durch Disposition und Abwicklung,
  • ein transparentes und leistungsorientiertes Provisionssystem.
In vergleichbaren, leistungsstarken Autohäusern können Top-Verkäufer 150.000 bis 250.000 Euro im Jahr erreichen. Das ist kein garantiertes Gehalt und kein Geschenk.

Dein tatsächliches Einkommen hängt von deiner Verkaufsleistung und den geltenden Provisionsbedingungen ab.

Wer durchschnittlich arbeitet, verdient durchschnittlich. Wer Leads diszipliniert bearbeitet, Kunden begeistert und Abschlüsse macht, wird richtig gut verdienen.

Wir versprechen dir nicht, dass es bequem wird.
Wir geben dir aber den Rahmen um erfolgreich zu sein.

Bereit für Vollgas?


Dann bewirb dich jetzt bei Kraska Automobile.

title: Senior Functional Safety Engineer
description:
Here at Humanoid, we believe in a future where robots amplify human potential. That’s why we’ve set out on a mission to build the world’s most capable, commercially-scalable, and safe humanoid robots. We’re bringing that mission to life with HMND‑01 Alpha - our rapidly developed humanoid platform now running in real industrial pilots - and we’re growing the team to take it even further.

About the Role

We are seeking a Senior Functional Safety Engineer to join our Systems Engineering team. This is a senior-level position that provides a unique opportunity to contribute to the development of cutting-edge safety-related systems for our industrial and home robots. You will be working closely with the Functional Safety Lead to support safety lifecycle activities and will be leading the development of safety-critical subsystems.

This is a high-impact role for an experienced engineer with a proven record of delivering and certifying safety-related systems, preferably in the robotics domain. A strong understanding of functional safety fundamentals & industry standards, capacity for detailed work on complex systems, and innovative thinking are essential qualities to be successful in this role.

What You'll Do

  • Lead or support activities across the complete safety lifecycle, starting from initial concept all the way to validation and certification.

  • Lead or support the development of novel safety assurance arguments, for individual subsystems as well as the overall products.

  • Contribute to safety concept definition and support detailed technical development of safety-critical systems.

  • Collaborate across disciplines to define, communicate, and validate safety-critical requirements for sensors, controllers, actuators including hardware and software.

  • Perform safety analyses like FMEA, FMEDA, FTA etc.

  • Create and maintain technical documentation to support the safety case, such as risk assessments, functional safety concept, safety requirement specifications (SRS), verification & validation plans and test reports.

  • Ensure compliance with regulations and international standards.

What We're Looking For

  • 5+ years experience and deep technical expertise in development of safety-critical systems.

  • Proven record of applying functional safety standards for embedded systems (e.g. IEC 61508, IEC 62061, ISO 13849, ISO 26262 or similar).

  • Experience with robotics safety standards (e.g. ISO 10218, ISO 13482, ISO 3691-4).

  • Experience with safety case development and certification, especially for autonomous systems.

  • Familiarity with safety standards for sensors and actuators (e.g. IEC 61496, IEC 62998, IEC 61800-5-2).

  • Understanding of AI functional safety standards is a plus (e.g. ISO/IEC TR 5469, ISO PAS 8800 or similar).

  • Hands-on experience of design, implementation and validation of safety systems for mobile robots or dynamic systems (e.g., AMRs, bipedal robots, exoskeletons, mobile manipulators or robots with collaborative applications).

  • Strong verbal and written communication skills; author technical documentation, navigate deep technical analysis with expert engineers, and deliver high-level summaries to an executive audience.

What We Offer

  • Meaningful time off to rest and recharge: 23 days of annual leave (accrued), 15 days of paid sick leave, and paid company holidays.

  • Fully funded private healthcare for UK employees, with broad provider access, virtual and in‑person care, and strong mental health and serious illness support.

  • Equity included–we believe builders should share in what they build.

  • Pension scheme with a total 8% contribution (5% employee, 3% employer) on full earnings.

  • Free daily breakfast, catered lunch, and snacks in‑office.

  • Collaboration with top‑tier engineers, researchers, and product experts in AI and robotics.

  • Freedom to influence the product and own key initiatives.

title: Finance Manager
description:
Position: Finance Manager

Location: Remote, within Nairobi

Reports to: Director of Operations, NurtureFirst

Dotted line: Chief Executive Officer, NurtureFirst

Contract Type: Full-time

About GDI

The Global Development Incubator, Inc. (GDI Africa), is a non-profit organization independently incorporated in Kenya in April 2017 and legally registered under the Non-Governmental Organizations Coordination Act of Kenya. GDI Africa is governed by a locally constituted Board and directly employs over fifty staff across the country and the African region. GDI is renowned for launching and operationalizing initiatives aimed at systemic and scaled change. GDI's mission is to bring together ideas, leaders, and capital to build and scale the next generation of social solutions. More on GDI's approach and work can be found at: www.gdiafrica.org


About NurtureFirst

NurtureFirst strengthens the systems that support Home-Based Childcare (HBCC) providers — locally and globally — so the world's youngest children can thrive in quality, nurturing childcare settings. We partner with governments, funders, and communities to design and scale policies and practices that uplift HBCC providers and transform early childhood outcomes.

Our work operates at two interconnected levels:

  • In-country systems change: We partner with governments, funders, and communities to design and scale policies and practices that uplift HBCC providers and transform early childhood outcomes. Currently operating in Kenya as our first country of focus, we plan to expand to additional countries over the next decade.
  • Global movement-building: Alongside our country-level work, we are building a Global HBCC Alliance with partner organizations to elevate the visibility of home-based childcare worldwide, share evidence and best practices, and accelerate adoption of supportive policies across contexts.

NurtureFirst is currently incubated as an initiative of GDI and is in the process of independent registration, expected to be completed in late 2026.


Role Overview

NurtureFirst is seeking a Finance Manager to design, establish, and steward the organization's financial function as it transitions from incubation to operational independence.

This is not a role that maintains an existing finance system; it is a role that helps build one.

The successful candidate will lead the design and implementation of NurtureFirst's financial architecture, controls environment, reporting capability, compliance framework, and audit readiness while supporting the transition from GDI's infrastructure to an independent operating model. This includes establishing the organization's standalone financial backbone, including financial controls, budgeting and reporting processes, payroll and statutory compliance, grant financial management, and audit readiness.

The role will initially own both the strategic and operational aspects of the function - from establishing financial systems and presenting to the Board, through to risk management, supporting payroll, grant reporting, statutory compliance, cash flow management, and monthly financial close processes.

Working closely with leadership, programs, fundraising, operations, external advisors, and the Board, the Finance Manager will serve as the primary steward of financial integrity, visibility, and compliance as NurtureFirst establishes itself as a credible, sustainable, independent organization.

This role requires both technical depth and judgment to operate effectively within a small, fast-moving team. The ideal candidate is comfortable building systems from scratch, engaging with auditors and tax advisers, supporting Board discussions, and translating financial information into clear insights for decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

Financial Infrastructure and Controls

  • Design and establish NurtureFirst's standalone financial architecture, including chart of accounts, financial policies, approval thresholds, delegations of authority, and reporting templates.
  • Implement maker-checker processes and internal controls from day one with a target of audit readiness before the first audit period.
  • Lead the selection and implementation of accounting, payroll, expense management, and financial reporting systems appropriate for NurtureFirst's size and complexity.
  • Establish banking arrangements, treasury processes, and cash management practices.
  • Develop and maintain a finance compliance calendar covering statutory obligations, donor requirements, audit deadlines, and internal reporting cycles.
  • Monitor regulatory and policy developments in Kenya that could pose compliance risk, and escalate emerging issues to the CEO in a timely manner

Budget Management and Financial Visibility

  • Own and maintain the organizational budget
  • Establish and run monthly and quarterly budget-versus-actual review processes with budget owners.
  • Provide leadership with clear visibility on burn rate, cash runway, committed expenditure, staffing costs, and forecast variances.
  • Lead cash flow forecasting and financial scenario modeling to support strategic decision-making.
  • Surface financial risks, underspend, savings opportunities, and emerging pressures proactively.

Grant Financial Management and Donor Reporting

  • Oversee the financial management of all grants and funding agreements.
  • Ensure expenditure is tracked against approved budgets and donor restrictions.
  • Review and approve donor financial reports prepared internally or through shared services arrangements.
  • Support proposal development through robust budgeting, cost modeling, and financial assumptions.
  • Oversee sub-award and partner financial management processes where applicable.

Governance, Audit and Compliance

  • Lead preparations for NurtureFirst's first independent audit and coordinate external auditors.
  • Develop finance reporting packs and dashboards for leadership and Board meetings.
  • Support the establishment and operation of the Finance and Audit Committee.
  • Ensure compliance with Kenyan statutory obligations, company law requirements, and donor compliance obligations.
  • Coordinate specialist tax, legal, transfer pricing, and regulatory advice where required.

Transition to Independence

  • Lead the financial decoupling from GDI Africa, including settling outstanding balances, transferring asset registers, reconciling sub-award accounts, and closing out the financial relationship cleanly
  • Establish NurtureFirst’s financial independence so the organization can receive, manage, and report on funds directly
  • Advise the CEO and Director of Operations on the financial implications of strategic decisions



Candidate Profile

Essential

  • Qualified accountant, CPA(K), ACCA, or equivalent professional certification
  • Minimum 8-10 years of progressive finance experience, including at least 3 years in a senior finance role within a nonprofit, INGO, or development organization
  • Demonstrated experience building financial systems and controls from scratch or during an organizational transition, not just maintaining existing ones
  • Knowledge of Kenyan statutory and regulatory requirements
  • Proven experience managing multi-donor grant portfolios with diverse reporting requirements, budget formats, and compliance standards
  • Experience preparing for and managing external audits, with a track record of clean audit outcomes
  • Strong financial planning and analysis capability, including budgeting, forecasting, cash flow management, and scenario modeling.
  • Proficiency in accounting systems (QuickBooks, Xero, or equivalent) and advanced Excel or Google Sheets
  • Ability to communicate financial information clearly to non-finance audiences; board members, program leads, and funders
  • High integrity, discretion, and professional judgment; trusted with sensitive financial information and organizational decisions
  • Experience working effectively in small, fast-moving environments where the ability to operate strategically and pragmatically is equally important.

Desirable

  • Experience with organizational spin-offs, entity setup, or financial decoupling from a parent organization
  • Familiarity with CLG (Company Limited by Guarantee) financial requirements in Kenya
  • Experience with transfer pricing, multi-entity financial flows, or fiscal sponsorship arrangements
  • Knowledge of international payroll or EOR arrangements for staff in multiple jurisdictions
  • Experience with early childhood, gender, or social infrastructure sectors

Why NurtureFirst

  • A defining moment: build the financial foundation of a new global organization from the ground up
  • Mission-driven team with a strong culture of care and excellence
  • Opportunity to shape not just finance, but the operational infrastructure of an organization at a pivotal inflection point

How to Apply

If this role resonates with you and you meet the qualifications above, please submit a single PDF document containing a cover letter and your CV. The cover letter should speak specifically to your experience building financial systems and managing multi-donor grant portfolios. Applications without a cover letter will not be considered.

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled and are to be submitted via https://www.gdiafrica.org/our-careers/

title: PL_KYC_Release Manager_Expert_TP#42820 (42450)
description:

Project

We are looking for an Expert Release Manager with extensive experience in large-scale enterprise environments to oversee release planning, governance, and execution for a leading Nordic bank, ensuring smooth and successful delivery of critical technology initiatives.

Responsibilities you'll have

  • Ensuring that new releases to Nordea's technology platforms and applications are planned and executed efficiently, with the highest quality for customers and operations, and in compliance with Nordea's standards.
  • Working with many different stakeholders such as other release managers, Test managers, Product owners, Execution leads, Scrum Masters, Operations teams etc, in order to secure a safe and successful delivery

  • Release and deployment planning and coordination of multiple parallel releases
  • Planning release go live and post-go-live activities.
  • Planning, coordinating and tracking change activities throughout the release process
  • Leading release decision meetings to secure stakeholder approval for each release milestone.
  • Planning and setting up handover sessions from development teams to operations teams
  • Increasing release quality and efficiency by integrating new techniques, automation capabilities, and DevOps ways of working to release orchestration

Expected competences and knowledge

  • 8+ years of experience in Release Management, IT Service Management, Change Management, or a related discipline
  • Proven experience in planning and coordinating go-live and post-go-live activities for complex IT releases
  • Strong ability to plan, coordinate, and track change activities across the entire release lifecycle in large-scale environments
  • Extensive experience leading release decision meetings and securing stakeholder approvals for key release milestones
  • Expert-level stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and align senior business and technical leaders
  • Strong risk management, problem-solving, and decision-making capabilities in high-pressure, fast-paced environments
  • Fluency in English and Polish (written and spoken) is required

Client – why choose this particular client from the Jit portfolio?

Jit Team has had an over-decade-long relationship with the leading financial group in the Nordic countries, and we are privileged to be our client's premier partner in Poland. At present, over 200 Jit personnel are engaged in the completion of more than 60 projects for this Norwegian major provider of financial services with a global presence and a strong focus on modern technology. Our customer's work atmosphere is epitomized by the Scandinavian culture, which is conducive to people who place emphasis on work-life balance and feedback culture. Furthermore, all projects are executed in international teams, giving constant exposure to the English language.

About Jit Team

The Human Factor of IT - it’s not just a slogan, it’s a philosophy. The foundation of Jit Team is people, which is why we prioritise you. We employ over 770 experienced experts, to whom we offer developmental projects, a wide range of benefits, and a proprietary professional development programme. Together with clients from around the world, we create innovative IT solutions delivered by highly specialised and efficient teams.

Behind our maxim are also charitable and educational activities. We support schools, universities, and educational institutions by conducting workshops, running educational projects, sponsoring events, and donating learning equipment. We offer internships that help launch careers in IT. We support water rescuers and hospitals by providing the necessary equipment. We also support numerous charitable organisations helping people in need, children, and the elderly. We are a Polish company and we share what we have achieved over more than 15 years of activity. By supporting local initiatives, we contribute to the circulation of good energy.

title: Assistant Manager/ Manager - Leasing (Retail)
description:
We are recruiting for a leading real estate investment and development company. They are well-know for their quality and owns a mix of residential, commercial and hospitality projects across Asia Pacific.

They are currently seeking an Assistant Manager – Leasing to join their team in Singapore. You will be supporting the leasing and marketing functions, execute strategic asset management strategies for the commercial portfolio. You will pro-actively engaging tenants and shareholders’ representatives to establish long-term business relationships, maximizing occupancy and yield of the portfolio. You will be involved in the budgeting and re-forecasting exercises and provide necessary analysis of the portfolio.

Key Responsibilities include:

You will develop and implement leasing strategy and tenant mix for retail properties to achieve maximum occupancy rate and income for portfolio. You will manage existing tenancies and establish/maintain good landlord-tenant relationships.

You will review lease proposals received and put forth recommendations as well as preparing and analysing monthly tenants’ sales performance report and work with tenants to improve revenue. You will ensure above-budget rent and competitive optimal lease terms for new or renewing tenants are achieved.

You will be engaging in negotiations to secure leasing deals and facilitate the proper execution of necessary leasing documents - from managing leasing enquiries to completion of lease documentation and ensure compliance with company operating procedures.

You will work with the Marketing team to enhance the awareness of branding of the mall, and the planning and execution of the advertising and promotional campaigns in the mall. You will monitor and review leasing & marketing operations and ensure proper policies and procedures are followed.

Requirements:

  • At least 5 years relevant experience in retail marketing and leasing.
  • Strong commercial and business acumen, strategic tenant mix planning, and excellent negotiation and communication abilities.

All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence. Personal data provided will be used for recruitment purposes only.

Please apply with your CV in word document format. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. For other positions related to real estate or other alternative investments, please go to https://careers.tri-cap.com.sg/.

title: AI Infrastructure Engineer, Serving Platform
description:
As a Software Engineer on the ML Infrastructure team, you will design and build platforms for scalable, reliable, and efficient serving of LLMs. Our platform powers cutting-edge research and production systems, supporting both internal and external use cases across various environments.

The ideal candidate combines strong ML fundamentals with deep expertise in backend system design. You’ll work in a highly collaborative environment, bridging research and engineering to deliver seamless experiences to our customers and accelerate innovation across the company.

You will:

  • Build and maintain fault-tolerant, high-performance systems for serving LLMs and other models at scale.
  • Build an internal platform to empower LLM capability discovery.
  • Collaborate with researchers and engineers to integrate and optimize models for production and research use cases.
  • Conduct architecture and design reviews to uphold best practices in system design and scalability.
  • Develop monitoring and observability solutions to ensure system health and performance.
  • Lead projects end-to-end, from requirements gathering to implementation, in a cross-functional environment.

Ideally you'd have:

  • 4+ years of experience building large-scale, high-performance backend systems.
  • Strong programming skills in one or more languages (e.g., Python, Go, Rust, C++).
  • Experience with LLM serving and routing fundamentals (e.g. rate limiting, token streaming, load balancing, budgets, etc.)
  • Experience with LLM capabilities and concepts such as reasoning, tool calling, prompt templates, etc.
  • Experience with containers and orchestration tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes).
  • Familiarity with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP) and infrastructure as code (e.g., Terraform).
  • Proven ability to solve complex problems and work independently in fast-moving environments.

Nice to haves:

  • Experience with modern LLM serving frameworks such as vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, or text-generation-inference.

PLEASE NOTE: Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.

About Us:

At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Ernst & Young, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.

We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.

We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's Know Your Rights poster for additional information.

We comply with the United States Department of Labor's Pay Transparency provision.

PLEASE NOTE: We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our privacy policy for additional information.

title: Consultant (m/w/d) Business Process Management
description:
Akkodis ist ein global führendes Unternehmen in Engineering, IT und F&E. Mit unserem 360°-Angebot unterstützen wir Kunden in einer dynamischen Technologie- und Wettbewerbswelt dabei, eine nachhaltige und smarte Zukunft zu gestalten. Unsere 50.000 visionären Köpfe weltweit treiben Innovation mit Ideenreichtum und Einsatzkraft voran - für eine intelligentere Welt von morgen.

Kundendetails

Für unseren renommierten Kunden aus der Luftfahrtbranche suchen wir Verstärkung für unser Team in Hamburg - zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt.

Stellenbeschreibung

  • Abstimmen von modellierten Prozessinhalten und zugehörigen Dokumenten mit dem verantwortlichen Process Owner beziehungsweise Process Architect, moderieren von Prozess Design Workshops
  • Initiieren von Prozessverbesserungen/-anpassungen des Process Excellence-Prozesses beziehungsweise des Process Excellence-IT-Tools
  • Zusammenarbeit und Kommunikation im Einklang mit dem Prozessmanagementrollen-Konzept (FAR+)
  • Multiplikator der Process Excellence-Methodik/Berater im Kontext des Process Excellence-Prozesses innerhalb der Kunden Group, beantworten von Nutzerfragen
  • Sonderaufgaben wahrnehmen, Projekte bearbeiten und begleiten, Fachbereiche beraten

Profil

  • Abgeschlossenes Hoch-/Fachhochschulstudium oder gleichwertige Fähigkeiten und Kenntnisse
  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse der erforderlichen Teile des integrierten Managementsystems bzw. Bereitschaft zur Einarbeitung in die Methoden und Konzepte
  • Methodenkenntnisse des Business Process Management (BPM) und entsprechender Systeme
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse (verhandlungssicher)
  • Sehr gute EDV-Kenntnisse (MS-Office), sehr gute Kenntnisse und Beherrschung der für die Prozessdokumentation erforderlichen Arbeitsmittel wie Modellierungstool, Dokumentenmanagementtool sowie Projektmanagement
  • Sehr gut ausgeprägtes konzeptionelles und analytisches Denkvermögen, Fähigkeiten sich schnell in neue Sachverhalte einarbeiten zu können sowie Initiative, Engagement und Kreativität
  • Sprachliche und didaktische Fähigkeiten, um komplexe betriebliche Prozesse/Dokumente zielgruppengerecht, klar und präzise darstellen zu können
  • Sicheres Auftreten, Überzeugungs- und Durchsetzungsvermögen, hohe Belastbarkeit
  • Ausgeprägte Planungs- Koordinations- und Dispositionsvermögen; Entscheidungsfähigkeit
  • Bereitschaft zu Einsätzen im In- und Ausland, zu berufsbezogener Weiterbildungen und ggf. zu einer psychologischen Eignungsuntersuchung

Das Angebot

  • Work-Life-Balance

Die Benefits können je nach Position und Standort variieren. Eine Übersicht über unsere Benefits befindet sich auf unserer Webseite unterhalb Karriere und Benefits.

title: SPS-Programmierer (m/w/d)
description:
**Wir sind Seconsy:** von der Planung über Fertigung und Aufbau sowie Wartung und Instandhaltung von Industrieanlagen und Industrietechnik. Wir begleiten wir den gesamten Spezialauftrag bzw. ermöglichen einen Einstieg in jeder Stufe des Planungs- und Fertigungsprozesses.

Automatisierungstechnik, Sonderanlagen, Anlagen, Förder- und Hebetechnik sowie Widerstandsschweißtechnik bei Automobilherstellern gehören zu unseren Entwicklungs- und Umsetzungsschwerpunkten. Langjährige Erfahrung, hohe Fertigungstiefe und Mut zu ungewöhnlichen Lösungen zeichnen uns aus.

Dabei bieten wir Full-Service aus einer Hand. Von der Konstruktion einfacher Schnittstellen bis hin zu komplexen Arbeitsabläufen verstehen wir uns als kompetenter Berater und Entwickler für unsere Kunden.

Wir suchen ab sofort eine/n **SPS-Programmierer** (m/w/d).

**Ihre Tätigkeit umfasst:**
-Erstellung von anlagenspezifischer SPS-Software und Inbetriebnahme der Anlagen beim Kunden
-Realisierung unterschiedlicher SPS-Software, wie Siemens TIA-Portal, S7
-Erstellung, Anpassung und Neudesign der Visualisierungen, wie Siemens TIA-Portal, WINCC oder VB
-Parametrierung und Inbetriebnahme von Frequenzumrichtern, vorzugsweise SEW und Siemens
-Einweisung und Schulung der Anlagenbediener/in beim Kunden
-Erstellung der Dokumentation
-Support
Die Tätigkeit wird im Rahmen einer Vollzeitbeschäftigung über 40 Stunden/ Woche mit Möglichkeit zur Nutzung von gleitender Arbeitszeit angeboten. Wir bieten einen modernen Arbeitsplatz mit viel Know how.

**Sie bringen mit:**
-Abgeschlossenes Studium oder alternativ eine Weiterbildung als staatlich geprüfte/r Techniker/in im Bereich Elektro- oder Automatisierungstechnik bzw. Mechatronik
-Sie sind erfahren in der Programmierung und Inbetriebnahme von SPS-Steuerungen.
-Persönlich überzeugen Sie durch ein hohes Maß an Begeisterungs- und Kommunikationsfähigkeit sowie der Bereitschaft, zu reisen.
-Führerschein Klasse B. Das Fahrzeug stellen wir.
-Gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse runden Ihr Profil ab.

Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

title: Clinical Biomarker Operations Specialist (m/f/d)
description:
As Clinical Biomarker Operations Specialist, you will support operational planning, coordination, and execution of clinical biomarker, translational sample, and bioanalytical activities across CatalYm’s clinical trials. You will help ensure that biomarker-related trial activities are implemented reliably, samples are collected and processed according to protocol requirements, vendors are qualified, contracted, and managed effectively, and biomarker and bioanalytical deliverables are completed on time and to a high standard.

You will work closely with Translational Research, Clinical Development, Clinical Operations, Clinical Pharmacology and Bioanalytics, Data Management, Biostatistics, Regulatory Affairs, Legal/Procurement, external laboratories, central laboratories, and CROs to support robust operational delivery of the clinical biomarker & bioanalytical program.

Your responsibilities include:
  • Support the operational implementation and day-to-day coordination of clinical biomarker, translational sample, and bioanalytical activities across ongoing clinical trials.
  • Coordinate biomarker-related trial activities, including sample collection, processing, shipment, tracking, reconciliation, laboratory testing coordination, and data transfers.
  • Contribute to the preparation, maintenance, and updates of biomarker- and bioanalytics-related clinical trial documents, including protocols, laboratory manuals, sample handling instructions, and vendor documentation.
  • Support operational set-up activities, including vendor identification, vendor qualification, contracting support, study start-up, sample logistics, assay readiness tracking, data transfer processes, and report delivery.
  • Coordinate activities with external vendors, specialty laboratories, central laboratories, CROs, and other service providers involved in sample handling, biomarker testing, bioanalytical testing, data delivery, and reporting.
  • Monitor biomarker and bioanalytical activitiesto help ensure execution according to study timelines, protocol requirements, and applicable quality standards.
  • Track operational milestones, risks, issues, and dependencies, and escalate issues and support resolution in collaboration with internal functions and external partners.
  • Review vendor deliverables for completeness and quality and follow up on outstanding items where needed.
  • Facilitate cross-functional communication between internal functions, external partners, clinical sites, central laboratories, and specialty laboratories.
  • Maintain clear documentation of operational plans, decisions, vendor communications, sample status, data transfers, contracts, study-level deliverables, and open issues.
  • Support budget tracking, purchase orders, contracts, change orders, and invoice review for biomarker- and bioanalytics-related vendors.
  • Contribute to continuous improvement of biomarker and bioanalytical operations processes, templates, vendor oversight practices, and cross-functional ways of working.
Who you are:
  • University degree in life sciences, biomedical sciences, clinical research, biotechnology, pharmacy, or a related discipline.
  • 2-5 years of experience in clinical trial operations, biomarker operations, clinical sample management, central laboratory operations, or a related operational role.
  • Good understanding of clinical trial workflows, including sample collection, processing, shipment, tracking, reconciliation, laboratory testing coordination, and data delivery.
  • Experience managing external vendors such as central laboratories, specialty laboratories, or CROs.
  • Experience with vendor qualification and onboarding, contracting processes and day-to-day vendor coordination within clinical trials.
  • Familiarity with clinical trial documentation, including protocols, laboratory manuals, sample handling instructions, vendor scopes of work, data transfer specifications, and operational plans.
  • Good understanding of GCP, clinical trial quality requirements, sample traceability, vendor oversight, and documentation standards.
  • Experience working in oncology clinical trials and/or biomarker-related clinical studies is preferred.
  • Strong organisational skills with the ability to coordinate multiple studies, timelines, and stakeholders in simultaneously.
  • Structured, detail-oriented, proactive, and able to identify operational issues and proactively work with colleagues to resolve them.
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across clinical, operational, laboratory, legal/procurement, and external partner teams.
What we offer:
  • The opportunity to contribute to the clinical development of a promising, innovative cancer therapy.
  • An operational role at the interface of translational research, clinical biomarkers, clinical operations, and clinical bioanalytics.
  • A collaborative and purpose-driven team environment.
  • Flexible working arrangements, including partial remote work while maintaining regular on-site collaboration.
  • A secure, permanent employment contract with competitive compensation and attractive employee benefits.
