title: Senior Front-End Engineer (Pixi.js)
description:
RedCore is an international business group that creates technological solutions for digital markets. Our products and services cover fintech, marketing, e-commerce, customer service, communications, and regulatory technologies.

We are looking for a Senior Front-End Engineer (Pixi.js) to join our team!

Requirements:

  • 5+ years in frontend engineering, 2+ years with 2D JavaScript Framework experience (Pixi.JS, Phaser, or similar)
  • Expert in HTML5, CSS/SCSS, responsive layouts, and component-driven architecture.
  • Familiar and can apply TDD/BDD like practices
  • Performance optimization (change detection, lazy loading, bundle splitting)
  • API integration: REST, WebSockets; robust error/edge-case handling.
  • CI/CD familiarity; feature flags, environment config, release hygiene.

Security basics (OWASP for web), auth flows (OAuth/OIDC/JWT).

Responsibilities:

  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable frontend services using modern web frameworks.
  • Take part in technical discussions to come up with solutions for challenging issues;
  • Optimize application performance, scalability, and security.
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and other developers to deliver seamless user experiences.
  • Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code, following coding standards.

Our benefits to you:
☘️An exciting and challenging job in a fast-growing business group, the opportunity to be part of a multicultural team of top professionals in Development, Architecture, Management, Operations, Marketing, Legal, Finance, and more
🤝🏻Great working atmosphere with passionate experts and leaders, sharing a friendly culture and a success-driven mindset is guaranteed
🧑🏻‍💻Modern corporate equipment based on macOS or Windows and additional equipment are provided
🏖Paid vacations, sick leave, personal events days, days off
💵Referral program — enjoy cooperation with your colleagues and get the bonus
📚Educational programs: regular internal training sessions, compensation for external education, attendance of specialized global conferences
🎯Rewards program for mentoring and coaching colleagues
🗣Free internal English courses
✈️In-house Travel Service
🦄Multiple internal activities: online platform for employees with quests, gamification, presents and news, clubs for movie / book / pets lovers and more
🎳Other benefits could be added based on your location.

title: Contribute, Grow and Recruitment Partner - APAC
description:
WHAT YOU'LL DO​

Recruitment Delivery & Candidate Journey

  • Manage the full life-cycle of assigned roles, ensuring high expertise and quality throughout
  • Build attraction strategies to proactively source top talent and develop pipelines for key roles
  • Assess and steer the competence assessment process; prepare business interviews and advise on hiring decisions
  • Prepare, deliver, and negotiate job offers; provide feedback to candidates and ensure a smooth handover to pre-boarding

Talent Advisory & Business Partnership

  • Serve as a trusted advisor and partner to hiring managers, ensuring expectation alignment and effective communication
  • Connect recruitment strategy to the broader People roadmap, monitor performance KPIs, and define action plans
  • Enable seamless collaboration between People Lifecycle, People Specialist Partnering, and Business Functions

Process Implementation

  • Drive high‑quality implementation and adoption of global Grow & Contribute processes by embedding them into day‑to‑day operations, ensuring relevance to local context while maintaining global intent and standards
  • Act as the local subject‑matter partner for Grow & Contribute, enabling leaders, People Business Partners, and People Generalists to confidently own and consistently apply people processes and tools
  • Act as a bridge between global design and local reality, representing local needs and constraints while reinforcing consistency in philosophy, principles, and ways of working
  • Facilitate, curate, and implement relevant learning programs, supporting local adaptations and strategies
  • Align performance development and learning processes with goals and organizational objectives

Insights & Support

  • Monitor effectiveness, adoption, and outcomes using data and feedback; translate insights into concrete improvement actions and provide structured input to global People PLC Specialists / SMEs to strengthen process design and usability
  • Support the Head of People / People Manager with specialist insight and analysis to inform people strategy, prioritization, and forward‑looking decisions linked to business needs
  • Measure and analyze leadership performance and development needs

Capability Building & People Development

  • Build sustainable people and leadership capability through training, enablement, and coaching, ensuring long‑term, independent application of processes and practices
  • Provide personalized coaching to managers for targeted skill development
  • Facilitate leadership development interventions based on local needs
  • Coach people leaders on goal setting, performance metrics, and improvement strategies

Insights & Performance Monitoring

  • Monitor effectiveness, adoption, and outcomes using data and feedback; translate insights into concrete improvement actions
  • Provide structured input to global People PLC Specialists / SMEs to strengthen process design and usability
  • Support the Head of People / People Manager with specialist insight and analysis to inform people strategy and prioritisation
  • Measure and analyse leadership performance and development needs

WHO YOU ARE

Education

  • Relevant university degree (e.g. Human Resources, Business Administration, or similar)

Skills

  • Strong stakeholder management, advisory, and communication skills
  • Proficiency in sourcing tools, recruitment systems, and data-driven hiring approaches
  • Ability to translate global frameworks into practical, locally workable solutions
  • Proven capability to build leadership capability, influence behaviour, and drive sustainable change
  • Strong facilitation and coaching skills, with confidence to operate across functions, markets, and regions
  • Excellent negotiation abilities and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

Experience

  • Proven experience in full-cycle recruitment or talent acquisition
  • Experience implementing and enabling people processes in complex, multi-stakeholder environments
  • Demonstrated accountability for adoption, outcomes, and continuous improvement

WHO WE ARE

H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​

We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​

​JOIN US​

Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it's our people who make us who we are.​

Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.

*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.

title: Stage Finance
description:
Geen meekijkstage. Een project waar jij het verschil maakt.

Jouw stage is meer dan alleen ervaring opdoen. Je krijgt de kans om verantwoordelijkheid te nemen voor een echt Finance-vraagstuk, zelfstandig onderzoek te doen en zichtbaar resultaat neer te zetten.

Tijdens deze Finance stage krijg jij de ruimte om zelfstandig onderzoek te doen, met verschillende stakeholders samen te werken en jouw financiële inzicht toe te passen in de praktijk. Jij brengt mensen, cijfers, systemen en processen samen en helpt bouwen aan een betrouwbare basis voor de toekomst.

Jouw stageopdracht: geef onze vaste activa-administratie een nieuwe basis

Tijdens deze stage werk je aan een uitdagend Finance-project met directe impact op de organisatie. Door de jaren heen is de administratie van onze vaste activa gegroeid en is het tijd om deze kritisch te beoordelen en verder te optimaliseren.

Je gaat op onderzoek uit en legt de verbinding tussen onze financiële administratie en de werkelijkheid op de werkvloer. Van grote machines en installaties tot gereedschappen, apparatuur en andere bedrijfsmiddelen: jij onderzoekt welke activa nog aanwezig zijn, waar ze worden gebruikt en of de administratie nog aansluit bij de praktijk.

Je spreekt met managers en andere stakeholders, verzamelt informatie vanuit verschillende afdelingen en analyseert de huidige administratie. Met jouw bevindingen help je mee om de vaste activa-administratie op te schonen, te actualiseren en weer overzichtelijk en betrouwbaar te maken.

Wat bieden wij jou?

Bij ons krijg je meer dan alleen een stageplek. Je komt terecht in een omgeving waar je echt mee mag draaien, veel kunt leren en de kans krijgt om jouw talenten verder te ontwikkelen.

  • Een uitdagende stageopdracht met blijvende impact op onze organisatie. Jouw werk draagt bij aan het verbeteren van onze processen.

  • Persoonlijke begeleiding door ervaren collega's die jou helpen groeien en jouw ontwikkeling ondersteunen.

  • De ruimte om zelfstandig te werken, initiatief te nemen en jouw ideeën in te brengen.

  • Een stagevergoeding van € 600,- bruto per maand op basis van een fulltime stage.

  • Tussendoor een weekje uitrusten op een tropisch eiland? Een gezonde werk-privébalans vinden wij belangrijk. Daarom bouw je tijdens je stage verlof op en krijg je de ruimte om werk en persoonlijke tijd goed in balans te houden.

  • De mogelijkheid om waardevolle praktijkervaring op te doen en een sterke basis te leggen voor jouw toekomstige carrière.

Jij brengt mee:

  • Je volgt een opleiding op minimaal MBO-niveau, bij voorkeur richting Finance, Administratie, Bedrijfseconomie of een vergelijkbare studie.

  • Je bent beschikbaar voor minimaal 3 dagen per week.

  • Je bent nauwkeurig, nieuwsgierig en hebt een proactieve, projectmatige werkhouding. Je neemt initiatief, houdt overzicht en werkt zelfstandig aan jouw opdracht.

  • Je stelt graag vragen, zoekt actief de samenwerking op en wilt onderdeel zijn van ons Finance-team.

title: Estates Operator
description:
Join us as an Estates Officer where you will work across all Project sites supporting the Land, Estates, Environment and Permitting team in managing the wider Estate in line with agreed management plans.

Duties will include:

  • Undertaking land and estate management activities, including vegetation control, grass seeding, grass cutting, and tree management operations.

  • Delivering practical implementation of the Biodiversity Enhancement and Estates Management Plans through habitat creation, maintenance, monitoring, and management.

  • Assisting ecologists with ecological surveys, monitoring programmes, and other project-specific environmental requirements.

  • Carrying out environmental monitoring activities, including dust sampling, water sampling and monitoring, and management of site water discharges as required.

  • Inspecting, maintaining, and servicing environmental monitoring equipment, including noise monitors and associated battery replacements.

  • Maintaining site infrastructure, including ditches, fencing, and other estate assets.

  • Responding to environmental incidents and spillages occurring outside operational working platforms.

  • Undertaking site-wide litter collection and general housekeeping activities to maintain high environmental and operational standards.

  • Carrying out pest control activities across the site, including working platforms, welfare facilities, and other operational areas as required.

  • Supporting the delivery of general estate maintenance and improvement works.

  • Undertaking any other duties reasonably required in support of the project's land, estate, environmental, and biodiversity management objectives.

  • Tickets for Tractor, Telehandler, Chainsaw and Mini Digger desirable

  • Pesticide application certificates desirable

Technical Knowledge:

  • Knowledge of wildlife and habitat management

  • Experience of managing areas of special designation including SPA`s, SSSI`s and Ancient Woodland desirable but not essential

  • Experience of working within a busy construction environment

Other Requirements:

  • Be able to work across all project sites as required
  • Driving license is an advantage

How we are committed to your safety:

Nothing is more important to us than ensuring you return home safely after a day’s work. To make that happen, we have the most rigorous safety standards in the industry. Not only that, we’re also continually investing in new technologies – from drones to data analytics – that are helping to make mining safer.

Inclusion and Diversity:

Anglo American is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to promoting an inclusive and diverse workplace where we value and respect every colleague for who they are and provide equality of opportunity so that everyone can fulfil their potential.

How to apply:

To apply for this role, please complete our online application form. You will have the opportunity to upload your CV and other relevant documentation as part of the process.

title: Senior Project Manager – Global Logistics Integration (Life Sciences)
description:
Estamos colaborando con una compañía farmacéutica líder a nivel mundial en la incorporación de un Senior Project Manager para liderar un programa estratégico de integración logística en Latinoamérica.

Buscamos un perfil con experiencia gestionando proyectos internacionales complejos, capaz de coordinar equipos multidisciplinares y actuar como nexo entre negocio, IT y proveedores externos en un entorno global.

¿Cuál será tu misión?

Serás responsable de coordinar la implantación de la solución entre la organización y los diferentes proveedores logísticos (3PL), así como con los integradores encargados de coordinar múltiples operadores logísticos (4PL), garantizando una ejecución alineada con los objetivos del negocio, los plazos y los estándares de calidad.

Responsabilidades

  • Liderar un programa internacional de integración logística en LATAM.

  • Coordinar la relación entre negocio, IT, proveedores logísticos (3PL) e integradores (4PL).

  • Planificar y gobernar el proyecto, gestionando alcance, planificación, riesgos, dependencias e incidencias.

  • Preparar y presentar el seguimiento ejecutivo a los diferentes stakeholders.

  • Coordinar actividades de testing (SIT/UAT/OQ/PQ), cutover, migración de datos y despliegue.

  • Asegurar la correcta coordinación entre equipos locales, regionales y globales.

  • Impulsar la colaboración entre todas las partes involucradas para garantizar una implantación exitosa.

Buscamos un perfil con:

  • Más de 8 años de experiencia como Project Manager o Program Manager en proyectos internacionales.

  • Experiencia en implantaciones complejas dentro de entornos Life Sciences, Pharma o Healthcare.

  • Experiencia coordinando proveedores logísticos (3PL) y/o integradores logísticos (4PL).

  • Sólidas habilidades de gestión de stakeholders, gobierno de proyectos y coordinación de equipos internacionales.

  • Experiencia en procesos de testing, cutover, data migration y go-live.

  • Inglés fluido para trabajar en un entorno completamente internacional.

Se valorará especialmente

  • Haber trabajado previamente en Novartis o en proyectos para Novartis.

  • Conocimiento del ecosistema NVS/LDC.

  • Participación en despliegues internacionales, especialmente en Latinoamérica.

  • Experiencia en proyectos de Supply Chain, Logistics o Distribution.

¿Qué ofrecemos?

  • Participación en un programa global de alta visibilidad dentro del sector farmacéutico.

  • Entorno internacional con colaboración continua con equipos de Europa y LATAM.

  • Proyecto de transformación estratégica con elevado impacto en negocio.

  • Trabajo con equipos multiculturales y stakeholders de primer nivel.

En Eraneos creemos que la diversidad impulsa la innovación. Valoramos a las personas por su talento, experiencia y potencial, promoviendo un entorno de trabajo inclusivo donde todas las candidaturas son consideradas de forma equitativa, sin distinción de género, identidad o expresión de género, orientación sexual, origen étnico o nacional, edad, discapacidad, religión o cualquier otra circunstancia protegida por la legislación vigente. Nos comprometemos a ofrecer un proceso de selección basado exclusivamente en las competencias, la experiencia y el mérito profesional.

title: Steuerberater (m/w/d) in Hasloh, auf Wunsch mit Partnerperspektive, gesucht - mindestens 90.000€
description:
TaxTalente ist die Nr. 1 Job-Matching Plattform für Jobsuchende in der Steuerberatung.

Sie suchen eine positive berufliche Veränderung? Sie möchten den nächsten Schritt in Ihrer Karriere gehen und streben möglicherweise sogar eine Partnerschaft in einer zukunftsorientierten Kanzlei an? Sie möchten endlich wieder in einem positiven Arbeitsumfeld arbeiten, in dem Sie finanziell und persönlich wertgeschätzt werden? Sie suchen nach Tätigkeiten, die Sie auch wirklich weiterbringen?

Sofern Sie sich in mindestens einem der genannten Punkte wiederfinden, sollten Sie sich bei uns melden. Wir haben die attraktivsten Arbeitgeber der Steuerberatungsbranche in unserem Netzwerk und bringen zusammen, was zusammengehört. Grenzenlose Entwicklungschancen mit Partnermöglichkeiten, vielschichtige Tätigkeitsbereiche, Gehälter wie in der Industrie und vieles mehr – melden Sie sich bei uns und wir unterstützen Sie auf Ihrem weiteren Karriereweg!

Derzeit suchen wir nach einem Steuerberater (m/w/d) für eine unserer Partner-Kanzleien in Hasloh

Bei Rückfragen kannst du dich gerne jederzeit (auch nach deinem Feierabend) direkt telefonisch an Herrn Becker-Koch über folgende Nummer wenden: 015901431130

Aufgaben

Wir bringen zusammen, was zusammengehört. Dabei spielt das passende Tätigkeitsprofil natürlich eine entscheidende Rolle. Bei unserer Partnerkanzlei können Sie sich Ihren Aufgabenbereich auf Basis Ihrer persönlichen Stärken und Schwächen selbst zusammenstellen.

  • Erstellung und Review von Jahresabschlüssen
  • Erstellung von Steuererklärungen für Personen- und Kapitalgesellschaften
  • Steuerliche Sonderberatungsprojekte auf Basis individueller QualifikationenB. in den Bereichen der Erbschaftsteuer oder des Umwandlungssteuerrechts
  • Begleitung von Betriebsprüfungen
  • Teamleitung und Personalverantwortung (optional)
  • Weitere individuell wählbare Tätigkeitsbereiche, die Ihnen Freude bringen

Qualifikation

Am Ende sollte es für beide Seiten passen. Deshalb wäre es gut, wenn Sie sich in den folgenden Punkten wiederfinden.

  • Kommunikative Art und Freude am Austausch mit Mandanten
  • Sorgfältige, effiziente und gewissenhafte Arbeitsweise
  • Offen für eine digitale Arbeitsweise
  • Führungsverständnis und Verantwortungsbewusstsein gegenüber Mitarbeitern (optional)
  • Ein erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Steuerberaterexamen
  • Leidenschaft für steuerliche Themen

Benefits

Sie haben genug von 0815 Benefits, von denen Sie nicht wirklich profitieren? Dann sollten Sie einen Blick auf die nachfolgenden Benefits werfen.

  • Außerordentliche Vergütung von 90.000 € bis 130.000 € / Jahr, auf Wunsch auch Partnerperspektive
  • Flexible Gestaltung der Arbeitszeiten sowie die Option auf Home-Office
  • Nettolohnoptimierung mit monatlichen 50,00€ Sachbezugsgutscheinen
  • Umfangreiche Unterstützung bei Weiterbildungsmaßnahmen wie z.B. Fachberater Qualifikationen
  • Modernste Ausstattung mit digitalen Prozessen
  • Starker Teamzusammenhalt und regelmäßige Teamevents

Schnell, unkompliziert und effizient Ihren Traumjob finden – ein Versprechen an Sie, dass wir aufgrund unseres selektiven, breit gefächerten und deutschlandweiten Kanzleinetzwerks halten werden. lhre Vorteile mit uns auf einen Blick:

  • Sie führen keine unsinnigen Bewerbungsgespräche mehr, bei denen Sie während des Bewerbungsprozesses merken, dass es (bspw. wegen des Gehalts) sowieso nicht passt - wir klären vor Ihrem Bewerbungsgespräch all Ihre mit uns besprochenen Kriterien mit der Kanzlei ab.
  • Wir führen Ihre Gehaltsverhandlungen und kennen die Gehaltsstrukturen der Branche dabei bestens.
  • 100% Anonymität – solange Sie uns nicht das „Go“ geben und einem Kennenlernen mit Ihrem neuen potentiellen Arbeitgeber zustimmen, erfährt niemand von Ihrem Namen oder Ihren bisherigen Arbeitgebern – Ihre Ihnen wichtigen Kriterien können wir auch zunächst anonym mit unseren Partnerkanzleien klären.
  • Attraktive Kanzleien – die Kanzleien in unserem breit gefächerten Kanzleinetzwerk sind selektiv ausgewählt und heben sich klar von “normalen” Kanzleien ab (z.B. hinsichtlich der Vergütung, Benefits, Hierarchien, Digitalisierungsgrad, Ausstattung etc.).
  • Und das Beste: Unser gesamter Service ist unverbindlich und völlig kostenfrei für Sie.

Find more English Speaking Jobs in Germany on Arbeitnow

title: Køkken chef /Head Chef – Shawarma Masteren
description:

title: Kaufmännischer Mitarbeiter (m/w/d) Faktura und Rechnungsprüfung
description:
Seit über 120 Jahren steht die bauwelt Delmes Heitmann für Tradition, Innovation und erstklassigen Service im Baustoffhandel. Mit zwölf Standorten und rund 400 engagierten Mitarbeitenden bieten wir Ihnen beste Chancen, Ihre berufliche Zukunft in einem dynamischen und familiären Umfeld zu gestalten.

Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams am Standort Seevetal suchen wir eine engagierte Persönlichkeit für unsere zentral organisierte Abteilung Faktura und Rechnungsprüfung. Von hier aus werden die entsprechenden kaufmännischen Vorgänge für das gesamte Unternehmen bearbeitet. Als modernes Unternehmen setzen wir auf leistungsfähige ERP-Systeme und einen hohen Digitalisierungsgrad. Viele Routineaufgaben werden dadurch effizient unterstützt. So bleibt mehr Raum für kaufmännisches Denken, sorgfältige Prüfungen und die enge Zusammenarbeit mit den Fachbereichen.
  • Prüfung und Buchung von Eingangsrechnungen
  • Sicherstellung einer korrekten und termingerechten Fakturierung
  • Klärung von Rechnungsdifferenzen in enger Abstimmung mit den beteiligten Fachbereichen
  • Bearbeitung der kaufmännischen Vorgänge für die Ihnen zugeordneten Standorte innerhalb unserer zentral organisierten Abteilung
  • Mitwirkung bei der kontinuierlichen Optimierung und Weiterentwicklung von Abläufen und Prozessen im Bereich Faktura und Rechnungsprüfung
  • Unterstützung der Fachbereiche bei kaufmännischen Fragestellungen im Zusammenhang mit Faktura und Rechnungsprüfung
  • Abgeschlossene kaufmännische Ausbildung oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
  • Berufserfahrung in der Faktura, Rechnungsprüfung oder einem vergleichbaren kaufmännischen Tätigkeitsfeld – alternativ im Vertrieb oder in anderen kaufmännischen Bereichen des Baustoffhandels
  • Idealerweise bringen Sie Erfahrung aus dem Baustoffhandel mit und kennen die dortigen kaufmännischen Abläufe sowie den Umgang mit ERP-Systemen – vorzugsweise Prohibis
  • Ein gutes Verständnis für kaufmännische Prozesse und deren Zusammenhänge
  • Freude an der Zusammenarbeit im Team und mit verschiedenen Fachbereichen
  • Eine strukturierte, sorgfältige und eigenverantwortliche Arbeitsweise
  • Sicherer Umgang mit den gängigen MS Office-Anwendungen
  • Auf uns ist Verlass: Der starke Zusammenhalt in der bauwelt-Familie ist die Grundlage für unseren Erfolg
  • Sicher und fair entlohnt: Attraktives Gehaltspaket mit freiwilligen Sonderzahlungen und Prämien
  • Mehr fürs Leben: Monatlicher 50-€-Gutschein, einlösbar bei zahlreichen namhaften Anbietern
  • Starthilfe: Ein gezieltes Onboarding mit persönlicher Begleitung erleichtert Ihnen den Einstieg
  • Für Ihre Zukunft bestens vorgesorgt: Wir bezuschussen Ihre vermögenswirksamen Leistungen und bieten eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge
Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre vollständigen Bewerbungsunterlagen mit Angabe Ihrer Gehaltsvorstellung – gern per E-Mail.

bauwelt Delmes Heitmann GmbH & Co. KG

title: Clean Energy Co-Founder / Head of Sales (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

  • You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Clean Energy

  • You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management

  • You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs

  • You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding

  • You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively

  • You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate

  • You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues

  • You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: Senior Performance Marketing Manager (m/w/d)
description:
In den Weiten der Veranstaltersphären suchen wir ab sofort tatkräftige Unterstützung für unser Office in Dresden um weitere, höhere und schnellere Kreise im Eventhimmel zu ziehen. Du denkst Marketing nicht in einzelnen Posts, sondern in Zielen, Zielgruppen, Funnels und messbaren Ergebnissen? Dann solltest Du weiterlesen.

Für vibe suchen wir einen Senior Performance Marketing Manager, der strategisches Denken mit operativer Stärke verbindet. Du übersetzt unsere Unternehmens- und Marketingziele in wirksame Kampagnen, steuerst Budgets und entwickelst unsere digitale Leadgenerierung konsequent weiter.
  • Du übersetzt unsere Unternehmens- und Marketingziele in konkrete digitale Strategien, Kampagnen und Maßnahmen.
  • Du planst, steuerst und optimierst unsere digitalen Marketingaktivitäten mit Fokus auf qualifizierte B2B-Leads, Reichweite und Markenbekanntheit.
  • Du entwickelst unsere Paid-Social-Kampagnen ganzheitlich weiter – von Zielgruppen und Kampagnenstruktur über Botschaften und Creatives bis zum Testing.
  • Du verantwortest Kampagnenbudgets innerhalb eines abgestimmten Rahmens und steuerst sie datenbasiert anhand relevanter KPIs.
  • Du analysierst Kampagnen- und Funnel-Daten, leitest konkrete Optimierungen ab und machst die Wirkung des Marketings in aussagekräftigen Reportings sichtbar.
  • Du entwickelst gemeinsam mit dem Team Anzeigen, Landingpages und Inhalte und setzt bei Bedarf selbst Texte sowie einfache visuelle Assets um.
  • Du nutzt KI gezielt für Analysen, Kampagnenoptimierung, Content-Produktion und effizientere Marketingprozesse.
  • Du verfügst über mindestens fünf Jahre relevante Berufserfahrung im Digital- oder Performance-Marketing.
  • Du hast fundierte Erfahrung im Paid Social Marketing und kannst Kampagnen von der Strategie bis zur operativen Umsetzung steuern.
  • Du denkst sicher in Zielgruppen, Customer Journeys, Kampagnenstrukturen, Botschaften, Creatives und Landingpages.
  • Du arbeitest konsequent mit KPIs und kannst Kennzahlen wie Conversion Rate, Cost per Lead, Click-Through-Rate und Leadqualität fundiert bewerten.
  • Du hast Erfahrung in der Steuerung von Marketingbudgets und verstehst Marketing als messbaren Beitrag zu Unternehmenszielen.
  • Du setzt moderne KI-Tools sicher für Analysen, Optimierung und Content-Produktion ein und weißt sie durch Dein eigenes Gespür für Copywriting und Design zu bewerten.
  • Du verbindest strategischen Weitblick mit Hands-on-Mentalität.
  • Kenntnisse in SEA, CMS oder im B2B Scale-Up-Umfeld sind ein Plus.
  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten: Privaten und beruflichen Takt bringst Du bei uns in Einklang.
  • Individuelles technisches Setup: Du erhältst die Technik für einen flüssigen Workflow.
  • Lunch-Meal Bonus: Als Teammitglied erhältst Du einen Rabatt auf Deine Mittagsbestellungen.
  • Rabatte & Tickets: Als Partner vieler Events kommt unser Team in den Genuss exklusiver Tickets.
  • Persönliche Weiterbildung: Uns sind Freiräume wichtig, damit Du auch Neues dazulernen kannst.
  • Team Happenings: Ob Feierabendbier oder gemeinsames BBQ, wir feiern den Team Vibe.
  • Mindestens fünf Jahre Erfahrung im Digital- oder Performance-Marketing
  • Strategische und operative Verantwortung für digitale Marketingmaßnahmen
  • Fachlicher Schwerpunkt auf Performance Marketing und Paid Social
  • Fokus auf qualifizierte B2B-Leads, Reichweite und Markenbekanntheit
  • Sicherer Umgang mit KPIs, Reportings und Kampagnenbudgets
  • Fortgeschrittener Einsatz von KI für Analyse, Optimierung und Content-Produktion
  • Strategisches Denken kombiniert mit echter Hands-on-Mentalität

title: Housekeeping Room Attendant/General Cleaner
description:

title: Global Marketing Manager*
description:
Wir suchen ab sofort für unser Team in Deutschland einen Global Marketing Manager*.

Du bringst globale Marketingkampagnen und -projekte über mehrere Regionen hinweg auf die Straße - für unsere Specialist Teams in den Ökosystemen SAP, ServiceNow und Microsoft. Planung und Umsetzung liegen bei dir in einer Hand: Du denkst dir nicht nur was aus, du packst auch selbst mit an.

Diese Rolle ist explizit für Allrounder gemacht. Du denkst und arbeitest gerne über Disziplinen hinweg - von der Kampagnenplanung über Content und Web bis zur Marketing Automation - dann sprechen wir deine Sprache.


* Geschlecht egal, Hauptsache Du passt zu uns! Deine Aufgaben:
  • Du projektierst und steuerst globale Marketingprojekte und -kampagnen über unsere verschiedenen Regionen hinweg
  • Du koordinierst unsere Specialist Teams (SAP, ServiceNow, Microsoft) sowie die regionalen Marketing-Einheiten
  • Du setzt selbst um - über mehrere Disziplinen hinweg: Kampagnen, Content, Web, Marketing Automation
  • Du trägst zur Demand- und Lead-Generierung entlang unserer globalen Kampagnen bei
  • Du steuerst die operative Umsetzung entlang Timeline, Budget, Stakeholdern und Abhängigkeiten
  • Du sorgst für Markenkonsistenz und Qualität über alle Regionen hinweg
Das bringst du mit:
  • Mindestens 5 Jahre Erfahrung im Digital Marketing
  • Mindestens 3 Jahre B2B-Marketing-Erfahrung im Enterprise-IT-/SaaS-Umfeld
  • Ein breites Skillset über mehrere Marketing-Disziplinen hinweg: du bist Generalist aus Überzeugung, nicht aus Mangel an Tiefe
  • Ein gutes Verständnis für Marketing-Projektsteuerung
  • Verhandlungssicheres Deutsch und Englisch für die Zusammenarbeit in unserem internationalen Team

Das ist ein Plus:
  • Du hast Agenturerfahrung
  • Kenntnisse in Demand- und Lead-Generierung
  • Erfahrung im Ökosystem SAP, ServiceNow oder Microsoft (Partner- oder Dienstleisterumfeld)
  • Sicherer Umgang mit WordPress/Elementor, Asana, Google Analytics, HubSpot
Bei uns erwartet dich nicht nur ein Job, sondern eine werteorientierte Zusammenarbeit in einem globalen Team. Wir leben und fördern unsere selbstbestimmten Core Values Collaboration, Happiness, Trust, Commitment, Evolution und Entrepreneurship.

Was wir Dir abseits vom Standard anbieten:

  • Deine mentale Gesundheit liegt uns am Herzen! Deshalb bieten wir dir Zugang und Unterstützung zu allen mental health bezogenen Themen durch unsere Kooperation mit nilo.health. Von Präventionsangeboten bis hin zur Akutbetreuung - wir sind für dich da.
  • Flexibilität ist uns wichtig! Mit einem Remote Arbeitsvertrag kannst du bequem von zu Hause aus arbeiten. Wir unterstützen dich auch bei der Ausstattung deines Home-Office Arbeitsplatzes, damit du dich optimal entfalten kannst.
  • Vereinbarkeit von Job & Privatleben! Neben flexiblen Arbeitszeiten, bieten wir zusätzliche Kind-Krankheitstage an, um dir familiäre Herausforderungen wie die Kinderbetreuung zu erleichtern.
  • Wir investieren in deine Weiterbildung! Mit einem jährlichen Budget hast du die Möglichkeit, dich fachlich und persönlich weiterzuentwickeln. Nutze diese Chance, um neue Fähigkeiten zu erlernen und dich kontinuierlich zu verbessern.
  • Bei uns wird Teamarbeit großgeschrieben! Neben Gesundheitszuschüssen bieten wir dir auch Team-interne Wertschätzungstools, um deine Leistungen zu würdigen. Zudem erwarten dich spannende Veranstaltungen wie unsere Internationale Co-Working Week, Sommerfest, Weihnachtsfeier und Team-Co Workings.
  • Wir stehen für Gelebte Chancengleichheit! Bei uns sind alle willkommen, unabhängig von Geschlecht, Herkunft oder Lebenshintergrund. In Initiativen wie unserem Evora-Women-Call geben wir den Raum, spezifische Themen direkt und ohne Umwege ans Management zu bringen.
  • Wir belohnen deinen Erfolg! Nach Abschluss deiner Probezeit stehen dir verschiedene Optionen wie Firmenwagen, Firmenrad oder Bahncard zur Verfügung. Zudem bieten wir eine gehaltsbezogene Beteiligung an den Unternehmenszielen und -erfolgen.

Bist du bereit, ein Evorian zu werden und von unseren einzigartigen Benefits zu profitieren? Dann bewirb dich jetzt! Wir freuen uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen.

title: Porgera | Scaffolder - Scaffolding
description:
Scaffolder - Scaffolding

About New Porgera

New Porgera Limited (NPL) is a leading mining company dedicated to sustainable practices and community engagement in Papua New Guinea. As the operator of the Porgera Gold Mine, we focus on extracting high-quality mineral resources while prioritizing the well-being of our employees, the environment, and the local communities. Our commitment to safety, innovation, and responsible mining ensures that we not only contribute to the economy but also support the development and prosperity of the regions we operate in. At NPL, we strive to create a positive impact, fostering strong relationships with stakeholders and promoting sustainable growth for future generations.

New Porgera Limited, a leading name in the mining industry, is currently seeking a skilled and experienced Scaffolder to join our Fixed Plant Maintenance Department. The successful candidate will play a vital role in ensuring the safe and efficient setup and dismantling of scaffolding structures within our mining site.

In this role, you will:

  • Safely and efficiently set up and dismantle scaffolding structures according to project requirements.

  • Perform routine inspections and maintenance on scaffolding equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards.

  • Collaborate with maintenance and construction teams to plan and execute scaffolding activities.

  • Adhere to all safety protocols, guidelines, and regulations during scaffolding operations.

  • Reporting to the Supervisor immediately any actual or potential unsafe conditions or activities, mishaps, incidents, accidents, injuries or illnesses in the work place.

  • Assisting new employees in the application of the proper work procedures and practices.

  • Using personal protective clothing or equipment as provided.

  • Reporting any defective or damaged personal protective clothing, equipment, or gear, and returning the item to the Supervisor for replacement or repair.

  • Complying with instructions given pertaining to the use of personal protective clothing, equipment or gear or any matter in relation to health and safety

  • Not interfering with anything that has been provided in the interest of health and safety in the workplace.

  • Not engaging in any duties that require a certificate or written authorization unless the relevant certificate or permit is held.

  • Not attempting any task unless they are trained, capable and competent to safely perform the task.

  • Cooperating fully with any rehabilitation program to assist recovery from injury or illness for themselves or any other person.

About You

To succeed in this role, the ideal candidate will possess the following qualifications and experiences:

  • SA - Advanced Scaffold certificate

  • Proven experience in building complex and hanging scaffolds, preferably in a mining or heavy equipment environment.

  • Strong understanding of scaffolding techniques and safety procedures.

  • Ability to work at heights/Work at heights qualification

  • Confined space qualification

  • Ability to interpret and follow technical manuals and specifications.

  • Safety hazard awareness in JHA or JSA, Zero Tasol, Take 5, FLRA, Risk Assessments

  • Physical stamina and ability to lift and work in various weather conditions.

  • Excellent communication and teamwork skills.

Mobilization Requirement

This is a PNG National Award position, a site-based role on a Walk in Walk out arrangement with rotation of 20 days on and 8 days off. Must successfully pass a pre-employment medical including drug and alcohol screening. (This will be arranged only for successful candidates who progress after the interview stage) and have an up-to-date Police Clearance Certificate (acquired within the last 6 months). Must a be Local Porgeran (Cat 1A – SML, Cat 1B –LMP or Cat 2 – Other Porgera) candidate and available to start immediately. Females are encouraged to apply.

Join us at New Porgera Limited and be part of a forward-thinking team dedicated to excellence!

Note: This advert will be open until 31st of July 2026. If you do not hear from NPL within the next seven (7) days following the closing date, please consider your application unsuccessful for this opportunity.

title: Logistics and Operations Coordinator
description:
About the Role

As a Logistics and Operations Coordinator, you will support the day-to-day warehouse and logistics operations at our Romania Service Facility. You will be responsible for maintaining inventory accuracy, coordinating material movements, processing inventory transactions, and supporting workshop operations while ensuring compliance with NOV's Quality, Health, Safety, and Environmental (QHSE) standards.

Working closely with the Logistics & Inventory Supervisor, Workshop, Manufacturing, and Operations teams, you will contribute to operational efficiency, material availability, and continuous improvement across the facility.

Why Join NOV?

At NOV, you'll become part of a supportive and collaborative team where learning, development, and teamwork are valued. You'll receive training on our systems and processes, gain exposure to global operations, and have opportunities to expand your skills and responsibilities while building a long-term career with an industry-leading organisation.

About NOV

NOV delivers technology-driven equipment, systems, services, and software solutions that support energy production and industrial operations worldwide. Through engineering excellence, innovation, and operational expertise, we help customers improve safety, reliability, and performance across the energy sector.

Diversity & Inclusion

At NOV, we are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive. We value diversity in all its forms and believe that different perspectives drive innovation and success. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

  • Coordinate daily warehouse and logistics activities to support workshop operations.
  • Receive, visually inspect, store, pick, issue, transfer, and deliver materials to the appropriate workshop while maintaining inventory accuracy.
  • Perform cycle counts, investigate inventory discrepancies, and maintain accurate stock records in JDE.
  • Process inventory transactions including goods receipts, stock transfers, returns, and shipments.
  • Prepare and maintain shipping, receiving, and warehouse documentation.
  • Support the loading and unloading of materials in accordance with HSE procedures.
  • Maintain warehouse organisation using 5S principles and ensure proper storage, traceability, and preservation of materials.
  • Collaborate with Workshop, Manufacturing, Logistics, and Operations teams to ensure timely material availability.
  • Maintain accurate system records and support continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Comply with all NOV Quality, HSE, and company policies and actively contribute to a safe working environment.

Essential

Education

  • High school diploma or equivalent.

Experience

  • Previous experience in warehouse, logistics, inventory, or supply chain operations.
  • Experience working within a manufacturing, industrial, engineering, or service environment.

Technical Skills

  • Knowledge of inventory management, cycle counting, and shipping/receiving processes.
  • Good computer skills, including Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Outlook, and Word).
  • Basic English language skills.
  • Experience using ERP systems; JDE experience is an advantage.

Soft Skills

  • Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Excellent communication and teamwork skills with the ability to build effective working relationships across departments.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Self-motivated, proactive, and reliable with a willingness to learn.
  • Curious mindset with a continuous improvement focus and the ability to work independently.

Preferred

  • Technical qualification related to logistics, operations, or supply chain.
  • Forklift certification (or willingness to obtain one).
  • Experience with NOV JDE and TAT systems.
  • Knowledge of warehouse best practices and 5S methodology.

title: Feature Owner, ADAS
description:
The Role

In this pivotal role as a Product Feature Owner, you will take end-to-end responsibility for specific ADAS functionalities, such as TFL, High Beam Assist, Blind Spot Monitoring, Rear Cross & Front Cross Traffic Alert, Pre-Safe, Door Open Warning, Perception for HMI. Your focus will be to ensure these features meet customer (OEM) requirements, align with internal capabilities and roadmaps, and are delivered to spec. You will collaborate closely with OEMs, product teams, and engineering teams to define feature scope, set performance targets, and ensure compliance with industry standards. Your dedication to aligning customer needs with our strategic vision will directly enhance our vehicles’ capabilities and user experience.

Key responsibilities:

  • Feature Oversight and Delivery: Take full accountability for specific ADAS features, ensuring on-time delivery and alignment with agreed-upon specifications. Monitor the feature’s lifecycle from concept to production, addressing any deviations promptly.

  • Technical Collaboration and Hands-on Engagement: Collaborate with feature group owners and engineering teams to create feature specifications. Engage actively with the teams throughout feature development, providing early feedback, participating in testing, and supporting validation processes.

  • Customer Advocacy and Alignment: Deeply understand OEM customer needs and requirements, representing their interests internally without compromising Wayve’s objectives. Harmonise OEM requests across different customers to maximise the development of reusable assets and ensure scalability.

  • Cross-functional Communication and Coordination: Serve as the central liaison between OEMs, product teams, and engineering teams for your feature(s). Facilitate streamlined communication and reduce misunderstandings. Participate in agile processes and rituals.

  • Risk Mitigation and Strategic Alignment: Proactively identify and address potential issues before they escalate. Maintain and evolve the strategic feature roadmap as our technology progresses towards higher autonomy levels, ensuring alignment with both customer expectations and Wayve’s strategic objectives.


About you

In order to set you up for success as a Product Feature Owner at Wayve, we’re looking for the following skills and experience.

Essential

  • Experience with ADAS Feature Development: Expertise in specifying and facilitating ADAS feature delivery from concept to production, such as TFL, High Beam Assist, Blind Spot Monitoring, Rear Cross & Front Cross Traffic Alert, Pre-Safe, Door Open Warning, Perception for HMI, etc.

  • Automotive Background: Experience with automotive processes, including requirements management, V-model, ASPICE, quality, and validation.

  • Customer Advocacy and Communication Skills: Strong ability to understand and represent OEM customer needs, negotiate requirements, and align expectations between customers and internal teams.

  • Technical Competence and Hands-on Approach: Technical understanding of ADAS features, capable of engaging in discussions about trade-offs and feasibility, and actively involved in testing, evaluation, and validation processes.

Desirable

  • Experience Shipping ADAS Features into Production: Demonstrated success in delivering complete ADAS features into large-scale production automotive programs.

  • Knowledge of Automotive Quality and Safety Standards: Good understanding of automotive quality and safety standards (ASPICE, SOTIF, FuSa, …) as well as the compliance regime around them (GSR-II, DCAS, …)

  • Experience with machine learning, LLMs and recent generative AI methods and architectures

  • Strong Collaboration Skills: Ability to effectively bridge technical and business perspectives, ensuring clear communication and alignment across teams and stakeholders.

  • Innovative and Forward-thinking: A proactive approach to exploring and developing new ADAS features, keeping Wayve at the forefront of technological advancement in the industry.

This is a full-time role based in our office in Leonberg. At Wayve we want the best of all worlds so we operate a hybrid working policy that combines time together in our offices and workshops to fuel innovation, culture, relationships and learning, and time spent working from home. We operate core working hours so you can determine the schedule that works best for you and your team.

title: Customer Service Analyst
description:
As a Customer Care Professional, you will play a critical role in delivering exceptional customer experiences while supporting the processing of banking product applications. This role requires strong communication skills, attention to detail, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, customer-focused environment.

  • Handle inbound and outbound customer calls, along with case management activities, to process banking product applications accurately and efficiently.
  • Deliver exceptional customer service through phone in a fast-paced, structured environment.
  • Meet and exceed key performance metrics, including customer satisfaction, quality, average handling time, schedule adherence, and compliance standards.
  • Safeguard customer information by maintaining the highest level of integrity and adhering to all regulatory and compliance requirements.
  • Build meaningful customer relationships through empathy, active listening, and effective problem-solving.
  • Handle customer objections professionally and provide end-to-end solutions that meet customer needs.
  • Navigate multiple web-based systems efficiently while managing several tasks simultaneously.
  • Prior experience in a BPO or Call Center Voice Process.
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment.
  • Strong multitasking skills with proficiency in navigating multiple web-based applications.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build rapport and connect with customers.
  • Demonstrated commitment to delivering an exceptional customer experience.
  • Ability to apply customer service best practices with professionalism, empathy, and attention to detail.
  • Willingness to develop consultative communication skills, including objection handling, customer engagement, and solution-oriented conversations.
Preferred Qualifications
  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to identify opportunities for process improvement.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including active listening and the ability to adapt communication style to meet customer needs.

title: Senior Product Manager, Fraud and Compliance
description:
About Remote

Remote is solving modern organizations’ biggest challenge – navigating global employment compliantly with ease. We make it possible for businesses of all sizes to recruit, pay, and manage international teams. With our core values at heart and future focused work culture, our team works tirelessly on ambitious problems, asynchronously, around the world. You can find Remoters working from 6 different continents (Antarctica left to go!) and all of our positions are fully remote.

With Innovation as one of the core values, we have built Automation and AI capabilities into the requirements for every role.

We encourage every member of the Remote team to bring their talents, experiences and culture to the table to help us build the best-in-class HR platform.
If you are energetic, curious, motivated and ambitious, be part of our world. Apply now and define the future of work!

What this job can offer you

This is an exciting time to join Remote and make a personal difference in the global employment space as a Senior Product Manager, Fraud and Compliance, joining the Fintech area

As Remote grows and handles larger transaction volumes across countries, so does our exposure to fraud, credit risk, and compliance risk. This role owns the systems and strategies that protect Remote and its customers — from onboarding verification and screening, to credit risk assessment and reserve management, to fraud detection and enforcement across the full customer lifecycle.

You'll drive the evolution of our risk and compliance capabilities from reactive, rule-based controls toward proactive, automated systems that identify risk signals early and scale without proportional increases in manual effort. This is a high-autonomy, high-stakes role. You'll report to the Director of Product and work closely with Risk, Compliance, Treasury, Legal, Collections, and Engineering.

What you bring

  • Proficiency in Cursor and/or Claude Code is required. You must be able to autonomously build, debug, and ship functional code and prototypes — not just write specs.
  • AI fluency. You have a point of view on how AI and automation can transform risk operations — from pattern detection to anomaly scoring to compliance workflows.
  • Seasoned Product Manager, with a track record on platform, API, or products for operational teams.
  • Platform mindset. You build systems others can extend. Scalability means growing the business without proportional increases in manual work or complexity.
  • Strong UX sensibilities. Risk and compliance tooling doesn't have to be painful. You care about the admin experience as much as the customer-facing one.
  • Outstanding communication and collaboration skills. You partner effectively with compliance, engineering, design, treasury, and legal — and you lead by example on async workflows.
  • Data-driven decision-making. Comfortable querying data and working with visualisation tools, you let insights drive your prioritisation.
  • Fast learner with a growth mindset. You're humble, eager to learn, and get up to speed quickly in new territory, then pass on what you pick up so the whole team levels up.
  • Great judgment - making strategic (what to prioritize) and tactical (feature scope) decisions.
  • Language: Business-level proficiency writing and speaking English.
  • Domain advantage (nice to have): Experience with risk/compliance platforms and third-party screening or fraud detection tooling.

Key Responsibilities

  • Own credit risk assessment and reserve management: Drive the strategy for how Remote evaluates, scores, and mitigates credit risk. Evolve the underlying models and tooling to be smarter, faster, and more automated over time.
  • Fraud detection and prevention: Own fraud controls, freeze mechanisms, and dispute workflows. Build proactive strategies that detect and prevent fraud — not just react to it.
  • Compliance automation: Own end-to-end onboarding compliance — KYB verification, AML screening, and background checks. Drive automation to reduce manual effort while maintaining regulatory defensibility.
  • Build flexible, platform-oriented systems: Prefer solutions that operations teams can configure and extend without engineering involvement. Remove engineering as a bottleneck for responding to new risk and compliance patterns.
  • Collections and enforcement: Partner with Collections and Treasury on risk reserves, payment blocks, and enforcement actions. Own the admin and customer-facing surfaces that support this.
  • Ship iteratively at pace: Lead a product team delivering high throughput and high ROI. Advance key KPIs around automation rates, false-positive reduction, and risk accuracy.
  • Stakeholder management across risk, ops, and finance. Translate complex risk trade-offs into clear product decisions. Align with Compliance Operations, Treasury, Legal, and senior leadership.

Practicals

  • You'll report to: Director of Product
  • Location: For this position we welcome everyone to apply;
  • Start date: As soon as possible

Application process

  1. AI interview
  2. Interview with recruiter
  3. Interview with future manager
  4. Live session
  5. Team interview
  6. Bar Raiser Interview
  7. Interview with CTO
  8. Offer + Prior employment verification check

Remote's Total Rewards philosophy is to ensure fair, unbiased compensation and fair equity pay along with competitive benefits in all locations in which we operate. We do not agree to or encourage cheap-labor practices and therefore we ensure to pay above in-location rates. We hope to inspire other companies to support global talent-hiring and bring local wealth to developing countries.

At first glance our salary bands seem quite wide - here is some context. At Remote we have international operations and a globally distributed workforce. We use geo ranges to consider geographic pay differentials as part of our global compensation strategy to remain competitive in various markets while we hiring globally.

Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location, and our job titles may span more than one career level. The actual base pay for the successful candidate in this role is dependent upon many factors such as location, transferable or job-related skills, work experience, relevant training, business needs, and market demands. The base salary range may be subject to change.

At Remote, we foster internal mobility as a key element of our culture of employee growth and development, supported by a compensation philosophy that guarantees pay equity and fairness. Therefore, all compensation changes associated with an internal move will be reviewed by the Total Rewards & People Enablement team on a case by case basis.

The annual salary range for this full-time position is
$149,750—$168,500 USD

Benefits

Our full benefits & perks are explained in our handbook at remote.com/r/benefits. As a global company, each country works differently, but some benefits/perks are for all Remoters:
  • work from anywhere
  • flexible paid time off
  • flexible working hours (we are async)
  • 16 weeks paid parental leave
  • mental health support services
  • stock options
  • learning budget
  • home office budget & IT equipment
  • budget for local in-person social events or co-working spaces

How you’ll plan your day (and life)

We work async at Remote which means you can plan your schedule around your life (and not around meetings). Read more at remote.com/async.

You will be empowered to take ownership and be proactive. When in doubt you will default to action instead of waiting. Your life-work balance is important and you will be encouraged to put yourself and your family first, and fit work around your needs.

If that sounds like something you want, apply now!

How to apply

  1. Please fill out the form below and upload your CV with a PDF format.
  2. We kindly ask you to submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use here at Remote.
  3. If you don’t have an up to date CV but you are still interested in talking to us, please feel free to add a copy of your LinkedIn profile instead.

Not only do we encourage folks from all ethnic groups, genders, sexuality, age, abilities, disability status and any other under-represented group to apply, but we prioritize a sense of belonging. We have 4 ERGs (Women, Disability, Queer, Minorities in Tech) who meet regularly with the People team. During your interviews and beyond, we ask & encourage anybody who needs an accommodation to request one from their recruiter.

We will ask you to voluntarily tell us your pronouns at interview stage, and you will have the option to answer our anonymous demographic questionnaire when you apply below. As an equal employment opportunity employer it’s important to us that our workforce reflects people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences and this data will help us to stay accountable. We thank you for providing this data, if you chose to.

At Remote, we embrace AI as a valuable tool while prioritizing human creativity and authenticity. We look forward to meeting candidates who balance innovation with genuine expertise and experience. To learn more about Remote's AI guidelines check see here.

Please note we accept applications on an ongoing basis.

title: Building Engineer
description:
About the Role

We are seeking a qualified and detail-oriented BuildingEngineer to support and oversee BCA-mandated building inspections, includingPeriodic Façade Inspection (PFI) projects across residential, commercial,industrial, and mixed-use developments in Singapore.

The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuringcompliance with the Singapore Building Control Act, managing inspectionprocesses, preparing technical reports, coordinating façade inspections(including drone-assisted inspections), and liaising with clients, consultants,and authorities.

Key Responsibilities

Periodic Façade Inspection (PFI)

  • Plan, manage and execute façade inspection projects in accordance with BCA regulations.
  • Carry out visual and technical façade assessments using conventional methods and/or drone technology.
  • Coordination with external teams, architects, contracts and clients (MCST and property owners) to ensure project requirements are met.
  • Prepare and review inspection reports, drawings, and safety risks related to façade elements to support project deliverables.

Project & Stakeholder Management

  • Coordinate access arrangements, inspection schedules, safety requirements, and permit submissions.
  • Identify project risks and propose solutions to maintain project performance and efficiency.
  • Manage project timelines, budgets, documentation, and regulatory submissions.
  • Provide technical advice and recommendations to clients on building maintenance and compliance matters.
  • Other ad-hoc duties assigned by the management.

Regulatory & Compliance

  • Ensure all inspections and reports comply with BCA regulations and Singapore building codes.
  • Ensure compliance with façade inspection standards and reporting requirements.
  • Prepare and submit documentation to statutory agencies/authorities/government where necessary.
  • Maintain proper inspection records, technical documentation, and audit trails.
  • Ensure adherence to workplace safety and risk management standards.

Requirements

Mandatory Qualifications

  • Degree/Bachelor Degree in Civil Engineering, Structural Engineering, Building Engineering, or related discipline.
  • 3–5 years of relevant experience in building inspection, structural engineering, façade inspection, or building consultancy.
  • Preferred: Registered Professional Engineer (PE) with the Professional Engineers Board (PEB) Singapore or Registered Architect (RA) with Board of Architect (BOA) Singapore.
  • Familiarity with Singapore Building Control Act and BCA inspection requirements.
  • Experience in PFI projects, working with drone-assisted inspection methodologies is an advantage.
  • Knowledge of façade systems, structural defects, and building maintenance practices.
  • Experience preparing technical reports and authority submissions.

Skills & Competencies

  • Strong technical and analytical skills.
  • Good understanding of structural integrity and façade safety requirements.
  • Excellent report writing and communication skills.
  • Ability to manage multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously.
  • Strong attention to detail and compliance standards.
  • Proficiency in engineering and reporting software/tools.

title: Project Manager
description:
About Us:

We love going to work and think you should too. Our team is dedicated to trust, customer obsession, agility, and striving to be better everyday. These values serve as the foundation of our culture, guiding our actions and driving us towards excellence. We foster a culture of performance and recognition, allowing us to transform growth as we enable our employees to do the best work of their careers.

This position is located in Pune. You'll be working in a major tech center of Pune, India. Across the globe, our Centers of Energy serve as hubs where we accelerate productivity and collaboration, inspire creativity, and cultivate a culture of connection and celebration. Our teams coordinate their time in Centers of Energy to reflect how they work best.

To learn more about life at LogicMonitor, check out our Careers Page.

What You'll Do:

LogicMonitor® is the AI-first hybrid observability platform powering the next generation of digital infrastructure. LogicMonitor delivers complete visibility and actionable intelligence across on-premises, cloud, and edge environments. By anticipating issues before they strike, optimizing resources in real time, and enabling faster, smarter decisions, LogicMonitor helps IT and business leaders protect margins, accelerate innovation, and deliver exceptional digital experiences without compromise.

Our customers love LogicMonitor's ability to bring cloud and traditional IT together into one view, as seen in minimal churn rates, expansion business, and exciting new customer references. In fact, LogicMonitor has received the highest Net Promoter Score of any IT Infrastructure Management provider. LogicMonitor also boasts high employee satisfaction. We have been certified as a Great Place To Work®, and named one of BuiltIn's Best Places to Work for the seventh year in a row!

The Project Manager in Professional Services plays a critical role in driving the successful execution of customer engagements, leading infrastructure monitoring, observability, and digital operations projects while ensuring engagements are delivered efficiently, strategically, and with measurable business outcomes. This role partners closely with customers and internal teams to maximize the value of their LogicMonitor investment through effective project leadership, communication, and execution.

Here's a closer look at this key role:

  • Manage customer-facing LogicMonitor implementation projects, including standard and enterprise engagements, from kickoff through completion.

  • Consultative approach to coordinating project activities and deliverables, aligning timelines, resources, and milestones with customer and internal team availability.

  • Track progress across multiple projects simultaneously, ensuring they stay on schedule, within scope, and on budget.

  • Serve as the day-to-day point of contact for customers, ensuring clear, consistent communication and a consultative experience throughout the project lifecycle.

  • Collaborate with Customer Success Managers and Solutions Engineers to align on project scope, success criteria, and implementation priorities.

  • Develop and maintain detailed project plans, managing phases, tasks, and dependencies in tools like Asana, Mavenlink, or Confluence.

  • Facilitate regular status meetings and updates, proactively guiding customers through project phases while addressing questions and offering best practices when appropriate.

  • Document and maintain accurate project artifacts, including timelines, notes, action items, and next steps.

  • Identify potential risks or delays early, escalating appropriately and contributing to mitigation strategies with internal teams.

  • Foster productive customer relationships, taking a consultative approach to understand their goals and adapt delivery to support successful outcomes.

  • Provide structured recommendations and insights within the bounds of project scope, helping customers make informed decisions that support implementation success.

  • Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives by offering feedback on delivery processes, tools, and templates.

  • Participate in onboarding and knowledge-sharing activities to support team growth and operational consistency across the PMO.

What You'll Need:

  • 2–4 years of experience managing software, SaaS, infrastructure monitoring, observability, IT operations, or enterprise technology projects, preferably within a customer-facing implementation or professional services environment.

  • Working knowledge of project management principles, methodologies, tools, and best practices, with demonstrated experience managing project scope, schedules, risks, and dependencies.

  • Strong communication, organization, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to effectively engage technical and business audiences, including customer stakeholders and cross-functional teams.

  • Proven ability to manage multiple priorities, navigate complex customer environments, proactively address challenges, and drive projects to successful outcomes.

  • Familiarity with modern collaboration and project management tools such as Slack, Zoom, Confluence, Mavenlink, or similar platforms.

  • Demonstrates accountability, professionalism, strong attention to detail, and a customer-focused, service-oriented mindset.

  • Exposure to Artificial Intelligence (AI), Generative AI, AI-assisted workflows, AI-powered operational platforms, or AIOps concepts is highly desirable. Familiarity with LogicMonitor's Edwin AI capabilities and the application of AI to improve operational efficiency, observability, and customer outcomes is a plus.

  • PMP, CAPM, CSM (Certified Scrum Master), or equivalent project management certifications are preferred but not required.

Click here to read our International Applicant Privacy Notice.

LogicMonitor is an Equal Opportunity Employer
At LogicMonitor, we believe that innovation thrives when every voice is heard and each individual is empowered to bring their unique perspective. We’re committed to creating a workplace where diversity is celebrated, and all employees feel inspired and supported to contribute their best.

For us, equal opportunity means fostering a truly inclusive culture where everyone has the chance to grow and succeed. We don’t just open doors; we invite you to step through and be part of something bigger. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.

Notice Regarding Use of AI in Hiring

We use artificial intelligence tools to assist with reviewing job applications, such as matching skills and experience to job requirements. These tools support, but do not replace, human review. All hiring decisions are made by our recruiting and hiring teams. You may opt out of AI processing at any time, and your application will still be reviewed. To opt out, please contact us at opt.out@logicmonitor.com.

By submitting your application, you acknowledge this notice.

Our goal is to ensure an accessible and inclusive experience for every candidate.

If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or interview process under applicable local law, please submit a request via this Accommodation Request Form.

Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Please click here to review LogicMonitor’s U.S. Pay Transparency Nondiscrimination Provision.
