title: Sénior Quality Engineer
description:
🎯 Context

250+ local authorities rely on Vizzia to keep their streets cleaner and safer. Behind every camera, every connected station, every accessory deployed in the field, there's a question that never goes away: will it still work in 18 months, in 3 years, in 5 years? Hardware quality isn't a checkpoint at the end of the line. It's a discipline that has to be present from R&D to supply chain, and shared across every member of the hardware team.

Today, hardware quality is handled in addition to other responsibilities. The team needs someone whose entire focus is to set the standard, build the processes, and make sure they're applied — on our products, with our suppliers, and over time. The hardware scope at Vizzia covers cameras, stations, accessories, all the way through to deployment with manufacturing partners in China.

You'll report to David, the hardware team's lead. The role is transversal: no direct reports today, but expected to scale into a small quality team in the medium term. Your mission is to bring quality discipline to every step of Vizzia's hardware development, from design choices to field reliability, and to make it a shared standard, not a constraint imposed from the outside.

🛠️ Responsabilies

1. Define and own the hardware quality plan

  • Build a complete quality plan covering all Vizzia hardware products, with clear deliverables for each hardware function

  • Set quality standards and acceptance criteria that everyone in the team can refer to

  • Make sure the plan is shared, understood, and applied, not a document that lives in a drive

2. Set up processes across the development chain

  • Write, share and roll out quality processes across the full hardware development chain

  • Create the internal and external templates the team and suppliers will use day-to-day (test plans, audits, non-conformity reports, certification documentation)

  • Train and challenge the hardware team to embed these processes into their work

3. Run the hardware test plans

  • Define test plans for every Vizzia hardware product

  • Make sure plans are run systematically before products reach production

  • Trace results so nothing falls through the cracks

4. Drive supplier quality, including on-site in China

  • Set up structured supplier quality control, starting with Altior and extending to other manufacturing partners

  • Lead audits, define corrective action plans, follow them to closure

  • Travel to China around twice a year to audit suppliers and align on quality expectations on the ground

  • Bring down defect rates by 30 to 40% on current production

5. Secure certifications and field reliability

  • Make sure documentation tied to certifications (CE in particular) is complete, up-to-date and audit-ready at any moment

  • Build the traceability that lets us reconstruct the full history of any product version, any evolution, any non-conformity

  • Improve field reliability over time, longer mean time to failure, fewer field returns

✅ Profile

  • Min 5 years of experience in hardware quality engineering, in industries where quality really matters

  • You've worked in highly regulated industries (medical, defense, aerospace) and know what real quality discipline looks like

  • You master CE standards and can take a product through certification without surprises

  • You've audited and worked with manufacturing suppliers, ideally including in China, and you're comfortable traveling there

  • You combine rigor with diplomacy: hardware teams don't usually love quality, and you know how to bring them on board without becoming the quality police

  • You're patient and tenacious : quality is a long game, and you don't give up when the short-term pressure builds

At Vizzia, we care about precision, ownership and getting things to work in the real world. We're looking for someone who's genuinely interested in what reliable hardware means for the public sector.

Benefits

🏡 Hybrid work

🏝 Contrat cadre and RTT (between 8 and 12 days per year depending on public holidays)

💻 A Mac or PC depending on your preferences

💸 60-80 package +BSPCE

🍜 60% coverage of meal vouchers worth €9 per worked day

🚃/🚲 Sustainable mobility allowance

🏥 Mutuelle (Alan)

💼 Offices located in central Paris (9th arrondissement)

☀️ Annual offsite with the whole team and plenty of company events

If you've read this far, you're probably very interested in the role and/or Vizzia. We'd like to hear from you even if you feel ou don't check every box.

title: Manufacturing Engineer (LEAN/Software)
company: ELECTRO SCIENTIFIC INDUSTRIES SINGAPORE PTE. LTD
description:
The LEAN – Takt/Capacity Engineer will be responsible for analyzing production capacity, aligning takt time with customer demand, and driving lean initiatives to optimize manufacturing efficiency. This role ensures that production systems are balanced, resources are effectively utilized, and continuous improvement practices are embedded across the factory.

Job Duties:

Takt Time & Capacity Planning

  • Define and monitor takt time based on customer demand and production targets.
  • Conduct capacity analysis to identify gaps between demand and available resources.
  • Balance production lines to achieve optimal throughput and minimize bottlenecks.

Lean Manufacturing Implementation

  • Lead lean projects focused on waste reduction, flow improvement, and productivity gains.
  • Apply tools such as Value Stream Mapping, Kaizen, and 5S to enhance efficiency.
  • Support standardization of work processes and continuous improvement of culture.

Data Analysis & Reporting

  • Collect and analyze production data to evaluate performance against takt and capacity.
  • Develop dashboards and reports for management to support decision-making.
  • Provide recommendations for manpower, equipment, and layout adjustments.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Partner with Production, Quality, Supply Chain, and Engineering teams to align capacity with business needs.
  • Support new product introduction by ensuring takt and capacity requirements are met.
  • Train and coach operators and supervisors on lean principles and takt-based production.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field.
  • Strong knowledge of lean manufacturing principles and takt/capacity planning.
  • Experience in production line balancing and process optimization.
  • Proficiency in data analysis tools (Excel, Power BI, or similar).
  • Excellent communication and problem-solving skills.

Preferred Skills

  • Certification in Lean Six Sigma (Green Belt or higher).
  • Experience with ERP/MRP systems for capacity planning.
  • Familiarity with simulation tools for production modeling.
  • Prior experience in high-volume manufacturing environments.

title: Specialist, Settlement Services
description:
JOB SUMMARY / INTRODUCTION

Responsible for daily monitoring and processing of Settlement processes covering Domestic and Regional and International Market for equities, mutual funds, fixed income, hedge fund, structured funds, ETF, Local and International Securities Lending and Borrowing, Local Market Short Selling and ensuring highest quality of client service delivery as per international standards.
Responsible for handling extremely complex settlement transaction across Local, Regional, International Markets. Processing responsibilities include the receipt of client, market / sub custodian instructions. Validating the correctness of all instructions. Ensuring the matching and settlement of instructions in timely manner across all markets.
Communicate with many entities Clients, Counter parties, Markets, Sub Custodians, Regulators, Companies, Brokers, Paying Agents, Issuers covering Local, Regional, Global Markets, related to settlement process, support Due Diligence visits linking to Settlement process.
The incumbent is responsible for adhering to established quality control measures and procedures.
Responsible to provide the first level training for new specialists and will assist specialists in handling inquiries.
Handle the cash managements for the client to facilitate the smooth settlements across all markets.
Active participation in understanding the Operating module, including Securities Lending and Borrowing transactions, that are large in value and complex in nature, ensuring their successful settlement processing, avoiding loss and penalties to the bank and clients that could cause the bank Reputational, Financial and Operational Risk.
Current offering of Department: Position Management, Security/Cash Settlement, Corporate Actions, Reporting, Invoicing, Reconciliation, Swift, Market Security Movement, FX Management, Cash Management, Project Support, Proxy Services, Tax Reclaims, Income Management. Planned expansion into support of Securities Lending, Client Short Selling, Network Management Support, Claim Management, and other services.
Specialist will participate in section related projects such as system upgrade / changes, processing enhancements and off hours system testing.
Active participation in implementation of Settlement processing related to new clients SLA and SLD, Sub Custodian SLA, New Functions, Products, Market Rules Regulations and Procedures with relevant controls to provide highest level of client delivery.
Cope with market changes and subsequent upgradation of internal processes and to identify any potential risk and to create the appropriate controls.
Responsible to coordinate and work with various internal departments and external entitles and concerned persons as an integral part related to participation in Projects in order to Re-engineer the process for excellence client delivery and reduce manual activities.
Responsible for the end-to-end Settlements processing in accordance with regulatory and compliance requirements, with an increased focus on accurate and timely execution to enhance the client experience.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Responsible for daily monitoring and processing of Settlement processes covering Domestic, Regional and International Markets for products like equities mutual funds, fixed income, hedge fund, structured funds, ETF Local and International Securities Lending and Borrowing, Local Market Short Selling and ensuring highest quality of client service delivery as per international standards.
  • Responsible for handling extremely complex settlement transaction across Local, Regional, International Markets. Processing responsibilities include the receipt of client, market / sub custodian instructions. Validating the correctness of all instructions. Ensuring the matching and settlement of instructions in timely manner across all markets.
  • Ensure to track and respond to internal and external clients' queries by providing clear detailed Settlement information in line with the relevant market rules and regulations and Settlement best practice.
  • Responsible for communicating with various stakeholders like Markets, Sub Custodians, Agents, Companies, Depositories, Brokers, Issuers to ensure smooth accurate timely Settlement processing.
  • Ensure that all trade related statuses reports/Instructions are communicated to all stake holders in timely manners.
  • Responsible to have excellent knowledge related to Settlements swift messages and usages of the same.
  • Demonstrates reliability by taking necessary actions to continuously meet required deadlines and goals.
  • Articulates information clearly and presents information effectively and confidently when working with others.
  • Convince others by making a strong case, bringing others along to their viewpoint; maintains strong, trusting relationships while at the same time is comfortable challenging ideas.
  • Participation in section related projects, system changes and upgrades and new client’s requirements and implementation of sub custodian / client SLA
    Review processes and suggest and implement changes to enhance client satisfaction and reduce manual intervention and reduce overall cost.
  • Cash / FX Settlement: Review and validate all transactions inputted by the officer / another specialist. Escalate all mismatches to VP. Validate all data entered into the system and forward to VP for review. On settlement date of transaction ensure transaction settled and all appropriate accounting entries executed. Escalate any settlement issues / discrepancies to VP Validate and Sign any vouchers that need to be passed for FX / Cash execution for review by the VP
    Position / Cash Management: Review clients’ positions and holdings daily and reconcile positions with sub-custodians and market to ensure there are no discrepancies. Escalate any discrepancies to VP.
  • Daily review of client’s cash positions to ensure all clients have appropriate funding and escalating to VP and Middle Office any potential over drafts or if client is at risk of being insufficient.
  • SWIFTS: Validate swift instructions inputted by officer / another specialist into the system for VP to Authorize. Second Level of SWIFT generation process
    Reconciliation: Second level of reconciliation ensuring all appropriate checks are done to ensure there are no discrepancies and escalating any issues to the VP.
  • Ad Hoc Requests: Supporting the VP as required for business-as-usual matters and projects and initiatives
    Risk Indicators: Responsible to ensure controls and procedures are being adhered to by the section. Monitor all KPIs, KRIs, Capacity Planning. Submit any incidents and issues to VP for review. Monitoring transactions to ensure they are being processed according to Compliance / Audit standards (requirements)
    Follow all relevant departmental policies, processes, standard operating procedures, and instructions so that work is carried out in a controlled and consistent manner.
  • Demonstrate compliance to organization’s values and ethics at all times to support the establishment of a value drive culture within the bank
    Contribute to the identification of opportunities for continuous improvement and sustainability of systems, processes and practices considering global standards, productivity improvement and cost reduction.
  • Assist in the preparation of timely and accurate statements and reports to meet department requirements, policies and quality standards
    Participation on section related projects, systems upgrades / changes, new client requirements, new product functions related to corporate actions
    Understanding and active participation in Projects requirements and system upgrades and changes.
  • Ensure accurate and timely processing of client and client impacting instructions. Reconciling to confirm appropriate settlement of all transactions (FX, Cash, Securities, Corporate Action, Tax Reclaims). Volumes are significant, and as this is a new an up-and-coming business the volumes and complexity of the products and services being offered are increasing exponentially.
  • Managing transactions that are large in value and complex in nature, ensuring their successful settlement, avoiding loss and penalties to the bank and clients that could cause the bank Reputational, Financial and Operational Risk.
  • University Graduate / bachelor’s degree (or 5 years related work experience).
  • 5 + years related experience in security services and / or back-office operations e.g. managing securities settlements/ corporate actions.
  • Solid understanding of security services and operations and accounting activity related to security services.
  • Ability to work under pressure, to priorities and to manage several tasks at the same time.
  • Team oriented and ability to work under pressure.
  • Ability to achieve business objectives without compromising controls and risk parameters established.
  • Strong execution skills in a multi-tasking mode and follow-up effectively.
  • Self-starter with excellent attitude, communication and interpersonal skills.
  • High level of accuracy & attention to detail and excellent team working skills.
    Preferred / Beneficial
  • Specific experience in Securities related functions e.g. Settlement, Client Services, Corporate Actions, Data Maintenance & Billing or Custody Account Opening will be an added advantage.

REQUIREMENTS / QUALIFICATIONS

  • University Graduate / bachelor’s degree (or 5 years related work experience).
  • 5 + years related experience in security services and / or back-office operations e.g. managing securities settlements/ corporate actions.
  • Solid understanding of security services and operations and accounting activity related to security services.
  • Ability to work under pressure, to priorities and to manage several tasks at the same time.
  • Team oriented and ability to work under pressure.
  • Ability to achieve business objectives without compromising controls and risk parameters established.
  • Strong execution skills in a multi-tasking mode and follow-up effectively.
  • Self-starter with excellent attitude, communication and interpersonal skills.
  • High level of accuracy & attention to detail and excellent team working skills.
    Preferred / Beneficial
  • Specific experience in Securities related functions e.g. Settlement, Client Services, Corporate Actions, Data Maintenance & Billing or Custody Account Opening will be an added advantage.

title: Director of People Operations
description:
Why Coda
Coda is a global growth engine for commerce, connecting people, digital products, and payments through trusted monetization and distribution solutions.

With 600+ people from 57 nationalities across 23 locations, we’re a truly global team headquartered in Singapore, with offices in Amsterdam, Dubai, Bristol, Shanghai, Eindhoven, and across Southeast Asia.

We power global commerce through a dual model. Our B2B solutions, Codapay, Coda Webstore, Coda Links, Coda Distribution, and Giftcloud, enable publishers and brands to monetize and distribute digital content globally. On the consumer side, we operate a portfolio of trusted storefronts including Codashop, Recharge.com, Startselect.com, and mobiletopup.co.uk, that give customers secure and easy access to game top-ups, digital credits, vouchers, gift cards and prepaid products in over 70 markets.

Our culture is built on respect, ownership, and collaboration. We move fast, support each other, and celebrate wins together.

If you’re looking to grow your career and make a real impact in a global team, Coda is the place for you.


The Opportunity
We’re looking for a Director of People Operations and Experience to lead and evolve Coda’s global people operations team. This role owns the backbone of operational excellence across the entire employee lifecycle from payroll and compliance to mobility, systems, offboarding, and onboarding - with a relentless focus on making every interaction with the people team quick, reliable, and seamless.
Reporting to the SVP of People, you’ll lead a global team supporting a workforce of 600+ employees across 20+ countries, while partnering closely with internal stakeholders. This is a high-impact leadership role for someone who can roll up their sleeves, think strategically, and build for scale.

What you'll do

  • Lead, mentor, and scale a global people operations team that looks after:

  • HRIS administration and optimization (Rippling)

  • Payroll operations and vendor management

  • Employment compliance

  • Employee mobility, visas, and immigration processes

  • Day-to-day people operations and employee support (Jira)

  • Own the operations that power the employee experience across the lifecycle–define and meet SLAs for people ops requests, drive high CSAT, and optimize the employee journey from onboarding to offboarding
  • Design scalable processes, policies, and operating models that can grow with the business
  • Act as a trusted advisor to senior leaders on people operations risks, tradeoffs, process improvements, and best practices
  • Ensure strong controls, documentation, and audit readiness across payroll, compliance, and employee data
  • Partner with leaders in other people-related areas as needed–e.g., employee relations, total rewards, talent development
  • Play a key role in M&A integration, including due diligence, employee data migration, payroll and compliance alignment, and change management

    • What you'll bring

  • 8+ years of people operations experience, with a strong leadership track record of managing and developing distributed teams
  • Proven experience scaling people operations in a globally dispersed, mid-sized company (500–1,000 employees), preferably with some M&A experience
  • Proven ability to navigate and manage diverse employment laws, cultural nuances, and HR best practices across APAC and EMEA
  • Strong track record of building SLA- and CSAT-driven service models, with a mindset of operational excellence and employee satisfaction
  • Demonstrated success in influencing and driving action in a highly cross-functional environment, bridging cultures and teams across time zones
  • Strong strategic thinking skills paired with a roll-up-your-sleeves mindset
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills
  • Tech savvy, with experience using AI, Google Workspace, Slack, and a breadth of HRIS platforms (experience with Rippling is nice to have)
    • We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, embracing the unique qualities of every individual, regardless of gender, race, age, religion, disability, or other local protected classes. Our goal is to foster an inclusive environment where everyone feels welcome and valued.
    Due to the large number of exceptional applications we receive, we can only reach out to shortlisted candidates. If you don't hear from us, rest assured there may be another opportunity at Coda that aligns better with your unique abilities. Remember to check our Careers Page for more exciting job openings!

title: Fachinformatiker/in  FR Anwendungsentwicklung
description:
**Dein Einstieg in die Welt des Maschinenbaus – mit einer Ausbildung bei der IMA Schelling Group**

Du willst nicht einfach nur zuschauen, sondern selbst anpacken? Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig!

In deiner Ausbildung bei der IMA Schelling Group gestaltest du die Technologien von morgen aktiv mit – gemeinsam mit deinem Team und modernster Technik. Dabei lernst du alles von der Idee bis zur fertigen Maschine und arbeitest an innovativen Anlagen für die holz-, metall- und kunststoffverarbeitende Industrie.

Unsere Unternehmenskultur verbindet internationale Ausrichtung mit persönlicher Atmosphäre. Du wirst Teil eines Teams, in dem langjährige Erfahrung und frischer Nachwuchs Hand in Hand arbeiten – auf Augenhöhe und mit echtem Zusammenhalt.

Ob technisch oder kaufmännisch: Wir bieten dir eine fundierte Ausbildung, individuelle Betreuung und beste Perspektiven für deinen weiteren Weg.

**Wir bieten**

  • **Teil der Innovation**: Du nimmst Teil an spannenden Projekten in einem der weltweit führenden High-Tech Unternehmen unserer Branche.
  • **Umfassendes Leistungspaket**: Bei uns profitierst du von den attraktiven Konditionen des IG Metall Tarifs: 35h-Vertrag, 30 Tage Urlaub
  • **Wachstumsorientierte Arbeitsweise**: Nutze laufende Schulungen, Workshops und eine klar strukturierte Karriereentwicklung.
  • **Flexible Arbeitsmöglichkeiten**: Profitiere von flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und Home-Office-Optionen, um Beruf und Privatleben optimal zu verbinden.
  • **Teamorientierte Unternehmenskultur**: Arbeite in einer unterstützenden Teamkultur, die auf Zusammenarbeit und gemeinsamen Erfolg setzt.
  • **Offene, freundliche Kultur**: Wir fördern eine transparente Arbeitsweise, in der alle per "Du" kommunizieren.

**Deine Aufgaben**

Zu deinem Daily Business in deiner Ausbildung zum Fachinformatiker (m/w/d) FR Anwendungsentwicklung gehört:

  • Programmierung und Entwicklung von spezifischer Software und Schnittstellen
  • Neuentwicklung individueller Software oder Änderung bestehender Anwendungen
  • Entwicklung anwendungsgerechter Bedienoberflächen
  • Festlegung von Datenbankstrukturen
  • Nutzung von Programmiersprachen und Werkzeugen wie Visual Studio und C#
  • Übernahme von Aufgaben der Projektplanung, -durchführung und -kontrolle
  • Fehlerbehebung mithilfe von Diagnosesystemen
  • Beratung und Schulung von Anwendern
  • Erstellung von Dokumentationen

**Dein Profil**

  • Voraussetzung für die Ausbildung ist mindestens ein Sekundarabschluss 1 mit sehr guten Noten, insbesondere in Informatik, Mathematik und den naturwissenschaftlichen Fächern.
  • Du hast Interesse an digitalen Prozessen und Themen und kannst dich dafür begeistern.
  • Du bist teamfähig, selbständig und bringst Engagement und Lernbereitschaft mit.

Interessiert? Dann freuen wir uns auf deine Online-Bewerbung:

IMA Schelling Deutschland GmbH | Timon Ewald | Industriestraße 3 | 32312 Lübbecke | Germany T +49 5741 331-0 | F +49 5741 4201 | www.imaschelling.com

title: Accounts Payable Specialist
description:
About Us

At Cloudflare, we are on a mission to help build a better Internet. Today the company runs one of the world’s largest networks that powers millions of websites and other Internet properties for customers ranging from individual bloggers to SMBs to Fortune 500 companies. Cloudflare protects and accelerates any Internet application online without adding hardware, installing software, or changing a line of code. Internet properties powered by Cloudflare all have web traffic routed through its intelligent global network, which gets smarter with every request. As a result, they see significant improvement in performance and a decrease in spam and other attacks. Cloudflare was named to Entrepreneur Magazine’s Top Company Cultures list and ranked among the World’s Most Innovative Companies by Fast Company.

At Cloudflare, we’re not looking for people who wait for a polished roadmap; we’re looking for the builders who see the cracks in the Internet that everyone else has simply learned to live with. We value candidates who have the instinct to spot a "normalized" problem and the AI-native curiosity to create a solution using the latest tools. Our culture is built on iteration, leveraging AI to ship faster today to make it better tomorrow, while ensuring that every improvement, no matter how small, is shared across the team to lift everyone up. If you’re the type of person who values curiosity over bureaucracy, and that AI is a partner in solving tough problems to keep the Internet moving forward, you’ll fit right in.

Available Locations: Bengaluru, India APAC Region

About the role:

At Cloudflare, we have our eyes set on an ambitious goal: to help build a better Internet. Today the company runs one of the world’s largest networks that powers approximately 25 million Internet properties, for customers ranging from individual bloggers to SMBs to Fortune 500 companies. Cloudflare protects and accelerates any Internet application online without adding hardware, installing software, or changing a line of code. Internet properties powered by Cloudflare all have web traffic routed through its intelligent global network, which gets smarter with every request. As a result, they see significant improvement in performance and a decrease in spam and other attacks.

Cloudflare was named to Entrepreneur Magazine’s Top Company Cultures list and ranked among the World’s Most Innovative Companies by Fast Company. We realize people do not fit into neat boxes. We are looking for curious and empathetic individuals who are committed to developing themselves and learning new skills, and we are ready to help you do that. We cannot complete our mission without building a diverse and inclusive team. We hire the best people based on an evaluation of their potential and support them throughout their time at Cloudflare. Come join us!

Role Summary

Reporting directly to the Manager, Operations Accounting - India, the Accounts Payable Specialist is a high-impact operational role serving as the primary right-hand support to leadership in driving the quality, speed, and accuracy of Cloudflare's core finance processes. In this role, you will assist in supervising and guiding the day-to-day accounts payable operations executed by our workforce in Pune, India, ensuring high team performance and strict SLA maintenance. You will directly work on weekly and ad hoc global payment runs, assist in investigating complex supplier escalations, and ensure global vendors and internal employees receive exceptional support. Additionally, you will champion modern finance innovation by leading and assisting in project-related initiatives, including Oracle Fusion enhancements, AI-driven process automation, system integrations, and continuous workflow improvements.

Role Responsibilities

  • Operational Leadership & SLA Maintenance: Serve as the primary right-hand operational lead to the Manager, Operations Accounting, overseeing daily AP and employee expense processing to ensure service level agreements (SLAs) for processing speed and accuracy are consistently met or exceeded by our team in Pune.
  • Global Payment Execution: Assist in executing, reviewing, and optimizing weekly and ad hoc corporate payment runs for the Global Accounts Payable department, enforcing strict controls, timeliness, and multi-currency payment accuracy.
  • Supplier Escalation Support: Support the management team in investigating and resolving complex supplier escalations, payment failures, and vendor inquiries, leveraging local time zone advantages for rapid issue closure.
  • Financial Close Tasks: Assist the global finance team with critical month-end and quarter-end closing tasks, including subledger reconciliations, accrual reporting, and ensuring all AP transactional data is correctly booked prior to general ledger cutoffs.
  • Automation & AI Solutions: Drive and execute project-related work focused on modernizing finance operations, including designing process improvements, building scalable workflow solutions, and implementing AI-driven automation to streamline manual AP operations.
  • System Integration & Testing: Support ongoing financial system projects and ERP initiatives by participating in requirement gathering, Unit and User Acceptance Testing (UAT), data validation, and post-implementation process optimization.
  • Control & Audit Execution: Ensure the local operational workforce adheres strictly to Procure-to-Pay (P2P) internal controls, maintaining complete documentation to support SOX compliance and external audit reviews.

Role Requirements (Must-Have Skills)

  • Oracle Fusion Proficiency: Direct, hands-on experience utilizing Oracle Fusion for enterprise accounts payable workflows, payment processing, and transaction management is strictly required.
  • Vast AP & Payment Experience: 4+ years of progressive experience in corporate Accounts Payable and global payment execution within an international or high-growth technology environment.
  • Project & Automation Track Record: Proven experience assisting with or leading process improvement projects, financial system integrations, or AI-driven automation initiatives.
  • Escalation & Operational Management: Demonstrated ability to act as an operational anchor, supporting high-volume transaction exceptions, assisting in vendor escalations, and maintaining SLA standards.
  • Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical capabilities and attention to detail, with a proven track record of proactively identifying bottlenecks and delivering effective solutions.
  • Education & Application: Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. Applicants are required to submit an updated resume for consideration.

Nice-to-Have Skills

  • A CPA, Chartered Accountant (CA), or equivalent international accounting certification is highly preferred.
  • Prior experience working within or overseeing workflows across a global shared services center (SSC) or contracted workforce model (e.g., Bengaluru/Pune teams).
  • Experience with advanced OCR technology, robotic process automation (RPA), or generative AI tools applied to Procure-to-Pay processes.
  • Familiarity with SOX compliance standards and internal control frameworks in a publicly listed tech company.

What Makes Cloudflare Special?

We’re not just a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company. We’re a highly ambitious, large-scale technology company with a soul. Fundamental to our mission to help build a better Internet is protecting the free and open Internet.

Project Galileo: Since 2014, we've equipped more than 2,400 journalism and civil society organizations in 111 countries with powerful tools to defend themselves against attacks that would otherwise censor their work, technology already used by Cloudflare’s enterprise customers--at no cost.

Athenian Project: In 2017, we created the Athenian Project to ensure that state and local governments have the highest level of protection and reliability for free, so that their constituents have access to election information and voter registration. Since the project, we've provided services to more than 425 local government election websites in 33 states.

1.1.1.1: We released 1.1.1.1 to help fix the foundation of the Internet by building a faster, more secure and privacy-centric public DNS resolver. This is available publicly for everyone to use - it is the first consumer-focused service Cloudflare has ever released. Here’s the deal - we don’t store client IP addresses never, ever. We will continue to abide by our privacy commitment and ensure that no user data is sold to advertisers or used to target consumers.

Sound like something you’d like to be a part of? We’d love to hear from you!

Please note that applicants who progress to the offer stage of the interview process may be asked to attend an in-person interview within one of the Cloudflare Offices or Cloudflare Hubs. More details about this will be available at that stage of the interview process.

This position may require access to information protected under U.S. export control laws, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations. Please note that any offer of employment may be conditioned on your authorization to receive software or technology controlled under these U.S. export laws without sponsorship for an export license.

Cloudflare is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all people and place great value in both diversity and inclusiveness. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to their, or any other person's, perceived or actual race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, family care status, or any other basis protected by law. We are an AA/Veterans/Disabled Employer.

Cloudflare provides reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities. Please tell us if you require a reasonable accommodation to apply for a job. title: Field Marketing Manager DACH (f/m/d)
description:
Location: Germany - remotely

We Are:

RTB House is a next-generation performance demand-side platform (DSP) that uses proprietary Deep Learning AI algorithms to help brands grow. The company is the market leader in driving performance using Deep Learning across the entire purchase funnel.

Founded in 2012 and now operating in 90+ markets, RTB House has always been private-by-design. It embraces first-party advertising and a relentless approach to innovation. RTB House offers end-to-end Deep Learning-powered AdTech products and solutions to maximize conversion, drive new customer acquisition, create engagement, and fuel long-term demand for a global base of clients.

In 2026, RTB House launched rtb.com, a self-service platform specifically designed to allow small and medium-sized businesses and agencies to deploy auto-built creatives and maximize performance on any budget.

Our team in DACH consists of over 40 people and is an integral part of our company’s global presence. We combine local expertise with our broader company vision. We embrace a "remote-first" approach, allowing our team members the flexibility to work remotely.

The Marketing team is crucial in driving our company’s success, collaborating cross-functionally to deliver data-driven strategies, impactful campaigns, and strong brand communication that fuel growth and support our commercial priorities across the DACH market.

You Will:

Field Marketing Planning and Performance

  • Execute the DACH marketing plan in line with local business goals;
  • Work with Sales and Account Management to prioritize activities supporting pipeline, deal progression, retention, and upsell;
  • Track performance, and optimize activities based on results;
  • Manage the regional marketing budget with a focus on efficiency and ROI.

Targeted Campaigns

  • Plan and execute targeted lead-generation campaigns for defined prospect and client lists;
  • Create relevant touchpoints that help warm up priority accounts and support commercial conversations;
  • Coordinate campaigns from briefing and audience selection through execution, follow-up, and reporting.

Events

  • Plan and execute owned and external events from concept to post-event follow-up;
  • Define clear target audiences and business objectives for each activity;
  • Measure event success based on account engagement, influenced opportunities, follow-up, and ROI;
  • Coordinate speakers, suppliers, invitations, logistics, and on-site delivery.

Collaboration

  • Work closely with the DACH Regional Business Director, Sales, Account Management, Data Analyst and Inventory teams;
  • Maintain regular alignment with HQ Marketing and Regional Marketing & PR teams;
  • Coordinate stakeholders and suppliers to deliver projects on time and within budget.

Desired Experience:

  • 3+ years of experience in B2B marketing, preferably within the technology, digital, advertising, SaaS, or related industries;
  • Practical experience in field marketing, lead generation, demand generation, or sales-supporting marketing activities;
  • Strong understanding of Account-Based Marketing principles and experience engaging targeted groups of prospects or clients;
  • Hands-on experience planning and executing events, campaigns, and other marketing projects;
  • Ability to work with defined target-account lists and create relevant touchpoints that support commercial conversations;
  • Experience measuring marketing performance and using data to improve campaigns and event execution;
  • Strong project management and organizational skills, with the ability to manage several activities and stakeholders simultaneously;
  • A practical, delivery-oriented mindset and willingness to take ownership of both planning and execution;
  • Strong cooperation and communication skills;
  • Exceptional communication skills in German and English (C1 or above)

We Offer:

  • We believe in a healthy work-life balance and therefore prioritize flexible working arrangements- you decide whether you want to work remotely, from our DACH office space or even temporarily from any other location in the world
  • We offer a top of the market compensation package, consisting of an above market-average base and an attractive bonus scheme;
  • You’re starting out with 28 vacation days- that can turn into 30 days after a 2-year tenure
  • Choose between an EGYM Sports club membership or a monthly Pluxee allowance that can be used towards purchases in various grocery stores, shops, and restaurants;
  • You will have a structured and interactive onboarding, accompanied by a supporting team member assigned to guide you during your orientation phase to make sure you’re settling in with ease
  • We care about your development and help you continuously grow your career with us as part of your personalized development plan- supported by a generous yearly training budget
  • An international working environment inspires you and enables you to develop projects as part of a highly qualified cross-functional team

Apply now!

You don't need to tick every box to apply. If you are passionate about digital marketing, send us your CV, and we'll review it.

title: (Big) Data Engineer
description:
About AppsFlyer:
AppsFlyer provides a SaaS marketing analytics platform that measures and optimizes marketing activities across mobile, web, CTV, PC, and console applications. AppsFlyer is known for its massive backend production. At any given moment, thousands of servers are consuming 150+ billion mobile app events, crunching our users’ data, serving requests, and communicating on a massive scale.

To maintain the universe we call AppsFlyer, we practice modern production operations with a complete self-serve CI/CD platform, a highly integrated observability stack for our microservices, backends, and infrastructure, and a culture of ownership and eagerness for quality.

The Analytics group is responsible for showing our clients the stories their data is telling. Through complex aggregation, mission-tailored analytical databases, and carefully crafted APIs, we are able to provide slice-and-dice analysis in our beautiful dashboards and through the use of external integrations.

About the Role:
As a Data Engineer(Big Data) within the Analytics group at AppsFlyer, you will own the end-to-end journey of event data - from ingestion and processing to storage, modeling, and API delivery.

You will design and implement data pipelines using Apache Airflow and Spark, choose and optimize analytical databases, and integrate new components in our hybrid microservices environment. You will collaborate with product managers and cross-functional teams, troubleshoot production incidents, and influence our migration toward Google Cloud and BigQuery. This role offers autonomy over feature design, real impact on a platform handling billions of events daily, and the chance to contribute to open-source projects or speak at industry forums.

Key Responsibilities:
Develop and maintain data processing pipelines using Apache Airflow, Scala Spark or PySpark.
Select, model and optimize analytical databases for high-throughput, low-latency queries.
Design and implement backend services and APIs for data ingestion, aggregation and client consumption.
Monitor system performance and stability; diagnose and resolve production issues.
Collaborate with product managers and engineers to plan and deliver complex features.
Drive migration efforts to Google Cloud Platform, BigQuery and modern data tooling.
Participate in on-call rotations, alerting via PagerDuty and incident response procedures.
Document system architecture and data flows; maintain code quality and tests.

Required Competence and Skills:
3+ years of hands-on experience building and operating production Big Data systems
2+ years of proven experience designing, developing, and maintaining large-scale distributed data processing pipelines
Strong practical experience with distributed data processing technologies such as Apache Spark or equivalent Big Data frameworks.
Experience with workflow orchestration tools such as Apache Airflow or similar.
Strong SQL skills and experience designing data models for analytical workloads.
Familiarity with JVM languages (Scala, Java, Clojure) or Python for backend development.
Experience working with cloud platforms and modern data warehouses like Google Cloud Platform and BigQuery or similar.
Experience owning production systems, including monitoring, troubleshooting, and responding to production incidents.
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate effectively within distributed engineering teams.
B.Sc. in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience.

Nice to Have:
Production experience with Google BigQuery, including ETL pipelines and analytical workloads.
Experience developing backend services in Go or Clojure.
Experience contributing to open-source projects or presenting at technical conferences or meetups.
An AI mindset, including practical use of tools such as GitHub Copilot, Cursor, ChatGPT, or similar AI-assisted development workflows.

Benefits Package includes:
Health Insurance
Paid Unlimited Vacation Days + all national holidays + additional recharge days
Meals Reimbursement
Sport Reimbursement
Breakfast in the office
Mental health program
Team building, happy hours, and other team activities
Paid sick days
Snacks, fruits & ice-cold beer
All new team members are provided with a brand-new Mac laptop with 2 monitors and a Starter package

title: Program Manager, Spares Readiness, Data Center Operations - Singapore
description:
Product area

The Data Center team designs and operates some of the most sophisticated electrical and HVAC systems in the world. We are an upbeat, creative, team-oriented group of engineers committed to building and operating powerful data centers.

Job description

A problem isn’t truly solved until it’s solved for all. That’s why Googlers build products that help create opportunities for everyone, whether down the street or across the globe. As a Program Manager at Google, you’ll lead complex, multi-disciplinary projects from start to finish — working with stakeholders to plan requirements, manage project schedules, identify risks, and communicate clearly with cross-functional partners across the company. Your projects will often span offices, time zones, and hemispheres. It's your job to coordinate the players and keep them up to date on progress and deadlines.

Additional job description

At Google, we own and operate the most advanced fleet of data centers and core technical infrastructure to power services and features used by people around the world. Data Centers Central Operations is responsible for keeping these sites operating efficiently and safely.

Within the Data Center Central Operations function, our Spares Readiness team will be accountable for improvement/sustainment programs that support our mission of assuring that data centers have their spare parts available to them when they need them.

Qualifications

Job responsibilities

  • Lead efforts during emergency recovery events to help operations identify, find, and receive spare parts for repairs.
  • Balance project data and analytics and stakeholder input, and use levers to improve project performance against expectations and milestones.
  • Establish strong and aligned linkages to our supply chain partner teams in planning, procurement, global commodity management, and logistics to provide exceptional customer satisfaction.
  • Coordinate/lead project work between field and central operations, manufacturers, engineering, and administrative vendor groups to identify and create Google part numbers for unique critical spare parts.
  • Work closely with site leaders, site teams, and cross-functional teams to identify gaps, make improvement recommendations, orchestrate change, and establish robust relationships.

Minimum qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
  • 2 years of experience in program or project management.
  • Experience in infrastructure operations, supply chain/operations, data center operations, manufacturing, or technology based business units.

Preferred qualifications

  • Lean or Six Sigma certification.
  • 5 years of experience managing cross-functional or cross-team projects.
  • Experience with SAP, SQL, dashboarding, and advanced data analytics/statistical analysis.
  • Experience coordinating projects or programs across global teams, spanning multiple offices, time zones, and regional environments.
  • Experience navigating technical blockers or emergency recovery events.

title: Commercial Partnerships Manager (Bangkok Based)
description:
Here at AppsFlyer, it’s all about the people; each and every one of us has our own specialty, a unique flavor that we bring to the table. We are all driven individuals whose interests are just as diverse as we are. You could say we’re #PeopleObsessed. When we say obsession, we mean we will do everything to make you feel those AF vibes; whether it’s company breakfasts, happy hours, and parties, our connection to each other is always a core focus.

Few markets in the world demand the level of commercial sophistication, cultural fluency, and strategic agility that Korea and Japan do. As the Commercial Partnership Manager, you will manage AppsFlyer's partner growth across Korea and Japan, shaping an ecosystem at the heart of our Asia-Pacific ambitions.

This is a role is based in Bangkok, Thailand for someone who thrives on ownership. You will manage relationships from first contact to signed agreements, co-build go-to-market plans with key partners, and be the person who makes things happen in one of our most important global markets.

What you'll do:

Commercial Growth and Revenue

  • Drive partner-led revenue by identifying, developing, and closing high-value commercial partnerships across the Korea and Japan mobile ecosystem
  • Own partner-driven pipeline, including new partner acquisition, activation, and retention initiatives
  • Develop and execute joint business plans with key partners to unlock new revenue streams
  • Negotiate and close commercial agreements and partnership contracts
  • Execute go-to-market strategies for new products and market opportunities within Korea and Japan

Partnership Development and Ecosystem Expansion

  • Build and grow relationships with strategic partners — including DSPs, media networks, agencies, OEMs, and technology platforms active in Korea and Japan.
  • Identify emerging trends, distribution channels, and niche partnership opportunities specific to the Korea and Japan markets
  • Represent AppsFlyer at industry events, conferences, and partner summits across the region
  • Drive co-marketing initiatives, cross-promotions, and strategic alliances

Cross-functional Execution and Alignment

  • Work closely with Sales, Marketing, Product, and Legal teams to align partnership strategies with broader business priorities
  • Lead partner integration discussions and support product adoption across the Korea and Japan ecosystem
  • Provide ongoing partner enablement, training, and performance reviews
  • Maintain clear and consistent reporting on pipeline, partner health, and growth metrics

Market Intelligence and Continuous Improvement

  • Translate Korea and Japan market dynamics, partner feedback, and competitive intelligence into actionable internal recommendations
  • Leverage AI-powered analytics and data-driven insights to prioritize partnership efforts, spot emerging opportunities, and continuously optimize commercial outcomes across the Korea and Japan ecosystem.
  • Ensure compliance with local regulations and maintain strong commercial discipline.

Who you are:

  • 5+ years in partnerships, business development, or commercial sales within mobile AdTech, SaaS, or digital advertising
  • Proven revenue growth through partnerships and complex deal execution
  • Deep knowledge of the Japan & Korea mobile ecosystem - platforms, Ad tech players, and local business culture
  • Korean and Japanese fluency (written and spoken) and strong English proficiency - both required
  • Solid grasp of Korea and Japan markets - App Ecosystem, SDK data practices
  • Daily AI user - you embed AI into research, prioritization, and decision-making as a matter of habit, not curiosity
  • Entrepreneurial, self-directed, and able to present complex ideas simply to any audience

Preferred Requirements

  • Direct experience managing MMP, DSP, Ad Network, or agency partnerships in the Korea and Japan market
  • Experience with SaaS product partnerships or mobile analytics platforms
  • Strong existing network within the Korea and Japan mobile advertising or marketing technology ecosystem
  • Being introduced by an AppsFlyer team member

Requisition ID: 2862

AppsFlyer is a global company with 20 offices worldwide. Through mental health programs like Be Well, the Global Employee Exchange Program, and fitness programs, we empower our people to thrive physically, mentally, and professionally.

Here, titles don’t define us—ideas do. You’ll thrive in a culture where everyone’s input shapes the future.

title: Social Commerce Shark Opportunities
description:
About Us

SharkNinja is a global product design and technology company, with a diversified portfolio of 5-star rated lifestyle solutions that positively impact people’s lives in homes around the world. Powered by two trusted, global brands, Shark and Ninja, the company has a proven track record of bringing disruptive innovation to market and developing one consumer product after another has allowed SharkNinja to enter multiple product categories, driving significant growth and market share gains. Headquartered in Needham, Massachusetts with more than 4,100 associates, the company’s products are sold at key retailers, online and offline, and through distributors around the world.

AI at SharkNinja

At SharkNinja, we’re building an AI-native culture. We’re not waiting for the future; we’re creating it. Our people are expected to experiment boldly, adopt new tools, and continuously raise what’s possible to create meaningful impact for our consumers. If you believe the best way to do your job hasn’t been invented yet, you’ll fit right in.

Location: London, Paris, Frankfurt, Italy, and Spain

Full-time, start date 1st September - end date 31st December

Join the SharkNinja Early Edge Experience: Built for early career talent. Geared toward impact. Driven by speed. Whether you're in a co-op or internship, our early talent program is designed to provide valuable exposure to the industry and a fast-paced professional environment. You'll dive into meaningful projects and tasks that sharpen your skills and deepen your knowledge. Along the way, you'll benefit from professional development opportunities, community events, and connections that make your experience well-rounded and enriching. Your time with us will culminate in an opportunity to showcase your contributions and learnings to SharkNinja leaders because this is a program for those ready to shape what's next — fast.

This role: ShopSharks power a new way SharkNinja approaches eCommerce. You'll own a market-and-category cell (e.g., Beauty – France) with a mandate of total coverage: hunt every affiliate creator in that space, know their content cold, and bring the best into the SharkNinja program — working inside our eCommerce affiliate/selection platforms, our CRM, and alongside the affiliate leads already driving this work.

What you'll do:

  • Map and hunt every relevant affiliate in your market × category using our eCommerce affiliate selection platforms and AI-powered discovery
  • Review creators' content and memorialize their highest-performing work in the CRM to ground selection decisions in evidence
  • Run a structured selection process and extend targeted invitations with differentiated, target-tier commission structures
  • Issue product invitations with time-bound response windows and a disciplined follow-up cadence; educate creators and fast-follow with samples
  • Drive posts toward aggressive early-view and engagement targets; trigger performance-based paid amplification behind winning posts, with creators continuing to earn commission
  • Manage the full CRM pipeline — outreach, SLAs, samples, go-lives, performance — and recommend which creators to graduate, boost, or exit
  • Use AI to scale personalized outreach and reporting; present pipeline health and market learnings to leaders

What you'll bring:

  • Education: Must be currently enrolled in a bachelor's or master's program, or have graduated within the past year
  • Native TikTok fluency: creator ecosystem, trends, and what converts; eCommerce seller/affiliate platform experience a strong plus
  • A hunter's mentality — high-volume, personalized outreach with relentless follow-up — plus disciplined CRM habits
  • Experience managing creator or influencer campaigns end-to-end — briefing, product seeding and sample logistics, content review and approvals, go-live, and reporting
  • Strong analytical instincts — comfortable with social listening, sentiment, and monthly KPI reporting that turns creator data into decisions
  • Demonstrated experience using AI tools to scale outreach or analysis, with examples you can speak to
  • Experience at a social platform, beauty/prestige brand, consumer products company, talent agency, or high-velocity social commerce team is a strong plus
  • Strong communication and market cultural fluency; French required for Paris, German for Frankfurt

Embodies SharkNinja's Success Drivers: Rarely Satisfied • Progress over Perfection • Details Make the Difference • Winning is a Team Sport • Communicate for Impact.

Due to high application volume for early talent roles, response times may vary. Thank you for your patience!

This is an advert for roles awaiting approval.

At SharkNinja, we don’t just raise the bar—we push past it every single day. Our Outrageously Extraordinary mindset drives us to tackle the impossible, push boundaries, and deliver results that others only dream of. If you thrive on breaking out of your swim lane, you’ll be right at home.

What We Offer

We offer competitive health insurance, retirement plans, paid time off, employee stock purchase options, wellness programs, SharkNinja product discounts, and more. We empower your personal and professional growth with high impact Learning Programs featuring bold voices redefining what’s possible. When you join, you’re not just part of a company—you’re part of an outrageously extraordinary community. Together, we won’t just launch products—we’ll disrupt entire markets.

At SharkNinja, Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion are vital to our global success. Valuing each unique voice and blending all of our diverse skills strengthens SharkNinja’s innovation every day. title: Software-Ingenieur für Cybersicherheit m/w/d
description:
**Wir reichen dir die Hand, zu deinem neuen Job!**

Ganz nach unserem Motto:
*Deine Zufriedenheit ist unsere größte Wertschätzung!*

**Aktuell suchen wir:**

## Software-Ingenieur für Cybersicherheit m/w/d

**Deine Aufgaben:**

Du unterstützt unser Team in allen Bereichen der Softwareentwicklung mit Fokus auf Cybersicherheit. Dabei arbeitest Du sowohl beratend als auch operativ – von der Konzeption über die Programmierung bis hin zur Wartung und kontinuierlichen Verbesserung unserer Sicherheitslösungen.

**Programmierung:**

  • Weiterentwicklung und Optimierung unserer internen Kryptografie-API
  • Durchführung von OS-Updates und Sicherheitsprüfungen (STIGs)
  • Implementierung und Pflege von Software Bill of Materials (SBOM)-Scannern
  • Entwicklung von Lösungen für Secure Token Authentication (STA)
  • Weiterentwicklung unseres Imaging-Tools zur automatisierten Image-Erstellung

**Wartung & Sicherheit:**

  • Analyse und Bearbeitung von SBOM-Schwachstellen
  • Pflege und Weiterentwicklung unserer Management-Webanwendung zur Bewertung von Sicherheitsrisiken
  • Durchführung von statischen und dynamischen Anwendungssicherheitstests (SAST/DAST) sowie Code-Analysen
  • Konfiguration und Absicherung von System-Images (AppLocker, Firewall, BitLocker, Netzwerkeinstellungen, Sicherheitspolicies)

**Beratung & Wissenstransfer:**

  • Schulung und Beratung von Kolleginnen und Kollegen zu Themen der Cybersicherheit
  • Erstellung und Pflege von Image-Checklisten
  • Fehleranalyse und Problemlösung im Bereich Image-Management
  • Signierung von Updates und Unterstützung bei Penetration Tests
  • Überwachung, Analyse und Bewertung von Schwachstellen und Sicherheitsvorfällen
  • Mitwirkung bei Cyber-Risikoanalysen und Sicherheitskonzepten

**Dein Profil:**

  • Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Studium (Bachelor oder Master) in Informatik, Software Development oder einem vergleichbaren Fachgebiet.
  • Du verfügst über Berufserfahrung mit .NET und setzt auf Clean Code sowie moderne Softwarearchitektur.
  • Themen wie Cybersicherheit sind dir vertraut, idealerweise bringst du hier bereits praktische Erfahrung mit.
  • Du kommunizierst sicher auf Deutsch und Englisch, sowohl mündlich als auch schriftlich.

**Darauf kannst du dich bei uns freuen:**

  • Ein familiäres, ehrliches und freundliches Miteinander
  • Arbeitsplatz mit Zukunft
  • Ein übertarifliches und attraktives Gehalt
  • Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld
  • Mit unserer Aktion -Mitarbeiter werben Mitarbeiter-

sicherst du dir eine Prämie

  • Und vieles mehr ….

**Du siehst dich in diesem interessanten Job?**

Dann bewerbe dich jetzt unter:
pforzheim@teampower-gmbh.de

Bei Rückfragen stehen wir dir gerne unter 07231 424790 oder persönlich in unserem Büro zur Verfügung.

title: Ehrenamtliche*r / Übungsleiter*in (d/w/m) im Fachbereich Leben und Wohnen
description:
Sie möchten sich sozial einbringen, wertvolle Erfahrungen sammeln und Menschen mit Behinderung im Alltag unterstützen? Wir suchen für unseren Dachbereich Leben und Wohnen Ehrenamtliche, die Lust haben sich zu engagieren und in einem sinnstiftenden Umfeld helfen möchten.

Schenken Sie Zeit und Lebensfreude. Als Alltagsbegleitung unterstützen Sie unsere Bewohnerinnen und Bewohner dabei, am gesellschaftlichen Leben teilzuhaben und individuelle Wünsche zu verwirklichen.

Ihre Aufgaben:

  • Einzelbegleitung: Sie begleiten Bewohnerinnen und Bewohner individuell bei Spaziergängen, kleinen Ausflügen, Einkäufen oder Kulturveranstaltungen.

  • Freizeitangebote: Sie unterstützen bei Angeboten im Haus oder gestalten diese eigenständig (z. B. Basteln, Vorlesen, Gesellschaftsspiele, Musik oder Kochen/Backen).

  • Soziales Miteinander: Sie schenken Zeit für persönliche Gespräche und gemeinsame Aktivitäten, um Einsamkeit vorzubeugen und die soziale Teilhabe zu stärken.

☐ Herzblut: Spaß an der Arbeit mit Menschen und eine positive Ausstrahlung.

☐ Zuverlässigkeit: Du bist verbindlich und man kann sich auf dich verlassen.

☐ Das gewisse Extra (kein Muss!): Eine Übungsleiter-Lizenz (z.B. C-Lizenz) oder pädagogische Vorerfahrung ist toll, aber keine Voraussetzung.

Hauptsächlich an Nachmittagen oder Wochenenden freuen wir uns über Ihre Unterstützung.


Aus datenschutzrechtlichen Gründen können wir Bewerbungen ausschließlich über unser Karriereportal verarbeiten.

Hört sich gut an? Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

title: Paid Marketing Internship
description:
A paid marketing internship at Atlas will provide you with the opportunity to jump-start your career by gaining practical experience in various areas of marketing while working in a well-established company. This is a great way for you to be able to apply your areas of study to hands-on work.


During your internship, you will be given the opportunity to gain experience in the day-to-day running of the marketing department. The main emphasis would be on assisting with administrative duties related to media bookings, invoicing, printing and signage orders, and basic design, whilst also contributing to the Marketing team’s various campaigns and projects.


To excel in this role you must:


  • Be personally motivated to learn
  • Be working towards achieving a Marketing degree
  • Have good organisational skills and attention to detail
  • Be ready to contribute ideas and form part of a dynamic and lively team
  • Knowledge of Adobe Photoshop and InDesign would be considered an asset but is not mandatory


During your internship, you will be given comprehensive training needed to work independently and productively in several different areas of marketing, with plenty of opportunities for on-the-job training.

We encourage you to apply so that we can get in touch with all the details!

title: Executive Secretary | Legal, Finance & HR Support
description:
• 5 Work Days Per Week
  • Current Office Near to Kaki Bukit MRT, Singapore
  • Will Relocate to Tai Seng MRT in August 2026
  • Medical & Dental Benefits Provided
  • Entitled to Yearly Bonus & Performance Bonus

ROLE PURPOSE

  • The Executive Secretary provides comprehensive administrative, secretarial, and reception support to the Singapore International Headquarters (IHQ), serving as a key administrative partner to the Legal, Finance, and Human Resources (HR) functions.
  • The role is responsible for coordinating legal and corporate documentation, executive administration, front-of-house reception, and general office operations, while supporting regional activities across the APAC entities to ensure efficient day-to-day business operations and a professional workplace environment.

LEGAL & DISCIPLINARY SECRETARIAT SUPPORT (50%)

  • Prepare, format, proofread, and file legal documents, contracts, NDAs, board resolutions, and corporate templates in line with group standards.
  • Maintain and organize the group's contract register, legal document repository, and corporate secretarial records across all APAC entities.
  • Support corporate secretarial matters including board minutes, statutory filings, entity KYC documentation, and coordination with company secretaries in each jurisdiction.
  • Track key contract dates, renewal deadlines, and compliance timelines, and flag upcoming actions to Legal Management.
  • Liaise with external law firms, notaries, and government agencies (e.g., ACRA, IRAS-related filings where applicable) on administrative matters.
  • Assist with trademark, IP, and licensing administration, including maintaining registers and supporting renewal / filing processes.
  • Assist with data gathering and document compilation for ongoing corporate matters, including transfer pricing documentation, entity restructuring, and regulatory correspondence, as directed.
  • Perform other duties as assigned by Legal Management to support the broader Finance, Legal and HR function of the Singapore IHQ.

ADMINISTRATION (50%)

  • Provide day-to-day administrative and secretarial support to the General Manager, APAC Legal Manager and HR Manager, including calendar management, meeting scheduling, and correspondence handling.
  • Manage front-desk / reception duties including greeting visitors, clients, and external counsel, and maintaining visitor sign-in and access records.
  • Coordinate meeting room bookings, set-up, and hospitality arrangements for internal meetings, board meetings, and external visits.
  • Liaise with building management, landlord, and facilities vendors on office maintenance, repairs, security access, and workplace safety matters.
  • Assist in organizing internal events, staff engagement activities, and off-site meetings.
  • Support ad hoc office matters

REQUIREMENTS

  1. Diploma on Business Administration, Paralegal Studies, or a related field.
  2. Minimum 2 – 4 years of experience in an executive assistance, legal assistant, paralegal, corporate secretarial, or legal administrative support role, ideally within a multinational or group corporate environment.
  3. Prior exposure to corporate secretarial practices and familiarity with ACRA / Singapore company law requirements is an advantage.
  4. Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to liaise professionally with internal stakeholders, external counsel, and government agencies across multiple jurisdictions.

title: AI Co-Founder / CTO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description:
We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups.

We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B+ companies.

Our offer:

  • A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to €500k in funding.

  • 1:1 sparring with unicorn founders on a weekly basis

  • Community: Access to the top 0.1% of founders, peers and investors

  • Team building: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals)

  • Distribution: Support in reaching product-market-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine

  • Funding support for securing a multi-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise > €2M after our Grand Pitch)

One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre-seed round by a first-time founder, securing a €12M pre-seed investment.

Tasks

  • You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as AI

  • You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management

  • You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs

  • You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding

  • You will iterate your product with us until having reached product-market-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively

  • You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate

  • You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M+ in revenues

  • You have excellent communication skills in the English language

title: Account Manager
description:
At AKQA (part of WPP), we believe in the imaginative application of art and science to create beautiful ideas, products and services. With an entrepreneurial and collaborative approach, we design adaptive digital services and applications that capture the imagination, delivering impressive returns for our clients.

Our Client Services teams are the champions for project excellence and enduring client relationships, being highly organised and credible sparring partners. They nurture and build client relationships founded on shared values and aspirations – and are motivated by work that makes a meaningful and sustained impact with a passion for our clients’ business, industry best practices and state-of-the-art techniques.

The Account Manager is responsible for developing strong client relationships and is committed to driving their business forward through innovative work that inspires and motivates audiences. The role requires the leadership of internal teams to deliver highly successful projects for the client and the agency. The role fosters effective teamwork within the team to drive results and positive change.

Located in Berlin, you will oversee work for global brands on both comms and digital projects. You will be responsible for developing strong client relationships and are committed to driving the business forward through innovative work that inspires and motivates audiences. You will be surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields.

ROLE REQUIREMENTS

  • Establish collaborative partnerships with key clients to deliver against business objectives
  • Facilitation of effective workflow between project team members and project streams.
  • Accountability for project performance and flawless execution.
  • Work with seniors to effectively manage client expectations, status, objectives, budgets and timelines.
  • Develops a deep understanding of their client’s business environment and audiences.
  • Ensures that the brand's strategies are communicated to internal teams
  • Markets field knowledge and expertise to client and ensure client remains educated on the latest and greatest
  • Successfully manages multiple suppliers, partners and clients to ensure we reach successful outcomes for both the client and AKQA.
  • Strong understanding of digital landscape and content production and best practices
  • Reviews deliverables to ensure they are 100% in-line with client expectations.
  • Demonstrates AKQA strategic thinking, e.g., able to formulate strategic propositions and communicate these via client-ready decks.
  • A positive, proactive and collaborative attitude and passion for innovative work that is both ground-breaking and change-making
  • Ensures AKQA shares relevant solutions that meet client business objectives.
  • Ability to manage international project teams in collaboration with Delivery under high time pressure.

QUALITIES AND CHARACTERISTICS

  • 2-4 years account management experience at a leading creative studio, digital agency, or similar organisation.
  • Successful track record of building client relationships; necessary capability, presence and professionalism to inspire confidence and credibility with clients and colleagues.
  • A passion for storytelling and innovative campaigns.
  • Fully proficient in English, German speaking is a plus
  • Foster a culture of proactivity, passion and energy for clients, businesses and projects.
  • Strong communication and presentation skills.
  • Embrace a culture of continuous improvement, championing diversity and inclusion.
  • Highly organised, adaptable, self-motivated, and able to solve problems.


We provide an extensive range of benefits. To find out more click here.

We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

AKQA is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

About WPP

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com.

title: Lead Software Engineer (Oracle PL/SQL)
description:
Why This Role Matters

This role focuses on software development and technical support, particularly in Oracle PL/SQL environments, involving building, testing, and optimizing applications based on specifications. It includes troubleshooting issues, supporting system implementation, and ensuring performance efficiency while delivering reliable, high-quality solutions.

How You’ll Make a Difference

  • Develop and test programs according to functional and/or technical specification within timed estimates as part of a team and on individual capacity during full development cycle or as per individual user request from clients.
  • Analyze and tune programs to meet optimum performance and operational efficiency.
  • Design and/or propose sound technical solutions for elementary and fundamental requirements.
  • Review gap/functional specifications and map the requirements to standard functionalities of the product. Analyze the potential scope and risk of the proposed business requirements and prepare appropriate document as needed including justifiable estimated change effort for elementary and fundamental requirements.
  • Troubleshoot and correct software defects during development, implementation and maintenance.
  • Refer unresolved problems to senior team members for technical and functional guidance.
  • Provide technical and application support to clients and other internal groups.
  • Assist the bank in migrating their legacy data to ensure adherence to existing strategy.
  • Assist in any ad-hoc project tasks as assigned and necessary.
  • Execute technical and functional tasks with minimum supervision from senior team member.
  • Apply functional and technical knowledge of core banking modules, including Limits and Risk Management, General Ledger, Trade Finance, Bank Guarantees, Deposits, Lending, when analyzing requirements, designing solutions, troubleshooting issues, and supporting implementation activities.

What You’ll Bring

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, Software Engineering or a related field.
  • Minimum 10 years of related working experience.
  • Strong working knowledge of SQL and PL/SQL, including writing and optimizing complex queries and database objects.
  • Solid understanding of relational database management systems (RDBMS), particularly Oracle Database.
  • Experience in optimizing SQL and PL/SQL performance to support high-volume, mission-critical transaction processing systems.
  • Experience working across multiple operating systems, including Windows and Linux/Unix.
  • Familiarity with Oracle development tools such as Oracle Forms Builder, Reports Builder, and SQL Developer.
  • Strong understanding of database design concepts and data modeling.
  • XML & Oracle Data Types: Experience in XML processing and Oracle-specific data types, including XMLTYPE,CLOB, BLOB, and user-defined types (OBJECT, VARRAY, nested tables).
  • Messaging (Oracle AQ): Familiarity with Oracle Advanced Queuing (AQ), including queue management and message-based asynchronous processing, is an advantage.
  • Tools &Utilities: Experience with SQL*Plus, TOAD, Oracle SQL Developer, or similar tools.
  • Version Control: Proficient in Git and/or Subversion.
  • Experience working in Agile environments, with a strong understanding of Agile methodologies and software delivery practices.
  • Functional and technical knowledge of core banking systems, such as Limits and Risk Management, General Ledger, Trade Finance, Bank Guarantees, Deposits and Lending, is an advantage.
  • Proven ability to write clean, well-designed, efficient, and thoroughly documented code.
  • Demonstrated expertise in developing testable code and ensuring high-quality software delivery.
  • Willing to travel and accept overseas assignments.
